The stories they have to tell will be shared across the [Root.Owner.GetName] but for now, let the party begin! The crowds cheer as the starship flies past Starfleet HQ.
STH_caretaker.1005.desc_02: News has reached the senate and even the most stoic senator cannot help but raise a small smile - The Havra and her crew, long thought dead, have returned home. The inquisition can wait for tonight and even the Tal Shiar are prepared to hold on for the moment, for tonight the Empire celebrates the return of a lost bird back to the nest is worthy of a cask of Romulan Ale.
STH_caretaker.1005.desc_03: The public announcement screens across Cardassia Prime all have the same story - Gul Evek and the Vetar have returned to Cardassian space! This is a triumph for Cardassian resilience and engineering and testament to the nature of the Cardassian people. Investigations will happen in private and the tactical debrief will occur behind closed doors, but for today let the kanar flow and let the rest of the Quadrant know the Cardassian people will prevail!
STH_caretaker.1005.name: The Endgame
STH_caretaker.1006.desc_01: Now that the crew of Voyager has had a chance to reunite with their families, and reports have been submitted. It is time to reward those members of the crew that made outstanding contributions and represented everything that Starfleet strives to be.
STH_caretaker.1006.desc_02: Now that the crew of Havra has had a chance to reunite with their families, and reports have been submitted. It is time to reward those members of the crew that made outstanding contributions and represented everything that makes the Romulan race superior.
STH_caretaker.1006.desc_03: Now that the crew of the Vetar has had a chance to reunite with their families, and reports have been submitted. It is time to reward those members of the crew that made outstanding contributions and represented everything that makes us proud to be Cardassian.
STH_caretaker.1006.name: The Endgame - Home
STH_caretaker.101.desc: After the staff briefing, the senior team point out that they have enough remains from the first caretaker to investigate a possible toxin that could be useful in the event the new Caretaker is not as benevolent as the last one. This would pose quite the moral dilemma, but in times like these sometimes the hard decisions need to be made.
STH_caretaker.101.name: Cold Fires - A New Array
STH_caretaker.102.desc: As the ship approaches the Array a previously inactive pylon sparks into life, a high energy pulse is fired towards the ship, but luckily only manages to make a glancing blow. Even so consoles spark and the shields stutter under the impact, - it is clear that this array, whilst smaller, is no less advanced or powerful as the Array that brought us here in the first place. Worryingly that appeared to have only been a warning shot. What do we do Captain?
STH_caretaker.102.name: Cold Fires - A New Array
STH_caretaker.103.c: It seems we have no choice.
STH_caretaker.103.desc: Welcome, [Root.GetName] we apologise for the attack, but there are many species after the power that lies within this station. We are the Ocampa and we journed here with our Caretaker, Suspiria many generations ago. Our Caretaker has allowed us to evolved beyond the kin we left behind and now we speak for her in all matters. We recognise that you were brought here against your will wish to meet with you so that we can seek to explain to our beloved Suspiria what has become of her mate and companion.
STH_caretaker.103.name: Cold Fires - The Ocampa
STH_caretaker.104.desc: Whilst it is clear that the Ocampa are far more evolved than their kin back on Ocampa V, [intrepidAdmiral.GetName] is certain that they are only a mouthpiece for the elusive Suspiria and that if the crew are going to make it home then only the new Caretaker could make this happen. Pleading with the representatives the crew implore the Ocampa to obtain an audience with Suspiria. At the mention of this the lead member of their guests goes very pale, 'you would want that' he says in a quiet voice, this time the Captain insists louder, very quietly the Ocampa point to the conference room table and in unison whisper ... she is here.
STH_caretaker.104.name: Cold Fires - The Meeting
STH_caretaker.105.desc: Without warning a small child, no older than ten, appears in the centre of the room. On first glance she appears innocent, almost angelic, but on deeper inspection there is a pain, worse an anger behind those still eyes that betrayed the inescapable fact that this is no child. A voice fills the mind of all onboard and in an piercing voice states, you killed my love, you are the ones that destroyed my companion and now you will suffer... Then all that the crew could feel was pain, as if the very fabric of space was tearing them apart and not just the crew, the very ship itself screamed with the raw emotion that emanated from the child. A pain and power that could destroy them all.
STH_caretaker.105.name: The Second Caretaker
STH_caretaker.106.desc: The plees of the crew have no impact on the small child, who continues to repeat herself over and over whilst the ship and crew scream and shudder under the onslaught.
STH_caretaker.106.name: The Pain Continues
STH_caretaker.107.desc: you killed my love, you are the ones that destroyed my companion and now you will suffer... you killed my love, you are the ones that destroyed my companion and now you will suffer... you killed my love, you are the ones that destroyed my companion and now you will suffer...
STH_caretaker.107.name: The Pain Increases
STH_caretaker.108.a: He ASKED us to do it.
STH_caretaker.108.desc: The ship is now vibrating at such a frequency and all the crew know is the fury of the small innocent looking child, they now only have seconds left.
STH_caretaker.108.name: ...Almost Too Much to Handle
STH_caretaker.109.desc: As suddenly as it started the pain stops, and the ship stops trying to tear itself apart. The child finally looks the Captain in the eyes. This time when the voice is heard it is softer, ''I have read your minds and I can see what you were asked to do, I am glad to know that my love was able to complete the mission which I could not.''
STH_caretaker.109.name: Cold Fires - Suspiria
STH_caretaker.11.desc:
The crew wakes up on a tranquil farmhouse scene, the only other person in sight is an old man playing the banjo.

He explains that he is here to undo a wrong caused many years ago, but that you cannot help him and he must continue his work before time runs out.

Before the Captain can ask any more questions the crew are returned to the Ship, where sensors have picked up a small unidentified vessel being chased by three more powerful ships.
STH_caretaker.11.name: Aunt Adah's Farm
STH_caretaker.110.desc: The intense pain is finally too much for those onboard to handle and one by one the crew collapse into unconsciousness. The relief is all too brief as the sound of the structural integrity field giving way fills the remaining air as the ship disintegrates.
STH_caretaker.110.name: Cold Fires - The End
STH_caretaker.112.desc: After contemplating the request Suspiria finally agrees, and speaks ''I will undo this final wrong done to you and her people. Prepare to go home.''
STH_caretaker.112.name: Cold Fires - Time to Go Home
STH_caretaker.113.desc_01: Although she did not admit it, the Captain could sense that Suspiria was relieved that the Ocampans were not being abandoned to their fate. ''I make my way to Exosia knowing that I have finally paid our debt.''
STH_caretaker.113.desc_02: The child shakes her head slowly, ''I am sorry I cannot grant this request, I cannot cause further destruction just to undo this wrong, however bad I feel about what has happened to you all.''
STH_caretaker.113.name: Cold Fires - The Next Chapter
STH_caretaker.115.desc: On the distant planet of Ocampa V orbiting a star 70,000 light years from the Alpha Quadrant a small band of Ocampa head towards the settlement of their ancestors. Who knows what the future has in store?
STH_caretaker.115.name: The Ocampan Renaissance
STH_caretaker.116.desc: On the distant planet of Ocampa V orbiting a star 70,000 light years from the Alpha Quadrant dust and sand collect, no sign of any visitors and the future for those on this planet remains as bleak as ever.
STH_caretaker.116.name: The Ocampan Renaissance
STH_caretaker.12.desc: Our weapons appear to have no impact on the giant structure, Tactical reports a massive power build up from within the Array, without warning it unleashed a massive power surge towards its attacker. The Captain only has time to utter one last command.
STH_caretaker.12.name: Open Fire on the Caretaker Array
STH_caretaker.13.d: Alien lives do not concern us.
STH_caretaker.13.desc: The vessel opens hailing frequency claiming to be a trader from the [contact_species_delta.GetSpeciesName] and urgently needs help. They are willing to offer whatever assistance they can provide and claim to be an expert in this region. The enemy vessels are closing in and it's clear the small ship would stand no chance of surviving without help, but our ship is in no shape for a confrontation right now.
STH_caretaker.13.name: An intervention
STH_caretaker.14.desc_01: As we move to intervene the alien vessels fall back firing a warning shot causing minor damage, but swiftly fall back to a respectable distance. The alien visitor is most impressed and over excited to join the crew, promising to help in whatever way they can. Impressed by our actions the image of the old banjo player appears on screen. The Alien claims to be the Caretaker and he is looking to repay a great debt to the small primitive civilisation on the planet below. With a sadness in his eyes he explains how he is from a race of explorers whose technology inadvertently destroyed the eco-system of the planet and he has been their caretaker ever since, but his end is near... and he has been seeking a replacement.\n\nThe caretaker expresses a wish that he could return all those taken home but only has enough power for one journey or to secure the future of those living on the planet below.
STH_caretaker.14.desc_02:
The alien vessels open fire on the small ship which doesn't stand a chance though the extra time has allowed us to restore a number of ship systems and to treat our injured crew. Clearly disappointed by our lack of action the image of the old banjo player appears on screen.

The Alien claims to be the Caretaker and he is looking to repay a great debt to the small primitive civilisation on the planet below. With a determination in his eyes he explains how he is from a race of explorers whose technology inadvertently destroyed the eco-system of the planet below, and he has been their caretaker ever since.

Unable to find a suitable replacement before he dies, the Caretaker is determined to secure the future of his charges. When pressed he explains that he has enough power to send you home, but it would mean the death of all those he has protected for so long.
STH_caretaker.14.name: A debt that can never be repaid
STH_caretaker.150.b: This feels like a trap, cut communications.
STH_caretaker.150.desc_01: Captain, our long range sensors are picking up a communication coming in on a federation channel... 'This is Captain Ransom of the Federation Starship Equinox! We're under attack! We need assistance!'\n\nThe computer is aware of the name, the USS Equinox was lost and presumed destroyed many months earlier. How did they end up in the Delta Quadrant? What are your orders Captain?
STH_caretaker.150.desc_02: Commander, our long range sensors are picking up a communication coming in with Tal Shiar codes... 'This is Sub-Commander Charvanek of the Romulan Warbird Eisn ! We're under attack! We need assistance!' Rekar served in the imperial academy with Charvanek, which is why he knew that the sub-commander was last reported to be on a secret mission out in the far reaches of Romulan space. How did they end up in the Delta Quadrant? With no time to ponder this question, Rekar orders the helm officer to display the ships location. What are your orders?
STH_caretaker.150.desc_03: Gul Evek, our long range sensors are picking up a communication coming in on a channel we recognise, it appears to be a Cardassian frequency... 'This is Glinn Dukain of the CDS Bestenes! We're under attack! We need assistance!' The name was instantly recognisable to Evek, as he was second cousins with Dukain and this is why he knew the vessel was reported destroyed fighting raiders along the border over eight years ago. How did they end up in the Delta Quadrant? With no time to ponder this question, Evek orders the helm officer to display the ships location. What are your orders sir?
STH_caretaker.150.name: Equinox
STH_caretaker.151.desc_01: Voyager arrives on the scene, the Equinox is heavily damaged and being swarmed by assailants, its shields failing and fires are clearly visible beneath the exposed hull. The science ship was only meant for localised missions and is obviously no match for whoever the attackers are. Going to red alert Voyager flew in weapons hot, the superior firepower of the Intrepid Class ship made short work of the unknown aliens. The Equinox is safe for now.
STH_caretaker.151.desc_02: The Havra arrives into the battle weapons firing, the Eisn is heavily damaged and being swarmed by assailants, its shields failing and fires are clearly visible beneath the exposed hull. The state of the art weapons on the Havra are enough to cause the attackers to withdraw. The Eisn is safe for now.
STH_caretaker.151.desc_03: Adopting a fire first approach the Vetar unleashes a barrage of torpedoes at the vessels swarming the Bestenes, which was rapidly losing shields and life support. The smaller ship appears to be in much worse shape than previously thought, multiple scorch makes adorn the hull and it looks like rescue could not have come soon enough.
STH_caretaker.151.name: Equinox - Under Attack
STH_caretaker.152.desc_01: Captain Ransom stands in front of Janeway, it's clear from the state of his uniform that the crew of the Equinox have had a tough ride. The captain explains how they were surveying a nebula before being dragged into the Delta Quadrant by a being called the Caretaker. It's clear from the story that the smaller ship has suffered greatly and lacking the resources of its sister ship has held itself together with determination. Ransom explains that some months ago they discovered a race of Nucleogenic Aliens who can be used as fuel to greatly improve the speed in which the ship travels. Ransom explains that they managed to collect enough lifeforms to make one last jump which should take them back to the Alpha Quadrant, however the Equinox may not be able to make the journey. The aliens that attacked them were determined to have revenge and would have succeeded had Voyager not arrived on time. Ransom offers to provide the technology and help both crews return home. The crew sit in stunned silence, the first voice to speak up is the Science officer, 'I will not support this Captain, this is inhumane and I will do everything in my power to prevent you from taking this course of action, this is my duty as a Starfleet officer'.
STH_caretaker.152.desc_02: Charvanek stands in front of Rekar, it's clear from the state of her uniform and the scars on her brow that she is not alone in suffering during the journey home. The Sub-Commander explains how they on a mission for the Tal Shiar before being dragged into the Delta Quadrant by a being called the Caretaker. It's clear from the story that the smaller ship has suffered greatly and lacking the resources of its sister ship has held itself together with determination. Charvanek explains that some months ago they discovered a race of Nucleogenic Aliens who can be used as fuel to greatly improve the speed in which the ship travels, this research will greatly benefit the Empire. Charvanek explains that they managed to collect enough lifeforms to make one last jump which should take them back to Romulan space, however her ship is not in any fit shape to be able to make the journey. The aliens that attacked them were determined to have revenge and would have succeeded had Havra not arrived on time. Charvanek offers to provide the technology and help both crews return home. The crew sit in stunned silence, the first voice to speak up is the Science officer, 'I will not support this sir, this is monstrous and I will do everything in my power to prevent you from taking this course of action, I will not let this sully the name of the Romulan Star Empire'.
STH_caretaker.152.desc_03: Dukain stands in front of his second cousin, Evek, it's clear from the state of his uniform that the crew of the Bestenes has had a tough voyage. The Glinn explains how they were following a raider into a nebula before being dragged into the Delta Quadrant by a being called the Caretaker. It's clear from the story that the smaller ship has suffered greatly and lacking the resources of its fellow warship it has held itself together with Cardassian will power. Dukain explains that some months ago they discovered a race of Nucleogenic Aliens who can be used as fuel to greatly improve the speed in which the ship travels. Dukain explains that they managed to collect enough lifeforms to make one last jump which should take them back to the Alpha Quadrant, however his ship may not be able to make the journey. The aliens that attacked them were determined to have revenge and would have succeeded had Evek and his crew not arrived on time. Dukain offers to provide the technology and help both crews return home. The crew sit in stunned silence, the first voice to speak up is the Science officer, 'I will not support this Gul Evek, this is not the Cardassian way and I will do everything in my power to prevent you from taking this course of action'.
STH_caretaker.152.desc_04: Captain Ransom stands in front of Janeway, it's clear from the state of his uniform that the crew of the Equinox have had a tough ride. The captain explains how they were surveying a nebula before being dragged into the Delta Quadrant by a being called the Caretaker. It's clear from the story that the smaller ship has suffered greatly and lacking the resources of its sister ship has held itself together with determination. Ransom explains that some months ago they discovered a race of Nucleogenic Aliens who can be used as fuel to greatly improve the speed in which the ship travels. Ransom explains that they managed to collect enough lifeforms to make one last jump which should take them back to the Alpha Quadrant, however the Equinox may not be able to make the journey. The aliens that attacked them were determined to have revenge and would have succeeded had Voyager not arrived on time. Ransom offers to provide the technology and help both crews return home.
STH_caretaker.152.desc_05: Charvanek stands in front of Rekar, it's clear from the state of his uniform and the scars on her brow that she is not alone in suffering during the journey home. The Sub-Commander explains how they on a mission for the Tal Shiar before being dragged into the Delta Quadrant by a being called the Caretaker. It's clear from the story that the smaller ship has suffered greatly and lacking the resources of its sister ship has held itself together with determination. Charvanek explains that some months ago they discovered a race of Nucleogenic Aliens who can be used as fuel to greatly improve the speed in which the ship travels, this research will greatly benefit the Empire Charvanek explains that they managed to collect enough lifeforms to make one last jump which should take them back to Romulan space, however her ship is not in any fit shape to be able to make the journey. The aliens that attacked them were determined to have revenge and would have succeeded had Havra not arrived on time. the Tal Shiar officer offers to provide the technology and help both crews return home.
STH_caretaker.152.desc_06: Dukain stands in front of his second cousin, Evek, it's clear from the state of his uniform that the crew of the Bestenes has had a tough voyage. The Glinn explains how they were following a raider into a nebula before being dragged into the Delta Quadrant by a being called the Caretaker. It's clear from the story that the smaller ship has suffered greatly and lacking the resources of its fellow warship it has held itself together with Cardassian will power. Dukain explains that some months ago they discovered a race of Nucleogenic Aliens who can be used as fuel to greatly improve the speed in which the ship travels. Dukain explains that they managed to collect enough lifeforms to make one last jump which should take them back to the Alpha Quadrant, however his ship may not be able to make the journey. The aliens that attacked them were determined to have revenge and would have succeeded had Evek and his crew not arrived on time. Dukain offers to provide the technology and help both crews return home.
STH_caretaker.152.name: Equinox - A Moral Decision
STH_caretaker.153.desc: [intrepidAdmiral.GetName] I cannot let you do this and I will not help integrate the technology. I have other members of the crew who feel the same way. You will have to kill me, sir reconsider your decision.
STH_caretaker.153.name: Equinox - Dissent
STH_caretaker.154.desc: The crews combined and the Nucleogenic alien bodies attached to the engines, the Captain gives the command. Travelling several hundred times faster than thought possible, the ship arrives back in the Alpha Quadrant, after many years of toil the crew are home. The crews morals may have been left behind, but that is small concern next to the sight of recognisable stars.
STH_caretaker.154.name: Equinox - Home, the Bad Way
STH_caretaker.155.a: A tragic situation - resume the journey home.
STH_caretaker.155.desc: The Captain knew in their heart that the offer could not be accepted, it goes against everything they stand for and looking at the ship makes them feel sick and furious. Ordering security to apprehend the officers from the other ship, however they were too quick managing to beam back to their ship. The vessel powers up its weapons and opens fire, desperate to avoid opening fire [intrepidAdmiral.GetName] pleads for them to surrender, but they do not listen. Firing a barrage of weapons fire leaving no choice but for the ship to defend itself, unfortunately the smaller ship had suffered too much damage and in a blinding flash of light its core breached and the ship explodes.
STH_caretaker.155.name: Equinox - Righting a Wrong
STH_caretaker.157.desc: The aliens defeated, but at the cost of numerous lives, was this worth sacrificing everything we stood for.
STH_caretaker.157.name: Nucleogenic Aliens Defeated
STH_caretaker.16.a: This technology is not safe in your hands. Destroy the Caretaker Array!
STH_caretaker.16.b: This technology is not safe in your hands. Destroy the Caretaker Array!
STH_caretaker.16.c: This technology is not safe in your hands. Destroy the Caretaker Array!
STH_caretaker.16.d: This technology is not safe in your hands. Destroy the Caretaker Array!
STH_caretaker.16.desc_01: Greetings [voyaging_ship_leader.GetName] we are the [delta_quadrant_hostile_1.GetSpeciesName] and we have long had ambitions to secure the Array in this system for ourselves. We have been outcast from our society and apologise for the earlier misunderstanding.\n\nThis technology will give us the power to rise up and undo the wrongs brought upon us. We overheard your message and know that you want to return home. Do so with our blessing and leave the Array in our safe hands. Think of your crew Captain.
STH_caretaker.16.desc_02: Greetings [voyaging_ship_leader.GetName] we are the [delta_quadrant_hostile_1.GetSpeciesName] we see and admire your impressive vessel, your technology is most impressive, we have long had ambitions to secure the Array in this system for ourselves, having been outcast from our society to fend for ourselves. This technology will give us the power to rise up and undo the wrongs brought upon us. We overheard your message and know that you want to return home. Do so with our blessing and leave the Array in our safe hands, think of your crew wise Captain.
STH_caretaker.16.e: We must go home; the Array is yours.
STH_caretaker.16.f: We must go home; the Array is yours.
STH_caretaker.16.g: We must go home; the Array is yours.
STH_caretaker.16.h: We must go home; the Array is yours.
STH_caretaker.16.name: Incoming Transmission from [delta_quadrant_hostile_1.GetSpeciesName].
STH_caretaker.18.desc_01: The Caretaker sends one final message 'thank you' and a burst of energy towards the small planet before lowering its shields. Voyager raises its shields and fires two high yield torpedoes which causes a chain reaction within the ancient station. The shockwave encompasses the hostile ships who beat a hasty retreat.\n\nOnce the dust settles Captain Janeway knows that in securing the future of the innocent beings unaware of the action above them, she has trapped her crew and ship so many miles from home. But isn't this the duty of every Starfleet Captain and a mission to explore strange new worlds and go where no man has gone before...
STH_caretaker.18.desc_02: The Caretaker sends one final message acknowledgement and a burst of energy towards the small planet before lowering its shields. The Havra raises its shields and fires two high yield torpedoes which causes a chain reaction within the ancient station. The shockwave encompasses the hostile ships who beat a hasty retreat. Once the dust settles Commander Rekar knows that with knowledge comes power but at the cost of a chance to return home. However the Commander knows that Havra means 'voyager' in his native tongue and this new domain could bring untold advantages for the empire.
STH_caretaker.18.desc_03: The Caretaker acknowledges the sacrifice of the Vetar and sends a burst of energy towards the small planet before lowering its shields. Vetar raises its shields and fires two high yield torpedoes which causes a chain reaction within the ancient station. The shockwave encompasses the hostile ships who beat a hasty retreat. Once the calm is restored Gul Evek knows that the sacrifices made today could lead to benefits for all Cardassians and ultimately it is the duty of all loyal Cardassians to serve the Union.
STH_caretaker.18.name: The adventure continues
STH_caretaker.2.desc: Long-range scans have picked up the last known location of the missing ship and the crew are ready to depart eager to test out the capabilities of the new vessel.\n\nMorale is high and the crew are eager to return home after putting Voyager through her paces.
STH_caretaker.2.name: For I dipt into the future' ...
STH_caretaker.200.desc_01: Captains Log: We have identified a conduit inside a nebula, which appears to open a gateway. Our science team believe that it could pose our best and last chance of making it home. It is unclear who or what built the conduit. The crew are excited and determined a chance to get home doesn't come around often and they are committed to do everything in their power to get home. Voyager has been our home and has gotten us through impossible situations before, one last push and we can complete this journey I will offer the crew the choice in the morning.
STH_caretaker.200.desc_02: Captains Log: We have identified a conduit inside a nebula, which appears to open a gateway. Our science team believe that it could pose our best and last chance of making it home. It is unclear who or what built the conduit. The crew are excited and determined a chance to get home doesn't come around often and they are committed to do everything in their power to get home. I will offer the crew the choice in the morning.
STH_caretaker.200.desc_03: Captains Log: We have identified a conduit inside a nebula, which appears to open a gateway. Our science team believe that it could pose our best and last chance of making it home. It is unclear who or what built the conduit. The crew are excited and determined a chance to get home doesn't come around often and they are committed to do everything in their power to get home. I will offer the crew the choice in the morning.
STH_caretaker.200.name: Endgame
STH_caretaker.201.desc: Our mission home has just escalated, on entering the nebula we detected Undine ship signatures. This is clearly the staging ground for an eventual attack! It's unclear when or where they plan to attack, but with this technology any power in the Alpha Quadrant could be within their sights. We have no choice we cannot let this facility remain active.
STH_caretaker.201.name: Endgame - Staging Ground
STH_caretaker.202.a: Sacrifice the ship, this conduit must be destroyed.
STH_caretaker.202.c.response: The science officer steps forward, using the deflector dish we could send out a pulse that will overload the conduits ability to tap into Fluidic space, this will cut our journey time home, we could warn everyone.
STH_caretaker.202.d.response: We could modify the cure we investigated into a weapon, it will do the most damage and permanently shut down the conduit. With luck it will destroy the Undine vessels on the way through.
STH_caretaker.202.desc_01: Federation scum, we are the Undine - the weak will perish and your species are next. You are too late, we have already sent an advanced party through the conduit towards your homeworld.
STH_caretaker.202.desc_02: Romulan scum, we are the Undine - the weak will perish and your species are next. You are too late, we have already sent an advanced party through the conduit towards your homeworld.
STH_caretaker.202.desc_03: Cardassian scum, we are the Undine - the weak will perish and your species are next. You are too late, we have already sent an advanced party through the conduit towards your homeworld.
STH_caretaker.202.name: Endgame
STH_caretaker.203.desc_01: Species of the Alpha Quadrant - prepare to perish.
STH_caretaker.203.desc_02: Species of the Alpha Quadrant - prepare to perish.
STH_caretaker.203.desc_03: Species of the Alpha Quadrant - prepare to perish.
STH_caretaker.203.name: Endgame
STH_caretaker.205.desc: Multiple sectors are reporting strange conduits opening up and strange alien vessels coming through, they appear hostile and have powered up their weapons. All available vessels report to your fleet locations, we must defend ourselves.
STH_caretaker.205.name: Endgame - The Fluidic Invasion
STH_caretaker.206.desc: Multiple sectors are reporting strange conduits opening up and strange alien vessels coming through, they appear hostile and have powered up their weapons. All available vessels report to your fleet locations, we must defend ourselves.
STH_caretaker.206.name: Endgame - The Fluidic Invasion
STH_caretaker.208.a: Time to welcome home our voyagers.
STH_caretaker.208.desc: The Undine fleets have been pushed back into Fluidic space and we can see no further signs of them encroaching on our territories, now is the time to regroup for this is only a taste of their full power. Now is the time to celebrate our victory and welcome back our missing adventurers.
STH_caretaker.208.name: Endgame - Victory
STH_caretaker.215.a: The rifts are an opportunity, leave them open.
STH_caretaker.215.desc: Following our efforts to repel the Undine threat and the destruction of their invasion fleets, our scientists believe with a little research they can permanently close the rifts into our space and rid the threat of the Undine from our borders.
STH_caretaker.215.name: The Undine Rifts
STH_caretaker.216.desc: Our scientists believe a concentrated burst of tetryon radiation should be enough to destablise the rifts and close them for good, cutting off our access to Fluidic space and preventing further incursions from the Undine.
STH_caretaker.216.name: The Undine Rifts
STH_caretaker.3.desc: Suddenly a blinding burst of light fills the screen onboard the USS Voyager, someone reports a massive displacement wave inbound. With nowhere to run the Captain orders the crew to brace for impact.
STH_caretaker.3.name: ... Far as the human eye could see
STH_caretaker.4.desc: Despite all the preparation, conditions inside the nebula are proving far more dangerous than anticipated. Ion Storms rock the shields and the containment fields struggle to keep the ship intact. Just as all seems lost the science officer reports a massive inbound displacement wave.
STH_caretaker.4.name: ... Far as the human eye could see
STH_caretaker.40.desc_01: The Starship Voyager's first few months in this new land have been dangerous, for every new lifeform and anomaly to investigate there have been hostile species, eager to get their hands or claws on Federation technology. Thanks to Federation ingenuity the ship and crew remain intact and as determined as ever to return home.\n\nThe next few months will take the ship through a particularly hostile area of space, caution must be in place, but the Captain maintains that Voyager will keep up the proud Starfleet traditions and ideals. Maintaining the current course will be risky, but changing course will add years to the return home.
STH_caretaker.40.desc_02: The Havra's first few months in this new land have been dangerous, for every new lifeform and anomaly to investigate there have been hostile species, eager to get their hands or claws on all that makes the empire powerful. The determination of the loyal subjects of the empire remains intact and resolved to return home.\n\nThe next few months will take the ship through a particularly hostile area of space, caution must be in place, but the Commander will ensure that all that makes the Romulan naval proud will continue, even in these strange lands. Reports confirm that maintaining the current course will be risky, but changing course will add years to the return home.
STH_caretaker.40.desc_03: This area of space is harsh and hostile, but nothing that any Cardassian cant handle, the races in this space are primitive in comparison, but in enough numbers pose a risk to the safety of the Vetar. The next few months will take the ship through a particularly hostile area of space, caution must be in place, but Gul Evek is determined that the proud traditions of the Cardassian people will endure.\n\n Reports confirm that maintaining the current course will be risky, but changing course will add years to the return home.
STH_caretaker.40.name: Basics
STH_caretaker.41.a: Set a course, but go in easy. We don't want to provoke a new fight.
STH_caretaker.41.b: Red Alert. We cannot take any risks.
STH_caretaker.41.desc: Long range scans have detected a distress call coming from deeper within the nearby system, the ship reports that it has been under attack and is drifting out of control, they have a VIP passenger onboard with expert local knowledge and will defend themselves if needed.
STH_caretaker.41.name: Distress Call
STH_caretaker.42.desc: As the ship enters scanning range the chief engineer detects a sudden power surge, too late to raise shields or move out of the blast range the ship is rocked by the explosion. Damage reports shows extensive damage to internal systems. Intruder alert alarms ring out, it is clear the ship is lost.
STH_caretaker.42.name: Ambush
STH_caretaker.43.a: We fought valiantly, but we must surrender.
STH_caretaker.43.desc: As the ship approaches the target vessel the science officer picks up a large build-up of energy - the self destruct has been set! Unable to escape the full force of the shock wave the ship is only able to move a small distance away from the blast zone. Damage reports indicate that internal defences and communications are down. Suddenly the intruder alert alarm sounds...\n\nThe crew fight valiantly but caught unaware are unable to repel the invading aliens. As the doors to the bridge are breached, the captain has no choice to surrender to spare the lives of the crew.
STH_caretaker.43.name: Ambush
STH_caretaker.44.a: Despite striking some blows, we have to surrender.
STH_caretaker.44.desc: As the ship approaches the target vessel the science officer picks up a large build-up of energy - the self destruct has been set! Unable to escape the full force of the shock wave the ship is only able to move a small distance away from the blast zone. Damage reports indicate that internal defences and communications are down. Suddenly the intruder alert alarm sounds as boarders enter the ship.\n\nThe crew fight valiantly managing to kill a number of the attackers, but caught unaware and outnumbered the crew are unable to repel the invading aliens. As the doors to the bridge are breached, the captain has no choice to surrender to spare the lives of the crew.
STH_caretaker.44.name: Ambush
STH_caretaker.45.a: We put up a worthy fight, but we have no option.
STH_caretaker.45.desc: As the ship approaches the target vessel the science officer picks up a large build-up of energy - the self destruct has been set! Unable to escape the full force of the shock wave the ship is only able to move a small distance away from the blast zone. Damage reports indicate that internal defences and communications are down. Suddenly the intruder alert alarm sounds!\n\n The crew fight valiantly managing to wound a number of the attackers, but caught unaware and outnumbered the crew are unable to repel the invading aliens. As the doors to the bridge are breached, the captain has no choice to surrender to spare the lives of the crew.
STH_caretaker.45.name: Ambush
STH_caretaker.46.a: We have no option but surrender.
STH_caretaker.46.desc: As the ship approaches the target vessel the science officer picks up a large build-up of energy - the self destruct has been set! Unable to escape the full force of the shock wave the ship is only able to move a small distance away from the blast zone. Damage reports indicate that internal defences and communications are down. Suddenly the intruder alert alarm sounds...\n\nThe crew fight valiantly but take many casualties, caught unaware and outnumbered the crew are unable to repel the invading aliens. As the doors to the bridge are breached, the captain has no choice to surrender to spare the lives of the remaining crew.
STH_caretaker.46.name: Ambush
STH_caretaker.47.desc: The crew look on as their attacker's limp away with their damaged ship, having been marooned on this strange and hostile planet. A wave of fear ripples through the remaining crew and the distant rumble of a very active volcano can be heard in the distance, [intrepidAdmiral.GetName] knows that the only hope the crew has, is to keep moving and seek out the essentials. Knowing that volunteers will be few, it is time to make a decision on who to send on a scouting mission, that could very well determine the fate of all under their command.
STH_caretaker.47.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.48.desc: Despite their best efforts, the Science Officer was unable to find any sustenance, the crew morale drops, but we cannot give up.
STH_caretaker.48.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.49.desc: Great news! The Science Officer returns with a selection of wild berries, grubs and the location of a fresh water supply, enough to keep the crew alive for a number of weeks.
STH_caretaker.49.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.5.desc: [Root.Owner.GetName], our follow up recovery crews have only been able to withstand the effects of the nebula for a limited time, but they have uncovered what appears to be the wreckage of our lost ship. There were no survivors.
STH_caretaker.5.name: Vessel lost with all hands
STH_caretaker.50.desc: Tragic news, after no sign of the officer for a number of hours a search party is sent out to investigate. All they could find of the officer was a blood stained remnant of uniform.
STH_caretaker.50.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.500.desc: Long-range scans have picked up the last known location of the small raider, which is attempting to evade our forces by hiding deeper in [caretaker_start_location.GetName]. Our forces are determined to show these fools the price of messing with the great Cardassian people. The Vetar is fully stocked with torpedoes and read to depart.
STH_caretaker.500.name: Caretaker - The Departure
STH_caretaker.51.desc: Great news! The Captain returns with a selection of wild berries, grubs and the location of a fresh water supply, enough to keep the crew alive for a number of weeks.
STH_caretaker.51.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.52.desc: Despite their best efforts, the Captain was unable to find any sustenance and with a slight limp, the crew morale drops, but cannot give up.
STH_caretaker.52.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.53.desc: Tragic news, after no sign of the officer for a number of hours a search party is sent out to investigate. All they could find of the officer was a blood stained remnant of uniform.
STH_caretaker.53.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.54.desc: A cheer erupts as the crew member returns with a selection of wild berries, grubs and the location of a fresh water supply, enough to keep the crew alive for a number of weeks.
STH_caretaker.54.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.55.a: It is not worth the risk.
STH_caretaker.55.desc: With the food situation dealt with, attention turns to the darkening skies and rapidly cooling temperatures, finding shelter becomes the priority! A nearby mountain range seems to offer hope in the form of a cave formation, but there is no indication of whether anything more sinister resides within those dark tunnels. Once again the Captain is forced to make a decision on who to send into the darkness.
STH_caretaker.55.name: Marooned on a hostile planet
STH_caretaker.56.desc: The decision to spend the night in the open turns out to be a wise decision, the lookouts report strange noises from within the caves, the crew seem relieved to have avoided whatever monstrosity dwells within those dark tunnels. With a roaring fire keeping the crew warm, it's an ideal opportunity to rest and take stock of the situation.
STH_caretaker.56.name: Marooned - looking for shelter
STH_caretaker.57.desc: The decision to spend the night in the open turns out to be a mistake, the temperature plummets and the crew struggle to keep a fire lit. This takes its toll on the already injured members of the crew and sadly a number do not make it through the night. Hopefully the situation can only get better.
STH_caretaker.57.name: Marooned - looking for shelter
STH_caretaker.58.desc: The risk pays off and the cave provides excellent shelter from the harsh overnight conditions. The crew welcome the rest and the break from the rain hammering outside.
STH_caretaker.58.name: Marooned - looking for shelter
STH_caretaker.59.desc: The cave was not empty! A hostile beast was found living inside the cave and it apparently has a taste for [root.owner.GetSpeciesName] the crew are able to escape, but not before losing an unlucky crewman.
STH_caretaker.59.name: Marooned - looking for shelter
STH_caretaker.6.desc: After what seemed like forever reports start coming in from all stations, whilst damage appears to be minimal, there is no sign of our target and it appears our efforts were in vain. There is nothing left to do but return home and wonder what could have been.
STH_caretaker.6.name: Contact re-established
STH_caretaker.60.desc: The Science officer and team return from the cave network with good news - it looks like it will provide a safe shelter for the entire crew. The scouting party found a number of dangerous drops which could have caught out the unsuspecting crew.
STH_caretaker.60.name: Marooned - looking for shelter
STH_caretaker.600.desc: Long-range scans have picked up the last known location of our operative, the information which contains information vital to the empire, the [caretaker_start_location.GetName] is an ideal rendezvous location The Havra is ready to depart on your command.
STH_caretaker.600.name: Caretaker - The Departure
STH_caretaker.61.desc: The cave was not empty! A hostile beast was found living inside the cave and it apparently has a taste for [root.owner.GetSpeciesName] and seemed to really enjoy the taste of our Science officer and their team. A lucky escape for the crew, but not the unfortunate few who entered the cave.
STH_caretaker.61.name: Marooned - looking for shelter
STH_caretaker.62.b: We will make this planet our home.
STH_caretaker.62.desc: Just as rescue looks more and more unlikely one of the scouts spots a ship landing on the horizon. The crew are contacted by one of the species local to this area who saw the attack on the ship and having been hounded by the more aggressive species in this region for years have opted to fight back with the help of the crew from the Alpha Quadrant.\n\n The Alien captain suggests that they have been living in fear for many generations and believe now is the time to strike back, the captain asks whether his crew can join in the retaking of the ship, the captain asks for more time to make the decision. The crew are tired and could make a home on this hostile world - the crew will have a hard life, but it would end the confrontation. A decision must be made.
STH_caretaker.62.name: Basics - Rescue at last
STH_caretaker.63.desc: The decision has been made, this will be our new home, a hard life awaits - but free from conflict and further battles with the species in this region. We will make this planet home and a little piece of the Alpha Quadrant here in these strange uncharted lands. The adventure may have been brief, but we will pass the story of how we ended up here down through the generations.
STH_caretaker.63.name: Basics - a new home
STH_caretaker.64.desc: The rescuers track down the ship, which has still not been repaired, apparently our technology is proving a challenge. With the shields down our boarding parties should be able to board the ship easily enough. Our ship is our home and our chance to get home, we cannot abandoned her to these alien aggressors. Prepare the crew to take back the ship. Our alien rescues restate their desire to help stake a blow against the species that has continually hounded them, however the risk to the aliens would be great and many of them could be killed due to their inferior training. Should we accept their help or storm the ship solo.
STH_caretaker.64.name: Basics - Retaking the ship
STH_caretaker.65.desc: The battle was intense, but the superior training and skill of the crew from the Alpha Quadrant was able to make short work of the thieves. With the ship safely back under the Captains control and the aliens secured in the brig, help from their rescuers was not required.
STH_caretaker.65.name: Basics - The ship is ours
STH_caretaker.66.desc: The battle was intense, but the superior training and skill of the crew from the Alpha Quadrant was able to make short work of the thieves. Unfortunately there were some casualties and not all the crew were able to survive With the ship safely back under the Captains control and the aliens secured in the brig, help from their rescuers was not required.
STH_caretaker.66.name: Basics - The ship is ours
STH_caretaker.67.desc: Captain, we have regained control of the ship, but faced heavy resistance, not all the crew made it. Unfortunately we lost our science officer as we attempted to take back Main Engineering.
STH_caretaker.67.name: Basics - The ship is ours
STH_caretaker.68.desc: Captain, we have regained control of the ship, but faced heavy resistance, not all the crew made it. Unfortunately we lost Neelix as we attempted to take back Main Engineering - his sacrifice will not go unnoticed.
STH_caretaker.68.name: Basics - The ship is ours
STH_caretaker.69.desc: Captain, we have regained control of the ship, but faced heavy resistance, many of the crew were injured and sadly we lost a number of the senior officers including our Science officer and Neelix, our victory came at high price.
STH_caretaker.69.name: Basics - The ship is ours
STH_caretaker.7.desc: Our long distance sensors are no longer able to detect the signal from our mission target. It appears that they are no longer transmitting, with no signal to track there is nothing more we can do.
STH_caretaker.7.name: Mission Over
STH_caretaker.70.desc: The combined might of the two forces was too much for the thieves and systematically the ship was cleared, deck by deck in a professional and calculated manor seldom seen in these parts of the Delta Quadrant. Such was the success of the operation that no injuries were sustained and collateral damage to the ship itself was minimal. One could describe this as the very definition of a textbook operation.
STH_caretaker.70.name: Basics - The ship is ours
STH_caretaker.700.desc_01: As Voyager and her crew continue their journey home, scans pick up the signs of a recent battle, searching for a ship in distress Voyager arrives at the scene. \n\nAhead of them a massive Borg Cube lies drifting, powerless, and in bad shape. Huge tears ripped across its black piped hull... suddenly a smaller ship with a distinctly organic look to it appeared from behind a section of debris, could this be a return of Species 8472?
STH_caretaker.700.desc_02: As Voyager and her crew continue their journey home, scans pick up the signs of a recent battle, searching for a ship in distress Voyager arrives at the scene. \n\nAhead of them a massive ship lies drifting, powerless, and in bad shape. Huge tears ripped across its black piped hull... suddenly a smaller ship with a distinctly organic look appeared from behind a section of debris. Could these be the same aliens we faced previously?
STH_caretaker.700.name: The Scorpion
STH_caretaker.701.desc_01: The Borg and the new ship continue to exchange blows, it seems unlikely that this smaller ship could take on and defeat the larger Borg vessel, but suddenly a massive power surge is detected from the smaller ship, which seems to be glowing with some strange living energy, it fires at the Borg cube, which explodes in a massive explosion of debris. However, the new ship seems to have noticed Voyager and turns towards the Starfleet vessel.
STH_caretaker.701.desc_02: The black cube and the new ship continue to exchange blows, it seems unlikely that this smaller ship could take on and defeat the larger cube-shaped ship, but suddenly a massive power surge is detected from the smaller ship, which seems to be glowing with some strange living energy, it fires at the larger ship, which explodes in a massive explosion of debris. However, the new ship seems to have noticed Voyager and turns towards the Starfleet vessel.
STH_caretaker.701.name: The Scorpion
STH_caretaker.702.b: What do you intend to do?
STH_caretaker.702.c: Helm, get us out of here.
STH_caretaker.702.desc: A message fills the screen and the minds of the crew and a single phrase is felt. \n\n The weak will perish.
STH_caretaker.702.name: Inbound Psysic Message
STH_caretaker.703.a: Can the Borg be trusted Captain?
STH_caretaker.703.c: What other choices do we have?
STH_caretaker.703.desc_01:
Captains Log: 

We have managed to evade the hostile ship inside a nearby nebula, but I suspect we are fortunate that they currently have little interest in our ship. However, this appears to be where our good fortune ends as our long-range scans pick up multiple battles between this new threat and the Borg effectively blocking our most direct route home. 

I am reminded of the old earth saying The enemy of my enemy, is my friend and whilst the Borg could never be our allies if we want to continue this route home perhaps a temporary alliance is required.
STH_caretaker.703.desc_02:
Captains Log: 

We have managed to evade the hostile ship inside a nearby nebula, but I suspect we are fortunate that they currently have little interest in our ship. However, this appears to be where our good fortune ends as our long-range scans pick up multiple battles between these two factions, effectively blocking our most direct route home. 

I am reminded of the old earth saying The enemy of my enemy, is my friend and whilst there is something offputting about both sides, I fear a temporary alliance is our only hope of crossing this sector of space and getting home.
STH_caretaker.703.name: The enemy of my enemy...
STH_caretaker.704.a: We offer you our help to hold back these invaders.
STH_caretaker.704.b: Non-sense, you are clearing losing this war!
STH_caretaker.704.name: Contact established, with the Borg
STH_caretaker.705.c: We offer you all our resources.
STH_caretaker.705.desc:
Species 5618, your communication methods are primitive, we have assigned this Borg Drone, designation Seven of Nine, Tertiary Adjunct of Unimatrix 01.

 We fail to see how you could be of assistance, your vessel will be assimilated and your knowledge will then aid the collective. Cooperation is irrelevant when assimilation brings us closer to our goals. 

The threat from Species 8472 must be contained, they seek the destruction of all life deemed inferior and our attempts to bring them closer to perfection have all failed, the Collective is under threat.. research methods continue to elude us.
STH_caretaker.705.name: Contact established, with the Borg
STH_caretaker.706.desc:
The scientific and engineering minds of Voyager, combined with the vast knowledge of the Collective soon begin to formulate a plan. 

Our previous run-in with the biological aliens in the Northwest Passage has given us a potential avenue to progress, using Borg Nano-probes to attack Species 8472 at a cellular level. 

The breakthrough comes at an invaluable time, as a Fluidic rift opens ahead of Voyager. It appears Species 8472 have come to end the alliance.
STH_caretaker.706.name: An uneasy alliance
STH_caretaker.707.a.response: The Cargo bay containing the Borg Drones is decompressed, killing Seven of Nine and the Borg Drones now threatening to break free and take control of Voyager. The USS Voyager will then continue it's journey home.
STH_caretaker.707.a: Get these Borg off my ship.
STH_caretaker.707.name: Collective Fury
STH_caretaker.708.a.response: This ends the journey home of the USS Voyager, leaving the Borg to tackle the threat of the Undine alone. We shall soon see if this mysterious threat spills out into the Alpha Quadrant.
STH_caretaker.708.desc: The nanoprobe-infused torpedo is fired at the 8472 bio-ship but has little effect on the hostile vessel, which shrugs off the attack before unleashing a deadly barrage of it's own, Voyager's shields begin to fail, Janeway orders the crew to abandon ship, but it is too late as Voyager and her crew are destroyed.
STH_caretaker.708.name: Voyager Destroyed
STH_caretaker.709.a: What do the Borg want now?
STH_caretaker.709.desc: The nanoprobe-infused torpedo is fired at the 8472 bio-ship which appears to glow a sickly yellow colour before all signs of life fade and the ship explodes, the weapon has worked! \n\nThe news of our victory doesn't take long to reach our Borg .. guests.
STH_caretaker.709.name: Voyager Victorious
STH_caretaker.71.desc: After a heavy battle with casualties on both sides the superior numbers and training of the allies finally broke through the defences of the aliens and whilst the ship itself sustained internal damage from the assault, the crew from the Alpha quadrant were successful in wrestling the ship away from its captures.
STH_caretaker.71.name: Basics - The ship is ours
STH_caretaker.710.desc: Janeway, give us the weapon schematics, this alliance is terminated. You will be assimilated, you will join the Borg. We are Borg!
STH_caretaker.710.name: Borg Betrayal
STH_caretaker.711.desc: As the Cargo Bay doors open sucking the air from the room pulling the drones from their attempt to break through the door locks. \n\nVoyager departs the area with a weapon capable of defeating Species 8472, but now firmly a target in the many eyes of the Borg Collective.
STH_caretaker.711.name: Borg Drones Killed
STH_caretaker.712.desc:
As the Cargo Bay doors open sucking the air from the room pulling the drones from their attempt to break through the door locks. 

The security teams enter the room to find a single drone remaining, Seven of Nine. Separated from the Collective and alone, the crew commits to helping her restore her lost humanity. 

Voyager departs the area with a weapon capable of defeating Species 8472, but is now firmly a target in the many eyes of the Borg Collective.
STH_caretaker.712.name: A single Drone Survives
STH_caretaker.713.desc: I feel the loss of the Collective each day but am committed to my journey to regain the humanity the Borg stole from me when I was just a child named Annika Hansen. Voyager can become my home, I was reborn there. She is my home. Her crew, my family.
STH_caretaker.713.name: Seven of Nine
STH_caretaker.72.desc_01: We have lost many fine officers today amd learned a great lesson about life so far from home, without the support of our fleets and soldiers. We must not forget the lives lost on the planet and retaking our ship. Yet there is hope and good people willing to help. The science officer and Mr Neelix both had much more to give before being cruelly cut down. But the mission remains and there is still a crew to return home.
STH_caretaker.72.desc_02: We have lost many fine officers today amd learned a great lesson about life so far from home, without the support of our fleets and soldiers. We must not forget the lives lost on the planet and retaking our ship. Yet there is hope and good people willing to help The Captain takes a moment to reflect on the contributions of all the crew who did not make it, pausing to note the contributions of one man who represents everything that [root.owner.GetSpeciesName] represents, their knowledge at the science station will be a big loss and who knows how this will impact the journey home.
STH_caretaker.72.desc_03: [intrepidAdmiral.GetName] turns to face the crew, proud at the work each of them put into the last few weeks, pleased to see so many of the crew alive, but knowing this will not be the last challenge they face together, reciting the core values of the [root.owner.GetSpeciesName]. New friends have been made today and we have demonstrated that we will defend ourselves. The Chief Engineer reports that the return home can begin shortly and damage to the ship is superficial. Relieved that the crew is still together, the Captain takes a moment to reflect on the adventure ahead.
STH_caretaker.72.name: Basics - conclusion
STH_caretaker.73.desc: The ship repaired and restocked the crew eager to resume their journey home. The Captain takes their seat on the bridge, What adventure will come next. \n\n<Dev Team Note> congratulations on completing the 2nd mission for the crew of the Starship Voyager/Havra/Vetar, their next adventure will be along soon.
STH_caretaker.73.name: The next adventure awaits
STH_caretaker.74.desc:
Captains Log: A year has passed since the crew retook their ship and whilst their journey has not been an easy one, it has been filled with more positives than negatives. Many new discoveries and first contacts have been made in the last year than in [Root.Owner.GetSpeciesName] history, bonds have been forged and tested in multiple scenarios and the crew has become an efficient and effective force. 

However, this peace is unlikely to last, we have travelled into a region of space that is contested by an ongoing war between two powerful sides - one in which we would be vastly outmatched. Our astrometric department has identified a corridor of space that appears free of conflict, which has been dubbed the North West Passage.

The options are clear risk the passage or add nearly 3 months onto the journey and travel around this sector.
STH_caretaker.74.name: North West Passage
STH_caretaker.75.c: It is too risky, resume course.
STH_caretaker.75.desc: As the ship traverses through the North West Passages sensors pick up the signs of a derelict vessel. It appears to be one of the vessels involved in the war raging outside of the passage. The ship is unlike anything that has been seen before, scans suggest that it is almost organic in design, but that cannot be possible! The passage must be interfering with our scanners...\n\n There appears to be a single life-sign on board with weak life signs. The First Officer suggests this could be a one-off chance to understand the conflict and maybe gain a new means of protecting the ship or getting home faster. The Security chief recommends sending a security team, whilst the scientists believe that they are best prepared to investigate .
STH_caretaker.75.name: A Derelict Ship
STH_caretaker.76.desc: Deciding that it is too risky, the captain alters course away from the vessel and deeper into the unknown.
STH_caretaker.76.name: A Derelict Ship
STH_caretaker.77.desc: The away team beam into a dimly lit corridor, the interior of the vessel appears to be badly damaged, yet there are signs that it is begins to heal itself. The ship appears to be unlike anything that the away team has encountered before, a strange organic compound pulses through conduits above the away teams head and the ship almost appears to be alive.\n\nFollowing the corridor deeper into the interior of the vessel in search of the life-sign the Away team come across further signs of conflict, the remains of humanoid bodies are piled in the centre of an open room, organic and synthetic components stacked together in an almost ritualistic manner. Suddenly the scanners pick up movement and a large alien bursts into the room, eyes full of malice and hostility, in one swift motion it swipes a long arm at the closest officer.
STH_caretaker.77.name: North West Passage
STH_caretaker.78.desc_01: As the ship moves away from the alien vessel, in sickbay the ship's doctor examines the stricken officer, who appears to be in the process of being consumed alive by whatever the alien contaminant is. The doctor believes that he can save the crewman, but it will take time and resources to complete, but the dying science officer has little of either.\n\nLuckily the Science Officers scanner managed to pick up a few vital details - This 'Species' is a tripedal species who do not possess vocal cords, however do possess eyes which serve as their sensory organs. They appear to be telepathic which they use to great effect in scanning the minds of other races, vitally they possess a near flawless immune system that attacks and destroys any form of 'infection'; both biological and technological.
STH_caretaker.78.desc_02: As the ship moves away from the alien vessel, in sickbay the ship's doctor examines the security officer, who appears to be in the process of being consumed alive by whatever the alien contaminant is. The doctor believes that he can save the crewman, but it will take time and resources to complete, but the dying woman has little of either.\n\nLuckily the Security Officers tactical assessment picked up a few vital details - This 'Species' is a tripedal species and appear capable of surviving in the vacuum of space without any form of protection; which suggests that they do not respire as humanoids do, the appear to process far superior strength and reflexes, beyond that of anything encountered the Alpha Quadrant.
STH_caretaker.78.name: North West Passage
STH_caretaker.79.desc: The Ship's doctor notes the time of death in their log, without a cure they did not stand a chance against the infection raging within. Their last words were to express the wish that the remaining crew see home once again.
STH_caretaker.79.name: Science Officer's death
STH_caretaker.80.desc: The Ship's doctor notes the time of death in their log, without a cure they did not stand a chance against the infection raging within. Their last words were to express the wish that the remaining crew see home once again.
STH_caretaker.80.name: Security Officer's death
STH_caretaker.81.desc: The doctor reports that a cure has been found, using modified nanoprobes, the idea of turning one of our greatest threats into a cure is not lost on those involved. The Doctor remarks that with a little work this cure could be transformed into an active defence against these creatures. Back on the bridge the communications officer reports that we are being approached by an alien vessel requesting the chance to speak with us.
STH_caretaker.81.name: A cure at last
STH_caretaker.82.a: We have a cure to the Undine infection.
STH_caretaker.82.b.tooltip: Hand over your science offer, but keep the cure.
STH_caretaker.82.b: Our Science Officer is exceptional and worthy of a higher calling.
STH_caretaker.82.c.tooltip: Accept the risk and harder choice, battle on without sacrificing the cure or officer.
STH_caretaker.82.c: We have nothing to offer you.
STH_caretaker.82.desc_01: Greetings [root.GetName], we are the the collective known as the Think Tank, we have followed your ship through this sector and using our advanced cloaking technology we have remained hidden, observing your struggles and looking for a sign of something that may be of interest to us. We see that your species is intelligent and resourceful and to have survived an attack from the Undine peaks our interest. The species that you encountered go by many names - the Undine to most, Species 8472 to some, but to all they are a deadly foe - who seek out nothing but the destruction of anyone they encounter. But Human, this is of little interest to us, we seek only what is rare and of value ... knowledge. If you can share something of value to us, we will help you through this dangerous territory, does this sound like a fair trade human?
STH_caretaker.82.desc_02: Greetings [root.GetName], we are the the collective known as the Think Tank, we have followed your ship through this sector and using our advanced cloaking technology we have remained hidden, observing your struggles and looking for a sign of something that may be of interest to us. We see that your species is uniquely cunning and deceptive and that you managed to have survived an attack from the Undine peaks our interest. The species that you encountered goes by many names - the Undine to most, Species 8472 to some, but to all they are a deadly foe - who seek out nothing but the destruction of anyone they encounter. But Romulan, your struggles are of little concern to us, we seek knowledge, science and the unknown. If you can share something of value to us, we will help you through this dangerous territory, does this sound like a fair trade ?
STH_caretaker.82.desc_03: Greetings [root.GetName], we are the the collective known as the Think Tank, we have followed your ship through this sector and using our advanced cloaking technology we have remained hidden, we are continually amazed that you have made it this far and although we suspect luck or brute force we suspect there maybe something we have missed in your ship and crew. We see that your species is devious and resilient and to have survived an attack from the Undine peaks our interest. The species that you encountered go by many names - the Undine to most, Species 8472 to some, but to all they are a deadly foe - who seek out nothing but the destruction of anyone they encounter. But Gul Evek, this is of little interest to us, we seek only what is rare and of value ... knowledge. If you can share something of value to us, we will help you through this dangerous territory, does this sound like a fair trade or shall we explain it slower?
STH_caretaker.82.name: Greetings from the Think Tank
STH_caretaker.83.desc: Pleased with the offering, the Think Tank are true to their word and escort the ship safely through the North West Passage and closer to home. The Captain can only hope that the trade was worth it.
STH_caretaker.83.name: The Scorpion - The escape
STH_caretaker.83.tooltip: The adventure will continue later on.
STH_caretaker.84.a.tooltip: The Adventure will continue later on.
STH_caretaker.84.desc: The journey may have been extended without the help of the Think Tank, but [intrepidAdmiral.GetName] is certain that the cost of their help was not worth the price offered. This crew will make it home, but will do so on our own terms.
STH_caretaker.84.name: The Scorpion - The escape
STH_caretaker.90.desc: One of the species first encountered in the Delta Quadrant were the Vidiians, a species ravaged by a deadly virus called the Phage, whilst we were unable to help them at the time we now believe we have the capability to offer them assistance with a cure. Any contact with this desperate race carries huge risk, but can we morally turn our backs on those in need of aid?
STH_caretaker.90.name: The Phage
STH_caretaker.91.desc: Success! Our scientists think they have found a breakthrough.
STH_caretaker.91.name: The Phage
STH_caretaker.92.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], I am sorry to report that our efforts were unsuccessful. The cure to the Phage still eludes us.
STH_caretaker.92.name: The Phage
STH_caretaker.999.a: Damn the Ocampa, send us home.
STH_caretaker.999.c: We ask nothing, go in peace Suspiria.
STH_caretaker.999.desc_01:
The toxin has the desired impact and the pain stops, the small child takes a step back, clearly gathering herself to make the next move. The opportunity is there to speak and explain all that occurred during those first days in the Delta Quadrant. Unable to act Suspira can only listen to the story, which appears to break through the anger.

Suspiria explains that her species, the Nacene, who were explorers, did a great wrong to the population of Ocampa V, the Caretaker and her were chosen to stay behind to undoing this great wrong, but Suspira saw that by protecting the Ocampa they were also entrapping them and so with great sorrow she left with a handful of Ocampans.

However it is now her time to leave as well, but she would sacrifice her last energy to grant us one final request. Return home, but in turn destroy the Array and the Ocampa who refuse to leave or send the Ocampa back to their homeworld to be with their own kind.
STH_caretaker.999.desc_02: Suspiria explains that her species, the Nacene, who were explorers, did a great wrong to the population of Ocampa V, the Caretaker and her were chosen to stay behind to undoing this great wrong, but Suspira saw that by protecting the Ocampa they were also entrapping them and so with great sorrow she left with a handful of Ocampans.\n\nHowever it is now her time to leave as well, but she would sacrifice her last energy to grant us one final request. Return home, but in turn destroy the Array and the Ocampa who refuse to leave or send the Ocampa back to their homeworld to be with their own kind.
STH_caretaker.999.name: Cold Fires - Suspiria's Offer
STH_cause_effect.1.b: We have no time for this.
STH_cause_effect.1.desc: Our long range scans have picked up a localised distortion within the space time continuum, something of this magnitude warrants further investigation. We should dispatch a ship to investigate as soon as we can.
STH_cause_effect.1.name: Cause and Effect
STH_cause_effect.10.desc: An officer reports that something is emerging from the distortion and an unidentified starship emerges. The ship is on a collision course with the [cause_effect_leader.GetName], just a few seconds from impact and the helm still fails to respond. There is no response when the [cause_effect_leader.GetName] hails the other ship. The Captain asks for suggestions. Someone suggests putting the engines into full reverse in a bid to avoid the collision, while the Science Officer alternatively suggests using the tractor beam to alter the ship's trajectory.
STH_cause_effect.10.name: Caught in a time loop
STH_cause_effect.102.desc: Greetings, I'm glad you're listening, our long-range scans have detected an unusual reading close to [investigation_target_c_e.GetName], the USS Bozeman has recently been refitted with an upgraded sensor suite and would be the ideal ship to carry out this investigation.
STH_cause_effect.102.name: Mysterious Signal Detected
STH_cause_effect.103.desc: We have lost contact with [bozemanShip.GetName] whilst they were investigating an unusual anomaly, The last report from [bozemanAdmiral.GetName] seemed to suggest that there was an unusually high amount of Chroniton readings in the area. \n\nA team sent to investigate was unable to find any sign of the [bozemanShip.GetName] or the anomaly they were sent to investigate.
STH_cause_effect.103.name: [bozemanShip.GetName] has vanished!
STH_cause_effect.11.a: The ship is destroyed in a massive explosion.
STH_cause_effect.11.desc_01: Despite the best efforts of all involved on the ship the mystery ship collides at high speeds, neither ship stood a chance as a huge explosion rocks both vessels destroying the smaller ship. The [cause_effect_ship.GetName] is in dire straits, with the starboard warp nacelle having suffered extreme damage from an impact. As casualty reports flood in and the ship's systems start failing, The Commander orders all hands to escape pods as the Engineering chief tries desperately to shut down the warp engines. At that moment, the nacelle completely blows out, sending the [cause_effect_ship.GetName] into a helpless spin. The engine shutdown failed, ejection systems are offline, and a warp core breach is imminent. As a fire engulfs the bridge, [cause_effect_leader.GetName] orders all hands to abandon ship. Alas, it is too late, as the ship explodes, killing everybody aboard.
STH_cause_effect.11.desc_02: Putting the ship into full reverse fails, as the ship is unable to move in time, the second ship unable to stop ploughs straight into [cause_effect_ship.GetName] causing devastating damage The [cause_effect_ship.GetName] is in dire straits, with the starboard warp nacelle having suffered extreme damage from an impact. As casualty reports flood in and the ship's systems start failing, The Commander orders all hands to escape pods as the Engineering chief tries desperately to shut down the warp engines. At that moment, the nacelle completely blows out, sending the [cause_effect_ship.GetName] into a helpless spin. The engine shutdown failed, ejection systems are offline, and a warp core breach is imminent. As a fire engulfs the bridge, [cause_effect_leader.GetName] orders all hands to abandon ship. Alas, it is too late, as the ship explodes, killing everybody aboard.
STH_cause_effect.11.name: Caught in a time loop
STH_cause_effect.12.desc_01: The captain calls all of the senior staff to the observation lounge early in the morning seeking a suggestion as to what was causing all of the unusual events that happened recently on the Ship, the sense of deja vu cannot be ignored. The Chief Engineer believes that the Ship has most likely been caught in a temporal causality loop, where they have been repeating events over and over again but they have no knowledge of how many times they have repeated events or how long they have been stuck in the loop or what action causes them to remain stuck. One of the crew points out that the strange occurrences have all featured the number 3 and suggests that if they were to hide a message in the computer a Level 3 diagnostic would reveal it.
STH_cause_effect.12.desc_02: The captain calls all of the senior staff to the observation lounge early in the morning, the staff agree there is more to the recent strange activity and mysterious sense of deja vu than meets the eye. The Chief Engineer suspects that the Ship could be caught in a temporal causality loop, but they would have no knowledge of how many times they have repeated events or how long they have been stuck in the loop or what action causes them to remain stuck. The Doctor who had been sitting quietly chimes in with the feeling that the number 3 has been cropping up a lot. To which the Chief Engineer jokingly responds that they could always run a level 3 diagnostic!
STH_cause_effect.12.desc_02a: The captain calls all of the senior staff to the observation lounge early in the morning, the staff agree there is more to the recent strange activity and mysterious sense of deja vu than meets the eye. The Chief Engineer suspects that the Ship could be caught in a temporal causality loop, but they would have no knowledge of how many times they have repeated events or how long they have been stuck in the loop or what action causes them to remain stuck. The Doctor who had been sitting quietly chimes in with the feeling that the number 3 has been cropping up a lot. To which the Chief Engineer jokingly responds that they could always run a level 3 diagnostic!
STH_cause_effect.13.b: Full reverse must work this time.
STH_cause_effect.13.d: Surely it has to be the tractor beam.
STH_cause_effect.13.desc: An officer reports that something is emerging from the distortion and an unidentified starship emerges. The ship is on a collision course with the [cause_effect_leader.GetName], just a few seconds from impact and the helm still fails to respond. There is no response when the [cause_effect_leader.GetName] hails the other ship. The Captain asks for suggestions. Someone suggests putting the engines into full reverse in a bid to avoid the collision, while the Science Officer alternatively suggests using the tractor beam to alter the ship's trajectory.
STH_cause_effect.13.name: The Final Loop
STH_cause_effect.14.c: The tractor beam has never failed us.
STH_cause_effect.14.d: Full reverse has to be the answer.
STH_cause_effect.14.desc_01: Just as before the officer reports that something is emerging from the distortion and an unidentified starship emerges. The ship is on a collision course with the [cause_effect_leader.GetName], just a few seconds from impact and the helm still fails to respond. There is no response when the [cause_effect_leader.GetName] hails the other ship. The Captain asks for suggestions. Someone suggests putting the engines into full reverse in a bid to avoid the collision, while the Science Officer alternatively suggests using the tractor beam to alter the ship's trajectory, suddenly the Captain spies the commander out of the corner of his eye. His rank insignia with 3 markers there for all to see. 'Commander what do you suggest' The Commander boldly declares - blow the main shuttle bay doors.
STH_cause_effect.14.desc_02: Just as before the officer reports that something is emerging from the distortion and an unidentified starship emerges. The ship is on a collision course with the [cause_effect_leader.GetName], just a few seconds from impact and the helm still fails to respond. There is no response when the [cause_effect_leader.GetName] hails the other ship. The Captain asks for suggestions. Someone suggests putting the engines into full reverse in a bid to avoid the collision, while the Science Officer alternatively suggests using the tractor beam to alter the ship's trajectory, the Captain looks around for inspiration and at the last minute sees the commander out of the corner of his eye. His rank insignia with 3 markers there for all to see. 'Commander what do you suggest' The Commander takes a second and then says - blow the main shuttle bay doors.
STH_cause_effect.14.name: The law of three
STH_cause_effect.15.a: Scrap the ship for the resources.
STH_cause_effect.15.desc: The bay door opens with gas violently rushing out, successfully pushing the ship out of the path of the other ship and saving it, the Captain's belief in the power of the number three paid off. Power is restored and [cause_effect_leader.GetName] asks what happened, it looks like the ship had been stuck in the time loop for over a month.
