The [cause_effect_ship.GetName] is then hailed by the other ship, which explains that they have been stuck in the time loop for almost 100 years and are amazed to learn how much time they have lost. The Captain of the vessel lost in time is prepared to rejoin the fleet and provide a valuable and experienced asset. The data pulled from the ships computers will provide a wealth of research points for our scientists.
STH_cause_effect.15.name: Back to the future
STH_cause_effect.16.a: Search the wreckage for the resources.
STH_cause_effect.16.desc: The bay door opens with gas violently rushing out, but the result is only partially successful in pushing the ship out of the path of the other ship, the second vessel clips the larger vessel and explodes. However the [cause_effect_ship.GetName] is spared too much damage. Power is restored and [cause_effect_leader.GetName] asks what happened, the wreckage of the other vessels suggests that had also been stuck in the same time loop for over one hundred years.
STH_cause_effect.16.name: Free from the loop
STH_cause_effect.17.desc: The delay in making the decision appears to have been a crucial factor in enforcing the time-loop, those few seconds must have been crucial in keeping the loop intact. This information comes too late for the two ships locked in the tragic loop. The second ship unable to stop ploughs straight into [cause_effect_ship.GetName] causing devastating damage, with the starboard warp nacelle suffering extreme damage from an impact. As casualty reports flood in and the ship's systems start failing, The Commander orders all hands to escape pods as the Engineering chief tries desperately to shut down the warp engines. At that moment, the nacelle completely blows out, sending the [cause_effect_ship.GetName] into a helpless spin. The engine shutdown failed, ejection systems are offline, and a warp core breach is imminent. As a fire engulfs the bridge, [cause_effect_leader.GetName] orders all hands to abandon ship. Alas, it is too late, as the ship explodes, killing everybody aboard. The loop is broken, but both ships were destroyed in the process a tragic loss of two ships.
STH_cause_effect.17.name: The final destruction
STH_cause_effect.18.desc: An officer reports that something is emerging from the distortion and an unidentified starship emerges. The ship is on a collision course with the [cause_effect_leader.GetName], just a few seconds from impact and the helm still fails to respond. There is no response when the [cause_effect_leader.GetName] hails the other ship. The Captain asks for suggestions. Someone suggests putting the engines into full reverse in a bid to avoid the collision, while the Science Officer alternatively suggests using the tractor beam to alter the ship's trajectory. History is playing out the same way again...
STH_cause_effect.18.name: The final destruction
STH_cause_effect.19.a: The ship is destroyed a final time.
STH_cause_effect.19.desc: An officer reports that something is emerging from the distortion and an unidentified starship emerges. The ship is on a collision course with the [cause_effect_leader.GetName], just a few seconds from impact and the helm still fails to respond. There is no response when the [cause_effect_leader.GetName] hails the other ship. The Captain asks for suggestions. Someone suggests putting the engines into full reverse in a bid to avoid the collision, while the Science Officer alternatively suggests using the tractor beam to alter the ship's trajectory.
STH_cause_effect.19.name: The final destruction
STH_cause_effect.2.desc: The stellar cartography department has narrowed down the location of the anomaly and sent the location to the bridge ready for further research. Investigating could provide valuable data.
STH_cause_effect.2.name: Cause and Effect
STH_cause_effect.3.desc: Having chosen not to investigate the space time anomaly it appears to drop from our scanners, we may never know what adventure has been missed.
STH_cause_effect.3.name: Cause and Effect
STH_cause_effect.4.desc: The following morning, the senior staff are briefing each other on the vast undertaking of charting the anomaly in the observation lounge. As they are about to return to duty, the Doctor reports the voices that she heard the previous night. The other staff are curious about her report and they are interrupted when a call from the bridge reports from the bridge indicate unusual sensor readings in the vicinity of the Ship. The Captain orders the helmsman to back the ship away slowly but can't as the maneuvering thrusters fail to respond. The distortion begins to fluctuate and power levels drop.
STH_cause_effect.4.name: Cause and Effect
STH_cause_effect.5.desc: The [cause_effect_ship.GetName] is in dire straits, with the starboard warp nacelle having suffered extreme damage from an impact. As casualty reports flood in and the ship's systems start failing, The Commander orders all hands to escape pods as the Engineering chief tries desperately to shut down the warp engines. At that moment, the nacelle completely blows out, sending the [cause_effect_ship.GetName] into a helpless spin. The engine shutdown failed, ejection systems are offline, and a warp core breach is imminent. As a fire engulfs the bridge, [cause_effect_leader.GetName] orders all hands to abandon ship. Alas, it is too late, as the ship explodes, killing everybody aboard.
STH_cause_effect.5.name: Cause and Effect
STH_cause_effect.6.desc: The following morning, the senior staff are briefing each other on the vast undertaking of charting the anomaly in the observation lounge. As they are about to return to duty, the Doctor reports the voices that she heard the previous night. The other staff are curious about her report and they are interrupted when a call from the bridge reports from the bridge indicate unusual sensor readings in the vicinity of the Ship. [cause_effect_leader.GetName] orders the helmsman to back the ship away slowly but can't as the maneuvering thrusters fail to respond. The distortion begins to fluctuate and power levels drop, again.
STH_cause_effect.6.name: Deja Vu
STH_cause_effect.7.desc: An officer reports that something is emerging from the distortion and an unidentified starship emerges. The ship is on a collision course with the [cause_effect_ship.GetName], just a few seconds from impact and the helm still fails to respond. There is no response when the [cause_effect_leader.GetName] hails the other ship. The Captain asks for suggestions. Someone suggests putting the engines into full reverse in a bid to avoid the collision, while the Science Officer alternatively suggests using the tractor beam to alter the ship's trajectory.
STH_cause_effect.7.name: Deja Vu
STH_cause_effect.8.a: The ship is once again destroyed.
STH_cause_effect.8.desc_01: Putting the ship into full reverse fails, as the ship is unable to move in time, the second ship unable to stop ploughs straight into [cause_effect_ship.GetName] causing devastating damage. The [cause_effect_ship.GetName] is in dire straits, with the starboard warp nacelle having suffered extreme damage from an impact. As casualty reports flood in and the ship's systems start failing, The Commander orders all hands to escape pods as the Engineering chief tries desperately to shut down the warp engines. At that moment, the nacelle completely blows out, sending the [cause_effect_ship.GetName] into a helpless spin. The engine shutdown failed, ejection systems are offline, and a warp core breach is imminent. As a fire engulfs the bridge, [cause_effect_leader.GetName] orders all hands to abandon ship. Alas, it is too late, as the ship explodes, killing everybody aboard.
STH_cause_effect.8.desc_02: The ship emits a tractor beam it is too late - the ship, unable to change course, collides with the starboard warp nacelle, causing it to explode and resulting in devastating damage. The [cause_effect_ship.GetName] is in dire straits, with the starboard warp nacelle having suffered extreme damage from an impact. As casualty reports flood in and the ship's systems start failing, The Commander orders all hands to escape pods as the Engineering chief tries desperately to shut down the warp engines. At that moment, the nacelle completely blows out, sending the [cause_effect_ship.GetName] into a helpless spin. The engine shutdown failed, ejection systems are offline, and a warp core breach is imminent. As a fire engulfs the bridge, [cause_effect_leader.GetName] orders all hands to abandon ship. Alas, it is too late, as the ship explodes, killing everybody aboard.
STH_cause_effect.8.name: Deja Vu
STH_cause_effect.9.desc: The following morning, the senior staff are briefing each other on the vast undertaking of charting the anomaly in the observation lounge. As they are about to return to duty, the Doctor reports the voices that she heard the previous night. The other staff are curious about her report and they are interrupted when a call from the bridge reports from the bridge indicate unusual sensor readings in the vicinity of the Ship. [cause_effect_leader.GetName] orders the helmsman to back the ship away slowly but can't as the maneuvering thrusters fail to respond. The distortion begins to fluctuate and power levels drop, this feels very familiar.
STH_cause_effect.9.name: Caught in a time loop
STH_cheronite.10.desc: An away team led by Captain [root.leader.GetName] of the [root.GetName] has beamed down to the irradiated ruins of one of the major cities. After spending hours investigating what was left from the city archive, a member of the team accidently disrupted one of the remaining supports of the building. As a result, the building collapsed leaving no survivors.
STH_cheronite.10.name: The Last Battlefield
STH_cheronite.3.desc: We have received some rather baffling reports from one of our deep space stations in the Beta Quadrant. Reportedly, a planetary crisis of some kind has recently devastated Cheron Prime. We are yet unable to determine the nature of this event, so perhaps it is wise to send a ship to investigate and look for survivors.
STH_cheronite.3.name: The Last Battlefield
STH_cheronite.5.desc: Once [root.GetName] have entered the [cheronite_system.GetName] system, Captain [root.leader.GetName] immediately ordered to begin scanning [cheronite_capital_world.GetName]. Scans have revealed no life signs on the planet. Several large cities are completely in ruins, planetary transportation systems, though quite extensive, are completely devoid of traffic. There is no evidence of natural disaster, yet there are vast numbers of unburied corpses in all cities. All this leads to a conclusion that a devastating war has destroyed the planet and all its population. To learn more Captain [root.leader.GetName] has decided to send an away mission to the planet surface.
STH_cheronite.5.name: The Last Battlefield
STH_cheronite.6.comm.desc: Despite all our efforts to reach Cheron Prime, a ship from [reached_cheron.GetName] has made it there first. Unfortunately, it means that we have to abort the expedition.
STH_cheronite.6.name: The Last Battlefield
STH_cheronite.6.noComm.desc: Despite all our efforts to reach Cheron Prime, a ship from an unknown origin has made it there first. Unfortunately, it means that we have to abort the expedition.
STH_cheronite.7.desc: An away team led by Captain [root.leader.GetName] of the [root.GetName] has beamed down to the irradiated ruins of one of the major cities. After spending hours investigating what was left from the city archive, a member of the team accidently found a miraculously well-preserved data disk. Some of the information was corrupted, however it was possible to retrieve most of it.\n\nFrom the disk [root.leader.GetName] had learned that the native people of Cheron were divided into two sub-species, their skin was exactly half-black and half-white, with some white on the right side and black on the left, and others the opposite. One of the sub-species had acquired supremacy and enslaved the other half of the population. Recently the oppressed part of the population has rebelled and the following civil war has completely destroyed their civilization.
STH_cheronite.7.name: The Last Battlefield
STH_cheronite.8.desc: An away team led by Captain [root.leader.GetName] of the [root.GetName] has beamed down to the irradiated ruins of one of the major cities. After spending hours investigating what was left from the city archive, a member of the team accidently found a miraculously well-preserved data disk. Half of the information was corrupted, however it was possible to retrieve the rest of it.\n\nFrom the disk [root.leader.GetName] had learned that the native people of Cheron were divided into two sub-species, their skin was exactly half-black and half-white, with some white on the right side and black on the left, and others the opposite. One of the sub-species had acquired supremacy and enslaved the other half of the population. Recently the oppressed part of the population has rebelled, and the following civil war has completely destroyed their civilization.
STH_cheronite.8.name: The Last Battlefield
STH_cheronite.9.desc: An away team led by Captain [root.leader.GetName] of the [root.GetName] has beamed down to the irradiated ruins of one of the major cities. After spending hours investigating what was left from the city archive, a member of the team accidently found a miraculously well-preserved data disk. Most of the information was corrupted, however it was possible to retrieve at least some of it.\n\nFrom the disk [root.leader.GetName] had learned that the native people of Cheron were divided into two sub-species, their skin was exactly half-black and half-white, with some white on the right side and black on the left, and others the opposite. One of the sub-species had acquired supremacy and enslaved the other half of the population. Recently the oppressed part of the population has rebelled and the following civil war has completely destroyed their civilization.
STH_cheronite.9.name: The Last Battlefield
STH_components.2003.desc: Our troops from [fromfromfrom.GetName] have successfully managed to board [fromfrom.GetName] during our battle with [from.GetName]. Our troops will attempt to disable the vessel to gain a tactical advantage before escaping the ship.
STH_components.2003.name: Ship Boarding in Process
STH_components.2004.desc: The [fromfromfrom.GetName] has successfully managed to get troops onboard one of our enemies ships, our forces will attempt to sabotage the ship and take it out of the fight.
STH_components.2004.name: Boarding Party Launched
STH_components.2005.desc: The onboard intruders are still onboard our ship, the [intruder_ship.GetName] from the [From.GetName], causing an interruption to ships functions with phaser fights going on across the ship.\n\nWhilst our teams attempt to keep the borders from accessing any of our crucial systems our ships ability to participate in the battle will be disrupted.
STH_components.2005.name: Intruders Onboard
STH_components.2006.desc: The intruders have successfully sabotaged [sabotaged_ship.GetName] one of our ships in [From.GetName] by gaining access to key systems and damaging them with spacial charges before exiting the ship.\n\nOur engineers believe it may be some time before we can get the ship fully operational once again.
STH_components.2006.name: Ship Sabotaged!
STH_components.2007.desc: The intruders have successfully disabled [disabled_ship.GetName] one of our ships in [From.GetName]. By fighting their way to the engineering section and destroying key systems with their weapons before exiting the ship.\n\nOur engineers indicate this will have caused major damage and will have left our ship unable to attack or defend itself.
STH_components.2007.name: Ship Disabled!
STH_components.2008.desc: The intruders have successfully defeated on [FromFrom.GetName] our brave security forces were able to launch a counter attack and defend the ship from the boarding force. \n\nThis victory has significantly improved the morale of the crew providing them with an extra edge in battle.
STH_components.2008.name: Ship Defended!
STH_components.2009.desc: Our efforts to board and disrupt the operations of our foes ship has suffered a blow, it appears the ship's crew were able to repel our forces and force them to retreat. \n\nTo make matters worse it appears the victory and emboldened their crew and they will now fight harder against us.
STH_components.2009.name: Ship Boarding unsuccessful
STH_components.2010.desc: Our troops from [prevprev.GetName] have successfully managed to board a combatant ship, reports from the mission commander confirm that there is a running battle throughout the ship, which is hampering their ability to effectively fight us.
STH_components.2010.name: Enemy Ship Successfully Boarded
STH_components.2011.desc: Our troops from [prevprev.GetName] have successfully managed to sabotage one of the ships engaged in combat against us, reports from the mission commander confirm that they have managed to damage several of their key systems including weapons and propulsion. \n\nIt will take them some time to effectively fight us.
STH_components.2011.name: Ship Successfully Sabotaged
STH_components.2012.desc: Our troops from [prevprev.GetName] have successfully managed to disable one of our enemies ships, reports from the mission commander confirm that they have managed to destroy many of their key systems including weapons and propulsion. \n\nIt will take them some time to rejoined the fight and they will be defenseless until they can.
STH_components.2012.name: Ship Successfully Disabled
STH_components.2013.desc: Our boarding party has successfully managed to capture [boarded_ship1.GetName]. The soldiers gained access to the ship's command centre, incapacitated the command crew, and took over the vessel. It is now under our command.
STH_components.2013.name: We have captured a ship
STH_components.2014.desc: Disastrous news! Enemy boarding parties have captured [boarded_ship1.GetName]. Unable to call on reinforcements from the fleet, the vessel was overwhelmed and the crew is presumed dead.
STH_components.2014.name: Ship has been captured
STH_components.2015.desc: Disastrous news! The [fromfrom.GetName] has been destroyed whilst repelling boarders from [from.GetName] it appears that a weapons fight close to the warp core resulted in a stray shot hitting the warp core. The resulting war core explosion destroyed the ship and crew.
STH_components.2015.name: Ship has been destroyed
STH_components.2016.desc: Whilst boarding the [fromfrom.GetName], the ship has been destroyed it is unclear whether this was as a result of our soldiers or an action from the crew in defence of their ship.
STH_components.2016.name: Ship has been destroyed
STH_components.2017.desc: It look like our enemy attempted to board [sh_shields_hold.GetName] in the [From.GetName] whilst in combat, thankful the Tactical officer was able to keep the shields in place and the intruders were unable to gain access to our ship.
STH_components.2017.name: Shields hold
STH_components.2018.desc: It look like our attempts to board [sh_shields_hold.GetName] by the [From.GetName] forces were unsuccessful as we were unable to penetrate their shields. We must defeat them in regular combat.
STH_components.2018.name: Enemies Shields Hold
STH_components.2021.a.tt: Attempt to rescue the leader from the Borg, as long as you de-assimilation tech and policies.
STH_components.2021.desc: Our [fromfrom.GetClass] [fromfrom.GetName] has been captured and assimilated by [from.GetName] during the battle in [fromfromfrom.GetName]. We have a limited chance to attempt a rescue, but should we fail we could lose more to the Borg.
STH_components.2021.name: [fromfrom.GetName] Assimilated
STH_components.2022.a: They will make an excellent drone.
STH_components.2022.desc: An important tactical asset has been captured, designated [fromfrom.GetName] and brought to an assimilation chamber to being to process of being integrated into the collective.
STH_components.2022.name: [fromfrom.GetName] Assimilated
STH_components.2023.desc: Our attempt to liberate our lost leader from the cluches of the Borg has failed, they are now part of the Collective along with many of our tactical secrets.
STH_components.2023.name: Attempt Failed
STH_components.2027.desc_01: Our [fromfrom.GetClass] [fromfrom.GetName] has been captured by [from.GetName] during the battle in [fromfromfrom.GetName]. They are looking to use this move as a miltary tactic to further their goals and are demanding info and power in return for [fromfrom.GetHisHer] life.
STH_components.2027.desc_02: As previously reported, our Commander [fromfrom.GetName] has been captured by [from.GetName] who are willing to a fee in exchange for [fromfrom.GetHisHer] life.
STH_components.2027.gor: Give them to the Gorn Infants.
STH_components.2027.name: [fromfrom.GetName] Captured
STH_components.2028.c: Send them to a Prison Camp.
STH_components.2028.desc: Following the unwillingness of [from.GetName] to pay for [fromfrom.GetHisHer] return, enemy Commander [fromfrom.GetName] remains in our custody. What is to be [fromfrom.GetHisHer] fate?
STH_components.2028.name: Refusal to Pay
STH_components.2029.desc_01: Our [fromfrom.GetClass] [fromfrom.GetName] has been captured by [from.GetName] during the battle in [fromfromfrom.GetName] and are being held as a prisoner of war.
STH_components.2029.desc_02: As previously reported, our Commander [fromfrom.GetName] has been captured by [from.GetName] and are currenty held as a Prisoner of War.
STH_components.2029.name: [fromfrom.GetName] Captured
STH_components.2030.desc: [from.GetName] appear unwilling to negoiate for the return of our prisoner, [fromfrom.GetName] return, as such they will remain a prisoner of war, until their release can be agreed.
STH_components.2030.name: Prisoner of War
STH_components.33.desc: The Temporal Incursion weapon fired successfully and as the new timeline begins to settle with the restoration of the Krenim Imperium now closer to 100%.\n\nOur new world and forces are ready to join our fight.
STH_components.33.name: Temporal Incursion Successful
STH_components.34.desc: The Temporal Incursion weapon fired successfully however our calculations must have been incorrect, our attempts to restore the timeline have been unsuccessful and we have moved further away from 100% restoration.\n\nOur foe has gained in strength as a result.
STH_components.34.name: Temporal Incursion Failed
STH_components.4001.desc: The destruction of [destroyed_escape_pod_ship.GetName] was a tragic loss to the [Root.GetName] navy, however, multiple escape pods have been found amongst the wreckage of the ship including one containing the commanding officer [escape_pod_leader.GetName].
STH_components.4001.name: [escape_pod_leader.GetName] rescued from the destruction of [destroyed_escape_pod_ship.GetName]
STH_components.4002.desc: Reports coming in following the destruction of [FROMFROMFROM.GetName] seems to indicate that despite launching a number of escape pods there were no survivors from the doomed ship.
STH_components.4002.name: [FROMFROMFROM.GetName] destroyed with no survivors
STH_confederacy_flavour.2.desc: Our victory over the Cardassian Union reaffirms Humanity's position as the superior race in the Alpha Quadrant. \n\nOur troops would like to present a gift to our glorious leader [Root.GetLeaderName] to commemorate the day we put the Cardassians in their place.
STH_confederacy_flavour.2.name: The spoils of war
STH_confederacy_flavour.3.a: Perhaps it was a good day to die.
STH_confederacy_flavour.3.desc: The Klingon's thought they could stand against the might of the Confederation Corps, but our forces demonstrated that their notions of honour and glory pale into insignificance against the phasers and torpedoes of our mighty warships. \n\nOur soldiers have brought back a trophy taken from one foolish old warrior who dared challenge one of our generals, we never promised a fair fight.
STH_confederacy_flavour.3.name: A good day to die
STH_confederacy_flavour.4.desc: The Ferengi have many rules of acquisition, however none of them cover being defeated by our forces. \n\nToday we proved the only profit to gain from war comes from being the victor. Our troops have brought back a large lobed trophy from Ferenginar, it might even earn us a small profit.
STH_confederacy_flavour.4.name: The Rules of Battle
STH_confederacy_flavour.5.desc: It was never logical for the Vulcan's to challenge us and yet they dared to challenge the might of Earth. The first extraterrestrial race that humanity was introduced to, it is only fitting they come to regret such a meeting.
STH_confederacy_flavour.5.name: Live long and prosper?
STH_confederacy_flavour.6.desc: The Borg came to assimilate us and add our biological distinctiveness to their own, our troops sent their cubes back to the Delta Quadrant is small pieces. \n\nRather than humanity adorning the walls of their cubes as drones, one of the drones now decorates our walls.
STH_confederacy_flavour.6.name: Resistance is not so futile after all
STH_confederacy_flavour.7.desc: The lizard people of Sauria put up a brave resistance but in the end their struggle was in vain, for none can stand in the way of the Confederation Corps. For in the end a safe galaxy is a human galaxy.
STH_confederacy_flavour.7.name: Basking in our glory
STH_control.1.a: Safety would be improved by AI predictions.
STH_control.1.desc: Our scientists are pleased to reveal that following the completion of latest research our new automated threat assessment algorithm has been developed. A test roll out has begun at one of our research facilities, and initial reports are that the scientists have reported a number of interesting breakthroughs, and a significant increase in our understanding of AI.
STH_control.1.name: Breakthrough in AI Tech
STH_control.10.desc:
We have received a distress call from from one of our Starbases before it was lost with all hands.

The last message received suggested they were experiencing severe computer malfunctions before we lost all contact.

At the moment we are unsure what could have caused this, but we must investigate further.
STH_control.10.name: Starbase destroyed!
STH_control.100.desc: Worrying reports across one of our settlements, our automated defences systems have stopped following orders and have turned on us. 'Control' seems to have gained control over our defences and is directing them at us.\n\nNo communications have been received, but casualty reports are already coming in. We must raise our defences and repel this takeover.
STH_control.100.name: Reports of attacks
STH_control.103.desc: The AI crisis is over and 'Control' has been defeated, who knows what its plan would have been, but its hostility towards organics was clear. This should be a lesson on tampering with the unknown and the dangers of artificial intelligence.
STH_control.103.name: AI Crisis Over...
STH_control.11.a: Send a team and start the investigation.
STH_control.11.desc: Several vessels that were working at one of our secure facilities have gone rogue and stopped responding, they have raised shields and powered weapons.\n\nReports suggest they were trialling a new threat detection algorithm and have a number of experimental weapons on board. Strangely our intel suggests that there are no life signs on board.
STH_control.11.name: Hostile ships detected and not responding to hails
STH_control.13.desc: Local rescue and incident services are reporting that there has been an explosion at one of the public forums with the loss of life.\n\nAll we know at the moment is that planetary engineers were installing a new security initiative.
STH_control.13.name: Explosion detected!
STH_control.15.a: Let our military correct the problem.
STH_control.15.b: Let our academics fix this mess.
STH_control.15.desc: Our investigations have been completed and we have uncovered a rogue subroutine that has stemmed from the new threat detection algorithm. The 'Control' program appears to have acted independently and whilst a number of our scientists believe that this was a computational mistake by the lead researcher, others believe the program evolved and acted on its own.\n\nEither way we must decide how to proceed, perfecting this research could catapult our technology forward decades, but many fear what has been created.
STH_control.15.name: Investigation Complete
STH_control.150.desc: Our leading military scientists and admirals across a number of bases and science labs have made a major breakthrough in the program which has been dubbed as 'Control'.\n\nOne report suggests that this was one of the easier projects, almost as if the secrets wanted to be revealed.
STH_control.150.name: AI Breakthrough!
STH_control.151.desc: Worrying reports suggest that a number of vessels prototyping the new 'Control' AI have stopped following orders and have turned on us. 'Control' seems to have gained control over our ships, upgrading them and directing them at us. No communications have been received, but casualty reports are already coming in. We must send the fleet before it can leave the system.
STH_control.151.name: Report of Rogue AI Fleet
STH_control.154.desc: Congratulations, our brave crew have managed to defeat all enemy ships taken over by the AI Control.\n\nThe lead ship NCIA-93 is still intact and our scientists believe there is still enough left of the original systems that will allow us to reclaim the vessel and add it to the fleet.
STH_control.154.name: Victory over Control
STH_control.20.desc: The Control program has proven it's worth and has produced a significant breakthrough in our current research path. This is welcome news.
STH_control.20.name: Control Program
STH_control.21.desc: Our science teams report that we failed to complete our investigations. Shortly after this report we lost communications with the team and our long range scans show a number of unresponsive vessels inside the system. They appear to have their weapons and shields active.
STH_control.21.name: Rogue ships detected
STH_control.51.desc: Our leading academics across a number of planetary labs and universities have made a major breakthrough in the program which has been dubbed as 'Control'.\n\nOne report suggests that this was one of the easier projects, almost as if the secrets wanted to be revealed.
STH_control.51.name: AI Breakthrough!
STH_control.6.desc: One of our research stations that is focusing on the application of AI in our threat detection protocols has fallen silent, this base is manned by crew from our cover operations group Section 31 and is potentially a target from other powers in the sector looking to gain access to our top secret research. Provide whatever assistance is required.
STH_control.6.name: Trouble at Research Base
STH_control.7.desc_01:
One of our research stations that is focusing on the application of AI in our threat detection protocols has fallen silent.

This base is manned by crew from our undercover operations group known as 'Section 31' and is potentially a target from other powers in the sector looking to gain access to our top secret research.

Provide whatever assistance is required.
STH_control.7.desc_02: One of our secret military installations, focusing on the application of AI in our threat detection protocols, has fallen silent.This base is manned by crew from our undercover operations group known as 'Section 31' and is potentially a target from other powers in the sector looking to gain access to our top secret research.\n\nProvide whatever assistance is required.
STH_control.7.name: Trouble at Research Base
STH_crime.10.desc: Our operation on [sth_crime_planet.GetName] has uncovered the location of a number of key individuals running the criminal operations on the planet. Thanks to the efforts of a joint operation between our forces and local officials we are in a position to deal a crippling blow against those who threaten the safety of our citizens. A team is prepared and stands ready to strike at the criminal bosses who hold this world in terror.
STH_crime.10.name: Criminal Underworld Operation
STH_crime.11.desc: Mission reports back from the strike team indicate they have successfully captured or eliminated a number of key members of the criminal organisation's top leadership thus dealing their operations on this planet a crippling blow.
STH_crime.11.desc_01: The Tal Shiar have handled the situation with the upmost efficency by infiltrating the base and surgically executing the mobs leadership, once eliminated the rest soon fell in line.
STH_crime.11.desc_02: The Obsidian Order rarely fail when set a target and they were successfully able to eliminate the criminal gang's leadership with ruthless efficiency that they have become renowned for. This will have caused them a major setback in their bid to rule the criminal underworld on this planet.
STH_crime.11.desc_03: The Starfleet strike team was able to surgically strike at the heart of the criminal organisation and with calculated efficiency capture and round up the criminal organisations leadership in one precise movement.
STH_crime.11.name: Criminal Leadership Defeated
STH_crime.12.desc: Our attempts to capture the criminal leadership have been unsuccessful, the attack on the organization was repelled by the mob forces. This failed attempt has likely strengthened their position on the planet.
STH_crime.12.name: Failed to capture criminal leaders
STH_crime.15.desc: Through a series of coordinated efforts over the last few months, our anti-crime teams have managed to round up and capture multiple gang leaders and place many of the gang's influential individuals in holding cells. This activity has forced both sides of the gangs off the streets and restored a measure of calm to the planet.
STH_crime.15.name: Gang Wars Over
STH_crime.16.desc: Our anti-crime operation has born significant victories over the criminal underworld on [sth_crime_planet.GetName] with key members of the underworld now being force fields and much of the illegal goods confiscated, authorities on the planet are claiming victory over the criminal gangs that were plaguing the planet.
STH_crime.16.name: Criminal Underworld
STH_crime.17.desc: Our efforts over the last few weeks have put us in a position to launch a final strike against the criminal underworld plaguing [sth_crime_planet.GetName]. Our operations have forced the underground into the open and as a result, we now have a clear target to send a strike team in to end the threat once and for all.
STH_crime.17.name: Criminal Underworld
STH_crime.18.desc: Our forces have successfully eliminated the last remaining segments of the criminal underworld and captured their resources which will effectively put an end to their criminal operations, at least for the time being.
STH_crime.18.name: Criminal Underworld Defeated
STH_crime.19.desc: Our operation was a complete failure, the criminal gangs were able to escape our grasp and retreat further underground and out of our reach. \n\nThis failure will have only strengthened their resolve and their hold over the planet.
STH_crime.19.name: Failed to defeat Underworld
STH_crime.20.desc: Our anti-crime operation has spent the last few months tracking down and seeking out the sources of corruption on [sth_crime_planet.GetName] and have determined that the branch offices opened on the planet are playing a significant part in corrupting the citizens and organising the criminal activities on the planet. \n\nThe local government on the planet has sanctioned a dawn raid against the branch office to close it down and force it off-planet but at the risk of upsetting the branch office owner.
STH_crime.20.name: Branch Offices
STH_crime.21.desc: Our raid against the illegal dens was a resounding success and overnight we managed to clear the dens and arrest the leaders forcing these hotspots of criminal activity to close down.
STH_crime.21.name: Branch Office Closed
STH_crime.22.desc: Our raid against the illegal dens was a huge failure, our teams underestimated the manner in which they were bedded in and the support they were receiving from organisations such as the Orion syndicate. Our failure to dislodge these pirate havens has allowed them further control over the planet's criminal elements and given them an aura of invincibility.
STH_crime.22.name: Branch Office Remains Open
STH_crime.23.desc: Our branch office on [sth_crime_planet.GetName] was forcibly closed by officials from the planet's government. They struck in an overnight operation and seized our property and latinum, massively setting back our efforts on this planet.
STH_crime.23.name: Branch Office Closed
STH_crime.24.desc: Government soldiers on [sth_crime_planet.GetName] attempted to forcibly close one of our branch offices, but they were unprepared for the resilience of our local den leaders. They will think twice before attempting such a move again.
STH_crime.24.name: Branch Office Remains Open
STH_crystalline_jewel.1.desc:
On the surface of the planet [From.GetName] The strange faint gravimetric readings lead the away team to a cave-like structure where they find a single crystalline jewel placed on a large flat stone base. No indications of the origin of this jewel are found from the planet's surface. 

The jewel found seems to have a quartz-like structure, but its molecular density and refraction index measure much higher than on any quartz previously discovered. 

The question now becomes what should we do with this find?
STH_crystalline_jewel.1.name: A Crystalline jewel is found
STH_crystalline_jewel.2.desc: After advertising the upcoming big auction for sometime buyers from all around the quadrant have gathered in anticipation of the event.
STH_crystalline_jewel.2.name: Relic auction
STH_crystalline_jewel.3.desc: After the auction has started it comes apparent that there is significant interest for the unique item with multiple potential buyers fiercely competing against each other to get it. As a result the price keeps rising several times securing us a very handsome sum.
STH_crystalline_jewel.3.name: The item draws significant interest
STH_crystalline_jewel.4.desc: After the auction starts a couple of interested bidders turn up and raise the price of the item while competing against each other. Securing us a decent return from the sale.
STH_crystalline_jewel.4.name: There is some interest for the item
STH_crystalline_jewel.5.desc: After the auction starts it comes apparent that there is very limited interest in the item on sale. The auctioneer's seeming lack of knowledge on how to properly highlight the uniqueness of the item and its features does not help matters either. After a long session of trying to get any sort of decent price for jewel the auctioneer finally has to settle for a disappointingly low bid.
STH_crystalline_jewel.5.name: The item doesn't seem to interest many buyers.
STH_dangerous_technology.1.desc: An outpost within the [OmegaFacilityDisaster.GetName] has reported instability with the recently discovered Omega particle, and so far all attempts to safely shut down the reactor have failed.\n\nWith only minutes to spare before the facility undergoes a catastrophe, what are your orders?
STH_dangerous_technology.1.name: Omega Molecule
STH_dangerous_technology.10.a: We do not have the resources. Evacuate as many as possible.
STH_dangerous_technology.10.b: Send in the crew. They will be remembered.
STH_dangerous_technology.10.desc:
The Omega facility on [Root.GetName] is reporting signs of instability, threatening to cause another Omega catastrophe. The majority of the safety measures upon [Root.GetName] have failed, leaving us with little options and little time to prevent another catastrophe like in the [OmegaFacilityDisaster.GetName] system. The head of the facility has two options for us:

The first is to attempt a manual shutdown by sending in trained personnel to shut down the reactor. This will be almost certainly a one way mission, and the risks are high within the facility but may prevent the colony from being destroyed.

The second measure is to inject more omega molecules into the chamber, in the hopes of causing a spontaneous harmonisation to stabilise the molecules.

What are your orders?
STH_dangerous_technology.10.name: Omega Instability
STH_dangerous_technology.11.desc: The Omega facility on [Root.GetName] has undergone an Omega event, causing immense damage to [Root.GetName]. The containment protocols stopped the subspace damage from occurring off planet, however the entire colony on [Root.GetName] has been destroyed and the planet ecosystem devastated.
STH_dangerous_technology.11.name: Omega Catastrophe
STH_dangerous_technology.12.desc: Despite heavy casualties as subspace tears began to form throughout the facility, the volunteers managed to reach the Omega chambers and managed to emit a harmonic resonance to stabilise the molecule manually.\n\nThe colony on [Root.GetName] has been saved.
STH_dangerous_technology.12.name: Omega Catastrophe Averted
STH_dangerous_technology.13.desc: The volunteers failed to reach the Omega molecule in time, causing an Omega catastrophe causing immense damage to [Root.GetName].\n\nThe containment protocols stopped the subspace damage from occurring off planet, however the entire colony on [Root.GetName] has been destroyed and the planet ecosystem devastated.
STH_dangerous_technology.13.name: Omega Catastrophe
STH_dangerous_technology.14.desc: Large amounts of Omega molecules have been passed into the Omega facility upon [Root.GetName], resulting in a spontaneous harmonisation of the existing Omega molecules as they reform into their natural state. Disaster has been averted upon [Root.GetName].
STH_dangerous_technology.14.name: Omega Catastrophe Averted
STH_dangerous_technology.15.desc: Large amounts of Omega molecules have been passed into the Omega facility upon [Root.GetName], resulting in a spontaneous harmonisation of the existing Omega molecules as they reform into their natural state... For 3.2 seconds before they immediately destabilised and caused an Omega event.\n\nThe containment protocols stopped the subspace damage from occurring off planet, however the entire colony on [Root.GetName] has been destroyed and the planet ecosystem devastated.
STH_dangerous_technology.15.name: Omega Catastrophe
STH_dangerous_technology.2.a: It is too dangerous, we will not utilise Omega.
STH_dangerous_technology.2.desc:
Upon entering the system, sensors detect that the local space has suffered a massive subspace disturbance, rendering all warp travel within the system inert and every planet barren.

As the [Root.GetName] draws closer to the site of the facility, readings are detected that show the Omega molecule was definitely the source of this catastrophe. Theoretically, whilst we might now be able to learn the lesson and stabilise Omega from this one disaster, there is always the inherent risk that another catastrophe could occur in future.

Will we risk the quadrant to satisfy our curiosity, or is it arrogant and irresponsible? The final frontier has some boundaries that shouldn't be crossed, and we are looking at one.
STH_dangerous_technology.2.name: Omega Investigation
STH_dangerous_technology.20.desc: The invention of the Interphase Cloaking device is a step forward in advanced cloaking technology, offering the ability to phase through solid objects.\n\nWhilst the theory is sound, real world applications of the technology must be undertaken first to fully ascertain the value of the cloak before it goes into full production. Without testing the device fully onboard a vessel, problems may occur when utilised on regular warships.
STH_dangerous_technology.20.name: Interphase Cloaking
STH_dangerous_technology.21.desc: The [Root.GetName] has been outfitted with the experimental interphase cloaking device, and is going to attempt to phase through a solid object. Sensor buoys will track and examine all aspects of the cloak to ensure it works correctly.
STH_dangerous_technology.21.name: Interphase Cloaking
STH_dangerous_technology.22.desc: The production of the Interphase cloak can wait no longer, and the design has been placed into full production. Whilst it works most of the time, there are key outlying cases where objects become stuck within other objects or crew members fall through the floors.\n\nOur engineers are attempting to fix the edge cases, but this will take some time.
STH_dangerous_technology.22.name: Interphase Cloaking
STH_dangerous_technology.23.a: They did not die in vain.
STH_dangerous_technology.23.desc: The field test onboard the [Root.GetName] has failed, with the [Root.GetName] emerging halfway through the object, merging at a molecular level and causing the deaths of all onboard.\n\nThe last readings from the sensor buoys show a issue with the plasma relays due to the interphase technology, which should allow our engineers to correct the design for actual use.
STH_dangerous_technology.23.name: Interphase Cloaking
STH_dangerous_technology.24.desc: The field test onboard the [Root.GetName] has been a success, with the [Root.GetName] phasing through the object, without being detected by conventional scanners.\n\nThe successful field test revealed issues with the plasma relays due to the interphase technology, however due to the skill of the crew, the [Root.GetName] managed to overcome the issue. With this data it will allow our engineers to correct the design for actual use.
STH_dangerous_technology.24.name: Interphase Cloaking
STH_dangerous_technology.25.desc: It appears our efforts to recover the Interphase Cloak have been spotted by a spying-cloaked Romulan Warbird. \n\nIt is now inevitable that the Romulan Star Empire is aware of our attempts to circumvent the cloaking ban treaty, we must now await the response from Romulus.
STH_dangerous_technology.25.name: Romulan Warbird Spotted!
STH_dangerous_technology.26.desc: Praetor! I bring you an urgent update from one of our ships monitoring Federation space, it appears that the humans have been developing a secret cloaking device, which would surpass even our own cloaking tech directly against the treaty signed at Algeron!
STH_dangerous_technology.26.name: Urgent Message from the Tal Shiar
STH_dangerous_technology.27.desc: Federation Fools! We have seen your attempts to deceive us and continue the use of cloaking technology! This act of treachery will not be allowed to stand, you have one final chance to relinquish this technology or our Warbirds will take it from you by force!
STH_dangerous_technology.27.name: Inbound Message from Romulus
STH_dangerous_technology.28.b: We cannot afford a war right now.
STH_dangerous_technology.28.desc: The Federation no longer considers itself bound by the restrictive measures of the Treaty of Algeron! The Interphase technology does not fall into the agreed cloaking technology ban, and so we will not cower to your demands... \n\nSend your warbirds and we will return them in pieces.
STH_dangerous_technology.28.name: Federation Reject Our Demand!
STH_dangerous_technology.30.desc: During the radiation bombardment, the [Root.GetName] has suffered a large radiation leak which threatened the crew. Due to heroic action on behalf of [Root.GetLeaderName], the reactor leak was fixed before the entire crew was wiped out.\n\nUnfortunately the radiation poisoning has severely affected [Root.GetLeaderName].
STH_dangerous_technology.30.name: Radiation Leak
STH_dangerous_technology.31.desc: During the radiation bombardment, the [Root.GetName] has suffered a large radiation leak which threatened the crew. Due to heroic action on behalf of [Root.GetLeaderName], the reactor leak was fixed before the entire crew was wiped out.\n\nUnfortunately the radiation poisoning was too severe for our medical teams to fix, resulting in the death of [Root.GetLeaderName].
STH_dangerous_technology.31.name: Radiation Leak
STH_dangerous_technology.32.desc: It appears the Romulan threat was a mere bluff and we have received no further actions from the Star Empire. It appears they currently have no appetite for war.
STH_dangerous_technology.32.name: Romulans Backdown.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.1.b: Scan the vessel in more detail.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.1.desc_01:
The [Root.GetFleetName] has successfully generated a static warp bubble around the ship trapped within [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName]'s event horizon. 

The [Root.GetFleetName] rocks as the main deflector struggles to maintain the field, but after several minutes the vessel has been pulled free. 

The bridge falls silent as the viewscreen resolves the ship and confirms its identity: the UESP Manhire, XCV-247. A Declaration class vessel reported lost en route to Vulcan in 2098. 

The Declaration class were Earth's first truly interstellar vessels, borrowing heavily from Vulcan design principles. That one somehow managed to become trapped within a black hole and survive is an amazing feat.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.1.desc_02:
The [Root.GetFleetName] has successfully generated a static warp bubble around the ship trapped within [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName]'s event horizon. 

The [Root.GetFleetName] rocks as the main deflector struggles to maintain the field, but after several minutes the vessel has been pulled free. 

The viewscreen resolves the ship and confirms its identity: the UESP Manhire, XCV-247. An Earth vessel of the Declaration class vessel reported lost en route to Vulcan in 2098. 

The Declaration class were Earth's first truly interstellar vessels, borrowing heavily from Vulcan design principles. That one somehow managed to become trapped within a black hole and survive is an amazing feat.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.1.desc_03:
The [Root.GetFleetName] has successfully generated a static warp bubble around the ship trapped within [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName]'s event horizon. 

The [Root.GetFleetName] rocks as the main deflector struggles to maintain the field, but after several minutes the vessel has been pulled free. 

The bridge falls silent as the viewscreen resolves the ship. While the vessel is of an unknown design, it is clearly primitive. That one somehow managed to become trapped within a black hole and survive is an amazing feat. 

Further scans of the hull show a series of markings in an alien writing: 'UESP Manhire'.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.10.desc_01: Thank you so much for saving the Manhire and my crew. When we became trapped in that black hole we were all sure that was the end... \n\nIt's been so long though, our families back home will have forgotten us surely?
STH_deDeclaratem_event.10.desc_02: Thank you so much for saving the Manhire and my crew. When we became trapped in that black hole we were all sure that was the end... \n\nCan we beg one more favour? Can you provide us passage home?
STH_deDeclaratem_event.10.name: Captain [UESPManhireCaptain.GetName]'s Thanks
STH_deDeclaratem_event.100.desc:
The [Root.GetFleetName] has successfully generated a static warp bubble around the ship trapped within [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName]'s event horizon. 

The [Root.GetFleetName] rocks as the main deflector struggles to maintain the field. 

As the power requirements increase exponentially, Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] is forced to collapse the warp bubble to prevent a core breach. 

In doing so however, the vessel trapped within the event horizon was effectively 'bounced' away and destroyed as it spiralled closer to the singularity.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.11.b: Scan the vessel in more detail.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.11.desc: The bridge falls silent as the viewscreen resolves the ship and confirms its identity: the UESP Manhire, XCV-247. A Declaration class vessel reported lost en route to Vulcan in 2098. \n\nThe Declaration class were Earth's first truly interstellar vessels, borrowing heavily from Vulcan design principles. That one somehow managed to become trapped within a black hole and survive is an amazing feat.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.12.desc: The [deDeclaratemFinder.GetName] have alerted us to the presence of an old Declaration class vessel, which they have successfully pulled from beneath the event horizon of [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName]. \n\nThe UESP Manhire XCV-247, was lost in 2098 en route to Vulcan. The descendents of the crew's families are urging an immediate mission to investigate.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.12.name: De Declaratem
STH_deDeclaratem_event.2.desc_01:
Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] leads an away team to the UESP Manhire. While sensors were unable to detect lifesigns due to the interference from [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName], it is soon apparent that the crew are long dead. 

The ship appears to have been rigged to maintain a low level warp field, which was sufficient when coupled with conventional thrusters to preserve the vessel in a long spiral around the singularity. The subroutines written to oversee the process while the crew retreated to cryo-stasis pods appear to have a number of flaws which resulted in power distribution shifting from the pods to the warp drive, killing those inside. 

The descendents of the Manhire's crew can take some solace in knowing the true fate of the vessel, which after repairs is fully functional.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.2.desc_02:
Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] leads an away team to the UESP Manhire. While sensors were unable to detect lifesigns due to the interference from [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName], it is soon apparent that the crew are long dead. 

The ship appears to have been rigged to maintain a low level warp field, which was sufficient when coupled with conventional thrusters to preserve the vessel in a long spiral around the singularity. The subroutines written to oversee the process while the crew retreated to cryo-stasis pods appear to have a number of flaws which resulted in power distribution shifting from the pods to the warp drive, killing those inside. 

The [Root.Owner.GetName] has agreed to pass the crew's remains to the nearest [humanCountry.GetName] outpost. 

After moderate repairs, the vessel appears to be fully functional.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.2.desc_03:
Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] leads an away team to the UESP Manhire. While sensors were unable to detect lifesigns due to the interference from [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName], it is soon apparent that the crew are long dead. 

The ship appears to have been rigged to maintain a low level warp field, which was sufficient when coupled with conventional thrusters to preserve the vessel in a long spiral around the singularity. The subroutines written to oversee the process while the crew retreated to cryo-stasis pods appear to have a number of flaws which resulted in power distribution shifting from the pods to the warp drive, killing those inside. 

After moderate repairs, the vessel appears to be fully functional.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.200.desc:
The [Root.GetFleetName] has successfully generated a static warp bubble around the ship trapped within [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName]'s event horizon. 

The [Root.GetFleetName] rocks as the main deflector struggles to maintain the field, but after several minutes the other vessel is free. 

Despite best efforts however, the [Root.GetFleetName]'s antimatter containment fields sustain heavy damage and are breached. Both vessels are annihilated.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.3.desc_01:
Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] leads an away team to the UESP Manhire. While sensors were unable to detect lifesigns due to the interference from [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName], it is soon apparent that the crew are alive. 

The ship appears to have been rigged to maintain a low level warp field, which was sufficient when coupled with conventional thrusters to preserve the vessel in a long spiral around the singularity. The subroutines written to oversee the process while the crew entered cryo-stasis pods flawlessly balanced the power requirements of the engines with those of the pods. 

While the crew require medical attention, all appear to have survived. The ship itself needs only minor repairs in order to return to full operational status.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.3.desc_02:

The subroutines written to oversee the process while the crew entered cryo-stasis pods flawlessly balanced the power requirements of the engines with those of the pods. 

While the crew require medical attention, all appear to have survived. The ship itself needs only minor repairs in order to return to full operational status.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.6.desc_01:
Scans prove mostly ineffective, forcing Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] to plan an away team to the UESP Manhire. 

However, moments before departure a power surge was detected in the Manhire's Warp Ring assembly. Within seconds the surge cascaded through the protective shielding of the Warp Core, triggering a core breach. While the [Root.GetFleetName] was relatively close to the Manhire, the core breach effect was heavily dissipated by the proximity to [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName], resulting in only minor damage. 

While [Root.Owner.GetName] historians and the descendents of the Manhire's crew will be glad to know the fate of the vessel, many will wonder if Captain [Root.GetLeaderName]'s haste may have killed a crew which had survived for so long within the black hole.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.6.desc_02:
Scans prove mostly ineffective, forcing Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] to plan an away team to the UESP Manhire. 

However, moments before departure a power surge was detected in the Manhire's Warp Ring assembly. Within seconds the surge cascaded through the protective shielding of the Warp Core, triggering a core breach. While the [Root.GetFleetName] was relatively close to the Manhire, the core breach effect was heavily dissipated by the proximity to [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName], resulting in only minor damage. 

While [humanCountry.GetName] historians and the descendents of the Manhire's crew will be glad to know the fate of the vessel, many will wonder if Captain [Root.GetLeaderName]'s haste may have killed a crew which had survived for so long within the black hole.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.6.desc_03:
Scans prove mostly ineffective, forcing Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] to plan an away team to the UESP Manhire. 

However, moments before departure a power surge was detected in the Manhire's Warp Ring assembly. Within seconds the surge cascaded through the protective shielding of the Warp Core, triggering a core breach. While the [Root.GetFleetName] was relatively close to the Manhire, the core breach effect was heavily dissipated by the proximity to [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName], resulting in only minor damage. 

Captain [Root.GetLeaderName] ponders whether [shipLeader.GetHerHis] haste may have killed a crew which had survived for so long within the black hole.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.7.desc: The [humanCountry.GetName] has been informed of the retrieval of their vessel from [deDeclaratemBlackHole.GetName], and a mission to salvage the vessel is being assembled. \n\nRelations between the [humanCountry.GetName] and the [Root.Owner.GetName] have benefited from the act.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.8.desc_01: The Manhire proves an easy target for the lightly armed [Root.GetFleetName]. The experience gained by the crew and materials salvaged seems to weigh poorly against the damage done to relations between the [Root.Owner.GetName] and the [humanCountry.GetName].
STH_deDeclaratem_event.8.desc_02: The Manhire proves an easy target for the lightly armed [Root.GetFleetName]. The experience gained by the crew and materials salvaged seems to weigh poorly against the potential for study of the vessel's unknown makers, however.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.9.b: The design has had its day.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.9.desc: The recent salvage of the Declaration class UESP Manhire has heightened interest in the old designs. The ability of the vessel to sustain itself so long within a black hole has led some teams to propose the reinstatement of the class using modern technologies, as an inexpensive exploration vessel.
STH_deDeclaratem_event.9.name: Research Opportunities
STH_deDeclaratem_event.name: De Declaratem
STH_decision_events.400.desc: Our efforts have been fruitful and a new cache of nanites has been deposited on our colony.
STH_decision_events.400.name: Metals Extracted
STH_decision_events.600.desc_01: The planet of [punished_planet.GetName] have defied the Terran Empire on too many occasions now, it is timed that the public leaders experienced what it is like to anger the [Root.GetRulerTitle] a session in the Torture chambers at highest setting should make our point very clear.
STH_decision_events.600.desc_02: The population of [punished_planet.GetName] have flaunted our roles and disrespected our instructions for too long now. It is time they were sent a message to remind them who rules over them. Our military have rounded up several prominent public figures who will be executed on command. This planet will think twice before it challenges the might of [Root.GetName].
STH_decision_events.600.name: Public Executions
STH_decision_events.601.desc_01: The will of the Founders should never be challenged, and just as the populan of Teplan discovered to their peril, the Dominion can create some prolific diseases. \n\nThe population of [punished_planet.GetName] continue to suffer from the disease that we have infected them with and will do so until we provide them with the cure.
STH_decision_events.601.desc_02: The disease inflicted on the planet of [punished_planet.GetName] continues to suppress the rebellious nature of the population and teach them a lesson.
STH_decision_events.601.name: A deadly punishment
STH_decision_events.602.desc_01: Cardassian Labour camps are feared across the galaxy, overseen by a prefect who knew exactly how to make the prisoners in their care produce the highest output. As punishments go this was backbreaking work and a stark reminder not to cross the Cardassian Union.
STH_decision_events.602.desc_02: The troublemakers have been rounded up and put to work in a series of labour camps across the planet, this will serve as a harsh reminder not to cause trouble on a planet owned by [Root.GetName].
STH_decision_events.602.name: Forced Labour Camps
STH_decision_events.650.a: Send a team led by a Scientist.
STH_decision_events.650.b: Send a team led by an Admiral.
STH_decision_events.650.desc: The local authorities on [THIS.GetName], have requested additional support to bolster their export of $consumer_goods$. \n\nWe should dispatch a team to secure the trade routes and improve the production of supplies on the planet.
STH_decision_events.650.name: Supply Line Operations
STH_decision_events.653.desc: With the cancelation of the ongoing operation to improve consumer goods production on [THIS.GetName] the ongoing benefits have been removed.
STH_decision_events.653.name: Operation Cancelled
STH_decision_events.654.desc: The operation on [This.GetName] has been completed successfully and whilst significant improvements were made whilst the team was in orbit will slowly return to normal levels, the planet will see a small boost for the foreseeable future whilst the improvements to the planets infrastructure remain in place.
STH_decision_events.654.name: Project Complete
STH_decision_events.655.desc:
The government on [This.GetName] have requested additional assistance from our medical agency to help resolve a number of local issues that are slowly the population growth speed on this planet. 

The team led by either a Doctor or Nurse will set up a number of labs and hospital facilities looking at a range of issues that could be causing the planet's pops numbers to rise slowly. 

Anything and everything from diseases to poor health practices will be checked in a bit to ensure this colony is in the best possible health.
STH_decision_events.655.name: Medical Assistance Required
STH_decision_events.657.desc: The medical assistance provided by our experts have managed to successfully complete its operation on the planet [This.GetName] with positive results, the improvements to the infrastructure and medical facilities have provided a number of important health benefits to the people of this world that will remain for a number of years.
STH_decision_events.657.name: Medical Assistance Provided
STH_decision_events.671.desc: The local scientific authorities on [This.GetName], has requested additional support to bolster vital ongoing research initiatives. \n\nWe should dispatch a science team to support the local research projects.
STH_decision_events.671.name: Assist Research
STH_decision_events.673.desc: Our science team has completed their agreed assignment to the science centre on [This.GetName] having provided the local scientists with additional leads and scientific breakthroughs, which they will now continue to work on for the next few months.
STH_decision_events.673.name: Assist Research Complete
STH_decision_events.674.desc: With the cancelation of the ongoing research operation on [This.GetName] the ongoing benefits have been removed.
STH_decision_events.674.name: Assist Research Cancelled
STH_decision_events.680.desc: [This.GetName] has been marked as ready for re-introduction into the collective, prepare redeployment of drones to establish collective presence at this location.\n\nThe collective grows stronger.
STH_decision_events.680.name: Re-Assimilate World
STH_disease.100.desc.bendii:
Peace and long life may be that which all [From.Species.GetName]s strive for, but it is not guaranteed.

[From.GetTitle] [From.GetName] has been diagnosed with Bendii Syndrome, a degenerative neurological illness affecting a minority of [From.Species.GetName]s. 

Characterised by wasting, weakness, fatigue, fever and a gradual but accelerating loss of emotional control, victims are prone to sudden bursts of emotion.
STH_disease.100.desc.dorek_all: [From.GetTitle] [From.GetName] has been diagnosed with early stage Dorek Syndrome, a fatal auto-immune disease affecting just one in five million [From.Species.GetName]. \n\nWith only a short time left, [From.GetName] prepares to put [From.GetHerHis] affairs in order. [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] has communicated the sympathies of the [Root.GetName].
STH_disease.100.desc.dorek_fer:
The twenty-third rule of acquisition states that 'nothing is more important than your health... except for your money'. 

[From.GetTitle] [From.GetName] has come to question the truth to this rule after [From.GetSheHe] was diagnosed with early stage Dorek Syndrome, a fatal autoimmune disease affecting just one in five million [From.Species.GetName]. 

With only a short time left, [From.GetName] prepares to put [From.GetHerHis] financial affairs in order. [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] has communicated the sympathies of the [Root.GetName], and offered to oversee the auction of [From.GetName]'s vacuum-desiccated remains. For a reasonable fee of course.
STH_disease.100.desc.irumodic: Following [From.GetHerHis] yearly physical, [From.GetTitle] [From.GetName] has been diagnosed with a structural defect in [From.GetHerHis] parietal lobe. While the defect is small, exposed only after a level-4 neurographic scan was performed, it could potentially develop into a number of neurological disorders. \n\n[From.GetName] is reassured that [From.GetSheHe] is in no immediate danger, but that [From.GetSheHe] will require more frequent testing as a result of the diagnosis.
STH_disease.100.desc.yarim_all: [From.GetTitle] [From.GetName] has been diagnosed with late stage Yarim Fel Syndrome, a fatal auto-immune disease affecting [From.Species.GetName]s. \n\nWith only a short time left, [From.GetName] prepares to put [From.GetHerHis] affairs in order. [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] has communicated the sympathies of the [Root.GetName].
STH_disease.100.desc.yarim_car:
All who serve the state hope to do so for many years. At the age of ascension, molars are given to the Bureau of Identification safe in the knowledge the state sees all and knows all. 

The state has long known that [From.GetName] carried a genetic predisposition to develop Yarim-Fel Syndrome, [From.GetHerHis] operators, monitors and administrators have long been aware. Now, with [From.GetHerHis] latest mandatory monthly blood test, it is certain. 

None doubt that [From.GetName] will continue to do [From.GetHerHis] duty; continue to the best of [From.GetHerHis] ability until [From.GetSheHe] can do no more.
STH_disease.100.name: [From.GetName] Diagnosed
STH_disease.1001.desc:
An outbreak of the [From.GetPlagueName] has been reported on [Target.GetName] in the [Target.Solar_System.GetName] system. 

While the local quarantine has so far succeeded in containing the infection within a handful of settlements on the planet, it may soon spread if untreated. 

Authorities on [Target.GetName] have requested the dispatch of a medical team to the planet immediately.
STH_disease.1001.name: [FromFrom.GetName]
STH_disease.1003.desc: Despite efforts to halt the spread of [This.GetPlagueName], medical staff are now reporting that the disease has begun to spread amongst the population, putting more lives at risk!
STH_disease.1003.name: [This.GetPlagueName] outbreak spreading on [Target.GetName]
STH_disease.1004.a: Urge the population to remain calrm.
STH_disease.1004.desc: The population on [Target.GetName] are no longer content to remain locked down, fueled by a combination of fear and anger at the situation many have taken to the streets to voice the frustrations.
STH_disease.1004.name: Riots on [Target.GetName]
STH_disease.1005.desc: Our medical experts have reported that the death toll on [Target.GetName] have started to take an alarming rise over the last few days. \n\nWith the situation deteriorating rapidly we can only hope the experts are able to resolve the situation
STH_disease.1005.name: Death rate increases!
STH_disease.1006.desc: The plague outbreak that is spreading across [Root.GetName] has now been upgraded to Pandemic Level, with further intervention required to halt the spread and prevent the loss of life.
STH_disease.1006.name: Disease outbreak now a Pandemic
STH_disease.101.desc.irumodic:
Several years after having been diagnosed with a structural defect in [From.GetHerHis] parietal lobe, [From.GetName] has been diagnosed with Irumodic Syndrome.

Being a degenerative neurological disorder that triggers deterioration of the synaptic pathways, the condition causes confusion, delusions, and eventually death.

While still capable of performing [From.GetHerHis] duties, eventually [From.GetName] may be forced to retire as [From.GetHerHis] situation deteriorates.
STH_disease.101.name: [From.GetName] Complications
STH_disease.1010.a: Share the breakthrough with all races.
STH_disease.1010.b: Share the breakthrough with friendly races.
STH_disease.1010.desc.arxolite_corruptio: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Arxolite Corruption has concluded trials with positive results.
Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Arxolite Corruption has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.centauran_flu_1: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Centauran Flu has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.centauran_flu_2: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Centauran Flu has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.kentaran_pox_1: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Kentaran Pox has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.kentaran_pox_2: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Kentaran Pox has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.laurentian_plague_: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Laurentian Plague has concluded trials with positive results.
Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Laurentian Plague has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.manxilite_corrupti: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Manxilite Corruption has concluded trials with positive results.
Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Manxilite Corruption has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.monoclinic_pagupha: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Monoclinic Paguphage has concluded trials with positive results.
Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Monoclinic Paguphage has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.rhombic_paguphage_: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Rhombic Paguphage has concluded trials with positive results.
Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Rhombic Paguphage has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.seripian_flu_1: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Seripian Flu has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.seripian_flu_2: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Seripian Flu has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.tanerian_plague_1: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Tanerian Plague has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.tanerian_plague_2: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Tanerian Plague has concluded trials with overwhelmingly positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.ynorian_pox_1: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a treatment to halt the progress of the deadly Ynorian Pox has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.desc.ynorian_pox_2: Medical researchers across the [Root.GetName] are celebrating as a vaccine for the deadly Ynorian Pox has concluded trials with positive results.
STH_disease.1010.name: Medical Breakthrough
STH_disease.1011.desc: The [From.GetName] has generously decided to share the results of their advances in developing a cure for the Tanerian Plague. The data provided will provide a significant boost to developing [Root.GetSpeciesName]-specific treatments.
STH_disease.1011.name: Shared Medical Advances
STH_disease.1012.desc: The [From.GetName] has generously decided to share the results of their advances in developing a cure for the Laurentian Plague. The data provided will provide a significant boost to developing [Root.GetSpeciesName]-specific treatments.
STH_disease.1012.name: Shared Medical Advances
STH_disease.1013.desc: The [From.GetName] has generously decided to share the results of their advances in developing a cure for the Seripian Flu. The data provided will provide a significant boost to developing [Root.GetSpeciesName]-specific treatments.
STH_disease.1013.name: Shared Medical Advances
STH_disease.1014.desc: The [From.GetName] has generously decided to share the results of their advances in developing a cure for the Centauran Flu. The data provided will provide a significant boost to developing [Root.GetSpeciesName]-specific treatments.
STH_disease.1014.name: Shared Medical Advances

STH_disease.1017.desc: The [From.GetName] has generously decided to share the results of their advances in developing a cure for the Rhombic Paguphage. The data provided will provide a significant boost to developing [Root.GetSpeciesName]-specific treatments.

STH_disease.1019.name: Shared Medical Advances
STH_disease.102.desc.irumodic: Having lived with Irumodic Syndrome for some time now, it has become apparent that [From.GetName] is no longer able to perform [From.GetHerHis] duties. \n\nAs [From.GetSheHe] plans to retire, the [Root.GetName] celebrates [From.GetHerHis] service.
STH_disease.102.name: [From.GetName] Retires
STH_disease.1020.desc: The [From.GetName] has generously decided to share the results of their advances in developing a cure for the Manxilite Corruption. The data provided will provide a significant boost to developing [Root.GetSpeciesName]-specific treatments.
STH_disease.1020.name: Shared Medical Advances
STH_disease.1100.desc: Whilst the efforts of the [Root.GetFleetName] and [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] have prevented the outbreak from spreading further, sadly they were too late to save the majority of those in the infected settlements whom have since died from the plague. \n\nThe outbreak has officially been declared over.
STH_disease.1100.name: [FromFrom.GetPlagueName] outbreak ends on [FromFrom.Planet.GetName]
STH_disease.1110.a: This is could have been avoided.
STH_disease.1110.desc: Without the support of a [Root.GetName] medical mission, the local authorities on [plaguePop.Planet.GetName] were left to their own resources to prevent the spread of the outbreak to another settlement. Whilst strict quarantine laws have succeeded in limiting the spread of the plague, they have also hampered efforts to treat those who were already infected, the majority of whom have now died. \n\nThe outbreak has officially been declared over.
STH_disease.1110.name: [FromFrom.GetPlagueName] outbreak ends on [FromFrom.Planet.GetName]
STH_disease.1120.a: This is could have been avoided.
STH_disease.1120.desc: Without the support of a [Root.GetName] medical mission, the local authorities on [plaguePop.Planet.GetName] were unable to prevent the spread of the outbreak to another settlement.
STH_disease.1120.name: [FromFrom.GetPlagueName] outbreak spreads on [FromFrom.Planet.GetName]
STH_disease.1200.desc:
The efforts of the [Root.GetFleetName] and [Root.Owner.GetCaptainTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName] have prevented the outbreak of [FromFrom.GetPlagueName] from spreading further. 

Better still, a cure to the particular strain of [FromFrom.GetPlagueName] affecting the colony was discovered in time to save the lives of those infected. 

The outbreak has officially been declared over.
STH_disease.1200.name: [FromFrom.GetPlagueName] outbreak ends on [FromFrom.Planet.GetName]
STH_disease.1501.desc:
An outbreak of the Tanerian Plague has been reported on [plaguePop.Planet.GetName] in the [plaguePop.Planet.Solar_System.GetName] system. 

While the local quarantine has so far succeeded in containing the infection within a handful of settlements on the planet, it may soon spread if untreated. 

Authorities on [plaguePop.Planet.GetName] have requested the dispatch of a medical team to the planet immediately.
STH_disease.1501.name: Tanerian Plague outbreak on [plaguePop.Planet.GetName]
