Captain [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] agrees and a communications link to the ship is established, where the details of detail of the trade is confirmed. 

The crew is transported off of Triskelion and back to the mineral survey site on [triskelion_original_planet.GetName], where a series of packaged boxes full of Dabo equipment are transported back to Triskelion.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.21.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.22.desc:
'Now let me tell you about the game of Chula', [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] says to the three brains, before talking at length on the thrill of the game and the gambling it entails. 

With each additional detail the brains voice murmurs of approval until at last one lights up and demands to initiate the trade of the crew's freedom for the installation of a fully equipped Chula table. 

Captain [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] agrees and a communications link to the ship is established, where the details of detail of the trade is confirmed. 

The crew is transported off of Triskelion and back to the mineral survey site on [triskelion_original_planet.GetName], where a series of packaged boxes full of Chula equipment are transported back to Triskelion.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.22.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.3.desc:
Beaming to the surface, Captain [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] and the away team prepare temporary accommodations to support the survey mission. The prefabricated structures are completed within three hours and the team begins the process of mapping the deposits in the immediate area. 

Several days pass and it has become increasingly difficult to confirm any of the mineral readings detected from orbit. The Captain orders the [Root.GetFleetName] to enter a lower orbit in order to boost the effectiveness of its sensors, but as the ship completes the maneuver a large energy spike is detected from the surface. 

The [Root.GetFleetName] attempts to contact Captain [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName], but it appears the entire away team has vanished from [From.From.GetName]. 

The ship spends several days scanning the surface, but no clues are found to the explain what happened to the missing crew.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.3.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.4.a: We will not submit to slavery.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.4.b: We have no choice but to obey, for now.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.4.desc:
Captain [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] and [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetHerHis] away team awake from unconsciousness to find themselves transported to an unknown world. The sun in the sky betrays that they are no longer on [triskelion_original_planet.GetName], and strange desert vegetation dots the landscape. 

Moments after composing themselves, the away team is beset by a group of aliens armed with primitive swords and wearing simple, cloth tunics. Energy weapons prove ineffective in the local environment, their batteries drained, and the team is quickly overpowered. 

The away team are led through the desert to the entrance of a city encircled by a great stone wall. A bald figure awaits them, clothed in a black and red robe. 

'I am Galt, Master Thrall of Triskelion, this world.' he says to [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName]. 'You are to be commended for your fighting spirit. The Providers will make good use of you...'
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.4.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.5.a: We have no choice but to obey, for now.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.5.b: We will NOT submit to slavery!
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.5.desc: The show of defiance does not move Galt, who simply nods to a sword-armed alien. The alien grabs the nearest of [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName]'s crew, an older male ensign, and with a quick slash of the sword cuts open his neck. The rest of the away team struggle, but are silenced as the Master Thrall booms: \n\n'Perhaps the situation has been made clearer. Disobedience will not be tolerated!'
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.5.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.6.desc:
Captain [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName], enraged by the death of [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetHerHis] officer lunges at Galt. They grapple while the rest of the crew engages the sword-wielding aliens. 

Despite their advanced training, the crew are unable to withstand the onslaught and are cut down one by one. 

Galt and the Captain continue to fight for a moment, until at last the aliens drag [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetHerHim] away and thrust a sword through [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetHerHis] heart.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.6.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.7.a: We will train, but escape is our goal.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.7.b: We will train, no enemy will defeat us.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.7.c: We must be able to strike a bargain!
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.7.desc:
The Captain agrees to submit and the away team is led through the gates of the city into a square of stone barracks, where they are stripped of their uniforms and weapons and clothed in the same cloth tunics the aliens wear. 

A day passes and they are met again by the Galt. 

'You will train yourselves for combat. Armed, unarmed, single and in groups. This you will do for the will of The Providers. Serve and you may live.' 

The group is then split up and led away to a series of training grounds littered throughout the city.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.7.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.8.desc:
Weeks pass and [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] and [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetHerHis] crew have followed the intense training regime Galt has provided for them without waver, while coming to learn more of Triskelion through the other slaves kept in the city. 

One afternoon after a challenging group combat session, [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] is able to slip away from the training grounds and investigate several of the nearby buildings before coming across what appears to be a transporter pad of some variety. 

Stepping onto the platform, a blinding light flashes and Captain [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] disappears from the chamber.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.8.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.9.a: Let us fight for our freedom!
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.9.desc:
[triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] awakens once more, having been transported inside a large cavern. Dim lighting fills a great hall, where on plinths in the centre stand what appear to be three brains suspended in large jars. One brain starts to glow and a voice says: 

'You show great promise. Tell us of your worth, so we may bid to own you.'

The Captain argues with the brains for several minutes, learning that they are the remains of the Triskelion civilisation - the Providers - having abandoned the need for their physical bodies. It becomes apparent that the brains live now for little more than the opportunity to wager sums of money on contests between captured aliens. 

Seeing this, Captain [triskelion_enslaved_leader.GetName] offers a proposal.
STH_gamestersOfTriskelion_event.9.name: The Gamesters of Triskelion
STH_gamma.110.b: It is not worth our time.
STH_gamma.110.desc: The crew of the [gamma_ship_name.GetName] have picked up an unusual planet on long range scanners that warrants further investigation. The science officer recommends setting course.
STH_gamma.110.name: Gamma Quadrant... Intresting Planet on sensors
STH_gamma.111.desc: On arriving in orbit of the mysterious planet, our science teams have found evidence of a primitive pre-warp race undergoing a technolgical and sociological revolution. The assembled science teams have requested a closer look at the species on the planet's surface. An undercover mission would enable us get better readings from the colony.
STH_gamma.111.name: Primitive Culture Found
STH_gamma.112.desc: After a week studying the colony the crews of the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] have brought back a wealth of information about the civilisation which will keep the anthropologists back in the [Root.GetName] occupied for years.\n\nWith the information collected it is time to resume the mission.
STH_gamma.112.name: Mission Successful
STH_gamma.113.desc: [Root.GetRulerName] reports in from the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] are troubling, the away team who went in disguised as the natives have been captured by forces on the planet. \n\nBelieving the away team to be part of an enemy faction. Our security forces have put together a plan to retrieve them, however it could lead to casualties on both sides.
STH_gamma.113.name: Away team captured!
STH_gamma.114.desc: The mission was successful and our security forces were able to rescue the hostages and safely return them to the fleet without harming the natives or blowing our cover. \n\nWhilst we were unable to gather as much information as we had hoped the team did manage to at least to find out about their justice system...
STH_gamma.114.name: Away team rescued
STH_gamma.115.desc: The mission to retrieve the away team has ended in failure, whilst attempting to infiltrate the stronghold one of the team was spotted by the guards resulting in a fire fight. \n\nSadly not all of the away team were retrieved alive and a number of the native population were injured in the attempt.
STH_gamma.115.name: Mission a disaster
STH_gamma.116.desc: [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] have uncovered a thriving civillisation on the planet and whilst they are are significantly smaller than us, they were able to provide our teams with vital information about the region and were kind enough to provide valuable research to our teams.
STH_gamma.116.name: New Culture Discovered
STH_gamma.117.desc: Not every world holds the recipe for adventure and unfortunately this newly discovered planet offers our teams nothing in the way of excitement. A barren world, with no life or remarkable features. \n\nAt least the crew got the chance to stretch their legs.
STH_gamma.117.name: A lifeless planet found
STH_gamma.118.desc: Whilst the newly discovered planet does not harbour any advanced lifeforms, our engineering teams are pleased to confirm that the planet does host an exceptionally rich amount of resources that we can harvest an return home with.
STH_gamma.118.name: MIneral rich planet found
STH_gamma.120.desc: [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] has detected an M-Class planet on long range sensors which appears to support a warp capable civilisation with deposits of Dilithium and evidence of advanced mineral refinement. It is possible we maybe able to trade for some of their resources.
STH_gamma.120.name: The Quickening
STH_gamma.121.desc_01: Our sensors have indicated that the planet is within the territory of the Dominion, readings from the planet surface seem very different from the long range estimates. It is clear that there is a population of humanoids living in the remains of a ruined society with the remnants of a once powerful civilisation in tatters all around them. \n\nOur CMO indicates that many of the life signs are erratic at best and it is clear they are in need of aid.
STH_gamma.121.name: The Quickening
STH_gamma.122.desc_01:
The species on the planet belong call themselves the Teplans and explain that they were once a powerful space faring culture, however they refused to submit to the rule of the neighbouring Dominion. After a fierce battle the Teplan forces were destroyed and as punishment for their defiance an incurable plague was placed on them by the Vorta scientists. 

Without warning the blight each teplan carries could quicken and bring a painful death. 

A stark reminder to anyone who dares to defy the might of the Founders.
STH_gamma.122.name: The Quickening
STH_gamma.124.desc_01: Despite the tireless efforts of the fleets Chief Medical Officer they were unable to undo the effects of the Dominion plague in the short span of time allocated. As the time draws on [gamma_leader_name.GetName] has made the decision that it is no longer safe to remain in Dominion space and has ordered the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] onwards and away from Teplan.
STH_gamma.124.name: Unable to find a cure for the Quickening
STH_gamma.125.a: This insult will not be allowed.
STH_gamma.125.desc: Mighty Founder, our listening posts have detected outsiders from [gamma_mission_teplan.GetName] attempting to cure the Teplan Blight we inflicted upon the unruly Teplan people. It seems a team of their medical experts beamed down to the planets surface and spent a number of days seeking to undo the punishment we applied. The fleet left the system before we could send in the Jem'Hadar who took appropriate action to remind the Teplan's of our might.
STH_gamma.125.name: Interference detected on Teplan
STH_gamma.126.desc_01: This is a message for [Root.GetRulerName] of [Root.GetName], your interference in curing the Teplan Blight has not gone un-noticed by the forces within the Dominion. Your actions will not be allowed to continue and there will be retribution for your acts against the Dominion, just as the punishment for the Teplans was severe.\n\nYou have been warned.
STH_gamma.126.desc_02: This is a message for [Root.GetRulerName] of [Root.GetName], your failed attempt to cure Teplan Blight demonstrates the solids ignorance, repeated interference in our affairs will lead to your destruction.\n\nYou have been warned.
STH_gamma.126.name: Incoming Transmission from the Dominion
STH_gamma.127.b: They are a threat to the Link.
STH_gamma.127.desc_01: The fools in the [gamma_mission_teplan.GetName] are standing by their actions on Teplan, their arrogance stance only further demonstrates why solids can not be trusted.\n\n This insolence should not go unpunished.
STH_gamma.127.desc_02: Those cowards in the [gamma_mission_teplan.GetName] have trembled in fear of our words and uttered an apology for their actions on Teplan. \n\nAs the Jem'Hadar say victory is life.
STH_gamma.127.desc_03: The idiots in [gamma_mission_teplan.GetName] believe they can lie to us and deny their actions on Teplan, however we see through their words and know the truth, any continued action against our interests should be dealt with most severely.
STH_gamma.127.name: Response to our message
STH_gamma.129.desc: Our medical team and scientists believe they have found a major breakthrough in deciphering the Teplan blight. With this hard work our Chief Medical Officer believes future generations of Teplans may be immune to the effects of this terrible disease. [gamma_leader_name.GetName] feels that the fleet has lingered to long in hostile territory and having helped the stricken Teplans we should move on before we are detected.
STH_gamma.129.name: The Quickening, a cure found
STH_gamma.130.name: Unusual Asteroid Detected
STH_gamma.131.desc: Our away team has returned from the facility with a wealth of new research on the stellar phenomenon occurring within the system.\n\nWe have collected the information and will send it back to our scientists at home for further investigation.
STH_gamma.131.name: Asteroid Facility
STH_gamma.132.desc: Our away team has returned from the facility disappointed, the previous occupants appear to have wiped all the facilities data banks and taken all information with them. Whilst it was an interesting experience for the crew, there is little scientific benefit from the mission.
STH_gamma.132.name: Asteroid Facility
STH_gamma.133.desc: On beaming down to the colony, the colony leader introduced themselves to [gamma_leader_name.GetName] as being part of an advanced team from a species called the Stakoron who had left their homeworld many year ago. The Stakorons seem extremely pleased to have met the away team and offer them a rare crystal that forms naturally on this asteroid, seemingly unaware of its importance.
STH_gamma.133.name: Asteroid Colony
STH_gamma.134.b: They can take care of themselves.
STH_gamma.134.desc: The crew from the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] are introduced to the leaders of the colony, who explain that they are the frequent victims of pirate raids, which explains their reluctance to engage the fleet in open communication. Once the colonists are convinced we mean them no harm, they offer the away team a hearty meal, warm hospitality and a sample of the Deuterium they have been refining. Our tactical officer advised that with a sample of the fleets boronite the colony could improve their defences and stand a better chance of repelling further attacks.
STH_gamma.134.name: Asteroid Colony
STH_gamma.135.desc: The colony appears to be home to a group of vicious thugs who attempt to ambush our away team, in an attempt to ransom them back to [Root.GetName], luckily the away team were able to defend themselves without taking too much damage. \n\nA number of the landing party were unfortunately injured and will carry the scars of what happened down on the asteroid.
STH_gamma.135.name: Asteroid Colony
STH_gamma.136.desc: The attack on [gamma_ship_name.GetName] was vicious and wild, but the suprior training and firepower of the [Root.GetName] vessels and crew was sufficent to repel the attack and beat back the agressors, who after taking severe casulties retreated leaving their ill-gotten cargo behind.
STH_gamma.136.name: Fleet Under Attack
STH_gamma.137.a: A tragic end to the mission.
STH_gamma.137.desc: The attack on [gamma_ship_name.GetName] took the Captain and crew by surprise, the smugglers managed to get in several lethal shots to the [gamma_ship_name.GetName] warp drive before they could raise their shields. Unfortunately the damage was severe and the ship was lost with all hands. \n\nThe Admiralty board has ordered the fleet to return to the Alpha Quadrant, the mission is over.
STH_gamma.137.name: Fleet Under Attack
STH_gamma.141.desc: [gamma_fleet_name.GetName]'s course through the Gamma Quadrant has taken them via a new type of nebula that has never been observed back in the Alpha Quadrant, the ship and crew spend several days cataloguing the findings and preparing the data to bring home to [Root.GetName].
STH_gamma.141.name: A new nebula discovered
STH_gamma.142.desc: [gamma_fleet_name.GetName]'s course through the Gamma Quadrant has taken them via a nebula rich in Deuterium.\n\nHarvesting the resource should not take too long and can be put to good use by [Root.GetName].
STH_gamma.142.name: A new nebula discovered
STH_gamma.143.desc: The [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] has been ambushed whilst exploring a nebula within the Gamma Quadrant, it appears a group of local pirates have been using the sensor blocking effects of the Nebula to catch unsuspecting vessels that get too close.
STH_gamma.143.name: Ambush at the Nebula
STH_gamma.144.desc: [gamma_ship_name.GetName] proved to be too powerful for the attackers and was able to defeat them in a decisive battle in the Nebula. A fine victory for [gamma_leader_name.GetName] and their crew.
STH_gamma.144.name: Fleet Victory
STH_gamma.145.a: A tragic end to the mission
STH_gamma.145.desc:
The attack on [gamma_ship_name.GetName] took the Captain and crew by surprise, the ambushers quickly overwhelmed the [gamma_ship_name.GetName].

 The damage was severe and the ship was lost with all hands. 

The Admirality board has ordered the fleet to return to the Alpha Quadrant, the mission is over.
STH_gamma.145.name: Fleet Defeat
STH_gamma.150.desc_01: [Root.GetRulerName] we have had reports that our fleet in the Gamma Quadrant is under attack by a group claiming to be rogue Jem'Hadar rebelling against the Founders and Vorta. It looks like they are suffering from some form of withdrawal. They have opened fire on our ships with a savage aggression.\n\nThe fleet is prepared to defend itself or attempt to flee.
STH_gamma.150.desc_02: Our fleet in the Gamma Quadrant has reported that they are under attack by unidentified assailants, they are deliberately targetting the lead vessel. \n\nShould we order the fleet to stand their ground or escape whilst they can.
STH_gamma.150.desc_03: Our fleets are under attack whilst on expedition in the Gamma Quadrant, it appears that they have been tracking our ships for a number of weeks waiting for the right moment to strike. \n\nThe pirates have us out flanked and out gunned, but lack our officers training and co-ordination.
STH_gamma.150.desc_04: [Root.GetRulerName] our fleets in the Gamma Quadrant are under attack by the Magisterial Cheka Kingdom, slavers from the planet Cheka deep within the Quadrant. It looks like they are eager to add [Root.GetSpeciesName] to their ranks of enslaved species.\n\nCrushing their fleets here will do a great deal to ease the slavery rackets terrorizing this sector.
STH_gamma.150.name: Fleet Under Attack
STH_gamma.151.desc: [gamma_ship_name.GetName] proved to be too powerful for the attackers and was able to defeat them in a decisive battle over their foes. A fine victory for [gamma_leader_name.GetName] and their crew.
STH_gamma.151.name: Fleet Victory
STH_gamma.152.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] I am saddened to report the lost of [gamma_leader_name.GetName] and the crew of [gamma_ship_name.GetName] in battle against their enemy. \n\nThis puts a serious dent in the expedition and the Admiralty board have decided to terminate the mission and order the fleet back home.
STH_gamma.152.name: Fleet Defeated
STH_gamma.2.desc: The discovery of the Bajoran Wormhole has opened up a new quadrant to explore. Our Admirals and Scientists are queuing up to explore the far reaches of this new frontier, exploring the sectors way from the major empires. Our teams have prepared our fleets for an expedition into unknown territories. Shall we set course for adventure?
STH_gamma.2.name: Expedition to the Gamma Quadrant
STH_gamma.210.desc: The [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] has encounted a group of Karemma Traders, the fleet has opened communications and the Karemman Captain is willing to trade a number of high profile materials in exchange for Latinum.
STH_gamma.210.name: Karemma Traders
STH_gamma.220.desc: We are receiving an SOS on our long range scanners, we are unable to tell who is calling for aid at this range, but it is our duty to respond to any call for help.\n\nShall we set course?
STH_gamma.220.name: SOS Call Recieved
STH_gamma.222.b: The Rakharian Free States send their thanks.
STH_gamma.222.desc_01: On arriving at the coordinates of the distress call our fleets come across a Nyberrite Cruiser testing a new warp drive system, it appears that during the test the engine failed leaving the cruiser drifting in space. Our engineers were able to render assistance to the vessel and restore the ships original engines allowing them to continue on their way home.
STH_gamma.222.desc_02: On arriving at the site of the distress call, the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] were only able to find the debris of what was once a large ship. A number of detailed scans have picked up a number of escape pods containing members of the Rakharian Free States. \n\nA swift rescue of the crews within the escape pods allows us to return them back to one of their Starbases, much to the gratitude of the Rakharians.
STH_gamma.222.desc_03: The [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] arrives at the scene of a battle and in the midst of the battlefield a small shuttle is transmitting a distress call. It looks as if a Argrathian diplomatic delegation has become caught in the battle by mistake. Engaging in a daring rescue mission our teams were able to extract the dignitaries from the battlefield and return them to Argrathian space.
STH_gamma.222.desc_04: We have found a transport submitting a distress call, communications with the ship are offline, as [gamma_leader_name.GetName] attempts to lock on to the transport another vessel drops out of warp next to our fleet. The transport is a prison transport that had been overrun. The Skrreeans are under the impression that we were attempting to help the prisoners escape. \n\nAfter persuading them that our intervention was purely well meaning, the Skrreeans ask us to be on our way.
STH_gamma.222.name: SOS Call Recieved
STH_gamma.223.b: The Rakharian Free States are offended.
STH_gamma.223.desc_01: As the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] arrives at the scene of the distress call, it soon becomes apparent that what appeared to be weapons fire is in fact the testing of a new weapons system onboard a Nyberrite cruiser.\n\nThe Nyberrite's are not pleased that we have placed ourselves on the scene of a secretive weapons test and order us to leave the vicinity immediately.
STH_gamma.223.desc_02: As the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] arrives at the scene of the distress call, the crew discover a small ship adrift in space, on attempting to rescue the crew [gamma_ship_name.GetName] is hailed by the Rakharian Free States accusing us of interfering with a right of passage ceremony for their adolescents. \n\nTheir wardens rather crudely ask us to leave the area quickly.
STH_gamma.223.desc_03: As the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] arrives at the scene of the distress call, the crew discover a small cruiser suffering from a mass power relay overload, our engineering team believe they were attempting to increase their power output. \n\nBefore we can offer help, the Argrathian Commonwealth contact us rudely asking us to leave, we are told quite simply that the Argrathian Commonwealth takes care of its own.
STH_gamma.223.desc_04: As the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] arrives at the scene of the distress call, the crew discover a small cruiser suffering from a hull breach, as our fleet approaches ready to attempt to help. The Skrreean Captain rudely tells us to mind our own business and leave them alone. \n\nYou can't help everyone...
STH_gamma.223.name: SOS Call Recieved
STH_gamma.224.desc_01: [gamma_leader_name.GetName] we are receiving a distress call from a Nyberrite Alliance freighter under attack from pirates, their shields are failing and weapons are offline. They will not be able to last much longer. \n\nIf we are to aid them, we must do it now.
STH_gamma.224.desc_02: [gamma_leader_name.GetName] we are receiving a distress call from a Rakharian Free States passenger liner, carrying dignitories from their home world. They are under heavy attack from Cheka mercenaries, their shields are failing and their limited weapons pose no risk to their attackers. They will not be able to last much longer. \n\nIf we are to aid them, we must do it now.
STH_gamma.224.desc_03: Sir, the distress calling coming from an Argrathian Commonwealth science vessel, it looks like they have strayed into the territory of an isolationist group called the Bekkir. The Bekkir are clearly determined to purge the intruders regardless of their pleas for mercy. Defending the science vessel would be interfering in another culture's affairs, but the ship will not survive much longer without our help.
STH_gamma.224.desc_04: [gamma_leader_name.GetName] we are being hailed by a Skrreean transport feeling from a T'Rogoran vessel, the transports engines are failing and they are in danger of being caught by the T'Rogorans. Our attempts to hail both vessels are being ignored and it looks like the fight is coming to our fleet whether we like it or not.
STH_gamma.224.name: SOS Call Recieved
STH_gamma.225.desc: [gamma_ship_name.GetName] proved to be too powerful for the attackers and was able to defeat them in a decisive battle over their foes. A fine victory for [gamma_leader_name.GetName] and their crew.
STH_gamma.225.name: Fleet Victorious
STH_gamma.226.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] I am saddened to report the lost of [gamma_leader_name.GetName] and the crew of [gamma_ship_name.GetName] in battle against their enemy. \n\nThis puts a serious dent in the expedition and the Admiralty board have decided to terminate the mission and order the fleet back home.
STH_gamma.226.name: Fleet Defeated
STH_gamma.230.desc: Greetings [gamma_leader_name.GetName] my name is Eav'oq, I am contacting you in need of assistance, we are the Tosk, the hunted. I have escaped with a number of my race, we have been hunted for as long as we can remember. \n\nI must get my people to freedom and we need your help.
STH_gamma.230.name: Communications from the Tosk
STH_gamma.231.a: What do you want with the Tosk?
STH_gamma.231.b: What will you do with them.
STH_gamma.231.d: You will not take them from us.
STH_gamma.231.desc: You have our Tosk and we want them back, they belong to us to hunt. This will be your only warning, return them to us now!
STH_gamma.231.name: Inbound Communications
STH_gamma.232.desc: Rejecting the Oschean Hunters was a risky decision and they have not responded positively, launching a swift attack on the [gamma_ship_name.GetName] quickly disabling the warp drive and boarding the ship. [gamma_leader_name.GetName] quickly sounds the intruder alert and calls for security teams to repel the boarders. \n\nThe Oschean's remind us that they are only after the Tosk and there is still time to hand them over.
STH_gamma.232.name: Fleet under Attack
STH_gamma.233.desc: Following a fierce firefight down the corridors of [gamma_ship_name.GetName] led by [gamma_leader_name.GetName] the [Root.GetName] forces were able to defend the ship and push the invaders back and force them into a full retreat just in time for the engineering teams to restore power to the engines to allow the fleet to make an escape to warp.
STH_gamma.233.name: Ship defence complete
STH_gamma.234.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] we have had a report back from the Executive Officer of the [gamma_ship_name.GetName], the report confirms that the ship was boarded and following a firefight the Commanding Officer and an alien guest Eav'oq were killed by an invading party whilst trying to protect an alien species. \n\nWithout a leader and with the ship heavily damaged it is clear the mission cannot continue and must be recalled.
STH_gamma.234.name: Ship's defences breached
STH_gamma.235.a: Allow them to settle on our capital.
STH_gamma.235.c: They are not welcome within our borders.
STH_gamma.235.desc: Once clear of danger the Tosk representative Eav'oq approaches [gamma_ship_name.GetName] formally asking for asylum for his people within the borders of [Root.GetName]. Once a suitable planet has been found the small community of Tosk will relocate and look to become productive members of [Root.GetName] society.
STH_gamma.235.name: Freedom for the Tosk
STH_gamma.310.desc: The Chief Science Officer of the [gamma_ship_name.GetName] has identified a planet of note in a nearby system. Deciding this mission only required the use of a single ship [gamma_leader_name.GetName] directs their ship to investigate the mysterious planet, whilst any remaining ships continue to survey a local stellar cluster.
STH_gamma.310.name: Children of Time
STH_gamma.311.desc: As the ship approaches the planet the ship is hit by a quantum energy barrier which causes the ship to shutter violently and damages the inertial dampers and the gyromagnetic stabilizers meaning that the ship will be unable to leave orbit for a few days. \n\nScanning the planet the crew pick up [Root.GetSpeciesName] life signs on the planet below!
STH_gamma.311.name: Children of Time
STH_gamma.312.a: Accept the plan and return home.
STH_gamma.312.desc: The crew beam to the planet's surface and come face to face with a small colony of races all found within the [Root.GetName]. The colony leader confirms that they are the descendants of [gamma_ship_name.GetName] which was thrust back in time when they attempted to leave the planet's orbit. The crew are reassured that their descendants have a plan that will ensure the survival of their colony and allow [gamma_leader_name.GetName] and their crew to return home.
STH_gamma.312.name: Children of Time
STH_gamma.313.desc: As [gamma_leader_name.GetName] sits in the command chair readying the ship for departure following the shield modification agreed on the planet below a last minute adjustment is sent in from an unknown source. Presenting the Captain with a choice between the original plan and this last minute change.
STH_gamma.313.name: Children of Time
STH_gamma.314.desc: After a period of violent shaking the ship passes through the mysterious barrier with only minor damage, but no casulties. Scanning the planet's surface to speak with the colony the communications officer reports that the planet below is uninhabited with no sign that a colony ever existed on the surface. \n\nThe crew saddened by the loss of those they had gotten to know over the last few days, they set course to rejoin the fleet and continue the expedition.
STH_gamma.314.name: Children of Time
STH_gamma.315.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] we have recieve a report from one of our fleets in the Gamma Quadrant, the flagship of the expedition fleet the [gamma_ship_name.GetName] has been reported missing in action. They were last seen investigating a planet with a mysterious energy barrior, but when a ship was sent to investigate we could find no sign of the ship or life on the planet. The only strange readings came in the form of 200 year old scattered debris on the northen continent.
STH_gamma.315.name: Children of Time
STH_gamma.4.a: Set course for the Denkiri Arm.
STH_gamma.4.desc: [gamma_mission_leader.GetName] has arrived in the Bajor System onboard the [gamma_ship_name.GetName] of the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName], final preparations have been made at the nearest Starbase and the crew are prepared to embark on the expedition. \n\nThe course and destination are now available to select.
STH_gamma.4.name: Expedition to the Gamma Quadrant
STH_gamma.400.desc: Every great expedition must come to an end eventually and so it is time for the [gamma_fleet_name.GetName] to return back to the Idran System and back through the Bajoran Wormhole. \n\nThe fleet will now recall their science teams and return home.
STH_gamma.400.name: Time to return home
STH_gamma.401.desc: The mission is complete and all that remains is to debrief the Captain and crew. A lot of experience will have been gained from the Gamma Quadrant, with many lives impacted for better or worse.\n\nThe expedition leader is free to return to duty and the fleet is now unlocked and ready to resume service.
STH_gamma.401.name: Fleet has returned home
STH_garidian.1.desc:
Thousand years ago the Lawgiver wrote four scrolls in order to establish the structure of Garidian society. The scrolls define such things as the Garidian code of honor, government, religion, as well as the privileges of the Patrician class.

There is an old legend about the fifth scroll that was supposed to outline privileges and responsibilities of the Plebeian class. However, as the legend states, the Lawgiver was forced to leave the planet before he could reveal the fifth scroll.

This story has recently become especially popular among the lower classes of Garidians.
STH_garidian.1.name: The Fifth Scroll
STH_gomtuu.1.desc: [Root.GetRulerName], our long range scans have picked up a rare galactic sighting! We believe we have found the last known location of the organism known by many as the Star Creature or 'Tin Man'.'\n\nThis could present a unique opportunity to study a wonder of the galaxy.
STH_gomtuu.1.name: The Tin Man Found
STH_gomtuu.10.a: Greetings from the [Root.GetName], tell us about yourself.
STH_gomtuu.10.b: What are you doing in this system?
STH_gomtuu.10.c: We demand to know what you are doing in our space!
STH_gomtuu.10.desc: The creature, who identifies itself as 'Gomtuu' gently takes control of [gomtuu_leader.GetName] and explains that it has telepathic powers and will need to communicate via our leader. It invites us to ask questions.
STH_gomtuu.10.e: Did you have an original crew?
STH_gomtuu.10.f: Why did you allow us to find you now?
STH_gomtuu.10.name: Communication from Gomtuu
STH_gomtuu.11.desc: Before Gomtuu can answer the question, the creature is interrupted by the sudden arrival of a band of space pirates.\n\nThese formidable ships appear heavily armed and hostile and are attempted to hail us. They appear to be from the same clan that attacked us earlier.
STH_gomtuu.11.name: Pirates spotted
STH_gomtuu.12.desc: Attention [Root.GetSpeciesName] vessel, I am [gomtuu_pirate_leader.GetName] and we claim this ship as our own. We have no quarrel with yourselves, but if you stand between us and our prize we will not hesitate to open fire. Leave now if you wish to leave with your pretty ship in one piece!
STH_gomtuu.12.name: Incoming Message
STH_gomtuu.13.desc: The Pirates attack, but do not seem concerned about what their target is, what is clear is that they must be defeated before we can resume our conversation with Gomtuu.
STH_gomtuu.13.name: The Pirates Attack
STH_gomtuu.16.c: Dissect and take what we can.
STH_gomtuu.16.desc: [Root.GetName], we have successfully defeated Tin Man in battle and the creature appears to be dead, our scans indicate that there is an abundance of technology and information within its memory banks, which our scientists would love to get hold of. Alternatively we could board the ship and attempt to salvage it adding it to our fleet.
STH_gomtuu.16.name: Gomtuu Defeated
STH_gomtuu.17.c: Now is our chance to attack Gomtuu.
STH_gomtuu.17.desc_01: The attacking ships stood no chance against the combined firepower of Gomtuu and our fleet. With the ships destroyed we are free to talk to Gomtuu once again.
STH_gomtuu.17.desc_02: Clearly fed up of the ongoing attack, something inside Gomtuu snaps. A massive energy wave spreads out from the ancient ship obliterating its attackers. Gomtuu turns to face [gomtuu_leader.GetName] we can only hope that it doesn't hold a grudge.
STH_gomtuu.17.name: Pirates defeated
STH_gomtuu.18.a.tooltip: You have enough resources to keep your leader.
STH_gomtuu.18.b: What will happen to our officer?
STH_gomtuu.18.d: We insist they stay with us.
STH_gomtuu.18.desc_01: Thank you for aiding me, I sense a bond between your [gomtuu_leader.GetRulerTitle] and myself that I have not felt since I lost my former crew.\n\nThis fills me with a new found desire for life, I offer you this once in a lifetime chance to join me on this adventure, but understand if this is not for you.
STH_gomtuu.18.desc_02: The anger for this injustice has rekindled my desire for life, I offer you this chance to join me on my quest [gomtuu_leader.GetName] - there is much we could do together, you are better than your cowardly masters.
STH_gomtuu.18.name: Gomtuu reaches out once more
STH_gomtuu.19.desc: It appears that Gomtuu remembers our lack of support and is showing little distinction between attacker and bystander.\n\nThe massive ship is determined to take its anger out on anything in its way and that includes our ships and planets. We must defend ourselves!
STH_gomtuu.19.name: Gomtuu seeks revenge
STH_gomtuu.2.desc: Our scientists have been scouring the last known coordinates of the location of the creature known as Tin Man.\n\nWorryingly it looks like we are not the only people tracking the creature - the race is on...
STH_gomtuu.2.name: Race to find the Tin Man
STH_gomtuu.20.desc: Unsatisfied with events that have taken place, Gomtuu powers up its Bio-drive and prepares to depart the system. The investigating crew receive no further word from the creature which promptly departs at high warp.\n\nOur long range scans can find no trace of Gomtuu. It looks like it has gone for good, leaving us with only the scans taken whilst it was here.
STH_gomtuu.20.name: Gomtuu departs unhappy
STH_gomtuu.21.desc: As [gomtuu_leader.GetName] transports onboard Gomtuu, our former leader appears on the viewscreen confident in the choice made. They explain that this is the start of a new voyage for the pair of them and with new worlds to explore they are eager to start this new journey.\n\n[gomtuu_leader.GetName] explains that Gomtuu would like to leave a final gift to say thank you for reminding them of what life has to offer. They bid us farewell and in a flash both are gone.
STH_gomtuu.21.name: [gomtuu_leader.GetName] & Gomtuu depart together
STH_gomtuu.22.desc: Gomtuu transmits one final message via [gomtuu_leader.GetName], they explain that this is the start of a new voyage and that it realises there is still much to be seen and new worlds having been inspired by meeting us.\n\n[gomtuu_leader.GetName] explains that Gomtuu would like to leave a final gift to say thank you for reminding them of what life has to offer. With that complete, the ship spins up its bio-drive and just like that is gone.
STH_gomtuu.22.name: Gomtuu departs alone
STH_gomtuu.23.desc: Gomtuu transmits one final message via [gomtuu_leader.GetName], they explain that this is the start of a new voyage and that it realises there is still much to be seen and new worlds having been inspired by meeting us. [gomtuu_leader.GetName] explains that Gomtuu would like to leave a final gift to say thank you for reminding them of what life has to offer. With that complete, the ship spins up its bio-drive and just like that is gone.\n\nHaving been forced to remain, it is clear that [gomtuu_leader.GetName] is not happy with the decision made on their behalf and they no longer have the motivation to progress at their previous rate, it is unlikely they will live up to their full potential.
STH_gomtuu.23.name: Gomtuu departs alone
STH_gomtuu.24.desc: The crew watch on as the pirates open fire against the old creature, the hull appears to withstand the onslaught, but the ancient ship appears unable or unwilling to defend itself.\n\nAs the once pristine hull darkens under the attack, this is hard to watch. We should leave the creature to its fate. Who knows how the encounter will impact each side of the battle?
STH_gomtuu.24.name: Gomtuu under attack from Pirates
STH_gomtuu.25.a: Our actions have returned to haunt us.
STH_gomtuu.25.desc: We have picked up a signal that appears to match the Gomtuu we encountered last year. It is impossible to know if it is the same creature or another member of the species, but it is acting hostile. It is clearly not happy that it was attacked whilst in our space, and is on an attack approach.
STH_gomtuu.25.name: Gomtuu Returns
STH_gomtuu.26.a: Our actions have returned to haunt us.
STH_gomtuu.26.desc: We have picked up a signal that appears to match the Pirates we encountered last year. They appear to have been impacted psychologically from their encounter with Gomtuu.\n\nWho knows what it did to them? Sensors indicate they appear to have upgraded their ships and are on a direct course for one of our planets!
STH_gomtuu.26.name: Gomtuu worshipping Pirates?
STH_gomtuu.28.desc: After a fierce battle the Gomtuu worshipping pirates have been defeated. We should take a look at their ships for any useful salvage.
STH_gomtuu.28.name: Pirates defeated
STH_gomtuu.3.b: This is a waste of time.
STH_gomtuu.3.desc: 'Sir! We have found the debris of a ship, it does not appear large enough to belong to the Tin Man, but we cannot be sure.' reports an officer.\n\nFurther investigation would be required in order to understand what has happened here. We should scan the debris for more information.
STH_gomtuu.3.name: Debris found
STH_gomtuu.30.a: Let us hope for the best.
STH_gomtuu.30.desc: Gomtuu has come through its battle against the pirates, it will not have gone unnoticed that we did not come to its aid. We can only wonder what its next action will be, it could come today, tomorrow, we simply do not know.
STH_gomtuu.30.name: Gomtuu Defeats Pirates
STH_gomtuu.4.desc: As we start our investigations, it is clear that a trap has been set and we have fallen into it. As predicted we are not the only ships tracking the Tin Man.\n\n Pirates are on a direct course with our fleet and appear intent on a fight. If we are to track down this mysterious entity, we will need to defeat these pirates first.
STH_gomtuu.4.name: It's a trap!
STH_gomtuu.5.desc: During the battle our Science Officer located the computer core from one of the Pirate ships. If our scientists can get a hold of the data held within this core we should be able to piece together what happened and track down where our target has fled to.\n\nAs long as it is safe to do so, we should call in a Science ship to investigate this clue.
STH_gomtuu.5.name: A Vital Clue Found
STH_gomtuu.6.desc: Detailed scans of the wreckage indicate it was destroyed by a massive electrical surge, which contains a unique signature. Our scientists believe we can use this to narrow down the location of our target.\n\nWho knows what state it will be in when we find it? Let us hope we are not too late.
STH_gomtuu.6.name: Tin Man located!
STH_gomtuu.8.c: We cannot let filth like this live.
STH_gomtuu.8.desc:
We have managed to track down the creature, which appears to be a living ship. The entity seems none the worse for its ordeal at the hands of the pirates.

The creature moves closer and suddenly the commanding officer can feel the creature reaching out telepathically.

We can either respond and talk to the creature or use this opportunity to attack.
STH_gomtuu.8.name: Tin Man found safe
STH_gomtuu.9.c: It does not deserve to live.
STH_gomtuu.9.desc:
We have managed to track down the creature, which appears to be a living ship. The entity seems to have suffered damage from its encounter with the pirates.

The creature moves cautiously closer, when suddenly the commanding officer can feel the creature reaching out telepathically.

We can either respond and talk to the creature or use this weakness as our moment to attack.
STH_gomtuu.9.name: Tin Man found injured
STH_gorn_mechanics.12.desc: We have received news that some of the Gorn armies that landed on [This.GetName] are ruthlessly attacking the civilian population and spreading fear and destruction in their wake. As a result of this recently one of the buildings on the planet´s surface was ruined.
STH_gorn_mechanics.12.name: Gorn armies terrorising the population!
STH_gorn_mechanics.13.desc: We have received news that some of the Gorn armies that landed on [This.GetName] are ruthlessly attacking the civilian population and spreading fear and destruction in their wake. As result of this a pop has been killed.
STH_gorn_mechanics.13.name: Gorn armies terrorising the population!
STH_gorn_mechanics.14.desc: Our infant hordes have succeeded in their terror operations on [From.GetName] and ruined a building.
STH_gorn_mechanics.14.name: Our infant hordes are doing their job
STH_gorn_mechanics.15.desc: Our infant hordes have succeeded in their terror operations and killed a pop on [From.GetName].
STH_gorn_mechanics.15.name: Our infant hordes are doing their job
STH_gravity.2.b: We should leave the dead in peace.
STH_gravity.2.desc:
Arriving at the scene all of sudden a gravity well opens up directly below the [Root.GetName] pulling the ship violently towards it. By quickly venting plasma from the engines while at the same time increasing power to the impulse engines [Root.GetName] narrowly avoids getting sucked into the well. 

After escaping the pull of the gravity well [Root.GetName]´s sensors are able to detect a planet located in a pocket of subspace inside the gravity well. Sending a probe in to further explore the planet it is discovered that on its surface there are several alien vessels that most likely fell victim to the well and crashed there. However no life signs are detected coming from the vessels. 

We could explore the option of trying to lift the crashed alien ships up from the surface of the planet and exploit their resources and possible new technologies.
STH_gravity.2.name: Crashed ships detected
STH_gravity.3.b: We can`t spare the resources for it.
STH_gravity.3.desc:
Arriving at the scene of the unusual readings all of sudden a massive gravity well appears seemingly out of nowhere, and despite of [Root.GetName] crew´s best efforts the ship is sucked in while having just enough time to send a distress call out. 

The distress call states that an entire solar system with 3 uninhabitable planets were detected on the other side of the gravity well existing in a subspace pocket. It is estimated that the crew of [Root.GetName] has enough rations to last for a year, assuming that the ship survived the entry into the well. 

Should we start a rescue mission for the lost crew and [gravity_leader.GetName]?
STH_gravity.3.name: Our ship has crashed
STH_gravity.4.desc: By first scanning the gravity well in detail and then heavily modifying some of our shuttlecrafts and their engines we were successful in entering the well and lifting up the remains of the alien ships. Both the materials gained and the knowledge of alien ship designs should benefit our future efforts.
STH_gravity.4.name: Lifting the remains of alien ships successful
STH_gravity.5.a: They were lucky to have survived this ordeal.
STH_gravity.5.desc: After arriving at the scene and scanning the massive gravity well it is clear that this is a monodirectional phenomenon, meaning that no ship going in can get out. However by sending a reinforced probe inside the well [Root.GetName]´s crew are able to first locate the missing leader and most of the crew of our crashed ship alive. And then by using the probe as a signal transmitter slowly beam them out of the gravity well in small groups. The survivors seem to have suffered only minor injuries and some malnourishment while waiting to be rescued. \n\nThe rescued leader assumes command of [Root.GetName].
STH_gravity.5.name: Saving crew successful
STH_gravity.6.desc: We have failed to rescue our leader and the crew of the crashed ship in time from the gravity well. It is estimated that by now all possible survivors have run out of supplies.
STH_gravity.6.name: Saving crashed ship's crew failed
STH_hazards.3.a: The Badlands is a dangerous area.
STH_hazards.3.desc: Whilst traversing the plasma storms in the region known as the [badlands_ship_damaged_minor.solar_system.GetName] the [badlands_ship_damaged_minor.GetName] has taken minor damage from one of the many plasma storms that occupy the region.\n\nThe risk of further damage will only increase the longer the ship remains in proximity to the area. [Root.GetName] Fleet Control recommends immediate withdrawal from the vicinity.
STH_hazards.3.name: [badlands_ship_damaged_minor.GetName] damaged in Hostile Region
STH_hazards.4.a: We must conclude our business there swiftly.
STH_hazards.4.desc: The [badlands_ship_damaged_major.solar_system.GetName] are a hostile and dangerous region of space and unfortunately the [badlands_ship_damaged_major.GetName] has suffered severe damage at the hands of one of the graviational anomalies that exist in the region.\n\nThe risk of further damage will only increase the longer the ship remains in proximity to the area. [Root.GetName] Fleet Control recommends immediate withdrawal from the vicinity.
STH_hazards.4.name: [badlands_ship_damaged_major.GetName] suffers serious damage in the Badlands
STH_hazards.5.desc: Many ships have been lost whilst attempting to travel within the region of space known as the Badlands and we have lost all contact with the [badlands_ship_destroyed.GetName], long range scans have picked up signs of debris along the last known coordinates of the ship. We must sadly assume the vessel was destroyed and hope that some of the crew were able to make it to safety.
STH_hazards.5.name: [badlands_ship_destroyed.GetName] lost in the Badlands
STH_hazards.50.d.tooltip: You will not receive any further chances to avoid this system or others like it. You know what you are doing after all.
STH_hazards.50.desc: The [This.GetName] has reached a galactic barrier, this signifies a tremendous distance across a vast stretch of space, and through the dangerous galactic barrier. \n\nBefore [This.GetName] begins the journey do you wish to abandon the venture across this void.
STH_hazards.50.name: The edge of the Galaxy
STH_hazards.6.desc: There has been some good news following the destruction of their ship, [badlands_ship_leader.GetName] and a number of their crew have been found alive in escape pods drifting on the outskirts of the storms. \n\nDespite the Captain and crew being severally shaken, there were in generally good health despite their experience.
STH_hazards.6.name: Crew Recovered
STH_hazards.7.desc: We have lost all contact with [caretakered_ship.GetName] whilst they were traversing the region known as the Badlands. \n\nThere has been no sign of the ship or its crew and no wreckage has been spotted. Plasma storms in the region are making any further investigation too dangerous and we can only assume the ship was lost with all hands.
STH_hazards.7.name: Ship Lost in the Badlands
STH_hero_age.21.desc: Following her joining with the Dax symbiont over 10 years ago, Ezri Dax has undergone a real transformation, both host Ezri and Dax have grown in confidence together and may see a future outside of the science division. A recent opportunity to take command has left Dax wondering if a transfer to the command division is in order.
STH_hero_age.21.name: Ezri Dax takes command
STH_hero_age.22.desc: Since being liberated from the Borg, the ex-drone, known now as simply 'Seven' has continued to demonstrate her exceptional skills and the admiralty believes the time has come to promote her to the command track.
STH_hero_age.22.name: Fun will now commence
STH_hero_age.29.a.tooltip: Convert Data to an Admiral capable of leading military ships. We will need to recruit him from the leader pool once again.
STH_hero_age.29.b: This position is not for him.
STH_hero_age.29.c.tooltip: Add the 'Hero Admiral' Trait, but remain scientist leader class.
STH_hero_age.29.desc: Following years of dedicated service to Starfleet, the Android known as Data has put in a request for a promotion and a command of his own. Data has an implecable service record, but not everyone in the fleet would be comfortable following the command of an artificial lifeform.
STH_hero_age.29.name: First Android in Starfleet
STH_hero_age.35.b: This position is not for him.
STH_hero_age.35.desc: Following years of dedicated service to Starfleet security, Worf has demonstrated leadership and the ability to command a crew his peers are recommending that he switches to the command division and put his considerable skills to use in this area.
STH_hero_age.35.name: An honourable opportunity
STH_hero_age.41.b: Remain amongst the rank and file.
STH_hero_age.41.desc: Following her rescue from her transport ship as a young girl, the young La'an Noonien-Singh was determined to repay Starfleet by joining the fleet and serving alongside Starfleet's finest.\n\nIs there a position within the fleet for Cadet Noonien-Singh?
STH_hero_age.41.name: La'an Noonien-Singh
STH_hero_age.44.b: Ask Chapel to remain on the ship.
STH_hero_age.44.desc: Having served with distinction during the expedition alongside their commanding officer [From.GetName], Nurse Chapel has excelled in her duties and has been recommended for a promotion form [From.GetName].
STH_hero_age.44.name: Nurse Chapel I presume?
STH_hero_age.45.b: Ask Ortegas to remain on the ship.
STH_hero_age.45.desc: Having served with distinction during the expedition alongside their commanding officer [From.GetName], Lt Ortegas has excelled in her duties and has been recommended for a promotion form [From.GetName] and a command of their own on her return to Earth.
STH_hero_age.45.name: Lt Ortegas
STH_hero_age.50.desc: Admiral Spock has served Starfleet with distinction for more years than almost any other commander in the fleet. As a distinguished officer, it is time for him to make the career decision that he has earnt. \n\nRemain within Starfleet, use his considerable political might as an Ambassador (governor) or retire from public life. The needs of the many really do outweigh the needs of the few... or the one.
STH_hero_age.50.name: The Logical Next Step
STH_hero_age.51.b: Move to the Admiralty governors board.
STH_hero_age.51.desc: Robert April is one of Starfleet's most decorated officers, his role on the bridge of the Enterprise and other notable Starships has become legend. Is now the time for his next career move?
STH_hero_age.51.name: This bridge is more like home than anywhere else
STH_hero_leader.20.desc: Georgiou attended Starfleet Academy from 2220 to 2224, meeting and befriending other notable Starfleet admirals such as Christopher Pike, who described her as the sharpest tool in the shed and a force to be reckoned with. He also remembered her drinking Pike and their fellow cadets underneath the table, but also for having every Starfleet regulation down by the second week. As a young Lieutenant, Georgiou was serving aboard the USS Archimedes and was involved in numerous first contacts. As a rising star in the fleet, many knew it wouldn't be long before this promotion came about. Should Georgiou become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.20.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.21.desc: Having been convinced to join Starfleet by renowned Starfleet admiral Alexander Marcus, Christopher Pike soon rose up the ranks, serving as a test pilot and served with distinction on at least three ships before earning this promotion. Brave and honourable Pike embodies what it means to be 'Starfleet'. Should Pike become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.21.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.22.desc: James Tiberius Kirk was the descendant of late 19th century American frontier pioneers and was inspired to join Starfleet due to the actions of his father. During his time in the Command Training Program, Kirk became famous for being the only cadet in history to beat the 'no-win' Kobayashi Maru scenario, and earning a commendation for original thinking. Should Kirk become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral!?
STH_hero_leader.22.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.23.desc: Jean-Luc Picard caused a stir in rural France when he left his family's vineyard to join Starfleet Academy, he became one of the most successful cadets in his class becoming an expert in archeology and command. Picard served on a number of prominent ships before taking the initiative during a crisis on the USS Stargazer and saving the lives of the crew with the now famous 'Picard Manoverver'. Should Picard become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.23.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.24.desc: John Harriman II, son of former Commander-in-chief 'Blackjack' Harriman, is an exceptional student who has risen up the ranks rapidly - in only a small part due to his father's influence. Born onboard the USS Sea of Tranquility, Harriman has spent only weeks planetside with most of his life spent onboard starships. Now, with his training complete, should Harriman become a Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship, or instead a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.24.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.25.desc: Rachel Garrett was born in Madison, Indiana on Earth. She has served with distinction on numerous vessels, though the psychological profile shows that her family life could be strained. Should Garrett become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.25.name: Career decision to be made
STH_hero_leader.26.desc: Georgiou attended the Imperial Starfleet Academy from 2220 to 2224. Through flattery and seduction she gained a lot of influential allies among notable Starfleet admirals, including Christopher Pike himself, who credited her as a 'dependent, but effective weapon of destruction'. With such connections it did not take her too long to graduate from the Academy and be assigned to the ISS Archimedes. As a Lieutenant, Georgiou participated in numerous combat engagements against alien fleets, while also steadily climbing the ranks, removing the obstacles with favours and assassinations. With her achievements appearing more and more in Starfleet Command's internal documentation, her accelerated promotion to the highest ranks was just a matter of time. Should Georgiou become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.27.desc: Joining the Imperial Starfleet on Admiral Alexander Marcus' recommendation, Christopher Pike quickly rose through the ranks - some say that he has been pushed by Starfleet Command to become a poster boy for the Empire, but his impressive kill record is a simple enough explanation. Ruthless and ambitious, Pike embodies what it means to be a Terran. Should Pike become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.28.desc: James Tiberius Kirk was the descendant of late 19th century American naval officers and joined the Imperial Starfleet for sake of the prestige and power he could obtain in the military. One of his very first achievements was eliminating more than two hundred Imperial officers, including Captain Stephen Garrovick, in what is now known as 'The Farragut Incident'. Should Kirk become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral!?
STH_hero_leader.29.desc: A dedicated Starfleet officer, Commander Archer's desire for his own command landed him in the center seat of an advanced starship. However, his quest for power and glory, and his ultimate desire to take over the Terran Empire were both his biggest strength and biggest weakness. Should Archer become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.30.desc: Captain Maximilian Forrest was a notable Terran leader in the 2150s. He was the commanding officer of the ISS Enterprise, developing an adversarial relationship with his first officer, Commander Jonathan Archer. Although more harsh and stern than his counterpart in the prime universe, relatively speaking, Forrest seemed to honestly care about the overall well-being of his ship and crew, and was legitimately concerned when a Terran Empire task force was destroyed fighting rebels at Tau Ceti. Should Forrest become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.31.desc: Self-serving and cruel, few would have predicted Sato's rise to power. Before serving on the Enterprise, Sato had been a teacher in Brazil. Serving mainly as Captain Forrest's woman, Sato had previously been involved with Jonathan Archer but left him for Forrest when he was promoted to a position of higher power.\nShould Sato become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.32.desc: Very few people could be considered as determined as Erika Hernandez. Having been passed up for command of Earth's first warp 5 starship, Hernandez vowed never to come in second place again and that she would take any means to ensure this she never did. Should Hernandez become a heroic Scientist capable of commanding a Flagship or a heroic Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.33.desc: Seven of Nine (full designation: Seven of Nine, Tertiary Adjunct of Unimatrix 01) was a former Borg drone, born Annika Hansen, a Human female. Assimilated by the Borg at the age of six, Seven was liberated by the crew of the USS Voyager in 2374. She was both a brilliant scientific mind and possessed considerable leadership skills forged through her service within the Fenris Rangers. Should Seven of Nine become a Scientist or an Admiral?
STH_hero_leader.34.desc: Bruce Maddox has had a chequered past: once a promising scientist in the field of cybernetics, his objection to giving Androids the status of living beings was well known within the scientific community. Since that point he has come to revere the work of Soong and Graves and can now be considered a leading mind in the field of positronic research.
STH_hero_leader.34.name: Synthetic expert recruited from the Daystrom Institute
STH_hero_leader.35.b: We do not need any more leaders.
STH_hero_leader.35.desc: We have been approached by a young warrior from the planet Capella, a number of years ago during an expedition to the planet one of our leaders saved the young man's life. He wishes to apply for Starfleet Academy and serve the country which saved his life all those years ago.
STH_hero_leader.35.name: Young Capellan Hero
STH_hero_leader.36.desc: Young Capellan, Leonard James Akaar has completed his training in Starfleet Academy with exemplary scores. Ready to take his place in the fleet and bring honour to his name.
STH_hero_leader.36.name: Capellan graduates with honours
STH_hero_leader.37.a: I'm a doctor, not a doorstop.
STH_hero_leader.37.desc: Having served as Voyager's Physician during its long journey home, the EMH has returned to Starfleet as a one of the most advanced and experienced Holograms in the fleet and after convincing many of his doubters wrong and thus earning recognition as a lifeform in his own right, the Doctor is ready to serve.
STH_hero_leader.37.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.38.a: I didn't notice a little box on my chair!
STH_hero_leader.38.desc: Despite managing to serve Voyager's tour of the Delta Quadrant as an Ensign, the unsung hero of the Starship Voyager Harry Kim has finally earned himself that promotion.
STH_hero_leader.38.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.39.a: Nothing is impossible if you want it badly enough.
STH_hero_leader.39.desc: The former Starfleet dropout and former Maquis engineer, B'Elanna Torres has been described by many as one of the finest engineers in the business. Torres' half-klingon temper often gets her in trouble, but her undoubted skills and abilities saved the crew more times than they could count, quickly earning her a full commission on Voyager's return.
STH_hero_leader.39.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.40.a: Are you telling me I'm impossible to resist?
STH_hero_leader.40.desc: Regarded as some as the best pilot in the fleet, and by others as a no good rogue Tom Paris has had a troubling career thus far. Son of famed Admiral Owen Paris and kicked from Starfleet for covering up a tragic accident, Paris has reformed his ways and served Voyager with distinction. His return to home and glowing references have convinced the Admiralty to give him another chance.
STH_hero_leader.40.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.41.a: The road to hell is paved with good intentions.
STH_hero_leader.41.desc: Once considered a promising officer before he defected to the Maquis to defend his homeland against the Cardassians, Chakotay served as Voyagers second in command throughout its journey home. Not afraid to challenge his captain, but fiercely loyal Chakotay was welcomed back into the fleet with open arms upon Voyager's return.
STH_hero_leader.41.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.42.a: Mr. Vulcan, nice to meet you!
STH_hero_leader.42.desc: A man of many talents Neelix served as guide, cook and moral officer... not always with permission! Far from his home, but with plenty to offer Neelix has the skills to govern and host his own banquets.
STH_hero_leader.42.name: Hero of the Delta Quadrant
STH_hero_leader.43.b: Offer a role as a Starfleet Commander.
STH_hero_leader.43.desc:
As Earth propels itself into the stars, the role of Starfleet becomes ever more important as it begins the process of rolling Earth's other military organisations into a singular spacefaring entity. 

Balthazar Edison, hero of United Earth's multiple early conflicts whilst serving with the MACOs is seeking a new role within Starfleet. 

Edison is one of many war veterans seeking a place in an unfamiliar world.
STH_hero_leader.43.name: A new role for a decorated Military Hero
STH_hero_leader.45.a.tooltip: This is not necessarily the end of Seven's journey, she is a force of nature and is determined to do good in the STNH galaxy.
STH_hero_leader.45.desc: Despite two of the finest captains in Starfleet going into battle for Seven, Starfleet's concerns and prejudices were not easily overcome, leaving Seven feeling that she could do more good to the galaxy outside of Starfleet and the Federation. \n\nSeeing the work that organisations such as the Fenris Rangers are doing in the border regions, Seven head to the frontier to change lives.
STH_hero_leader.45.name: Seven of Nine
STH_hero_leader.46.desc: It wasn't an easy conversation, but Seven had some high profile backers within the ranks of Starfleet and so, as a result the Admiralty have agreed that her exceptional skills and experience would only aid the Federation, offering her a fast-tracked position within the fleet.
STH_hero_leader.46.name: Seven of Nine
STH_hero_leader.47.desc: Considered by many to be one of the most decorated captains in Starfleet history, Robert April was responsible for launching the careers of many of Starfleet's greats, such as Pike, Number One & the Enterprise herself. Not to be outshone by others April was considered a pioneer of Starfleet and a commanding presence amongst the Admiralty.
STH_hero_leader.47.name: Robert April
STH_hero_leader.50.a.tooltip: Selecting this option will unlock a new decision on [Root.capital_scope.GetName], which will create the Daystrom Institute deposit.
STH_hero_leader.50.b: We have no need of this.
STH_hero_leader.50.desc: The death of noted Federation scientist Richard Daystrom has left a noticeable hole in the scientific community. \n\nA sizeable group of scientists have expressed a wish to set up a new scientific research institute named in honour of Dr Daystrom, it will become the leading light in scientific research and a place for the very best minds in Starfleet and the civilian sector to come together to produce the finest research for the federation.
STH_hero_leader.50.name: Death of noted Scientist Richard Daystrom
STH_hero_leader.60.a: They are welcome to remain with us.
STH_hero_leader.60.b: How dare they, but we need new slaves!
STH_hero_leader.60.desc: A mysterious presence living on [Root.capital_scope.GetName] has come forward, for thousands of years, the alien Lanthanites have been living amongst our people, undetected and unobserved... they now feel the time is right to reveal their society and are asking to join us formally.
STH_hero_leader.60.name: Lanthanites Revealed
STH_hero_leader.61.b: A new mad scientist sounds perfect.
STH_hero_leader.61.desc: Commander Pelia was a female Lanthanite Starfleet officer who lived on Earth from the 6th century BC through at least the 23rd century. She served as an instructor at Starfleet Academy before being assigned to Operational Support Services as chief fleet inspector, and later transferred to the USS Enterprise as chief engineer.
STH_hero_leader.61.name: I'm a Lanthanite. I've lived for thousands of years
STH_hero_mechanics.10.a: Archer's place is on the bridge.
STH_hero_mechanics.10.desc: After years of serving United Earth, there are many calls for Jonathan Archer to help [Root.Owner.GetName] in a new manner. The world of politics awaits and many would love to see Starfleet's hero take a leading role in governing and helping decide the future.
STH_hero_mechanics.10.name: Time for jonathan Archer to decide what's next
STH_hero_mechanics.11.desc: After a long and impressive career in service of [Root.Owner.GetName], [hero_retire.GetName]'s thoughts turn to [hero_retire.GetHerHis] future. Even though others may advise to never leave the bridge of your ship. Sometimes other opportunities to serve present themselves.\n\nSome leaders are suited to being Ambassadors, governors or serving as Fleet Admirals. Alternatively returning to the Academy as a lecturer maybe the right decision.
STH_hero_mechanics.11.name: Decision to be made for [hero_retire.GetName]
STH_hero_mechanics.11.tooltip: The Fleet Admiral trait is a unique trait for high ranking Admirals granting them perks to better manage the our fleets.
STH_hero_mechanics.15.desc: After treating many patients from many different species, [doctor_retire.GetName] has the opportunity to look at what they want to do in the future. What choice should they make?
STH_hero_mechanics.15.name: Time for our Doctor to decide
STH_hero_mechanics.150.a: Tell the Federation to keep their pets in line.
STH_hero_mechanics.150.desc: Our colony on [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName] has been attacked, reports from our local Obsidian Order operatives suggest that it was orchestrated by a Bajoran cell, not yet ready to move on from the past. \n\nWhat is more concerning it appears they used Federation technology to carry out this attack!
STH_hero_mechanics.150.name: Terrorist Attack on [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName]
STH_hero_mechanics.151.desc: Our intelligence services report that the Cardassian colony on [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName] suffered a significant terrorist attack, with preliminary reports suggesting a rogue Bajoran cell being to blame for the destruction.
STH_hero_mechanics.151.name: Cardassian planet attacked
STH_hero_mechanics.152.b: We do not follow your orders Cardassian.
STH_hero_mechanics.152.desc: Greetings Starfleet, you will no doubt by now be aware of the callous attack on our innocent civilians on the peaceful colony world of [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName]. \n\nOur reports suggest that your 'allies' the Bajorans have not moved on from the events of the past and continue to habour a grudge for the missgivings of the past. We invite you to deal with this situation before we send our fleets in to make a more permanent solution.
STH_hero_mechanics.152.name: Incoming Message from Cardassia Prime
STH_hero_mechanics.153.desc: Those cowards in the Federation have refused to condemn those Bajoran fools and will not stand with Cardassia in the face of these attacks. This insult will not go unpunished or forgotten by those on Cardassia Prime.
STH_hero_mechanics.153.name: Federation refuse our Request
STH_hero_mechanics.154.desc: The Federation has seen sense and condemned the acts of those Bajorans fools, pledging their support to track down the source of the leaked weapons and bring a stop to these senseless attacks on our planets.
STH_hero_mechanics.154.name: Federation condemns the Bajoran Republic
STH_hero_mechanics.155.desc: The Federation has expressed a desire to fully investigate the matter to determine who launched the attack on us and how they obtained the use of Starfleet-grade weapons. The Central Government has allowed them limited time to investigate on the condition that when they reach the logical conclusion, they act swiftly and decisively.
STH_hero_mechanics.155.name: Federations wishes to investigate
STH_hero_mechanics.156.a: Tell me more about Ro Laren.
STH_hero_mechanics.156.desc: Greetings I am [hero_choice_1.GetName] and I am preparing to lead the investigation into the bombings on [bajoran_rebels_target.GetName]. \n\n Starfleet has provided the services of a Bajoran national, Ro Laren, currently serving time in a penal colony for causing the deaths of a number of her crew mates whilst on a mission with the USS Wellington. Our diplomatic services believe that Ro would be able to provide valuable insights into the Bajoran cell but be warned of her reputation with her fellow officers. Many believe she has no right to be free from her confinement.
STH_hero_mechanics.156.name: Ro Laren
STH_hero_mechanics.157.desc_01: We have made contact with a respected Bajoran leader named Keeve Falor, who has become the senior member of this small colony of Bajorans displaced during their struggle with Cardassian. Keeve could provide valuable intel on who has been organising these attacks on Cardassian settlements, however, Ensign Ro recommends that we disregard this valuable lead and locate a made known only as Orta. \n\nIt is unclear where she obtained this information from or how reliable her sources are, but following an unverified source would be risky.
STH_hero_mechanics.157.desc_02: We have made contact with a respected Bajoran leader named Keeve Falor, who has become the senior member of this small colony of Bajorans displaced during their struggle with Cardassian. Keeve could provide valuable intel on who has been organising these attacks on Cardassian settlements.
STH_hero_mechanics.157.name: Contact made with Bajorans
STH_hero_mechanics.158.desc: Unfortunately Keeve Falor was unable to provide any meaningful evidence or information about the attacks, either he is unwilling to provide answers, or more likely has been left out of the loop. We must inform the Cardassians of our failure... they will not be pleased.
STH_hero_mechanics.158.name: Dead End...
STH_hero_mechanics.159.desc: The Starfleet officer in charge of investigating the attacks on our planet has reported that they have been unable to identify who was responsible for the bombing of our planet. We have no choice but to hold any and all Bajorans responsible for the atrocities and our faith in Starfleet remains justifiably low.
STH_hero_mechanics.159.name: Federation Failure
STH_hero_mechanics.16.b: A role as a Governor awaits.
STH_hero_mechanics.16.c: They should teach our new warriors.
STH_hero_mechanics.16.desc: One of our honoured soldiers who has answered the call of the warrior over many years has the chance to serve the Empire in new ways. [hero_retire_kdf.GetName] has defeated all enemies in their way over many years now and new challenges await them within the Empire.
STH_hero_mechanics.16.name: Honoured Warrior
STH_hero_mechanics.160.b: Turn Orta over to the Cardassians.
STH_hero_mechanics.160.desc: The away team is directed to a hidden facility in the hills overlooking the Bajoran camp and introduced to an individual known only as 'Orta', who appears to head up the remains of a cell. By the looks of his scarred appearance, it would seem he has been battling the Cardassians for most of his life. \n\nOrta explains that his cell is in no longer in a fit state to continue their operation against the Cardassians and that they have knowledge that heavily implies that the attack against the Cardassians was a setup, by a small corner of the Obsidian Order, intending to turn the Federation against the Bajorans and allow the Cardassians to blame them and retaliate and wipe out those who resisted during the Occupation.
STH_hero_mechanics.160.name: Meeting with Orta
STH_hero_mechanics.161.b: Instruct the Order to deal with it.
STH_hero_mechanics.161.desc: The Starfleet investigation into the recent attacks indicates that the Bajoran sect we suspected of the attacks is not responsible and is in no fit condition to have attacked anyone. Despite our inquiries, the Starfleet Captain claims they were unable to record the location of this cell, either that or they are unwilling to share. The likelihood of this being an internal job seems unlikely, but the Obsidian Order stands ready to investigate.
STH_hero_mechanics.161.name: Message from Starfleet
STH_hero_mechanics.162.desc_01: We have listened to your 'statement' Starfleet and whilst many would believe your statements you will find that the Cardassian Union is not so easily fooled by lies, we will continue the search for the terrorist cells without your aid, but we at least acknowledge you attempted to help us. Should these attacks continue you will find that we are not so cautious in how we respond. \n\nUntil we are next forced to speak.
STH_hero_mechanics.162.desc_02: We have read your report, perhaps we had too much confidence in your ability to track down those responsible, but we suppose you did your best given your limited abilities.
