STH_naval_museum.1000.desc_01: Greetings, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. My name is Commodore [starfleet_naval_museum_administrator.GetName] and I am the Starfleet liaison to this facility. \n\nHow can the Starfleet Historical Society assist you today?
STH_naval_museum.1000.desc_02: Long live the Empire, [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. My name is Commodore [starfleet_naval_museum_administrator.GetName] and I am the Imperial Starfleet liaison to this facility. \n\nHow can the Imperial Starfleet Historical Agency serve the Empire today?
STH_naval_museum.1000.desc_03: A safe galaxy is a Human galaxy! [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. My name is Commodore [starfleet_naval_museum_administrator.GetName] and I am the Confederation Corps liaison to this facility. \n\nHow can the Earth Naval Museum serve the Empire today?
STH_naval_museum.1000.name: Naval Museum
STH_naval_museum.1001.desc_01:
Frigates are small vessels designed for system defense duties and other limited operations. They are built for speed and maneuverability. Usually, frigates would fight in small numbers. The term frigate originated in the Mediterranean - Earth in the late 15th century, referring to a lighter galleass type ship with oars, sails and a light armament. Famous frigate the U.S.S. Constitution known as 'Old Ironsides' due to her sturdy construction, the oldest still intact ship in America serves as a museum in Boston, Massachusetts. Still afloat, she had an usually long service life, having remained in commission on and off between 1797 all the way to the Civil War, after which she was made a training ship and continued sailing periodically right up to her final decommissioning in 1881. Please select the Frigate class you wish to recommission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1001.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1002.desc_01:
A light cruiser is a type of small- to medium-sized multipurpose starship. Through the history they have served in a variety of roles, primarily as convoy escorts and destroyer command ships, but also as scouts and fleet support vessels for battle fleets. On Earth the first small steam-powered cruisers were built for the British Royal Navy with HMS Mercury launched in 1878. Germany took a lead in small cruiser design in the 1890s, building a class of fast cruisers - the Gazelle class - copied by other Earth nations. Please select the Light Cruiser class you wish to Recommission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1002.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1003.desc_01:
In starship classification, an explorer ship is a type of spacecraft designed for exploring unknown regions of space. The 21st century NASA ship Charybdis was classified as an 'explorer ship'. Please select the Explorer class you wish to Recommission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1003.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1004.desc_01:
The rise of the Multi-Mission Vessel class has mirrored the evolving nature of maritime power in the 21st century on Earth. Dr. Gareth Evans reports on how high-tech craft, capable of undertaking various roles, were becoming the mainstay of 21st century navies. Over the history the Multi-Mission Vessels addressed the evolving nature of naval and later interstellar warfare, facing also challenges such a smuggling and piracy. Please select the Multi-Mission Vessel class you wish to Commission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1004.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1005.desc_01:
Taking the class name from the ancient naval warships heavy cruiser is a type of cruiser starship designed for long range and high speed with their intended targets being other cruisers and smaller vessels. The last ancient heavy cruiser in existence is the USS Salem, now a carefully preserved museum ship in Quincy, Massachusetts. Commissioned in 1949, she was the Earth's last heavy cruiser to enter service and the only one still in existence. She was decommissioned in 1959, after serving in the Atlantic and Mediterranean. Please select the Heavy Cruiser class you wish to Recommission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1005.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1006.desc_01:
Advanced Cruisers are more capable and more streamlined than a normal Heavy Cruiser. This design is reliable and versatile, and is still in active service alongside much newer ships. Please select the Advanced Cruiser class you wish to Recommission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1006.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1007.desc_01:
Space: the final frontier. In the fine Star Fleet tradition the Exploration Cruiser is meant to explore strange new worlds, to seek out new life and new civilizations, to boldly go where no one has gone before. Please select the Exploration Cruiser class you wish to Commission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1007.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1008.desc_01:
The Heavy Escort whose primary purpose is to accompany and protect other vessels. They are ideal for small tactical formations and cavalry raids into enemy territories - 'Cannon to right of them, Cannon to left of them, Cannon in front of them Volley'd and thunder'd; Storm'd at with shot and shell, Boldly they rode and well, Into the jaws of Death, Into the mouth of Hell Rode the six hundred.' An old Earth poem. The Heavy Escort function is similar to Light Cruisers, however, they are much more advanced and versatile starships. Relatively overpowered for their size, small yet sturdy are like angry hornets, which can take down much larger but less maneuverable starships. Please select the Heavy Escort class you wish to Commission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1008.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1009.desc_01:
The Troop Carrier's primary purpose is to ensure that marines, precious cargo and other valuable items make it to their destination. The Transports function is similar to Light Cruisers, but with heavier shielding and hulls, but fewer weapons. Please select the Carrier class you wish to Commission.

Obsolete ship classes will automatically be retired into the museum, they will still be designable for 5 years after being retired. Once they have been fully retired, we can recommission old ship classes again, consuming one slot of the Recommissioned Ship Classes Roster. Recommissioning old ship classes requires a huge amount of personnel to maintain production, resulting in a 5 year cooldown period before we are able to retire this class again.

Slots: [root.GetMuseumNumber]/[root.GetMuseumMax]
STH_naval_museum.1009.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.1100.desc_01: Qapla', [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName]. My name is [naval_museum_administrator.GetName] and I am the curator of this facility. \n\nHow can I serve?
STH_naval_museum.1100.name: wo' noH 'angweD
STH_naval_museum.1101.name: wo' noH 'angweD

STH_naval_museum.1103.name: wo' noH 'angweD
STH_naval_museum.1104.name: wo' noH 'angweD
STH_naval_museum.1105.name: wo' noH 'angweD

STH_naval_museum.1200.name: Praetorian Naval Museum
STH_naval_museum.1201.name: Praetorian Naval Museum
STH_naval_museum.1202.name: Praetorian Naval Museum
STH_naval_museum.1203.name: Praetorian Naval Museum
STH_naval_museum.1204.name: Praetorian Naval Museum
STH_naval_museum.1205.name: Praetorian Naval Museum
STH_naval_museum.3000.desc: The Federation member world ships are an example of Federation membership pulling together to combine ideas and designs and these ships combine the best of Starfleet and the member worlds principles.
STH_naval_museum.3000.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.3010.desc: Federation Scouts a fast nimble ships designed to operate at the fringes of Starfleet territory. Once these ship designs have served their time they can be retired.
STH_naval_museum.3010.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.3020.desc: Federation Support Craft operate from Starbases and Outposts. Once these ship designs have served their time they can be retired.
STH_naval_museum.3020.name: Starfleet Historical Society
STH_naval_museum.4000.name: wo' noH 'angweD
STH_naval_museum.5000.desc: Every fleet reaches a point where operationally maintaining it becomes unproductive, especially as new vessels are commissioned. \n\nMothballing a fleet allows you to power down and put the vessel into storage within drydock, ready to be activated at a later date or converted into a museum ship. Mothballed fleets cannot be used until they are reactived, but have a significantly lower upkeep compared with active ships.
STH_naval_museum.5000.name: Fleet Mothballing Operations
STH_naval_museum.5001.a.tooltip: This fleet will move to the naval museum to begin the process of going into mothball.
STH_naval_museum.5001.desc: [This.GetName] has reached the Starfleet Naval Museum and the crews have disembarked and been reassigned, over the next few weeks and months Starfleet Corp. of Engineers will begin removing systems and packing away items onboard the ships.
STH_naval_museum.5001.name: Fleet Ready for Mothballing
STH_naval_museum.5003.desc: Placing a mothballed fleet back into service takes place here, there could be many reasons to reactive a fleet, such as defense or an offensive campaign. Reactivated Fleets will take some time to get back up to operational standards, so make sure you plan ahead. \n\nDev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5003.name: Reactivate a Mothballed Fleet
STH_naval_museum.5004.a.tooltip: The restored fleet will take some time to become fully operational and until that time will operate under normal parameters.
STH_naval_museum.5004.desc: Our engineers have confirmed that [This.GetName] is now operational, albeit operating with a skeleton crew and with a number of systems still undergoing maintenance. It will take a little while for the ships to get up to full speed.
STH_naval_museum.5004.name: [This.GetName] Now Active
STH_naval_museum.5010.desc:
A mothballed fleet can be converted into a museum fleet and dispatched into the orbit of one of our colonies. A museum fleet can provide the colony with a big boost in popularity, as history fans flock in to view a piece of Starfleet history. 

Once the fleet is converted into a museum it can no longer serve as an active part of fleet operations. n
Dev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5010.name: Convert to a Museum
STH_naval_museum.5011.desc: Now that a fleet has been selected to be converted into a museum fleet, we must decide which of our colonies should accept this honour.
STH_naval_museum.5011.name: Museum Fleet
STH_naval_museum.5012.desc: The Fleet museum above [This.GetName] is now open to all who wish to come and experience some of Starfleet's finest vessels preserved for all to visit and marvel at.
STH_naval_museum.5012.name: Museum Open to the Public
STH_naval_museum.5030.desc: All noble vessels must come to an end, and whilst a ship cannot make the glorious to Sto-Vo-Kor, it can still serve a purpose whilst mothballed. \n\nMothballing a fleet allows you to power down and put the vessel into storage within drydock, ready to be activated at a later date or converted into a war museum ship. Mothballed fleets cannot be used until they are reactivated, but have significantly lower upkeep compared with active ships.
STH_naval_museum.5030.name: Fleet Mothballing Operations
STH_naval_museum.5033.desc: The call of the warrior has taken place and you want to place a mothballed fleet back into service, there could be many reasons to reactive a fleet, such as defense or an offensive campaign. Reactivated Fleets will take some time to get back up to operational standards, so make sure you plan ahead. \n\nDev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5033.name: Reactivate a Mothballed Fleet
STH_naval_museum.5040.desc:
A mothballed fleet can be converted into a museum fleet and dispatched into the orbit of one of our colonies. A museum fleet can provide the colony with a big boost in popularity, as history fans flock in to view a piece of our glorious conquests. 

Once the fleet is converted into a museum it can no longer serve as an active part of fleet operations. n
Dev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5040.name: Convert to a Museum
STH_naval_museum.5050.desc: Every fleet reaches a point where operationally maintaining it becomes unproductive, especially as new vessels are commissioned. \n\nMothballing a fleet allows you to power down and put the vessel into storage within drydock, ready to be activated at a later date or converted into a museum ship. Mothballed fleets cannot be used until they are reactived, but have a significantly lower upkeep compared with active ships.
STH_naval_museum.5050.name: Fleet Mothballing Operations
STH_naval_museum.5053.desc: The senate has a call for a mothball fleet to return to service, there could be many reasons to reactive a fleet, such as defense or an offensive campaign. Reactivated Fleets will take some time to get back up to operational standards, so make sure you plan ahead. \n\nDev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5053.name: Reactivate a Mothballed Fleet
STH_naval_museum.5060.desc:
A mothballed fleet can be converted into a museum fleet and dispatched into the orbit of one of our colonies. A museum fleet can provide the colony with a big boost in popularity, as history fans flock in to view a piece of our glorious conquests. 

Once the fleet is converted into a museum it can no longer serve as an active part of fleet operations. n
Dev Note: Make sure you rename the fleet you plan to reactive to help identify it from the list.
STH_naval_museum.5060.name: Convert to a Museum
STH_nemesis.1.a: Denied! Return them to the dark!
STH_nemesis.1.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle], after years of sulking in their dark pits on that pitiful rock of a planet Remus, we have been approached by a group of Remans represented by an individual who only refers to himself as the 'Viceroy'. They are requesting equal rights within the [Root.GetName] and the same status as the ordinary Romulan citizen. Granting them these rights would appease the Reman population, but would cause uproar across the Romulan Empire and massive discontent within our Romulan population, who would never approve of being considered equal to these Reman veruul! \n\nOur military leaders have also reminded us that this would also hit our Dilithium production and rid us of valuable foot soldiers.
STH_nemesis.1.name: Reman Discontent
STH_nemesis.10.desc: The Romulan military was far too powerful for the small band of attack ships that the Remans were able to produce and they were quickly overwhelmed and destroyed. With the destruction of their small rebellion the Reman's returned to the shadows and dark tunnels within the near endless mines on Remus, defeated but unrepentant in their quest to emerge from under the wings of the Romulan raptor.
STH_nemesis.10.name: Reman forces destroyed
STH_nemesis.100.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] we have concerning news, those foul beasts from Remus have turned their backs on us and have dared to declare war against us in a bid to gain their independence. We have mobilized our fleets and will crush these foul beasts and return them to that black rock from which they came.
STH_nemesis.100.name: Reman's battle for indepance
STH_nemesis.101.desc: The Reman's have declared war on their former masters the Romulans in a quest for freedom. The Galaxy will soon see who is the stronger side.
STH_nemesis.101.name: Remans declare war!
STH_nemesis.11.desc: It seems a small rebellion that started on the planet Remus and spread to the skies above Romulus was crushed by the Romulan military. It is unclear what provoked the small band of Reman soldiers to attack the superior Romulan forces, but whilst their bravery was not in question they stood little chance against the might of the Romulan Navy.
STH_nemesis.11.name: Reman forces destroyed
STH_nemesis.2.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle], news is escaping the Romulan Star Empire, it appears the long oppressed Reman people made a bold move to speak out against their living conditions and requested more freedom from their Romulan overlords. \n\nHowever as expected these freedoms were quickly rejected. The Romulan people rarely afford equality on those they consider beneath them.
STH_nemesis.2.name: Reman equal status rejected
STH_nemesis.20.a: What is the meaning of this intrusion.
STH_nemesis.20.b: What do those wretched creatures want?
STH_nemesis.20.c: Prepare the Senate for the [Root.GetRulerTitle]'s arrival.
STH_nemesis.20.d: Enough is enough, the Reman's must die.
STH_nemesis.20.desc: My [Root.GetRulerTitle], please let me introduce yourself, I am Senator Tal'Aura and I approach you now to ask for an audience to finally put the question on the Reman situation to an end once and for all. The discontent amongst the Reman population on Remus continues to grow whilst the output from the Dilithium mines continues to shrink. I have the backing of a number of high ranking Senators who now wish for this concern to be removed for good. \n\nWe await your presence on the homeworld to conclude this discussion.
STH_nemesis.20.name: The Reman Question
STH_nemesis.21.a: We have detected a small fleet heading this way.
STH_nemesis.21.desc: With the decision to overrule and disregard the request from Senator Tal'Aura and the high ranking officials to discuss the plight of the Reman's in the Romulan senate, all that remains is to dispatch a fleet of the Empire's most powerful warbirds to put the Reman's back in their place once and for all.
STH_nemesis.21.name: Time to end the Reman threat
STH_nemesis.23.a: What is the meaning of this treachery.
STH_nemesis.23.desc: You may not know or recognize me Romulan, I am Shinzon of Remus, a discarded plot that found a home amongst the discarded and abandoned deep within the Dilithium mines of Remus. For too long have my brethren and I suffered at the cruel whip of our Romulan masters... but no longer. We are desperate and desperation brings courage. Prepare to meet the Scimitar as we turn it's mighty weapons on the jewel that is Romulus.
STH_nemesis.23.name: Message from Shinzon of Remus
STH_nemesis.24.a: Wipe out the remaining Remans on Remus.
STH_nemesis.24.b: There has been enough death for one day.
STH_nemesis.24.desc:
With a mighty explosion the mighty Reman Warbird Scimitar is destroyed taking with it the last hope the Reman's have, along with their leadership and beloved Shinzon. 

The last outstanding questions is what should become of the remaining Reman population on the planet's surface. Purging them from the surface will serve as a stark reminder to all subjects of the empire that rebellions will not be tolerated, as well as a better understanding of how to build a replacement for the Scimitar - however it is unlikely the galactic community will take kindly to this action. 

Sparing them will boost our Dilithium output and demonstrate we are not nothing like the mindless Klingon's who would surely not hesitate to wipe out their foes.
STH_nemesis.24.name: Shinzon defeated
STH_nemesis.25.desc: Our tactical reports indicate that their has been a final showdown between the Romulan and Reman people. An uprising by an individual that our intelligence services can only identify as a Reman named Shinzon was put down by the Romulan fleet. What followed appears to have been a massacre, with Romulan forces wiping out the remaining Reman's dwelling on their homeworld.
STH_nemesis.25.name: Reman population slaughtered
STH_nemesis.26.desc: Our tactical reports indicate that their has been a final showdown between the Romulan and Reman people. An uprising by an individual that our intelligence services can only identify as a Reman named Shinzon was put down by the Romulan fleet. In an unexpected measure of mercy it appears the remaining Reman lives were spared any further punishment. Although many commentators suspect working conditions within the mines will become a lot less pleasant, if that is even possible!
STH_nemesis.26.name: Reman population slaughtered
STH_nemesis.3.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle], in surprising news it appears the long oppressed Reman people made a bold move to speak out against their living conditions and requested more freedom from their Romulan overlords. \n\nIn a shock move it appears the Romulan senate granted the Reman's this request and have made them equal within the Star Empire.
STH_nemesis.3.name: Reman equal status granted
STH_nemesis.30.a: Send them back to that black rock they came from.
STH_nemesis.30.b: Allow the Reman's their freedom in society.
STH_nemesis.30.c: Set the Remans free to fend for themselves.
STH_nemesis.30.desc: As organised by Senator Tal'Aura and a number of high ranking military and political figures, the case for further integration of the Reman population into the Romulan empire was put forward. With assistance from the Reman forces the group believe the Star Empire could be in a position to challenge any empire in the Alpha or beta quadrant within the next few months, but only if a full alliance was struck with the Reman's and granting their representative, a mysterious figure only referred to as Shinzon, a high ranking position in the Senate.
STH_nemesis.30.name: The Reman Problem
STH_nemesis.31.a: What is the meaning of this...
STH_nemesis.31.desc: Before any further discussion or argument could take place, a number of Senators and military leaders stand and make their way from the Romulan Senate room, clearly believing that the discussion was indeed over. Further indication that the Reman's were inferior and without the wit or wisdom to last within the complex and cut throat world of Romulan politics. \n\nJust as [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName] was about to move onto to discuss a proposal from the delegation from Celus III, a small device left behind by one of the departed opens, sending a cascade of greenish energy particles over the room.
STH_nemesis.31.name: The Reman Problem
STH_nemesis.32.desc: Confused, the Praetor calls for security to bring the group back to the Senate room, but it is too late, the entire Senate begin to decay from inside, and the Praetor himself topples to the ground and crumbles into dust.
STH_nemesis.32.name: The Reman's Revenge
STH_nemesis.35.b: What do we know about the new regime?
STH_nemesis.35.desc: We have been approached by our contacts within the Romulan Star Empire. It appears there has been a Coup d'état within the Star Empire beginning with the elimination of the previous Praetor and most of former senate. The new government appears to be closely linked to the Reman's, an elusive caste within the Romulan empire. The spokesperson for the new regime, a high ranking Senator named Tal'Aura has specifically requested we send a delegation to meet with the new leadership, a Romulan named Shinzon, and he has asked that we send [nemesis_leader.GetName] in exchange the Romulans promise a lucrative future.
STH_nemesis.35.name: Urgent Message from Romulus
STH_nemesis.36.a: Continue to wait for a sign...
STH_nemesis.36.desc: On reaching the designated coordinates [nemesis_leader.GetName] and their crew have received no communications from Praetor Shinzon or from the Romulan government. The senior officers look out across the empty void, nervously awaiting a message from their hosts, keenly aware they are a long way from the protection of [Root.GetName].
STH_nemesis.36.name: Waiting in the Raptors Nest
STH_nemesis.37.desc: Just as the crew were about to abandon the wait and return to [Root.capital_scope.GetName] a massive vessel decloaked in front of [nemesis_fleet.GetName]. The massive ship could only be described as a predator, armed to the teeth and built with a single purpose and protected by the most perfect cloak that [nemesis_leader.GetName] had ever seen. \n\nThe beep beep of the incoming hail rang out in sharp contrast to the overwhelming silence that was on the bridge.
STH_nemesis.37.name: Out of the Shadows
STH_nemesis.38.a: You are doing this for the Remans?
STH_nemesis.38.b: How many Romulans died for your freedom?
STH_nemesis.38.c: What is a human doing leading the Romulan Empire?
STH_nemesis.38.desc: Greetings [nemesis_leader.GetName], I hope you'll forgive the darkness. ...We're not comfortable in the light, please let me introduce myself ... I am Shinzon of Remus, proud leader of the Reman people. You may ask what we seek & I say unity, Captain! ...Tearing down the walls between us to recognise that we are one. ...I'm talking of the thing that makes us the same. ...Peace. We want peace. ...Right now, you're thinking this all sounds too good to be true? But you're also thinking that the chance of peace is too promising to ignore. ...Am I right?
STH_nemesis.38.e: What is it you want from me?
STH_nemesis.38.f: What do you want between our two worlds?
STH_nemesis.38.g: We must think about all that has been said here.
STH_nemesis.38.name: Nemesis
STH_nemesis.38.ufp: How did you end up on Remus?
STH_nemesis.39.desc_01:
As Picard and the away team returned to their ship orbiting Romulus concerned and confused by the what had transpired in front of Shinzon. Was this individual really a clone of the Jean-Luc Picard and what does it mean for the future of the Federation-Romulan relations? Just as the question was being discussed Picard disappeared in the buzz of a Reman transporter. 

Aboard the Scimitar in the darkness the Captain found himself chained to an operating chair. Shinzon stepped forward and addresses Picard, 'I need your blood Jean-Luc, to repair the damage to my cells. I'm afraid you won't survive to witness the victory of the echo over the voice and once I have your future... We will no longer bow before anyone as slaves. Not the Romulans and not your mighty Federation.' 

'We ...are a race bred for war. And conquest'.
STH_nemesis.39.desc_02:
As [nemesis_leader.GetName] and the away team returned to their ship orbiting Romulus a number of concerns were raised. Was this individual really a clone of a Starfleet captain and does that make him more or less trustworthy? Suddenly [nemesis_leader.GetName] vanished in the hum of a Reman transporter. 

Aboard the Scimitar in the darkness [nemesis_leader.GetName] found themselves secured in the ships dark brig. Shinzon stepped forward and addressed the commanding officer, explaining that he is an imperfect clone and requires [Root.GetName] hand over one of their scientists in exchange for [nemesis_leader.GetName]'s life. The scientist will serve as a prisoner whose expertise will be used to keep the young human alive. 

With a knowing grin Shinzon declares, we will no longer bow before anyone as slaves. Not the Romulans and not your mighty Federation. 

'We ...are a race bred for war. And conquest'.
STH_nemesis.39.desc_03:
As [nemesis_leader.GetName] and the away team returned to their ship orbiting Romulus a number of concerns were raised. Was this individual really a clone of a human captain and could a fragile human really survive the harsh conditions on Remus? Suddenly [nemesis_leader.GetName] vanished in the hum of a Reman transporter. 

Aboard the Scimitar in the darkness [nemesis_leader.GetName] found themselves secured within a dark brig. Shinzon stepped forward and addressed the commanding officer, explaining that he is an imperfect clone and requires [Root.GetName] hand over one of their scientists in exchange for [nemesis_leader.GetName]'s life. The scientist will serve as a prisoner whose expertise will be used to keep the young human alive. 

With a knowing grin Shinzon declares, we will no longer bow before anyone as slaves. Not the Romulans, the Federation and certainly not the mighty [Root.GetName]. 

'We ...are a race bred for war. And conquest'.
STH_nemesis.39.name: Nemesis
STH_nemesis.4.a: Return the Remans to their proper place.
STH_nemesis.4.b: We do not submit to terrorists.
STH_nemesis.4.desc: In the early hours of the morning a small branch of the Romulan military, potentially supported by the Tal Shiar and an organised civilian group destroyed a Reman habitat on Remus in protest against the decision to grant the Reman's equal rights within the Empire. The group calling themselves 'The true Romulan way' have demanded that the Remans are once again forced into slavery and any notion of granting them freedom is removed. \n\nOur loyal military commanders believe they have enough evidence to destroy this faction or we can bow to their demands.
STH_nemesis.4.name: Reman population attacked
STH_nemesis.40.desc: The First Officer knew that [nemesis_leader.GetName] would not want to put their crew at risk to save their life, but also that they would lay down their life for any member of their crew. The chief of security presents an ambitious plan to rescue the [nemesis_leader.GetName], but it would be risky and risk their chances of a surprise escape. Alternatively making a surprise bid for freedom and heading towards the nearest system free from the communications blackout set up by the Scimitar would greatly increase the chances of escaping the clutches of Shinzon and his ship.
STH_nemesis.40.name: Nemesis
STH_nemesis.41.desc: In a daring rescue the crew of [nemesis_ship.GetName] were able to trace the transporter signal and locate and rescue [nemesis_leader.GetName] from the clutches of Shinzon and the Reman guards holding them captive. The extraction team were able to cause a small amount of damage to the Scimitar before escaping via a number of light attack ships. With [nemesis_leader.GetName] safely back onboard the [nemesis_ship.GetName] is free to rendezvous with the fleet at [nemesis_battle.GetName].
STH_nemesis.41.name: Nemesis - Leader rescued!
STH_nemesis.42.desc: Unfortunately for the the crew of [nemesis_ship.GetName] the rescue of [nemesis_leader.GetName] was a failure and the Remans were able to fend off the attempt killing [nemesis_leader.GetName] in the process. With their leader dead First Officer [nemesis_leader_1.GetName] is forced to step into the command chair and attempt to escape to rendezvous with the fleet at [nemesis_battle.GetName].
STH_nemesis.42.name: Nemesis - Rescue Failed
STH_nemesis.45.desc: Failing to reach the rendezvous point in time to mount a significant defense from the Scimitar left [nemesis_ship.GetName] vulnerable to the powerful Thalaron weapons harboured onboard Shinzon's vessel. With the ship destroyed Shinzon and his Reman forces are free attack the surrounding systems and create havoc.
STH_nemesis.45.name: Ship Fails to escape
STH_nemesis.46.desc_01: The failure of [nemesis_ship.GetName] to reach freedom before it was attacked and destroyed by the Scimitar and its destruction has caused concern across [Root.GetName]. Fleets have been put on alert for any further sign of the Scimitar leaving Romulan space. \n\nIt appears the Romulan military has taken this moment to rise up and reclaim the Empire in the name of the Romulan people. It is clear this is now an internal matter for the Romulan's to deal with.
STH_nemesis.46.desc_02: Our long range scanners are picking up the destruction following what appears to have been a brief battle involving one of our neighbours and a mysterious vessel claiming to be of Reman design. Whatever has occurred seems to now be an internal matter for the Romulans to deal with.
STH_nemesis.46.name: Reman Scimitar Victorious
STH_nemesis.47.desc: As [nemesis_ship.GetName] approaches the coordinates at [nemesis_battle.GetName] the astrometrical display flickers ominously, seeking clarity on the situation the communications officer explains that they are approaching a naturally occuring sensor blind spot and that should clear shortly. Spotting the danger quickly [nemesis_leader.GetName] calls out for Red Alert, shortly before torpedoes hammer into the ships hull. The Scimitar has tracked them and having had the chance to call for backup whilst the CO was rescued bears down on the [Root.GetName] vessels weapons raised and ready to destroy all that stand in its way.
STH_nemesis.47.name: Nemesis - Battle at [nemesis_battle.GetName]
STH_nemesis.48.desc: As [nemesis_ship.GetName] approaches the coordinates at [nemesis_battle.GetName] the astrometrical display flickers ominously, seeking clarity on the situation the communications officer explains that they are approaching a naturally occuring sensor blind spot and that should clear shortly. Spotting the danger quickly [nemesis_leader.GetName] calls out for Red Alert, shortly before torpedoes hammer into the ships hull. The fast escape means that the Scimitar was unable to call for any further Reman backup, but even on its own the deadly Reman Warbird poses a deadly adversary.
STH_nemesis.48.name: Nemesis - Battle at [nemesis_battle.GetName]
STH_nemesis.49.a: We owe you a bottle of Romulan Ale.
STH_nemesis.49.desc: As the battle was beginning to intensify, a new fleet decloaked ready to join the battle. A young romulan Subcommander named Donatra hailed the [Root.GetName] fleet. \n\nIt seems the Romulan's see this as an internal matter and Donatra and her fleet have been sent to see that the matter remains discreet.
STH_nemesis.49.name: Romulan Backup arrives
STH_nemesis.5.desc: It appears all is not well within the Romulan Empire since the decision to grant the Reman people equal status within the Empire. One of our covert operations teams provided us with evidence that the Romulan military had to put down a small uprising against the Reman people. \n\nIt looks like the Romulan government are now back in control of the situation, but this is clearly a situation they would prefer to have avoided.
STH_nemesis.5.name: Dissatisfaction amongst the population of Romulus
STH_nemesis.50.desc: The battle was fierce and many lives were lost as the fleets clashed, but finally the Scimitar lay defeated and with its Thalaron core going critical exploded into countless pieces of debris bringing an end to the rebellion and the threat that Shinzon posed to the galaxy.
STH_nemesis.50.name: Shinzon defeated
STH_nemesis.51.desc_01: With the crews from the [Root.GetName] and the Valdore both surviving the battle the ships are now free to return to their respective homeworlds knowing that they are now safe from any further attack from Shinzon. It was easy for the Commanding Officer to understand that circumstances could shape the lives of individuals very differently and the plight of the Reman people very much remained a matter of concern.
STH_nemesis.51.desc_04: In a complete victory for Shinzon and his Reman conspirators left the ships from the Romulan Star Empire and [Root.GetName] defeated, unable to match the power of the mighty Thalaron weapons. The [Root.GetName] must now wait and see what the fallout from the battle will be.
STH_nemesis.51.name: Shinzon defeated
STH_nemesis.52.desc: Hello [Root.GetRulerTitle] let me get straight to the point, we Reman's are a proud people and it is hard for us to approach you with this request. We have fought and died to obtain our freedom and the right to live our lives free from the Romulan whip and the darkness that is Remus. All we ask is for what you take for granted, the opportunity to rule ourselves. Please add your voice to ours and maybe Romulus will finally listen.
STH_nemesis.52.name: Incoming hail from Remus
STH_nemesis.53.a: Welcome to the Reman Free States.
STH_nemesis.53.desc: After years of struggle against the Romulans, the people of Remus can finally call themselves free, having won their independence from the Star Empire. They were granted the planet [reman_new_world.GetName] as part of a historic treaty with the Romulan [romulan_star_empire.GetRulerTitle] [romulan_star_empire.GetRulerName]. Time will now see how the Reman's fare in the future.
STH_nemesis.53.name: The Reman Free State
STH_nemesis.54.desc: Despite international pressure from multiple states the Romulan Empire has rejected the proposals to set the Reman's on Remus free. With all their options now exhausted the Remans must now resign themselves to remain second class citizens within the Empire.
STH_nemesis.54.name: Reman freedom rejected
STH_nemesis.55.desc: After many years of struggle the Reman's have forced their freedom from their Romulan oppressors and have been granted a small world of their own with the freedom to choose their own destiny.
STH_nemesis.55.name: Reman freedom granted
STH_nemesis.56.desc: Terrible news! Despite putting up a valiant fight against Shinzon [nemesis_leader.GetName] and their ship were defeated in battle. \n\nShinzon is now free to continue his reign of terror.
STH_nemesis.56.name: [nemesis_leader.GetName] killed in action
STH_nemesis.6.desc: It looks like the decision to grant the Reman's equal rights within the Romulan Star Empire was short lived according to our spy network. Following a brief uprising against the Remans on the planet Romulus the Praetor has once again rescinded the Reman's liberty.
STH_nemesis.6.name: Reman returned to slavery
STH_nemesis.7.a: Demand?! Suffer the wrath of a Romulan disruptor.
STH_nemesis.7.b: Return to work Reman or your people will suffer.
STH_nemesis.7.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] \n\nI am Viceroy [reman_rebel_leader.GetName] representing the Reman's of Remus, for too long we have been condemned to the dark and forced to dig the Dilithium that powers the empire, whilst we are treated little better than the machines that toil those deep mines. No longer will we be subject to your oppression, we demand the same rights as any Romulan or you will know our fury.
STH_nemesis.7.name: A demand from the Reman Free State
STH_nemesis.8.a: The talk of freedom is over.
STH_nemesis.8.desc_01: The meer threat of an attack from our mighty warbirds has sent those cowardly vermin back to that black hell hole of a planet where they belong and they will think twice before returning to the senate with such ludicrous ideas.
STH_nemesis.8.desc_02: As expected those vile cowards crumbled at the thought of a confrontation with our military, we shall insist that their working conditions are tightened over the next few months to ensure they are reminded that there are consequences for threatening the Empire.
STH_nemesis.8.name: Reman's back down
STH_nemesis.9.desc: The audacity of those Reman traitors knows no bounds, it appears they have managed to secure a number of attack ships from various sources and have launched an attack on our capital system. Whilst it appears only a very small section of the Reman population has dared to join in on this attack, we must not let this threat to our authority go unpunished.
STH_nemesis.9.name: Under attack from Reman Forces
STH_notifications.100.desc: A number of scientists across the [Root.GetName] believe they have identified signs of a change to the timeline, following a wave of chroniton radiation being detected sweeping across the quadrant at superluminal velocities. \n\nSo far no-one has been able to identify the supposed changes.
STH_notifications.100.name: Temporal Anomaly
STH_notifications.101.desc: A number of scientists across the [Root.GetName] believe they have detected signs of a change to the timeline, following a wave of chroniton radiation being detected sweeping across the quadrant at superluminal velocities. \n\nSo far no-one has been able to identify the supposed changes, though given the amplitude of the chroniton radiation wave, the changes are expected to be substantial.
STH_notifications.101.name: Temporal Anomaly
STH_observation.100.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], the diplomatic efforts of the [Root.GetName] have succeeded in convincing my people to participate more fully in galactic affairs. \n\nWhile we had forgone the exploitation of warp technology in order to focus our energies toward affairs on [FromFrom.GetName], the underlying technologies have been known to us for some time. I firmly believe our peoples can prosper together, in peace.
STH_observation.100.name: Diplomatic Success On [FromFrom.GetName]
STH_observation.110.d: [missing_scientist.GetHeSheCap] will remain in prison for [missing_scientist.GetHisHer] breaking the laws of our people.
STH_observation.150.car.desc: Obsidian Order operatives on [From.GetName] have located some strategic sites around the planet which would be the ideal place for military outposts and fortresses. Some subtly smuggled in materials could be used to construct hidden military outposts across the planet.\n\nSome well placed tunnel networks under the largest cities should also provide security for our operatives, and convince the [From.Owner.GetName] government that they have a safe haven against any rebel activity.
STH_observation.150.fed.a: High expenditure. Meet these protestors face to face.
STH_observation.150.fed.c: Whilst disappointing, it is their right.
STH_observation.150.fed.desc: Many citizens of the independence movement on [From.GetName] have begun protests against what they see as Federation meddling in their affairs. Exerting further diplomatic pressure and offering concessions to the [From.GetName] government may placate these protests.\n\nAlternatively, we could let the protests run their course, and instead focus our efforts elsewhere.
STH_observation.150.fer.a: Tariffs at spaceports? Buy their spaceports!
STH_observation.150.fer.b: Fine, but not one strip more than required!
STH_observation.150.fer.c: They'll not get a single strip!
STH_observation.150.fer.desc: Traders on [From.GetName] are coming up against obstinate politicians who are talking about imposing 'tariffs' and 'taxes' upon our erstwhile hardworking businesses!\n\nSome well placed latinum in the right hands could soon placate the [From.GetName] government. Eliminators will be on hand to ensure the additional funding should speed up the process of monopolising their economy and forcing the [From.Owner.GetName] into our empire.
STH_observation.150.name: Art of the Deal
STH_observation.150.rom.a: Ensure that the Tal Shiar is well funded.
STH_observation.150.rom.b: Fund only their most important request.
STH_observation.150.rom.desc: Our operatives on [From.GetName] are requesting further resources and supplies in order to secure the loyalties of key members of the [From.Owner.GetName] government. Whilst they can continue to operate with their current budget, additional funding should speed up the process of annexing the [From.Owner.GetName] into our empire.
STH_observation.152.car.a: Send a large kemocite shipment. Hit every target.
STH_observation.152.car.b: Send a small amount of kemocite. Target only the key structures.
STH_observation.152.car.c: Supplies are needed elsewhere. Request denied.
STH_observation.152.car.desc: Dissident groups have been infiltrated, riled up and made a target of by our operatives, and rumours that they are looking to target important civilian sites around the planet have been placed in the ears of the most important leaders on [From.GetName]. Key locations have been identified that are of important to the cultural heritage of the [From.Owner.GetName], which makes them a prime target for destruction.\n\nOur operatives on [From.GetName] are requesting a large supply of explosive material in order to frame these dissidents for these attacks, whilst simultaneously allowing Cardassian forces to capture the 'perpetrators', showcasing that only Cardassian order can bring safety to the government of [From.Owner.GetName].
STH_observation.152.fed.a: Cure this disease and provide a large amount of medical supplies.
STH_observation.152.fed.b: Aid their researchers, but let them find the cure.
STH_observation.152.fed.c: Until they join the [Root.GetName], this is their problem.
STH_observation.152.fed.desc: A rare disease, unrelated to our first contact, is spreading rapidly across the unified world of [From.GetName]. Many media outlets and political groups on [From.GetName] have blamed us for the fatalities and repeatedly suggested that the [From.Owner.GetName] government will be better off alone rather than as part of the [Root.GetName], as we have not shared our advanced medical technology with them. Protests have begun to spread in several major locations across [From.GetName], whilst the local government forces are attempting to quell the more violent areas.\n\nOur diplomats on [From.GetName] are requesting a large supply of medical research and equipment to be provided to the local hospitals in order to combat this disease, and to showcase our generosity and welfare to the populace.
STH_observation.152.fer.a: Send for the Eliminators! Pay what it takes to stop them!
STH_observation.152.fer.b: Bribe the Ferengi to stop their cult.
STH_observation.152.fer.c: Never spend more for an acquisition than you have to. Do nothing.
STH_observation.152.fer.desc: Many of the poorer citizens in the northern hemisphere of [From.GetName] have recently begun to worship a strange cult which has been propagated by entrepreneurial [Root.GetSpeciesName] traders using advanced technology against our direct orders, forming a secret religion in order to secretly subvert and take steal riches from the government! Whilst otherwise a worthy business endeavour, this 'religious' cult must be quelled before it gains any serious traction amongst the populace and subverts our market forces!\n\nOur Liquidators on [From.GetName] are requesting more latinum in order to stop these rogue traders, and recover their stolen wealth.
STH_observation.152.name: Hidden Dealings
STH_observation.152.rom.a: Send as many as is required.
STH_observation.152.rom.b: We can spare a few to minimise the damage.
STH_observation.152.rom.c: Request denied, let them believe what they will.
STH_observation.152.rom.desc: Many news outlets and media conglomerates on [From.GetName] are pushing stories about [Root.GetSpeciesName] operatives forming a secret society in order to secretly subvert and take over the [From.GetName] government! Whilst utterly true, this 'conspiracy' theory must be quelled before it gains any serious traction amongst the populace.\n\nOur operatives on [From.GetName] are requesting more manpower in order to combat the spread of rumours and lies coming from the media, and to ensure that our own tracks are covered.
STH_observation.153.car.desc:
Massive explosions across [FromFrom.GetName], striking at civilian and military institutions, has struck fear into the heart of the [FromFrom.Owner.GetSpeciesName] and made the [FromFrom.Owner.GetName] government look weak and ineffective.

Local media groups filmed our brave [Root.GetSpeciesName] troops as they struck back at the dissident groups 'responsible' for these explosions, and in a stunning display of military prowess brought all the terrorists to justice. 

The population on [From.GetName] is extremely pleased with the order and safety that our troopers bring, and many businesses and groups are offering to defray the cost of entertaining our troops.
STH_observation.153.fed.a: Excellent. We did what we could.
STH_observation.153.fed.desc: The rapid dispersion of medical supplies and equipment throughout [FromFrom.GetName] has stopped the deadly disease in its tracks, and support for the dissident groups has all but faded away. Many of the former protestors have had their minds changed by the free and open healthcare available to them now that Starfleet medical is on the scene.
STH_observation.153.fer.a: Anything worth doing is worth doing for money.
STH_observation.153.fer.desc: After some careful threatening of the lobes of the Ferengi in charge of the absurd religious uprising, the vast cargo cult following on [From.GetName] has been commanded to give up their worldly possessions and to return back to their regular workaday lives. An extremely profitable venture.
STH_observation.153.name: Impact of Hidden Deals
STH_observation.153.rom.desc: The 'Secret Society' on [FromFrom.GetName] has been exposed as nothing more than a laugh and a joke by the media companies and news outlets, which are now under mostly our control thanks to the additional manpower provided. Rumours of a [Root.GetSpeciesName] takeover of their planet has been relegated to the shunned conspiracy theorists in society.\n\nMeanwhile, our operatives in control of the [FromFrom.Owner.GetName]'s media outlets has dramatically increased our sway over the population. Hail Romulus.
STH_observation.154.car.desc: Disaster has struck our organisation on [FromFrom.GetName]. The dissidents have uncovered our plan to blame the terrorist attacks on them, and have exposed our shipment of kemocite to the [FromFrom.Owner.GetName] government!\n\nThis has caused a major setback to our plans here on [FromFrom.GetName] whilst we attempt to blame it on rogue elements of our military. Meanwhile, many of our shell companies used to subvert local government have been uncovered too, and our other operatives that remain have gone into hiding as our sway over the population fades. This will set back our operation by a few years.
STH_observation.154.fed.a: Unfortunate. We did what we could.
STH_observation.154.fed.desc: The rapid dispersion of medical supplies and equipment throughout [FromFrom.GetName] has stopped the deadly disease in its tracks, though this has riled up the protestors rather than quelled them, as they now yell that the [FromFrom.Owner.GetSpeciesNamePlural] will become dependent on the [Root.GetName] and will lose their independence if they become reliant on us. Many of the more extreme protestors have even broached the idea that we used our advanced medical technology to create the disease in the first place. This will set back Federation membership by a few years.
STH_observation.154.fer.a: Inconceivable! Never spend more for an acquisition than you have to...
STH_observation.154.fer.desc: After vast amounts of bribery given to the [Root.GetSpeciesName] in charge of the absurd religious uprising, the vast cargo cult following on [FromFrom.GetName] has instead risen up against our business interests, forcing numerous outlets and businesses to close, whilst the [Root.GetSpeciesName] cult founders laugh at our ineptitude and rake in all the wealth.\n\nWhilst our traders are regretful that this will set back our plans for the [FromFrom.GetName] for years, there is a grudging respect for the business acumen utilised by the entrepreneurial founders.
STH_observation.154.name: Impact of Hidden Deals
STH_observation.154.rom.desc: Disaster has struck our organisation on [FromFrom.GetName]. The 'Secret Society' issue has fully exposed many of our operatives working on the surface after a large investigation by the [FromFrom.Owner.GetSpeciesNamePlural] intelligence services. Rumours of a [Root.GetSpeciesName] takeover of their planet has been solidified due to the evidence found, and the formerly shunned conspiracy theorists have been hailed as heroes.\n\nMeanwhile, many of our shell companies used to subvert local government have been uncovered too, and our operatives that remain have gone into hiding as our sway over the population fades. This will set back our operation by a few years.
STH_observation.200.desc: After infiltrating every facet of the ruling body on [From.GetName], the operatives of the Tal Shiar have successfully convinced the provisional government of the [primitive_civ.GetName] that joining the Romulan Star Empire is in their best interests for a safe and stable future.\n\nAs subjects of the [Root.GetName], they will become second class citizens and subject to [Root.GetSpeciesName] rule. Dissent is not an option.
STH_observation.200.name: Tal Shiar Infiltration
STH_observation.300.desc: The Ferengi Commerce Authority has been selectively targeting the economic markets on [From.GetName] for many months now, and has finally gained control over many of the major trades and industries on the planet.\n\nLeveraging the markets of the [primitive_civ.GetName], the Liquidators now have ownership over much of the governing body and world markets, which in their infinite wisdom have decided that joining the [Root.GetName] is preferable to economic ruin. Traders are flocking to [From.GetName] to take advantage of the new opportunities.
STH_observation.300.name: Monopolising Civilization
STH_observation.400.desc: After infiltrating every facet of the ruling body on [FromFromFrom.GetName] and destroying some selective infrastructure, the operatives of the Obsidian Order have successfully convinced the provisional government of [primitive_civ.GetName] that allowing our troops to land to restore order and submitting themselves to the [Root.GetName] is in their best interests for a safe and stable future.\n\nWith our troops in position at every key juncture they will be now subject to our rule.
STH_observation.400.name: Occupation Complete
STH_observation.600.desc: Our diplomats on [From.GetName] have spent years extolling the virtues of the Federation, and preparing the insular [primitive_country.GetSpeciesNamePlural] people for Federation Membership, and today is the day of the signing ceremony. After the ceremony they will become a fully fledged member of the [Root.GetName], a momentous occasion.\n\nWhilst being a full member of the [Root.GetName], they will handle their own worldly affairs.
STH_observation.600.name: Federation Membership
STH_observation.700.desc: Our Generals confirm that the planet [From.GetName] is now under our control, their primitive armies stood no chance against the forces of [Root.GetName]. The primitive [From.Owner.GetName] is no more!
STH_observation.700.name: Planet now under our control
STH_observation.701.desc: Our attempts to integrate this world into our Empire has failed, and we must now wait until we can re-attempt the process.
STH_observation.701.name: Integration Ended
STH_occupation_situation.1.a: We must look outside Cardassia for resources.
STH_occupation_situation.1.desc:
Years of neglect and over taxation of the skant resources of Cardassia Prime have finally caught up with the people of the Cardassian Union and now a period of harsh stellar activity from the Cardassian sun has ruined the crop growth for this season and put a strain on the already over worked food replication power grids. 

Environmentalists have been warning about this day from behind the scenes for many years, but have been ignored by those in power. 

Experts are predicting unless more drastic action is taken Cardassia Prime will struggle for a number of years.
STH_occupation_situation.1.name: Starvation strikes Cardassia Prime
STH_occupation_situation.10.desc: The Cardassian Union has made its intentions clear, they convert the resources from the planet Bajor and they are prepared to take them by force.
STH_occupation_situation.10.name: Cardassia wishes to annex Bajor
STH_occupation_situation.101.desc: Praise the Prophets! After declaring war many felt that the power of Cardassia would be too much for our meager forces to defend against, but the spirit of the Bajoran people will not be defeated and our long oppression is finally over. The retreating Cardassian forces implemented a scorched earth policy and it will take some years to recover, but our future is back in our own hands.
STH_occupation_situation.101.name: The Occupation of Bajor is over
STH_occupation_situation.102.desc: The people of the planet Bajor have long been oppressed under a brutal occupation of their homeworld, it appears they no longer wish to live under these conditions and a number of resistance cells have started to rise up against their overlords. Our intelligence reports indicated there has been a wave of bombings and attacks against foreign targets on the planet. \n\nThe Bajoran resistance has formally demanded the withdrawal of all foreign forces from the planet within 48 hours.
STH_occupation_situation.102.name: The Bajoran Occupation
STH_occupation_situation.103.desc: The War between Bajor and Cardassia is over and despite their superior firepower the Cardassian Union has formally declared their withdrawal from Bajor. The guerilla tactics deployed by the resistance seems to have ground the Cardassian forces down and it seems the Central Command no longer deem the planet worth the effort of keeping. \n\nThe people of Bajor may rejoice today, but it appears there is a lot of work to do if the planet is to recover.
STH_occupation_situation.103.name: The Occupation of Bajor is over
STH_occupation_situation.105.desc: The time has come to rid our home of the [bajoran_overlords.GetName], the resistance cells have made significant inroads into their defences and we have secured an additional number of small ships to supplement our raiders. The Prophets guide us on this path and the path to a free Bajor. Our home will be ours once again.
STH_occupation_situation.105.name: The Occupation must end
STH_occupation_situation.106.a: Cardassia has always been a friend to the Bajoran people...
STH_occupation_situation.106.b: Bajor is no longer worth the effort.
STH_occupation_situation.106.desc: Cardassians, this is your final chance to withdraw your forces from Bajor, your illegal occupation and plunder of our world ends here and now.
STH_occupation_situation.106.name: Bajoran Freedom
STH_occupation_situation.107.desc: The Cardassians have rejected our demand on them to leave, this leaves us with no choice. If we are to be free then it must be war.
STH_occupation_situation.107.name: Cardassia Denies us Liberation
STH_occupation_situation.108.desc: In a shock decision we have been notified by the Cardassian civilian government that their 'assistance' and 'friendship 'will no longer be offered to the Bajoran people. Their forces have begun to evacuate, destroying any and all equipment that they can get their hands on, including vast the vast fields of the Dahkur Province and the slaughter at Gallitep. The former ore-processing station Terok Nor now belongs to us despite the massive damage done to it as the Cardassians left.
STH_occupation_situation.108.name: Cardassia withdraws from Bajor
STH_occupation_situation.109.desc: After years of occupation, the Cardassians have withdrawn from Bajor. The exact cause of the withdrawal is largely a matter of opinion; while the Bajorans attributed it to the efforts of the Bajoran Resistance, the Cardassians regarded it as an entirely political decision. In the days leading up to the withdraw, the Cardassians diverted warships away from Bajor in an attempt to shore up their border territories.
STH_occupation_situation.109.name: Cardassia withdraws from Bajor
STH_occupation_situation.11.desc: The people of Bajor are in dire need of the firm supportive hands that we can offer and their backwater beliefs and outlook cries out for the appointment of a Prefect. \n\nA number of standout candidates have put themselves forward for the role, which we may now appoint to secure our interests on Bajor.
STH_occupation_situation.11.name: A Prefect for Bajor
STH_occupation_situation.110.desc: The last of our transports has arrived back into Cardassian space following our decision to abandon the peasant Bajorans to their backwater planet. They will soon learn the meaning of crossing the Cardassian people and no doubt they will come crawling back to us begging for our help.
STH_occupation_situation.110.name: Cardassia withdraws from Bajor
STH_occupation_situation.112.desc: Today is a humiliating day to be a Cardassian, the civilian government has signed the withdrawal agreement forcing us to leave the planet and its precious resources behind. This is a humiliating blow for the Union and one that we must see avenged. The Bajorans have not seen the last of us.
STH_occupation_situation.112.name: Cardassian Forces defeated
STH_occupation_situation.113.desc: After years of living under the rule of the Cardassian Union, it appears the Bajoran people have had enough and led by their resistance movement have declared war on Cardassia in a bid to create a free Bajor.
STH_occupation_situation.113.name: Bajor Breaks from Cardassia
STH_occupation_situation.115.b: Attempt to root out the terrorists.
STH_occupation_situation.115.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] those treacherous Bajoran fools have betrayed the gracious offer of peace and reconciliation we have offered them. \n\nAn explosive planted on the [assassinated_ship.GetName] destroyed the ship and our delegate [assassinated_cardassian.GetName]. There are strong calls from Cardassia that we have been too lenient and must react with Cardassian vengeance.
STH_occupation_situation.115.name: Peace conference sabotaged
STH_occupation_situation.116.a.tooltip: Release Bajor from the occupation and allow them to become full subjects of the Union, but lose the benefits of being an occupational force.
STH_occupation_situation.116.b.tooltip: The Bajorans are only good for slaves, now is our chance to make slaves of all Bajorans on the planet.
STH_occupation_situation.116.desc:
The peace talks with the Bajoran leadership has been successful and in return for a greater standing within our great Union the Bajoran ministers and Vedeks have agreed to give up the location of the various resistance cells located across the planet. 

Obtaining the loyalty of the Bajoran leadership is truly a triumph of Cardassian might. 

We may now uphold our word and allow Bajor to become a true subject of Cardassia or follow the advice of many of our older Legates and betray the Bajorans and turn them into slaves.
STH_occupation_situation.116.name: Peace reached on Bajor
STH_occupation_situation.117.desc: After years of occupation the Cardassians and Bajorans have come to an understanding and Bajor has formally submitted to Cardassian rule. The occupation is over and the resistance has been defeated.
STH_occupation_situation.117.name: Bajor and Cardassia unite
STH_occupation_situation.118.desc: Despite years of resistance the Bajoran freedom movement has been ended in an act of betrayal from the Cardassian military which has left the Bajorans as little more than slaves to the will of the Cardassian machine.
STH_occupation_situation.118.name: Cardassians enslave the Bajorans
STH_occupation_situation.12.desc: Our acquisition of the Bajoran world has opened up new possibilities and new opportunities for us, but I put it to you that we cannot achieve these goals unless we fully occupy the planet with a fully armed military presence. \n\nThis occupation will help ensure that the Bajorans continue to serve the needs of our people, and of course our troops will play their part in aiding our Bajoran friends.
STH_occupation_situation.12.name: The Occupation
STH_occupation_situation.121.desc: One of our ships has suffered an unexpected malfunction resulting in a reactor breach that destroyed the ship. Whilst we have no evidence to prove it senior members of the Obsidian Order believe that it was no coincidence that the ship had recently received cargo that originated from Bajor.
STH_occupation_situation.121.name: Ship Sabotaged
STH_occupation_situation.122.desc: One of our leaders [killed_leader.GetName] has been found dead whilst on shore leave, it looks like they were murdered by the terrorist group calling themselves the Bajoran Resistance. They are nothing but criminals and will pay for their actions.
STH_occupation_situation.122.name: Cardassian Citizens Killed
STH_occupation_situation.123.desc: A high ranking member of the civilian council has gone missing whilst travelling back from the Bajoran system, she was inspected the Ore processing facilities on the Nor-Class station orbiting Bajor and has not reported back to their superior officer.
STH_occupation_situation.123.name: Ship Sabotaged
STH_occupation_situation.124.desc: One of our transports carrying valuable cargo has disappeared whilst travelling through the badlands. We have now lost the transport and the crucial cargo being carried to Cardassia from Bajor. We suspect this is the work of the Bajoran Resistance.
STH_occupation_situation.124.name: Transport Vanishes
STH_occupation_situation.13.desc:
Greetings citizens of Bajor, my name is [bajoran_occupation_prefect.GetName] and I will be your new Prefect and we want to welcome in a new era of peace and prosperity between our two worlds. 

Do not be afraid of the troops landing across the planet, they are here to help rebuild Bajor and ensure our interests are met. 

May this be the start of a long and prosperous future for our worlds.
STH_occupation_situation.13.name: Cardassian Occupation Begins
STH_occupation_situation.130.desc: Following months of work by Cardassian negotiators, diplomats and select interventions by the Obsidian Order, the Bajoran people have been pacified and their leadership has been persuaded to follow Cardassian rule. We can now truly consider Bajor a loyal servant of the [Root.GetName].
STH_occupation_situation.130.name: Bajoran population pacified
STH_occupation_situation.14.desc: The time has come to assign a new Prefect to oversee the Bajoran Occupation. \n\nA list of candidates has been put forward and one mus be selected to continue our interests on Bajor.
STH_occupation_situation.14.name: A New Bajoran Prefect
STH_occupation_situation.15.desc_01: Greetings [Root.GetLeaderTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName], I bring you the latest progress report from our operations on Bajor and I am pleased to say that all is going well, production targets are being met and the locals are providing little resistance. Bajor continues to be a rich source of resource for Cardassia.
STH_occupation_situation.15.desc_02: Greetings [Root.GetLeaderTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName], I bring you the latest progress report from our operations on Bajor and we continue to see a rich production of minerals and food from our mining operations across the planet, with our production targets being consistently met. Our contacts within the Obsidian Order have started to report signs of malconent from the locals, but they remain unfocused and mostly just noise.
STH_occupation_situation.15.desc_03: Time for another report [Root.GetLeaderTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName], whilst operations continue with typical Cardassian efficiency the ungrateful locals continue to cause some... minor concerns, nothing that our local troops cannot handle, but it has hampered some of our production targets.
STH_occupation_situation.15.desc_04: I have little time for this [Root.GetLeaderTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName], but as requested the local insurgents continue to make a nuisance of themselves, nothing that we cannot handle, but had I been given the funding required I would have quite easily dispatched these Bajoran pesks. The complete pacification of the native population maybe our most effective solution...
STH_occupation_situation.15.name: Report from Bajor
STH_occupation_situation.16.desc: A list of replacement candidates for the role of the Bajoran Prefect has been made available to Central Command for their approval.
STH_occupation_situation.16.name: A New Bajoran Prefect
STH_occupation_situation.17.desc: Our mines and labour camps are now ready to turn over the resources that we have collected from Bajor, we shall approach the Bajoran government to arrange for the transportation of these resources to our orbital facilities.
STH_occupation_situation.17.name: Harvest Resources
STH_occupation_situation.18.desc: Attention Bajoran Workers! It is once again time to lay down your tools and allow your Cardassian overseers the chance to collect the efforts of your labours. \n\nAny attempt to prevent us from completing this activity will be met with severe force and punishment for you and your families.
STH_occupation_situation.18.name: Resource Collection
STH_occupation_situation.19.desc_01: As promised we provide you with all that Bajor has to offer as we continue to look to work with our Cardassian friends.
STH_occupation_situation.19.desc_02: Your payment will be made ready for you to collect Cardassian, I believe there is nothing more for us to speak on today.
STH_occupation_situation.19.desc_03: Take what you must and let us be done with this conversation.
STH_occupation_situation.19.desc_04: You Cardassians take and take with no regard for the consequences, mark our words the Prophets will see better times for the Bajoran people.
STH_occupation_situation.19.name: Resource Collection
STH_occupation_situation.2.desc: Through careful management of the remaining resources and the hard work by planetary environmentalists and leading agricultural experts we have made a significant dent in the impact in the global starvation that has been plaguing Cardassia Prime.\n\nThe government has officially declared the crisis on the homeworld as over.
STH_occupation_situation.2.name: Cardassia Starvation Over
STH_occupation_situation.20.desc_01: As promised we provide you with all that Bajor has to offer as we continue to look to work with our Cardassian friends.
STH_occupation_situation.20.desc_02: Your payment will be made ready for you to collect Cardassian, I believe there is nothing more for us to speak on today.
STH_occupation_situation.20.desc_03: Take what you must and if you feel the need to punish us then so be it, we can do no more.
STH_occupation_situation.20.desc_04: This is all we can provide, mark our words the Prophets will see better times for the Bajoran people.
STH_occupation_situation.21.a: Get out of our sights Bajoran.
STH_occupation_situation.21.desc_01: Please forgive us, the harvests have been poor this year and we have so little to give you, we will work harder to ensure future loads meet the quota.
STH_occupation_situation.21.desc_02: This is all we have to give at this stage, we will continue to slave away to produce more for next time.
STH_occupation_situation.21.desc_03: Our people slave and suffer and we can produce no more, do what must be done.
STH_occupation_situation.21.desc_04: This is all you shall receive from Bajorans and be thankful that the resistance doesn't make you pay for your crimes against our people.
STH_occupation_situation.22.a: Our lives will never be the same.
STH_occupation_situation.22.desc: The first wave of Cardassian Troops have landed on our soil bringing with them their weapons and prowess. A sense of fear and concern washes over our population centres, but our Cardassian contacts continue to assure us that these forces are here to protect us and help build our infrastructure. For now we must trust this is true, but there is little we could do either way.
STH_occupation_situation.22.name: Foreign Troops on our soil
STH_occupation_situation.24.desc: As we continue to progress our plans for Bajor, our military and expansion advisors recommend installing a new more agreeable regime that is more supportive of our goals for the planet. \n\nThe 'Bajoran Occupational Government' will continue to serve the interests of all parties and legitimise our occupation of the planet in the eyes of our galactic neighbours.
STH_occupation_situation.24.name: A puppet state
STH_occupation_situation.25.desc: Greetings Bajorans and greetings from your friends on Cardassia, as part of our continued friendship we have decided that it would be in your best interests if we helped provide you with a new stable government and leadership. \n\nFrom this moment onwards the Bajoran Republic will be run by the newly created 'Bajoran Occupational Government' and together we will build a better future for our people.
STH_occupation_situation.25.name: A new Regime
STH_occupation_situation.26.a: For the good of both worlds.
STH_occupation_situation.26.desc: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] I bring you greetings and blessings from your loyal subjects on Bajor, the new governing body is now in place and ready to serve you in all things, just as the Prophets would desire.
STH_occupation_situation.26.name: Greetings from Bajor
STH_occupation_situation.27.desc: Children of the Prophets, I speak for the downtrodden and voiceless, for we are all loved by the Prophets... this partnership with the Cardassians is a shame, we live under occupation as our freedoms are eroded and our way of life is slowly taken from us. \n\nI urge all of us to stand united and in a single voice ask the Cardassians to leave our world.
STH_occupation_situation.27.name: Religious Leader Speaks Out
STH_occupation_situation.28.desc: It appears that a notable Bajoran religious leader has started actively speaking out against our presence on Bajor, this would not normally be a cause for concern, but those spiritualistic fools have started listening to these words risking unrest amongst those working the fields and mines. \n\nWe should instruct our planetary forces to act.
STH_occupation_situation.28.name: Bajoran Dissent
STH_occupation_situation.29.desc: Bajorans! Our forces have today arrested [BajoranReligiousLeader.GetName] for causing civic unrest, now is a time for unity among our people and the type of rhetoric being spouted will no longer be tolerated, let this be a lesson to all who wish to cause trouble, it will not be allowed!
STH_occupation_situation.29.name: Vedek Arrested
STH_occupation_situation.3.desc: Citizens from across the Union grow eager for Central Command to put our newest acquisition to good use, the fertile lands and rich mineral deposits of Bajor are ripe for use by the hardworking Cardassian's in need of the bounty.
STH_occupation_situation.3.name: Time to put the Bajoran's to Work
STH_occupation_situation.30.desc: Bajorans! You will no doubt now be aware that Cardassian forces moved in to arrest and detain Bajoran dissidents causing unrest and sowing ill will between our governments, they mean to harm our special relationship and they will face Cardassian justice. \n\nLet this be a lesson to anyone who wishes to speak out against us.
STH_occupation_situation.30.name: Population Arrested
STH_occupation_situation.31.desc: Bajorans! Today Cardassian troops moved in and executed known troublemaker [BajoranReligiousLeader.GetName] for crimes against Cardassia and Bajor. Troublemakers will not be tolerated and it will not stand. \n\nI hope this is the last I must speak to you on this subject.
STH_occupation_situation.31.name: [BajoranReligiousLeader.GetName] Executed
STH_occupation_situation.32.desc: Bajoran Citizens we know that there are those amongst you speaking out against our partnership, we offer this as a one-time warning, we will not tolerate this type of behaviour from this point forward religious gatherings will no longer be permitted. \n\nConsider yourself lucky that we were feeling benevolent.
STH_occupation_situation.32.name: A message from Cardassia
STH_occupation_situation.33.desc: Reports are coming in from the Kendra Province that a group of unarmed protesters were rounded up and arrested by Cardassian troops following a peaceful protest. \n\nThere has been no official word from our Cardassian Prefect on why they were arrested or where the protesters were taken following their arrest.
STH_occupation_situation.33.name: Pacifists Voice their Concern
STH_occupation_situation.34.desc: News coming in from Rakantha Province that there have been explosions at one of the Cardassian Military bases, the Cardassians are claiming that the matter was purely an internal incident and that there was no breach in security, however this has not stopped rumours circulating that a band of Bajoran youths manages to sneak into the base and ignite an ammunitions cache.
STH_occupation_situation.34.name: Explosions Heard
