STH_tomed.12.desc: Reports are coming in from our outposts on [tomed_incident_planet2.GetName] it appears an unknown force has launched an unprovoked attack on the planet. Starfleet Command has every reason to believe this is an attack from the Romulan Star Empire and has the intention of destroying the colony and all Federation citizens who live there.
STH_tomed.12.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.14.desc: Reports are coming in from our fleet, it appears they have made it into the Federation's territory and are preparing to launch their attack against the Federation. They will soon witness the full power of our forces.
STH_tomed.14.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.17.desc: The commander of our fleet has reported in and we are pleased to announce that they have delivered a crippling blow against the Federation. The world that mocked us has been rendered nothing but ashes. \n\nJustice has been delivered by the swift hand of the raptor.
STH_tomed.17.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.18.a: We must decide our next move.
STH_tomed.18.desc: Our fleets have successfully repelled the attack, defending our colonists and preventing a potentially devastating loss of lives. \n\nIt is clear from the wreckage that the attackers were from the Romulan Star Empire and that this is a clear act of open hostility towards us.
STH_tomed.18.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.19.desc: Word has reached the senate that our attack against the Federation has been repelled by their defence forces and the planned destruction of their colony was not completed. \n\nHowever our point has been made.
STH_tomed.19.name: The Tomed Incident
STH_tomed.2.c: This is not in our interests.
STH_tomed.2.desc:
Greetings Praetor, I am [tomed_romulan_leader.GetName] and I bring word from our Tal Shiar field operatives from within the United Federation of Planets 

Those Federation veruul appear to be on the cusp of a new technological breakthrough that could threaten our security. 

It may be the time that we remind those Federation cowards why they should fear our Imperial Might.
STH_tomed.2.name: Tal Shiar Report
STH_tomed.21.desc: Word has reached our ears that the Romulans have launched a cowardly attack against the United Federation of Planets, Many warriors will ask why this should concern us and we answer their recent actions at Khitomer have demonstrated a small degree of honour and this attack by those Romulan fools is cowardly and without honour. \n\nMeaning many of our warriors feel we should aid them and many are just hungry for war.
STH_tomed.21.name: Romulus attacks the Federation
STH_tomed.22.desc: Greetings President [Root.GetRulerName], I bring you aid from your friends in the Klingon Empire. We are aware of the cowardly attack against you by the Romulans and we offer you our warriors, who are eager to taste Romulan blood. \n\nGive us the word and we shall strike.
STH_tomed.22.name: Klingon aid
STH_tomed.23.desc: With the Klingon's at our side, we are in a strong position to respond to this attack from Romulus. There is a strong faction within Starfleet and the Federation that is strongly demanding we respond to this attack with the full force of Starfleet, bringing open war to the quadrant. \n\nYet there are calmer voices who urge that we resolve this with words and not torpedoes, if the Romulan's really wanted war, then surely they would have already declared war on us?
STH_tomed.23.name: Starfleet Security Meeting
STH_tomed.24.desc: We stand alone against the power of Romulus. There is a strong faction within Starfleet and the Federation that is strongly demanding we respond to this attack with the full force of Starfleet, bringing open war to the quadrant. \n\nYet there are calmer voices who urge that we resolve this with words and not torpedoes, if the Romulan's really wanted war, then surely they would have already declared war on us?
STH_tomed.24.name: Starfleet Security Meeting
STH_tomed.25.desc_01: We have been contacted by the Federation, backed by the Klingons, they wish to open talks with us in a bid to avoid a full-scale war. \n\nThey state that this is a chance to de-escalate the situation. This involvement from the Klingon Empire is an unwelcomed development...
STH_tomed.25.desc_02: We have been contacted by the Federation, they wish to open talks with us in a bid to avoid a full-scale war. \n\nThey state that this is a chance to de-escalate the situation.
STH_tomed.25.desc_03: We have been contacted by the Federation, backed by the Klingons, they wish to open talks with us in a bid to avoid a full-scale war. \n\nThey state that this is a chance to de-escalate the situation as well as discussing the future usage of the cloaking technology that they have been developing. This involvement from the Klingon Empire is an unwelcome development...
STH_tomed.25.desc_04: We have been contacted by the Federation, they wish to open talks with us in a bid to avoid a full-scale war. \n\nThey state that this is a chance to de-escalate the situation as well as to discuss the future usage of the cloaking technology that they have been developing.
STH_tomed.25.name: Incoming Message from Starfleet
STH_tomed.26.desc: The Federation has demonstrated a backbone that few in the senate believed they could possess and have declared war on us. \n\nWe shall crush them under the heel of the Romulan war machine.
STH_tomed.26.name: War has been declared
STH_tomed.27.desc: Our attempts to avoid war with Romulus have failed and they have declared war on us. We shall soon see who is the stronger force.
STH_tomed.27.name: War has been declared
STH_tomed.28.desc: We have received word from the Romulan Praetor, it appears they are willing to talk and have named [treaty_of_algeron_planet.GetName] as a suitable location to host this critical conference.
STH_tomed.28.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.29.a: Insist on the withdrawal of all troops.
STH_tomed.29.desc: As [algeron_negotiator.GetName] on board the [algeron_ship.GetName] arrives at the talks, both sides sit around the table and prepare to attempt a diplomatic solution.
STH_tomed.29.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.3.a: Provide our own demonstration of power.
STH_tomed.3.c: Speed up our cloaking tech research.
STH_tomed.3.desc: Sir! [tomed_federation_leader.GetName] reporting! Our sensors trained on the Romulan border have detected a massive build-up of the Romulan Navy, a clear demonstration of their military might, and a provocation against us. \n\nIn addition we have had multiple reports of Imperial Warbirds crossing the border and making incursions into our territory around [tomed_incident_planet2.GetName] which houses a number of vital research projects.
STH_tomed.3.name: Starfleet Security Report
STH_tomed.30.b: Agree, but insist on a cloaking ban.
STH_tomed.30.desc: As both sides sit down to talk, the Federation open the talks with a reasonable request for both sides to withdraw their ships to their own sides of the border. \n\nNow is our turn to propose a counter proposal.
STH_tomed.30.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.31.desc_01: The Romulan negotiator takes a moment and then agrees that both sides should de-escalate the situation and stand down their forces and withdraw behind the established borders. \n\nWith many now suffering on the worlds that were attacked, many are calling on reparations from Romulus to help the colony recover, however this is unlikely to be an agreeable option.
STH_tomed.31.desc_02:
The Romulan negotiator takes a moment and then agrees that both sides should de-escalate the situation and stand down their forces and withdraw behind the established borders. 

Additionally, the Romulans are demanding that we abandon our research into Cloaking and place a ban on all further research into this area. 

With many now suffering on the worlds that were attacked, many are calling on reparations from Romulus to help the colony recover, perhaps trading cloaking tech for aid helping our citizens is an agreeable trade off?
STH_tomed.31.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.32.desc_01: The Federation diplomats return to the meeting table, they agree to our terms and will implement a ban on all current and future research into Cloaking technology and abandon their current plans. However, as a compromise, they would like to see additional support and funds from Romulus to help recover the colony that was damaged during our attack.
STH_tomed.32.desc_02: The Federation diplomats return to the meeting table, they agree to our terms and will implement a ban on all current and future research into Cloaking technology and abandon their current plans. They state this is an act of good faith and a demonstration of their focus on peace.
STH_tomed.32.desc_03: The Federation diplomats return to the table with a new request. In order to establish peace, we need to undo the damage caused and as such we wish to receive reparations to help the damaged colony recover and repair the damaged caused in the attack.
STH_tomed.32.desc_04: The Federation diplomats return to the table their appearance is rigid, and their faces are tense. \n\nThey reject any proposal to ban cloaking and insist they will continue this line of research. Furthermore than are insisting that Romulus pays a form of reparation for the damage caused during the attack on their colony.
STH_tomed.32.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.33.desc: It appears the negotiations are over, as the Romulans storm away from the conference. Declaring that the talks are over and this will be settled by Phasers and Torpedoes.
STH_tomed.33.name: War declared
STH_tomed.34.desc_01: The Romulans return to the negotiating table and declare that the Romulan Star Empire will sign the Treaty of Algeron and as long as we abandon our plans to develop cloaking technology they will provide aid to our damaged colony.
STH_tomed.34.desc_02: The Romulans return to the negotiating table and declare that the Romulan Star Empire will sign the Treaty of Algeron and put an end to our research into Cloaking Technology.
STH_tomed.34.desc_03: The Romulans return to the negotiating table and declare that the Romulan Star Empire will sign the Treaty of Algeron and despite our refusal to put a ban on cloaking and insistence on receiving reparations, they are still willing to sign the treaty.
STH_tomed.34.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.35.desc: Today is a historic day between the United Federation of Planets and The Romulan Star Empire. The Treaty of Algeron has been signed creating a truce between our worlds and bringing both sides back from certain war. \n\nWe can only see what the future now holds.
STH_tomed.35.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.36.desc: It appears the Federation and Romulan Star Empire have signed a new treaty, which draws a line under their recent conflicts and maintains peace in the Alpha and Beta Quadrants for now.
STH_tomed.36.name: The Treaty of Algeron
STH_tomed.37.desc: The Romulan delegations storms out of the meeting room, loudly declaring they will not sign this 'sham of a treaty'. It looks like all chances of signing the treaty have now been lost and the Alpha and Beta Quadrants must hold their breath and await an uncertain future.
STH_tomed.37.name: Treaty Rejected
STH_tomed.39.desc: After the dedicated team of engineers, doctors and volunteers complete the many nights of hard work, much of the damage has been cleared and many parts of the core infrastructure have been returned to working order. Whilst many of the scars will remain long in the minds of the survivors at least much of the physical damage could be repaired.
STH_tomed.39.name: Colony receives help
STH_tomed.4.desc: It is clear the threat of directly challenging the Romulan Star Empire, was just too much for the cowards on [ufp_country_tomed.capital_scope] and they have backed down. The Romulan Star Empire remains supreme.
STH_tomed.4.name: Federation Backs Down
STH_tomed.40.desc: Our colony on [tomed_attacked_colony.GetName] has been attacked by a force of Romulan ships in an episode that has [tomed_attacked_colony.GetName] Incident. \n\nWork has already started to repair the colony and heal the wounds, we need a moment to gather our thoughts before we respond to this attack.
STH_tomed.40.name: Planet [tomed_attacked_colony.GetName] attacked
STH_tomed.41.b: It is not worth the risk.
STH_tomed.41.desc: Hi, I am [fed_science_leader.GetName] our science division has come to me with an exciting new proposal. Since we first engaged the Romulans and Klingon's we have been in fear and awe of their cloaking devices. \n\nOur scientists believe we have uncovered its secrets and with some investment could replicate this technology to bring us onto a level playing field. It is remiss of me to mention that proceeding with this line of research could be seen as an act of aggression towards the Romulan or Klingon Empires...
STH_tomed.41.name: Cloaking Technology
STH_tomed.42.desc: Our time and efforts have finally paid off, we have unlocked the secrets of cloaking technology, which is now available to complete via the research screen.
STH_tomed.42.name: Now they see us...
STH_tomed.5.a: This is not in our interests.
STH_tomed.6.b: We cannot confront them at this time.
STH_tomed.6.desc:
Our Tal Shiar operatives have completed their assignment and bring back troubling news. 

It seems that the Federation scientists have made a significant breakthrough in Cloaking Technology. This technology has given us an advantage over the Federation for many years now and the fact they are now actively progressing this line of research is a direct threat against the Empire! 

We cannot let this threat to our territory remain unchallenged.
STH_tomed.6.name: Federation Cloaking Technology
STH_tomed.7.desc: Our bid to uncover the secrets of the Federation's mystery technology has been unsuccessful and to make matters worse our operative has failed to return from Federation space. As any good operative should do, they will have ensured they were not taken alive.
STH_tomed.7.name: Tal Shiar Operation Failed
STH_tomed.8.c: We do not back down from bullies.
STH_tomed.8.desc:
Federation fools I bring you a threat from your superiors in the Romulan Star Empire. 

We know all about your attempt to duplicate our Cloaking Technology, which you no doubt stole from us in the first place. 

You will cease all efforts to duplicate our cloaking devices and withdraw your fleets from our borders or face the consequences of displeasing Romulus.
STH_tomed.8.name: Transmission From Romulus
STH_tomed.9.desc_01: It appears the Federation has more courage than we gave them credit for, and have refused to cower like the varuul that they are. \n\nOur monitoring stations are picking up increased activity along our borders and a build-up of Starfleet warships.
STH_tomed.9.desc_02: As expected the Federation has no backbone and they have crumpled beneath the threats we have made. \n\nOnce again the Romulan Star Empire has demonstrated its rightful place at the top of pack.
STH_tomed.9.name: Reponse from Starfleet Command
STH_traditions.50.d: There is no profit in this venture.
STH_traditions.50.desc: As rule of Acquisition 98 states, every man has his price... our agents have identified a number of key leaders from some foreign powers who might be worth testing how much latinum it would take for them to provide us with a few 'details' about their empire. This could be very profitable if one of our leaders could seal the deal.
STH_traditions.50.name: Every Man Has His Price
STH_traditions.51.d: There is no profit in this venture.
STH_traditions.51.desc: Now that we have selected who will lead the bribery bid, we must decide which of our relations we should target. Any information we can gleam from these foreign powers could be very profitable and will please our Nagus greatly.
STH_traditions.51.name: Every Man Has His Price
STH_traditions.52.d: There is no profit in this venture.
STH_traditions.52.desc: Now down to business, a bribe is only as successful as the amount of Latinum willing to be gambled. \n\nShould we gamble high or low in our attempt to obtain this intel.
STH_traditions.52.name: Every Man Has His Price
STH_traditions.54.desc: Never doubt the flow of the great material Continuum for it always delivers and once again it has provided. Our efforts to bribe the official in [bribe_country_choosen_intel.GetName] has been successful and [bribe_leader_choosen_intel.GetName] now has a wealth of information, we must put this to good use.
STH_traditions.54.name: Bribe Successful
STH_traditions.55.desc: Curses! Curses! Clearly [bribe_leader_choosen_intel.GetName] was not wise or capable of navigating the Great River and they have been grounded on its shores. \n\nAll this means that we have wasted our Latinum and failed to gain the precious intel on [bribe_country_choosen_intel.GetName].
STH_traditions.55.name: Bribe Failed
STH_traditions.60.desc: The spirit of Kahless lives in the heart of every Klingon warrior, no matter which house or faction. The Call of Kahless strengthens the bonds between the Great Houses and the Empire. Uniting the Klingons and making the empire powerful.
STH_traditions.60.name: The Spirit of Kahless
STH_traditions.70.a: Research the Excelsior II and Obena.
STH_traditions.70.desc: The great workhorse of our fleet the Excelsior is starting to show its age, it has served the fleet well for many years. Now the Starfleet Corp of Engineers has come to us with a proposal, the Excelsior II-Class and Obena-Class two major transformations of the Excelsior bringing the class into the modern era. \n\nCommissioning the research will allow us to build this new class of ship ready to take on the work of its venerable predecessor. Buildable as either the Excelsior II or the Obena.
STH_traditions.70.name: Time for the Next Great Experiment
STH_traditions.72.desc: The Deimos class was a 24th century Federation starship class, a Synth frigate and later pilot destroyer in Synth and Starfleet service in the 2380s and again from the 2410s decade. It was named for Deimos, the smaller moon of Mars, a planet in the Sol star system.
STH_traditions.72.name: The God of Fear
STH_traditions.80.desc: For too long our neighbours have converted our territory and claimed worlds that should be right belong to us. Our governing body has granted us the chance to right this wrong and place a casus belli against them.
STH_traditions.80.name: What is rightfully ours
STH_traditions.81.desc:
You have unlocked the Starfleet Ships of the Line Tradition tree, this grants access to technology which unlocks new models of Starfleet ships, loosely based off the series of the same name. These ships are optional extras which add a bit of extra flavour to your fleets. 

Each tradition unlocks a number of ships which then become available to add as research items via the Jupiter Station Research Megastructure (Science Station) once you reach the appropriate era. 

 Enjoy!
STH_traditions.81.name: Starfleet Ships of the Line
STH_traditions.85.desc: Section 31 stealth ships shared a design lineage that was based on the smaller intel science destroyer. Apart from a size difference, the S31 ship classes shared the design for warp nacelles, nacelle pylons, bridge module and deflector dish.
STH_traditions.85.name: Section 31
STH_traditions.86.desc: The Osler-type Federation starship was in use by Division 14 in the early 2380s. This ship type was smaller than California-class in size, and capable of atmospheric entry and landing. The Osler had a black hull with a darkened interior and was typically used in circumstances where a classically designed Federation ship would draw too much attention.
STH_traditions.86.name: Division 14
STH_tribbles.10.desc: In its weakened condition with systems failing due to Tribbles shorting out key systems the [tribble_ship.GetName] was unable to mount an adequet defence and was overwhelmed by its attackers and ultimately destroyed.
STH_tribbles.10.name: Ship destroyed!
STH_tribbles.11.desc: The trouble with Tribbles is they get everywhere including places they should not go. In their attempts to find more food to eat a number of the critters made their way into key systems aboard the [tribble_ship.GetName]. Their presence in these vital systems caused widespread damage as vital systems shorted out and overloaded, leaving the ship severely damaged. The good news is that power cores and Tribbles do not mix, leaving fewer animals for the crew to remove.
STH_tribbles.11.name: Ship damaged
STH_tribbles.12.desc: After the crews pulled double and triple shifts, the crew of [tribble_ship.GetName] managed to clear all Tribbles from within the ship and return life to normal.
STH_tribbles.12.name: A Tribble free zone
STH_tribbles.2.desc: [Root.GetLeaderTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName], we have had word from our colony on [tribble_invasion_planet.GetName] it appears the local Tribble population has begun breeding uncontrollable and has started destroying our harvests. It is predicted that if they continue to breed at this rate they will do significant damage to our food reserves on the planet. They may be cute and fluffy, but they most be stopped!
STH_tribbles.2.name: Troublesome Tribbles
STH_tribbles.21.desc: [Root.GetName]! One of our colonies on [tribble_crisis_planet.GetName] presents us with a glorious opertunity, the cursed Tribble patak have been sighted in the fields nearly the colony. We should organise a grand Tribble Hunt to round up and destroy every one of these beasts.\n\nGreat songs will be song of our victories here.
STH_tribbles.21.name: The Great Tribble Hunt
STH_tribbles.22.desc: Our weapons fire was unable to obliterate all of the accursed creatures and it seems like the fire has stirred up their bloodlust, resulting in increased breeding.\n\nWe now have a much larger population of Tribbles on the planet our estimates say it could take up to five years to fully clean up the planet.
STH_tribbles.22.name: Revenge of the Tribble
STH_tribbles.23.a: Songs will be song of this day.
STH_tribbles.23.desc: Klingon warriors search through the charred remains of the bombing site satisfied that no more Tribbles remain on the planet and although the damage was quite severe these Tribbles will think twice before challenging the Empire.
STH_tribbles.23.name: Roast Tribble for breakfast
STH_tribbles.24.desc: Songs will be song and legends created of the day the brave Klingon warriors did battle with their deadliest foe, the menace of the Tribble. With not a single fury patak left on the planet the colony is safe and honour satisfied. Now our warriors must sing and celebrate.
STH_tribbles.24.name: The hunt is complete
STH_tribbles.25.a: A grave day for the empire.
STH_tribbles.25.desc: Our hunties parties were not able to round up and destroy all of the acursed creatures and it seems like the hunt has stirred up their bloodlust resulting in increased breeding.
STH_tribbles.25.name: Revenge of the Tribble
STH_tribbles.26.desc: A great dishonour has befallen [kdf_klingon_tribble_leader.GetName] and the crew of [kdf_tribble_ship.GetName], a handful of Tribbles managed to get onboard the ship, within the hour there were thousands of Tribbles squeaking their way across the ship. The name of the [kdf_tribble_ship.GetName] will be laughed in the great halls of Qo'noS. The best [kdf_klingon_tribble_leader.GetName] can hope for is the swift removal of these beasts from the ship.
STH_tribbles.26.name: Tribbles loose on [kdf_tribble_ship.GetName]
STH_tribbles.27.desc: Despite clearing the Tribbles from [kdf_klingon_tribble_leader.GetHerHis] ship, [kdf_klingon_tribble_leader.GetName] could still hear the squeak, squeak, squeak of a Tribble, never in sight and always mocking them from some unseen location. As if haunted by the ghost of some evil Tribble.
STH_tribbles.27.name: The Klingon and the Tribble
STH_tribbles.28.desc: The sudden death of [kdf_klingon_tribble_leader2.GetName] has shocked many in the hall of warriors. Many whisper behind closed doors that they were plagued by the sound of an unseen tribble adversary for many years. It is unknown whether the constaint noise drove them to their death or whether something more nefarious occured & that tribble stalker took a deadly form of revenge.
STH_tribbles.28.name: Death by Tribble
STH_tribbles.3.b: Much honour has been won today.
STH_tribbles.3.desc: Our teams have successfully rounded up the Tribbles that were causing such a problem to the planet. Some say they were relocated to a safe harbour, others say they made excellent stuffing, others decided it was best not to ask. Either way the problem has been resolved.
STH_tribbles.3.name: No harm, no Tribble
STH_tribbles.4.desc: [Root.GetLeaderTitle]! The Tribbles fought back, no matter how many of the beasts we removed there were always more. Our teams were unable to remove the Tribble population from the planet and what is worse they continue to breed and their numbers are now truly out of control! We now have limited options, we can attempt to cordon off the area and starve them out or take more drastic measures.
STH_tribbles.4.name: The Trouble with Tribbles
STH_tribbles.5.desc: Our troops fought valiantly and not a Tribble remains on the planet to do any more harm. This is a mighty day for [Root.GetName] for we have defeated a great foe.
STH_tribbles.5.name: No harm, no Tribble
STH_tribbles.6.desc: Whatever we did, it did not work. It only seems to have encouraged the Tribbles to breed in greater numbers. They now cover great areas and can be found in fields, replicators and munching on any food they can find! Our only option now is to ride out this wave of Tribbles and wait for them to run out of food.
STH_tribbles.6.name: Tribbles, Tribbles, Tribbles!
STH_tribbles.7.desc: Captain! It appears that one of our crew members had a moment of madness and brought a single Tribble back aboard the [tribble_ship.GetName] and now the ship is over run by the little demons.\n\nCrew members are desperately pulling Tribbles out of the replicators, the warp core matrix and even the waste services. It won't be long before the whole ship has been tribbled!
STH_tribbles.7.name: Tribbles on the loose!
STH_tribbles.8.desc_01: Just as the situation could not get worse for [leader_tribble.GetName] and the crew of [tribble_ship.GetName] scans picked up an inbound raiding party from a gang of mercenaries looking for an easy target. With main weapons at reduced power and shields offline due to 'Tribbles in the wiring' the odds of defending the ship are severely impacted.\n\n[leader_tribble.GetName] calls for an emergency meeting, one crazy suggestion that just might work is attaching explosives to the dead Tribbles and firing them at the incoming ships, effectively creating a barrage of Tribble Missles.
STH_tribbles.8.desc_02: Just as the situation could not get worse for [leader_tribble.GetName] and the crew of [tribble_ship.GetName] scans picked up an inbound raiding party from a gang of mercenaries looking for an easy target. With main weapons at reduced power and shields offline due to 'Tribbles in the wiring' the odds of defending the ship are severely impacted
STH_tribbles.8.name: Vessel under attack!
STH_tribbles.9.a: Get those Tribbles off the ship!
STH_tribbles.9.desc_01: The barrage of exploding Tribbles managed to hit a crucial system on the lead mercenary ship causing massive power failures throughout their primary systems. Shocked and embarrassed they hastily call off their attack and retreat.\n\nLeaving the crew time to deal with their unwanted guests.
STH_tribbles.9.desc_02: A well aimed and lucky burst of fire from [tribble_ship.GetName] was able to hit a crucial system on one of the inbound attackers. Convinced that their target was not as easy as hoped they beat a quick retreat. Leaving the crew time to deal with their unwanted guests.
STH_tribbles.9.name: Victory!
STH_trill_flavour.1.desc:
A gentle bob and weave we make, down the river light.
Reeds and trees and swarms of bees, adventure is in sight.
Hearken me my friend you'll see, lest suffr'n be our plight.

Caster Tagin's fantasy tale Down the River Light has been released on [Root.Capital.GetName] to widespread acclaim, with particular appeal to young adults. Caster claims the work will eventually cover at least sixteen volumes.
STH_trill_flavour.1.name: Down the River Light
STH_trill_mechanics.10.a: What role does the Commission play?
STH_trill_mechanics.10.b: Review all Symbionts available within the caves.
STH_trill_mechanics.10.c: Attempt to recruit a Symbiont from the pools.
STH_trill_mechanics.10.desc_01: Greetings [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], I am Guardian [TSCleader.GetName] from the Trill Symbiosis Commission. The Great Caves of Mak'ala await your visit to consult with the Symbiots or to consider a joining between host and Symbiont.\n\nOur Guardians await your visit.
STH_trill_mechanics.10.desc_02: Greetings offworlder, we welcome your visit to Trill. I am [TSCleader.GetName] a Guardian from the Caves of Mak'ala, we tend to the Trill Symbionts whilst they empart their knowledge and wisdom upon us.\n\nWhat is it we can do for you today?
STH_trill_mechanics.10.name: Trill Symbiosis Commission
STH_trill_mechanics.100.b: Search for any available Trill hosts.
STH_trill_mechanics.100.desc_01: The death of any leader is always a tragedy, however [deadLeaderTemp.GetName] was a joined trill and there is now grave concern for the symbiont they carried. The options are limited, we can attempt to return the Symbiont back to the Symbiont Pools or we can search the fleet for a suitable Trill candiate to transfer the [symbiontLeader.GetName] symbiont to.
STH_trill_mechanics.100.desc_02: Trill Symbionts live long lives, but still the loss of their knowledge and experience is a tragic event and so is the case with the death of [deadLeaderTemp.GetName] symbiont. However time grows short for [hostLeader.GetName] who would be unable to survive without a new symbiont.
STH_trill_mechanics.100.name: A joined Trill has died
STH_trill_mechanics.101.desc: Our surgeons have confirmed that the joining has been completed successfully.\n\n[new_trill_leader.GetName] will continue the legacy as the new host.
STH_trill_mechanics.101.name: Emergency Joining a Success
STH_trill_mechanics.102.desc: Not every attempt to join is successful and emergency processes are full of risks, regrettably both the host and now the symbiont [from.GetName] have now died.
STH_trill_mechanics.102.name: Death of a Symbiont
STH_trill_mechanics.103.desc: Not every attempt to join is successful and emergency processes are full of risks, regrettably both the symbiont and now the host [from.GetName] have died.
STH_trill_mechanics.103.name: Death of a Host
STH_trill_mechanics.105.desc: The Trill Symbiosis Commission have managed to identify a replacement host, who was able to make it to the operating table in time to receive the symbiont. The new pairing will now be known as [replacement_host.GetFirstName] [From.GetName].
STH_trill_mechanics.105.name: Joining Successful
STH_trill_mechanics.106.desc: In a fortunate turn of events, there was an available and willing Trill in the versinity of the Symbiont, the joining was successful and although they will need time to adapt to a process they were not prepared for.
STH_trill_mechanics.106.name: Host found within [Root.GetName]
STH_trill_mechanics.11.desc:
The following Trill Symbionts are dwelling within the pools inside the caves.

[not_joined_1.GetName].
[not_joined_2.GetName].
[not_joined_3.GetName].
[not_joined_4.GetName].

[not_joined_5.GetName].

[not_joined_6.GetName].
[not_joined_7.GetName].
[not_joined_8.GetName].
[not_joined_9.GetName].
[not_joined_10.GetName]. 

We are also aware that the following Symbionts are currently joined: 

[joined_sym_1.GetName].
[joined_sym_2.GetName].
[joined_sym_3.GetName].
[joined_sym_4.GetName].
[joined_sym_5.GetName].
STH_trill_mechanics.11.name: Trill Symbiosis Commission
STH_trill_mechanics.15.a: Recruit a new Symbiont from the Pools.
STH_trill_mechanics.15.desc: The Symbionts rarely venture from their pools, but when they are ready to join with a host to share their experience and build new memories and lives, they move to the surface and begin the process of selecting an appropriate host to create a new merging.
STH_trill_mechanics.15.name: Recruit a new Symbiont from the Pools
STH_trill_mechanics.16.desc: The Symbionts have finished conveining and the [symbiont_1.GetName] Symbiont has emerged ready to find a new host and make new memories. The Symbiont will remain in the pool with its kin until it is ready to take a host.
STH_trill_mechanics.16.name: A Symbiont has emerged from the pools
STH_trill_mechanics.2.a.tooltip: The Commission is available via the Contacts menu and allows the recruiting and joining of Trill hosts and Symbionts.
STH_trill_mechanics.2.desc: Ever since the first marsupial Trill was joined with the Trill Symbiont, both species have lived in unison together, forming a partnership that has become the cornerstone of Trill society. The Trill Symbiosis Commission was setup to ensure that this harmony continues, they asses initiates and support the Guardians who tend to the Symbionts in their natural environment.
STH_trill_mechanics.2.name: Trill Symbiosis
STH_trill_mechanics.20.desc_01: This is a pinnacle moment in Trill initates life, a moment they would have been working towards their whole life. The joining of Trill and Host results in a unique individual a mixture of host, symbiont and the experiences of those who came before. The process is not without risk, and there is always the chance the process will not work. However this is the moment so many on Trill dream of. Available Symbionts are listed below.
STH_trill_mechanics.20.desc_02: Welcome to the Trill joining process [Root.GetName] a process where any member of Trill society, whether serving the republic or as part of a wider service can return to Trill and attempt to compete the initiate process and host a Trill Symbiont. The journey starts when a Symbiont from the pools of Mak'ala is selected.
STH_trill_mechanics.20.name: The Trill Joining
STH_trill_mechanics.21.name: The Trill Joining
STH_trill_mechanics.22.desc: The unthinkable has happened, despite all the preparation and tests the joining process was not successful and the joining did not happen correctly. Unfortunately the host died, but the Symbiont was saved, but will be traumatised by the process.\n\nThis is a sad day for all Trill.
STH_trill_mechanics.22.name: Host died during the joining
STH_trill_mechanics.23.desc: A day to celebrate, the joining of the Trill Host [trill_host.GetFirstName] and Trill Symbiont [trill_symbiont.GetName] has been a success, the new indiviual will now be known as [fromfrom.GetFirstName] [Root.From.GetName] and they will continue the legacy of the previous hosts.
STH_trill_mechanics.23.name: A new lifetime of memories begins
STH_twisted_event.1.a: Try to break loose from the phenomenon.
STH_twisted_event.1.desc: Our ship has discovered a strange space phenomenon, which surrounds the ship before it has time to do anything...
STH_twisted_event.1.name: A mysterious phenomenon appears
STH_twisted_event.2.a: Transfer all available power to the engines!
STH_twisted_event.2.b: As our attempt to break free wasn`t successful it might be best to stay put.
STH_twisted_event.2.desc: Our attempts to break free from the phenomenon that surrounds us have been unsuccessful as the phenomenon seems to have a tight grip of the ship. And what is worse it seems that the phenomenon has started to spread form outside of the ship to the inside severely distorting the parts of the ship it comes into contact with.
STH_twisted_event.2.name: We are stuck!
STH_twisted_event.3.a: While the odds of escaping aren`t good, transfer all power to the engines!
STH_twisted_event.3.b: Another option could be to just try and wait out the phenomenon.
STH_twisted_event.3.desc: While studying the phenomenon has yielded no viable alternative to break free from it. It seems that the phenomenon has now moved to inside the ship too and is severely distorting the parts of the ship it comes into contact with. Its grip of the ships hull in those parts also seems very strong. Making attempts to break free dangerous.
STH_twisted_event.3.name: We are stuck!
STH_twisted_event.4.desc: Pushing the engines to their absolute max to escape the phenomenon produces so much stress to the ship`s hull that the ship is ripped apart and totally destroyed. Killing everyone on board.
STH_twisted_event.4.name: Ship lost
STH_twisted_event.5.desc: Pushing the engines to their maximum causes severe stress to the ship's hull and it starts to break in areas mostly affected by the phenomenon, but finally the ship is able to break free and continue its journey in open space.
STH_twisted_event.5.name: Ship breaks free
STH_twisted_event.6.a: Almost like someone tried to communicate with us.
STH_twisted_event.6.desc: After filling the entire ship and distorting every part of it, the strange phenomenon seems to be gone as quickly as it first appeared. Later however it is discovered that more than 20 million gigaquads of data has been left into the ship's computers, while the entire ship's database has also been downloaded.
STH_twisted_event.6.name: The phenomenon is gone
STH_undine_anomaly.1.a: Attempt to break free of the field.
STH_undine_anomaly.1.desc: One of our Bio-Vessels the [undine_ship_1.GetName] has been trapped in a strange field whilst investigating an unusual phenomenon, the phenomenon enveloped our ship before it could react or retreat.\n\nIt is unlike anything we have encountered before.
STH_undine_anomaly.1.name: A disturbance in the Fluidic Realm
STH_undine_anomaly.2.b: All stop, we shall wait this out!
STH_undine_anomaly.2.desc: All attempts to use the engines to break free of the fields grip have proved ineffective. Our crews are reporting that the field has breached our ships outer membranes and is encroaching on the ships interior warping and distorting everything in touches.
STH_undine_anomaly.2.name: A disturbance in the Fluidic Realm
STH_undine_anomaly.3.a: Divert all power to the engines!
STH_undine_anomaly.3.desc: Studying the field has revealed no means of escape, reports from our lower decks has confirmed that the mysterious field has breached the outer membrane of our vessel and is now within our ship distorting everything it comes in contact with.
STH_undine_anomaly.3.name: A disturbance in the Fluidic Realm
STH_undine_anomaly.4.desc: Pushing the bio-drives beyond their tolerances in a bit to escape the phenomenon produces so much stress to the ship's hull that the ship is ripped apart with the lost of all Undine Warriors onboard.
STH_undine_anomaly.4.name: Bio Ship Lost!
STH_undine_anomaly.5.desc: Pushing the bio-drives over their tolerances causes severe stress to the ship's hull and it starts to break in areas mostly affected by the phenomenon, but finally the ship is able to break free after sustaining damage, but is free to continue it's mission.
STH_undine_anomaly.5.name: Ship breaks free
STH_undine_anomaly.6.a: Almost like someone tried to communicate with us.
STH_undine_anomaly.6.desc: The strange field completely absorbs , the strange phenomenon seems to be gone as quickly as it first appeared. Later however it is discovered that more than 20 million gigaquads of data has been left into the ship's computers, while the entire ship's database has also been downloaded.
STH_undine_anomaly.6.name: The phenomenon vanishes
STH_undine_crisis.1.desc: The paths to perfection are many, but one so far untaken lies within Fluidic Space. The [Root.GetName] has only limited data about this strange domain, at parallel yet wholly encircled by our own universe. \n\nWith sufficient time and effort, the Collective may be able to open a portal to Fluidic Space and learn the secrets within... whatever they may be.
STH_undine_crisis.1.name: Fluidic Space
STH_undine_crisis.100.name: INCOMING TRANSMISSION
STH_undine_crisis.2.desc: Long range scans have detected a series of odd ship deployments in Borg space. \n\nSo far our tacticians have been unable to determine the intent behind the Collective's actions.
STH_undine_crisis.2.name: Strange Readings
STH_undine_crisis.3.desc: Research into opening a portal to Fluidic Space continues, however the resource requirements have grown substantially. Progress requires a large investment from the Collective's energy reserves.
STH_undine_crisis.3.name: Fluidic Space
STH_undine_crisis.4.desc: The previously detected unusual activity in Borg space appears to be continuing, with particle emissions consistent with the formation of artificial singularities being observed increasingly often.
STH_undine_crisis.4.name: Strange Readings
STH_undine_crisis.5.desc_01: Efforts to open a portal into Fluidic Space have been successful. The Collective now has the means to enter the domain at will. \n\nInitial sensor telemetry suggests evidence of intelligent life, however this is not certain. Several options are available.
STH_undine_crisis.5.desc_02:
Following previous forays into Fluidic Space, the Collective has amassed a wealth of knowledge about the domain, as well as recovered valuable biological samples for assimilation. 

Continued exploration could return even more data. 

A new special project has been added to your mission log.
STH_undine_crisis.5.name: Fluidic Space
STH_undine_crisis.511.desc:
Distinctiveness: Fluidic Space Cell Sample
Status: Resistant

Microbial samples recovered from Fluidic Space continue to resist assimilation. Further analysis is required to determine necessary adaptations. Commit additional research nodes to this task.
STH_undine_crisis.511.name: Assimilation Failure
STH_undine_crisis.512.desc:
Distinctiveness: Fluidic Space Cell Sample
Status: Assimilated

Microbial samples recovered from Fluidic Space have been assimilated. Integration of acquired distinctiveness throughout the Collective is successful. Application of isomorphic cell properties can be used to enhance nanoprobe-based construction tasks.
STH_undine_crisis.512.name: Assimilation Success
STH_undine_crisis.521.desc:
Distinctiveness: Fluidic Space Reef Sample
Status: Resistant

Antibody immune response from sample preventing assimilation. All nanoprobes destroyed upon invasion of host body. Further analysis is required to determine necessary adaptations. Commit additional research nodes to this task.
STH_undine_crisis.521.name: Assimilation Failure
STH_undine_crisis.522.desc:
Distinctiveness: Fluidic Space Reef Sample
Status: Assimilated

Successful assimilation of Fluidic Space macroorganism confirmed. Distributing genetic data. Configuring large-scale maturation chambers. Deployment of assimilated organic structures will augment expansion of Collective-controlled spatial grids.

You may now freely construct Biosynthetic Cores, which may be upgraded into Subcomplexes.
STH_undine_crisis.522.name: Assimilation Success
STH_undine_crisis.531.desc: Further assimilation of samples brought back from Fluidic Space has yielded only minor breakthroughs in the Collective's current areas of research.
STH_undine_crisis.531.name: Minor Breakthrough
STH_undine_crisis.532.desc: Further assimilation of samples brought back from Fluidic Space has yielded moderate breakthroughs in the Collective's current areas of research.
STH_undine_crisis.532.name: Moderate Breakthrough
STH_undine_crisis.533.desc: Further assimilation of samples brought back from Fluidic Space has yielded significant breakthroughs in the Collective's current areas of research.
STH_undine_crisis.533.name: Major Breakthrough
STH_undine_crisis.6.desc: Several large fleets of vessels have been detected emerging from singularities across Borg space. Energy bursts consistent with high yield subspace munitions have been observed, suggesting ongoing combat between these invaders and the Borg.
STH_undine_crisis.6.name: Fluidic Invasion
STH_undine_crisis.7.desc: Several large fleets of vessels have been detected emerging from singularities across [Root.GetSpeciesAdj] space. These singularities are consistent with the portals that the Collective has opened to Fluidic Space. \n\nIt is clear now that signs of intelligent life detected within Fluidic Space were accurate. Analysis nodes indicate a high probability of hostility.
STH_undine_crisis.7.name: Fluidic Invasion
STH_undine_mechanics.1007.desc: The [LastKilledCountryName] have been destroyed, and with their loss the galaxy has been made safer.\n\nPicking through the ruins of the [LastKilledCountryName]'s ships and worlds will take months, but the potential for technological gain is significant.
STH_undine_mechanics.1007.name: The End of the Undine
STH_undine_mechanics.2001.desc: The Undine infestation has spread and converted another part of the population on [infested_planet.GetName] after they were infected with the Undine infestation virus.
STH_undine_mechanics.2001.name: Pop Converted on [infested_planet.GetName]
STH_undine_mechanics.3.desc: We will defend our realm and cleanse yours in the process.
STH_undine_mechanics.3.name: Incoming Transmission
STH_undine_mechanics.501.desc_1: The infestation centre has begun the process of infecting the planets ecosystem with the Fluidic virus, which will begin slowly converting this world, which was called [infesting_planet.GetName], once the process is complete the world will not be recognisable and will be the perfect home for our warriors.\n\nOur bio-sculptures predict the planet is no more than 0-25% of the way through the process.
STH_undine_mechanics.501.desc_2: The infestation process is starting to take a hold of [infesting_planet.GetName], as plants, trees and other organic structures slowly succumb to the effects as their genetic structures are slowly being re-written by our virus.\n\nOur bio-sculptures predict the planet is about 50% of the way through the process.
STH_undine_mechanics.501.desc_3: The infestation process is now firmly established on [infesting_planet.GetName], the majority of the natural environment has been converted, whilst our warriors and bio-sculpture look to dismantle the infrastructure. Our bio-sculptures predict the planet is now over 75% complete.
STH_undine_mechanics.501.desc_4: [infesting_planet.GetName] is now all but unrecognisable from the world it was before. We have remade it in our image and converted this alien planet into a world that we can call home.\n\nOur conquest of this realm is that much closer. The process will complete very shortly.
STH_undine_mechanics.501.name: Planetary Infestation
STH_undine_mechanics.511.desc: Our bio-engineers have made good progress in converting this alien world into a bio ecosystem. It appears that natural genetic makeup of the lifeforms that inhabit this world are no match for our biological superiority.
STH_undine_mechanics.512.desc: It seems the native inhabitants of [undine_infestation_target.GetName] have staged a resistance to our efforts to convert this world. They have sabotaged a number of facilities and there have been casualties on both sides. This will undoubtably slow any progress being made in converting this planet.
STH_undine_mechanics.512.name: Planetary Infestation
STH_undine_mechanics.521.desc: The report from our infestation teams working to convert [undine_infestation_target.GetName] is progressing well. We have successfully quelled any ongoing resistance from the locals and are now rounding them up to work as slaves for us.
STH_undine_mechanics.521.name: Planetary Infestation
STH_undine_mechanics.522.desc: We have hit a stumbling block in our efforts to infest this planet, it appears that some of the local wildlife has begun to develop a resistance to our virus, which is slowing progress.
STH_undine_mechanics.522.name: Planetary Infestation
STH_undine_mechanics.530.a: The planet is submitting to us.
STH_undine_mechanics.530.desc: [infested_planet_complete.GetName] is now a Proto-Bio world at a cost to the native inhabitants and the previous wildlife. The new world is a work of art and fit for habitation for our Vanguard forces and can be used as a staging ground for our future ventures outside of Fluidic Space.\n\nOur teams will continue to subdue the planet and convert it to suit our needs.
STH_undine_mechanics.530.name: Planetary Infestation
STH_undine_mechanics.541.desc: Our bio-engineers have completed construction on a number of new facilities to further our hold on our new world, additionally any local rebellion has been crushed once and for all.
STH_undine_mechanics.541.name: Planetary Infestation
STH_undine_mechanics.542.desc: There has been further aggressive activity against our infestation teams, an armed party in specially adapted suits beamed to the planets surface and detonate several tricobalt devices in a bid to derail our efforts.\n\nOur teams believe they can still complete the assignment, but this has set back our timescales.
STH_undine_mechanics.550.a: This world is fit for us.
STH_undine_mechanics.550.desc: Our masterpiece has been completed, the once impure world known as [undine_infestation_complete.GetName] has been made pure and fit for our warriors to thrive on. \n\nThis is but the next step in our efforts to wipe the impure from the galaxy.
STH_undine_mechanics.550.name: Planet Converted
STH_undine_mechanics.551.desc: Our efforts to take new worlds from the impure has given us the planet [undine_captured.GetName], this world is filthy and unfit for our habitation.\n\nWe may take the decision to hasten the process of infesting and converting this world to a magnificent Bio-World once a conversion centre has been built. Alternatively we could terraform the planet in a slower but more certain process.
STH_undine_mechanics.551.name: A potential new world for us to infest
STH_undine_mechanics.552.desc: Our infesters have started work converting the worthless planetoid [undine_infest_planet.GetName] into a Proto Bio World that we can call home amongst these strange stars. \n\nThe process will take time and our infesters will provide updates on the progress.
STH_undine_mechanics.552.name: Planet Infestation Underway
STH_undine_mechanics.553.desc: Our infesters have started work converting the proto-bio [undine_convert_planet.GetName] into a glorious Bio World fit for our warriors. \n\nThe process will take time and our infesters will provide updates on the progress.
STH_undine_mechanics.553.name: Planet Conversion Underway
STH_undine_mechanics.554.desc: Our infesters have completed the conversion process on the planet [undine_conversionn_complete.GetName] bringing our goal of spreading our infestation across the galaxy one step closer.
STH_undine_mechanics.554.name: A new Bio-World Forged
STH_undine_mechanics.600.desc:
Fluidic Space is wild and dangerous, and on certain occassions the deadly pathogens that form bound together to form a Macro-Virus. 

Our sensors have detected such a virus heading towards [Root.GetName] intent on feeding on our world. 

It must be stopped.
STH_undine_mechanics.600.name: Deadly Macro-Virus destined for [Root.GetName]
STH_undine_mechanics.602.desc: We have destroyed the Virus before it can reach our colony, saving the lives of many of our Undine warriors and once again proving we are masters of our realm.
STH_undine_mechanics.602.name: Macro-Virus Destroyed
STH_undine_mechanics.603.desc: The Virus has reached [Root.GetName] and the death toll will be high even with our supreme biology. The virus will reproduce and create more deadly viruses creating a plague within our worlds. \n\nWe must endure.
STH_undine_mechanics.603.name: Macro-Virus Reaches [Root.GetName]
STH_undine_story.10.a: Research the tech and recolonise Schima.
STH_undine_story.10.desc: Our scientists have come back with some exciting information having studied the Advendinus corpses. It appears that a substance they secrete has the ability to manipulate large collections of biomass and produce a planetary surface that could sustain an Undine colony.\n\nWe should conclude the research on this new technology and use it on the damaged world of Schima, which was abandoned and left to revert when the great divide occured.
STH_undine_story.10.name: New bio-sculpting method found
STH_undine_story.1000.desc: The Undine, later known as Species 8472 to the Borg, offer a unique experience and story within the New Horizons universe. Since the great divide over biological perfection split the Undine into two factions, with the Andine who believe that purity is secondary to knowledge leaving to parts unknown, leaving the Undine content with mastering their own domain until disturbed by the races of the Milky Way. This threat to their biological superiority is too much for them to take. Their focus, for now, is purely on the desire to purge the galaxy of all those they deem weak and as all Undine know... the weak shall perish!
STH_undine_story.1000.name: Masters of Fluidic Space
STH_undine_story.11.desc: Our colony pods have found many wonders on the planet Schima that were abandoned when the pathetic Andine left our hive perfection. One of which is an ancient temple, which appears to have been where the leaders of the Andine practiced their impure biological practices and where ultimately they staged their desertion of our domain.
STH_undine_story.11.name: Planet Schima recovered
STH_undine_story.2.desc: Our scouting pods have picked up a new lifeform dwelling within our domain, these parasitic life forms are a threat to our biological mastery of our space as they appear to feed on the biomass and disrupting the balance of the fluid.\n\nThis insult to our dominance cannot stand.
STH_undine_story.2.name: Parasitic Lifeforms detected
STH_undine_story.20.desc: Our archaeologists on Schima have been investigating the ruined temple and believe there may be key traces of where the Andine fled to.\n\nTheir continued existence has long been both a threat and insult to our biology and the call to investigate this temple and uncover any leads on their destination cannot be ignored. Our military leaders insist that we divert a science team to speed up the investigations within this temple.
STH_undine_story.20.name: Ancient Undine Temple
STH_undine_story.2000.b: We have better things to do.
STH_undine_story.2000.desc: Our scouts have sensed a strange vessel travelling slowly through Fluidic Space, it has intruded on our space and we should investigate to ensure that the intretity of our realm is not at risk.
STH_undine_story.2000.name: Strange Ship sighted
STH_undine_story.2001.desc: The strange ship appears to be organic in nature, the ship does not seem to have any hostile intentions, however it is worth a further look as we could possibly gain a further biological edge from the beast.
STH_undine_story.2001.name: Strange Ship sighted
STH_undine_story.2002.desc: The strange beast refer to itself as the Gomtuu and is more creature than ship. It is hard to determine its intentions, but it does appear to possess psychic abilities as well as a great many years of travel. \n\nOne of our Undine warriors should be able to easily open communications with the creature.
STH_undine_story.2002.name: Gomtuu Found
STH_undine_story.2003.a: Why do you tresspass in our domain?
STH_undine_story.2003.desc: << Psychic Message >> Greetings, I am Gomtuu, I travel the interdimensional realms seeking knowledge and experiences. I am returning to my mate, who I have not seen in many thousands of years. I greet you with peaceful intentions. << Psychic Message >>
STH_undine_story.2003.name: Communication from Gomtuu
STH_undine_story.2004.b: It is not worth our interest.
STH_undine_story.2004.desc: This strange creature pocesses much knowledge and power, power which could rightlyfully be ours. If it cannot hold onto its power, then it must be considered weak. We should attack it and take its gifts for the Undine.\n\nLetting it go would be a sign of weakness on our part.
STH_undine_story.2004.name: Gomtuu Found
STH_undine_story.2005.desc: Having chosen to leave the creature in peace, the Gomtuu begins its slow journey onwards to find its mate. As a parting gift the creature bestows a small gift on the Undine and adds the story to its long memory.
STH_undine_story.2005.name: Gomtuu Departs
STH_undine_story.2006.b: The power to spread our infestation far and wide.
STH_undine_story.2006.desc: The creature put up a worthy fight, but finally sucummed to the power of our forces. As its lights begin to fade our powerful telepaths search its mind for hidden powers and technologies.\n\nWe have the chance to pull one new tech from its mind before it passes. What shall it be?
STH_undine_story.2006.e: Take relic from the creatures hull.
STH_undine_story.2006.name: Gomtuu defeated
STH_undine_story.21.desc:
After pulling apart and investigating every inch of the temple our investigators have discovered a number of partially erased data nodes containing the evacuation and destination of the Andine fleet.

A remote sector which they have named 'Illumindine', a direct reference to their views on the mastery of the mind over biological purity.

Now that their home has been discovered we can plot a course and prepare their demise.
STH_undine_story.21.name: Andine homeworld located
STH_undine_story.22.desc_01: So Undine fools, you have found us, it matters not for we have mastered much new technology and powers since freed from your oppressive and narrow minded ways.
STH_undine_story.22.desc_02: Did you think your clumsy attempts to find us would go unnoticed? We will no longer be afraid of you and we launch attacks against you. There can only be one master of the Fluidic Realm.
STH_undine_story.22.name: Incoming message
STH_undine_story.23.desc: A scism that was formed many years ago between two different idealogies, with both sides determined to be the master species within the Fluidic Realm. The Undine and Andine will finally settle their differences and only one side can be declared the victory.\n\nAs the ancient Undine saying goes, the weak shall perish.
STH_undine_story.23.name: Undine Civil War
STH_undine_story.24.desc: The Andine were able to wield some impressive technology against us, ships and weapons that would benefit our evolution. Now that they have been defeated we should send a team to their world to harvest what is ours by right of conquest. What is clear is that we are now the true and undisputed masters of fluidic space.
STH_undine_story.24.name: Victory
STH_undine_story.2501.desc: Our scans of the Fluidic Space have picked up several packs of parasitic life making their way across our space.\n\nThey represent a challenge to our dominance and must be destroyed before they can cause too much harm.
STH_undine_story.2501.name: Fluidic Parasites detected
STH_undine_story.2502.a: Our space is clean once again.
STH_undine_story.2502.desc: We have defeated this latest infestation into our domain, we must remain vigilant for further attacks.
STH_undine_story.2502.name: Fluidic Parasites destroyed
STH_undine_story.30.desc: Dispite pulling the temple apart our investigators were unable to uncover any evidence of the Andine retreat or where those cowards could be hiding now. This is disapointing and we must commune with the hive masters to reflect on this failure.
STH_undine_story.30.name: No information found
STH_undine_story.31.desc_01: Our investigations into the location of the Andine appears to have triggered a response from our long lost kin. They have correctly perceived us as a threat and have launched a preemptive strike against our systems.\n\nWe must prepare our defences.
STH_undine_story.31.desc_02: It appears we were foolish to ignore the Andine threat, it seems those cowards have launched a sneak attack against our hives. However in doing so the fools have revealed the location of their homeworld.
STH_undine_story.31.name: Under Attack!
STH_undine_story.33.desc: Our investigators followed through several leads on the location of the Andine, but all leads proved to be dead ends. It seems that the Andine made it away from our homeworld, but were unable to establish a new home. Their flight from our world appears to have been as foolish as believed. The Andine are no more and we can rightfully claim the title as masters of our domain.
STH_undine_story.33.name: No trace of the Andine found
STH_undine_story.34.desc: One of our long lost ancient data nodes has been uncovered amongst the spawning points on the enemy homeworld. Clearly stolen from our homeworld when they fled, these data nodes contain the genetic key to spawn powerful new ships. This is truly a fantastic find.
STH_undine_story.34.name: Ancient Relic Found
STH_undine_story.35.desc: The bio-packet has been activated and introduced to the biomatter pools on the homeworld. We shall see what is spawned from the rapidly growing and developing cocoon.
STH_undine_story.35.name: Bio-Packet Activated
STH_undine_story.36.desc: Our engineer drones wait with anticipation to see what emerges from the pod. Two cleamingand brand new Behemoth warships have been birthed from the pod. They will make a magnificent additions to our fleet.
STH_undine_story.36.name: A new Behemoth Spawned
STH_undine_story.37.desc: Our engineer drones wait with anticipation to see what emerges from the pod. Two cleamingand brand new Infester warships have been birthed from the pod. They will make a magnificent additions to our fleet. Our enemies will learn to fear us.
STH_undine_story.37.name: A new Infester Spawned
STH_undine_story.38.desc: Terrible news!\n\nIt seems our usage of the bio-node has exhausted its limited resources and the resulting creation it was making has been destroyed taking the node with it. Meaning that we have now lost our ability to use this artifact.
STH_undine_story.38.name: Data pod destroyed
STH_undine_story.4.desc: With the successful removal of the lifeforms our scientists have dubbed the 'Advendinus Dwellers' we are once again the dominant species and balance has been restored. We have returned the carcasses of the creatures to our labs for further study.
STH_undine_story.4.name: Lifeforms eradicated
STH_undine_story.50.desc: [Root.GetRulerTitle] [Root.GetRulerName], we have detected an unusual ripples in the fluidic currents coming from [fluidic_breach_1.GetName]. Our bio-scientists suspect this could be a breach in the fluidic barrior, but are unclear what could have caused such an occurance. We must investigate immediately.
STH_undine_story.50.name: Fluidic Rift Detected
STH_undine_story.5000.desc: Our Bio-Chemists have successfully created a new breach in the fluidic barrior, we now need a target destination in which we can spawn a linking rift into. Allowing us to travel between our domain and the domain of the unclean.\n\nOur scouts have picked out the following systems that we could breach into...
STH_undine_story.5000.name: Fluidic Breach Created
STH_undine_story.51.desc:
The Bioship sent to investigate the rift has come back with a number of concerning findings. A strange rift has been found that must be investigated, it appears to link to another dimension and was forced open from the other side.

More concerning is that the invaders ship was found not far from the rift, heavily damaged with all the crew deceased. The aliens on board the ship are unlike anything we have seen before, a strange mix of organic and metalic compounds - a true heresy to organic superiority!

The rift remains open and available for exploration.
STH_undine_story.51.name: Alien ship found
STH_undine_story.52.desc:
A number of our leading experts in fluid have been studying the species through the rift, it is clear they are a threat to our superiority.

Several key locations have been marked where we can breach the barrier between dimensions and force our way into their space, much like they have dare do to us.

These unclean beings dared to enter our domain, this insult cannot go unpunnished, but first we must understand more about them if we are to end the threat they pose to us.
STH_undine_story.52.name: Fluidic Rifts
STH_undine_story.53.desc: Our Bioship has completed its investigation bringing us that much closer to understanding these strange aliens and bringing our devastating purge upon their unclean worlds.
STH_undine_story.53.name: Planetary Investigation Complete
STH_undine_story.58.desc:
We have gathered enough information on these inferior biological creatures, we can see that they pollute biology with chemicals and technology. The are unclean and pose too great a risk to remain a threat to our home. 

Using the DNA we have collected from the worlds we have scanned our genetic modifiers have created a virus which should break down their biology and slowly convert it to our superior biomass. We shall infest their worlds and purify them in our image.

Our infestation class ships will be unstoppable. The commune must conviene to decide our next actions.
STH_undine_story.58.name: The Weak Shall Perish
STH_undine_story.59.desc: The War Commune has been called to make a decision that will impact all Undine. These unclean creatures dared to enter the sanctity of our domain and they launched a fleet against us. This cannot go unchallenged. What will the decision be.
STH_undine_story.59.name: The Weak Shall Perish
STH_undine_story.60.b: What will this mean to the Undine?
STH_undine_story.60.d: The old ways serve us best.
STH_undine_story.60.desc: [Root.GetLeaderTitle] [Root.GetLeaderName], I am [leader1.GetName] and I represent a part of the hive wishing to evaluate our future. With the decision to avoid war against the impure, many within the commune believe now is the time to abandon our infestation ways and adopt a more adaptive approach to how we deal with these new lifeforms. \n\nAbandoning our ways will allow us to open traditional communication channels, but once we have started down this path there is no turning back.
STH_undine_story.60.name: Message from the Psi Commune
STH_undine_story.61.desc: The hives have emerged from their commune and the decisions have been made. We shall look to embrace co-existance with these inferior beings, but if they displease us we can still destroy them with our superior military might.
STH_undine_story.61.name: The Undine Supremacy
STH_undine_story.70.desc: The commune has decided, it is time to purge the universe of these lower lifeforms and extend the influence of the Fluidic species. We have learnt that there are four quadrants which need cleansing. In order to break their resitance we have targeted [delta_target_1.GetName] from the Delta Quadrant, [gamma_target_1.GetName] and [gamma_target_2.GetName] from the Gamma Quadrant, and [alpha_beta_target_1.GetName], [alpha_beta_target_2.GetName] and [alpha_beta_target_3.GetName] from the Alpha and Beta Quadrants. \n\nFor their insolence whatever option we take we will always declare war on the Borg. Now is our time to choose.
STH_undine_story.70.name: The Purge
STH_undine_story.71.a: Open rifts to the Delta Quadrant.
STH_undine_story.71.b: Open rifts to the Alpha-Beta Quadrant.
STH_undine_story.71.c: Open rifts to the Gamma Quadrant.
STH_undine_story.71.desc_01: With our targets decided, the Delta Quadrant is ripe for our invasion. Using the knowledge that we have learnt we can now open a single rift to each of our targets. Doing so will signal that our invasion is ready to begin.\n\nOpen a new rift via a special project to begin our staging ground for invasion.
STH_undine_story.71.desc_02: With our targets decided, the Alpha and Beta Quadrants is ready for our invasion. Using the knowledge that we have learnt we can now open a single rift to each of our targets. Doing so will signal that our invasion is ready to begin.\n\nOpen a new rift via a special project to begin our staging ground for invasion.
STH_undine_story.71.desc_03: With our targets decided, the Gamma Quadrant is ready to be purged by our forces. Using the knowledge that we have learnt we can now open a single rift to each of our targets. Doing so will signal that our invasion is ready to begin.\n\nOpen a new rift via a special project to begin our staging ground for invasion.
STH_undine_story.71.desc_04: The whole of the Milky Way will know our fury. Using the knowledge that we have learnt we can now open a single rift to each of our targets. Doing so will signal that our invasion is ready to begin.\n\nOpen a new rift via a special project to begin our staging ground for invasion.
STH_undine_story.71.name: Open Rifts to invade
STH_undine_story.72.desc: The have been opened, deploy our forces and begin the invasion of these aliens realms. Our first target should be to deploy our infestation virus into the system and convert it into a Fluidic zone and allow us to create a beachhead into their territory.
STH_undine_story.72.name: The Rifts are open
STH_undine_story.73.desc: Our infestation virus worked exactly as planned, we have infested their planets and allowed the fluidic realm to push through the rift to convert and invade all aspects of their system. It now belongs to us and a statement has been made. We are the Undine and we will purge you.\n\nThe Weak shall perish and the strong will triumph.
STH_undine_story.73.name: System Captured
STH_undine_story.82.desc: Our biosensors have detected a further incursion into our domain, by species identifying themselves as 'The Borg'. They are misguided in their assumption that our resistance will be futile. This unclean threat must be neutralised and our space cleaned of this vile presence.
STH_undine_story.82.name: Invasion Fleet Detected
STH_undine_story.85.desc: Our biological superiority has won through and the Borg have been removed from our space, this incursion to foul our territory cannot go unpunished.
STH_undine_story.85.name: Borg Threat Removed
STH_undine_story.999.desc: Our scientists have picked up a number of strange readings within the fabric of our Fluidic Space, our experts believe that it could be signs of a vessel or vessels attempting to breach the Fluidic barrier and breach our domain.\n\nThis could be an early warning for a pending invasion from beyond our space. Our military war chiefs recommend that we prepare for an invasion, whilst many of our non combatants feel this is an overreaction.
STH_undine_story.999.name: Strange readings detected
STH_united_earth_story.100.desc:
With tremendous gratitude the [Root.GetName] thanks the [From.GetName] for its assistance. As they depart [Root.Capital.Solar_System.GetName] for [From.Owner.Capital.GetName], the people of [Root.Capital.GetName] reflect upon what has transpired. 

The [From.GetName] and its crew risked death, to help defend [Root.Capital.GetName]. Not because there was something for them to gain in doing so other than an expression of their friendship and mutual respect. 

In the aftermath of the attack, many are left wondering whether growing our alliances might be the key to securing our future.
STH_united_earth_story.100.desc_fail: While the [Root.GetName] thanks the [From.GetName] for its assistance in the battle against the Xindi, the loss of the homeworld leaves a bitter sense among many that the [From.Owner.GetName] could have done more...
STH_united_earth_story.100.desc_vulcanAn:
It isn't often that the Vulcans offer Humanity such overt assistance. Not even the early warnings of the Xindi's plan stirred them into action. The Enterprise's rescue of the [From.GetName] from the Expanse changed that.

The [Root.GetName] can only hope that as the [From.GetName] returns to [From.Owner.Capital.GetName], its crew advocate more equal treatment and cooperation.

An unexpected positive from this whole ordeal is that the crews of the Seleya and Kumari worked really well together defending Earth, it could be a start in repairing the rifts between both species. A word from our liaisons on both homeworlds could go a long way to improving relations between Andor and Vulcan.
STH_united_earth_story.100.name: [From.GetName]
STH_united_earth_story.101.desc: The [From.GetName] has concluded its role in the pink-skin's valiant struggle against the genocidal Xindi. The [From.GetName]'s captain and crew exceeded the purview of their mission by getting so heavily involved. The Imperial Guard has forgiven them; they acted out of honour and have proven worthy of the prestigious vessel. \n\nThe Imperial Guard hopes that the pink-skins will remember their assistance when dealing with them in the future.
STH_united_earth_story.101.desc_fail: The [From.GetName] has concluded its role in the pink-skin's valiant if ultimately futile struggle against the genocidal Xindi. The [From.GetName]'s captain and crew exceeded the purview of their mission by getting so heavily involved. The Imperial Guard has forgiven them; they acted out of honour and have proven worthy of the prestigious vessel.
STH_united_earth_story.101.name: [From.GetName]
STH_united_earth_story.102.a: Time to start mending old wounds.
STH_united_earth_story.102.desc: It isn't often that the Vulcans offer Humanity such overt assistance. Not even the early warnings of the Xindi's plan stirred them into action. The Enterprise's rescue of the [From.GetName] from the Expanse changed that. \n\nThe [Root.GetName] can only hope that as the [From.GetName] returns to [From.Owner.Capital.GetName], its crew advocate more equal treatment and cooperation.
STH_united_earth_story.102.desc_fail: With the loss of the homeworld, even the assistance of the [From.GetName] does little to blunt public anger against the [From.Owner.GetName]. Despite years of supposed support and acquiescence to [From.Owner.GetSpeciesName] demands, when the [Root.GetSpeciesName] race was mortally threatened just a single ship was provided. \n\nWhile the [From.GetName] and its crew have earned the gratitude of the [Root.GetName], the same can not be said of [From.Owner.Capital.GetName] itself.
STH_united_earth_story.102.name: [From.GetName]
STH_united_earth_story.103.desc:
The [From.GetName] has survived the battle with the Xindi weapon and is returning to [Root.Capital.GetName] for repairs and debriefing. 

Sending the [From.GetName] was logical, after the Enterprise recovered the ship and treated its ailing crew. 

The aftermath of the attack won't be easy for Earth to deal with. The High Command hopes that however they face it, the [From.GetName]'s actions will have shown [Root.Capital.GetName]'s continued commitment to the historic friendship between the two peoples.
STH_united_earth_story.103.desc_fail:
The [From.GetName] has survived the battle with the Xindi weapon and is returning to [Root.Capital.GetName] for repairs and debriefing. 

Sending the [From.GetName] was logical, after the Enterprise recovered the ship and treated its ailing crew. 

The devastating aftermath of the attack won't be easy for Humanity to deal with. The High Command hopes that however they face it, the [From.GetName]'s actions will have shown [Root.Capital.GetName]'s continued commitment to the historic friendship between the two peoples, despite the loss of Earth.
STH_united_earth_story.103.name: [From.GetName]
STH_united_earth_story.104.desc: The [From.GetName] suffered heavy damage in combat against Xindi forces. It managed to escape destruction and has limped back to Andoria for repair. Captain Shran is most displeased with the outcome and has vowed to better uphold the [From.GetName]'s historic name in the future.
STH_united_earth_story.104.desc_fail: The [From.GetName] suffered heavy damage in combat against Xindi forces. It managed to escape destruction and has limped back to Andoria for repair. Captain Shran is most displeased with the outcome and has vowed to better uphold the [From.GetName]'s historic name in the future.
STH_united_earth_story.104.name: [From.GetName]
STH_united_earth_story.105.desc: The [From.GetName] and its crew have been killed while aiding the defence against the Xindi weapon. Regardless of the outcome of the battle, the [From.GetName]'s crew will be remembered for their sacrifice for the future of Humanity.\n\nThe High Command has decided that their memorial will be the continuation of the ship's name, with another vessel to be named the [From.GetName].
STH_united_earth_story.105.desc_fail: The [From.GetName] and its crew have been killed while aiding the defence against the Xindi weapon. Regardless of the outcome of the battle, the [From.GetName]'s crew will be remembered for their sacrifice for the future of Humanity.\n\nThe High Command has decided that their memorial will be the continuation of the ship's name, with another vessel to be named the [From.GetName].
STH_united_earth_story.105.name: [From.GetName]
STH_united_earth_story.11.desc:
We have successfully prevented the alien probe from entering [Root.GetName] orbit. Preliminary scans of the wreckage indicate the probe carried within it a single-use condensed energy weapon which, had it fired, would have been capable of destroying large regions of the [Root.GetName]'s surface.

[Root.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [Root.Owner.GetRulerName]'s administration has planned a series of celebrations to honour the men and women of Starfleet who averted this tragedy.

The question now remains, who sent this weapon of mass destruction?
STH_united_earth_story.11.name: Alien Probe
STH_united_earth_story.12.desc_01:
The alien probe cut through [Root.GetName]'s defenses with ease before taking up position in geosynchronous orbit.

After several minutes of apparent inactivity the probe fired a condensed energy beam at the surface of the planet, burning large swathes of the East Coast of North America to ashes before powering down.

The few remaining ground batteries and a contingent from the Mars Defence Perimeter on patrol near Jupiter at the time of the attack were able to take advantage of pause and successfully destroyed the vessel.

It will take weeks to fully assess the extent of the casualties in North America, but preliminary analysis indicates the total could reach several hundred million.

The people of [Root.GetName] are shocked by the losses, as they turn to [Root.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [Root.Owner.GetRulerName] to answer the question: who sent this weapon of mass destruction?
STH_united_earth_story.12.desc_02:
The alien probe cut through [Root.GetName]'s defenses with ease before taking up position in geosynchronous orbit.

After several minutes of apparent inactivity the probe fired a condensed energy beam at the surface of the planet, burning large swathes of the West Coast of Europe to ashes before powering down.

The few remaining ground batteries and a contingent from the Mars Defence Perimeter on patrol near Jupiter at the time of the attack were able to take advantage of pause and successfully destroyed the vessel.

It will take weeks to fully assess the extent of the casualties in Europe, but preliminary analysis indicates the total could reach several hundred million.

The people of [Root.GetName] are shocked by the losses, as they turn to [Root.Owner.GetRulerTitle] [Root.Owner.GetRulerName] to answer the question: who sent this weapon of mass destruction?
STH_united_earth_story.12.desc_03:
The alien probe cut through [Root.GetName]'s defenses with ease before taking up position in geosynchronous orbit.

After several minutes of apparent inactivity the probe fired a condensed energy beam at the surface of the planet, burning large swathes of the Indian subcontinent to ashes before powering down.

The few remaining ground batteries and a contingent from the Mars Defence Perimeter on patrol near Jupiter at the time of the attack were able to take advantage of pause and successfully destroyed the vessel.
