From initial reports, the complex is extremely ancient but advanced, and may be Tkonian or Iconian in nature. Most of the equipment is decayed beyond repair, but some data is still recoverable and from what little data there is a story of what occurred here begins to take place.

A great calamity struck their ancient empire, and the population could no longer sustain themselves on this world.

With only limited support they used their gateway network to abandon the planet and sealed it under a large mountain - to prevent it falling into enemy hands.

The data indicates a small chamber somewhere towards the center of the complex.
sth_iconian_dig_site.4.name: Underground Complex
sth_iconian_dig_site.5.desc:
[Leader.GetName] has managed to uncover what is known as an 'Iconian gateway', a large arch like structure capable of transportation across the entire galaxy.

Physically the gateway appears literally as a 'door' through which was the destination. According to the data recovered, the gateway network used to span the galaxy and allowed the Iconians to travel throughout the galaxy without the use of starships and was the primary means through which they controlled their vast empire, earning them a reputation among their primitive subject peoples as 'Demons of Air and Darkness'.

[Leader.GetName] reports that further study of the gateway should now be possible and, when we understand the technology better, a site could be constructed on this planet to utilise the gateway itself.
sth_iconian_dig_site.5.name: Iconian Gateway
sth_krenim_flavour.1.a.tooltip: This is not in our interest.
sth_krenim_flavour.1.desc: We discovered a log buoy protected by temporal shields. Unfortunately, we found out after deactivating the shields that the data was too damaged. Maybe we can adjust the scanners to find any more buoys.
sth_krenim_flavour.1.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.10.desc: »Logbook of senior medical officer. Stardate 51558.6. At nineteen hundred, thirty-nine, in my capacity as chief medical officer, and in accordance with the Starfleet Medical Ordinance 121, Section A, Captain Kathryn Janeway has been formally relieved of her command of the Federation spaceship Voyager. Which did not use much. I have told the Captain my suspicion that he has been suffering from a traumatic stress syndrome for weeks. Symptoms include irritability, insomnia, obsession, and reckless behavior. She tried to justify her behavior. I asked her to stay a few days in my temporary infirmary for observation. She refused. When I reminded her that I had the authority to relieve her of her duties, she threatened to disable my program. I should point out that she apologized immediately, but the fact that she even considered this idea to keep her position is further evidence of her obvious mental exhaustion. Eventually, she confirmed my order and then refused to obey him. Although I am ashamed to sully the file of one of the strongest, bravest, and devoted officers I know, it is my duty to record this incident in my official logbook. If these events lead to an indictment, it is up to others to decide who acted appropriately by us or if the Captain's actions are sufficient for release from Starfleet.«
sth_krenim_flavour.10.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.11.desc: »Captain's log. Captain Janeway. Stardate 51289.8. Thanks to the Temporal Shields developed by Seven, we can now protect ourselves against the chroniton torpedoes of the Krenim. This had an unexpected effect. Lt. Kim detected a temporal shockwave at a distance of 20 light-years. The Krenim Warship we encountered transformed into a much smaller ship. I have the sensor data transferred to the Astrometric Laboratory for further investigation.«
sth_krenim_flavour.11.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.12.desc: »Captain's log, stardate 51425.4: Our condition has left us vulnerable to spatial anomalies and to any alien species eager for a piece of hardware. We've taken refuge in a class 9 nebula.«
sth_krenim_flavour.12.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.13.b: Use the data to advance our temporal research.
sth_krenim_flavour.13.desc: Our analysis of the data suggests that the alien ship Voyager has finally managed to force and destroy the gunship built by Annorax into normal spacetime. We can not imagine another scenario that would have resulted in a complete restoration of the timeline we now know. None of our current scans show a trace of the timeline to which the logs refer. She completely collapsed. The launched, shielded buoys are the only records of their existence. Annorax himself has led a long and incredibly productive life and has peacefully died in his home on Kyana Prime nearly two hundred years ago, surrounded by his friends and family. Only those of us who know how sinister the other paths can be really appreciate the light we live in now.
sth_krenim_flavour.13.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.3.desc: We discovered another Chroniton signature and should send a Temporal Agent.
sth_krenim_flavour.3.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.4.desc: We discovered another Chroniton signature and should send a Temporal Agent.
sth_krenim_flavour.4.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.5.desc: We discovered another Chroniton signature and should send a Temporal Agent.
sth_krenim_flavour.5.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.50.a: To the restoration of our glory.
sth_krenim_flavour.50.desc: Annorax presents one the of the largest accomplishments from laboratories of the Temporal Authority, the Temporal Research and Build Yards offer the perfect location to continue development of the Time-Ship. \n\nWith advanced forcefields and temporal generators that will allow the research to progress to the next phase and when the time is right these yards will be used to constuct the Time Ship itself.
sth_krenim_flavour.50.name: The Great Time-Ship
sth_krenim_flavour.51.a: To the restoration of our glory.
sth_krenim_flavour.51.desc: Annorax is ready to present the next breakthrough in his bid to restore the Krenim Imperium to its former glory. The Temporal Jump Drive will produce enough power output to keep the timeship in a state of perpetual flux and travel great distances unbound by the normal flow of time.
sth_krenim_flavour.51.name: The Great Time-Ship
sth_krenim_flavour.52.a: To the restoration of the Krenim.
sth_krenim_flavour.52.desc: Annorax explains that the power requirements of the Time Ship will be larger then anything seen in the Imperium before, a new Temporal Power Core will need to be specifically constructed in order to power the vessel.
sth_krenim_flavour.52.name: The Great Time-Ship
sth_krenim_flavour.53.a: To the restoration of the Imperum.
sth_krenim_flavour.53.desc: What good is a mighty ship without a purpose asked Annorax. \n\nA high energy Chroniton beam with enough chroniton particles can cause a temporal incursion effect that if calculated properly can alter an objects history to benefit the Krenim.
sth_krenim_flavour.53.name: The Great Time-Ship
sth_krenim_flavour.54.desc: Annorax points out that in order to keep the Time Ship safe a new form of shielding will be required because should the temporal shielding fail or should our foe gain access to our temporal resonance frequency then the transition to normal space time could be catastrophic.
sth_krenim_flavour.54.name: The Great Time-Ship
sth_krenim_flavour.55.desc: Finally the moment that Annorax has been waiting for has arrived, a lifetime of waiting to restore the Krenim Imperium to its once glorious state has arrived. All the components are in place, the time ship is ready to begin its journey. Once researched and constructed at a temporal shipyards, the massive Time-Ship will be a once in a generation vessel capable of bringing the Krenim Imperium back to where it belongs.
sth_krenim_flavour.55.name: The Great Time-Ship
sth_krenim_flavour.56.desc: [Root.GetLeaderTitle], we have been approached by one of our scientists, a man named Annorax, he claims he has mastered time itself. He has created the ability to move an item out of phase with our time stream making advances on already formidable advances in temporal science. \n\nAnnorax promises with his help he will restore our Imperium to its former glory.
sth_krenim_flavour.56.name: The Great Time-Ship
sth_krenim_flavour.6.desc: We discovered another Chroniton signature and should send a Temporal Agent.
sth_krenim_flavour.6.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.61.desc: Following the [FromFrom.GetName]'s defeat at the hands of the Krenim's massive time shift, one of the surviving crew members has come to us with some positive news.\n\nDuring the last attack they were able to scan an unexploded Chroniton weapon lodged in their ships hull. As a result we now have the frequency in which the Time Ship is out of phase. We now have an opportunity to study these findings and adapt our weapons and shields to match this frequency. It is likely the Krenim will soon alter this frequency, but for a time it could give us a slight advantage.
sth_krenim_flavour.61.name: Chroniton Frequency Detected
sth_krenim_flavour.62.desc: Our scientists have managed to retrospectively decode the frequency of the Krenim Time Ship which will reduce its weapons damage and shield strength against our weapons for a short duration, before the Krenim modify their temporal frequency. We should use this opportunity to attack the ship before our advantage expires.
sth_krenim_flavour.62.name: Chroniton Frequency Exploited
sth_krenim_flavour.63.desc: One of our enemies has decoded the temporal flux frequency of our Time Ship, which will significantly reduce their weapons damage and shield strength until we can modulate and move to a different frequency. \n\nWe should look to keep the ship safe whilst this is going on.
sth_krenim_flavour.63.name: Chroniton Frequency Exploited
sth_krenim_flavour.7.desc: We discovered another Chroniton signature and should send a Temporal Agent.
sth_krenim_flavour.7.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.8.desc: »Captain's log, Captain Janeway, Stardate 51413.1. We have lost nine decks, more than half of the ship has been destroyed, and life support is broken. I swore I would not tear this family apart, but I have no choice but to evacuate the crew. I and my senior officers will stay on board trying to rescue Lt. Tom Paris and Cmdr Chakotay. «
sth_krenim_flavour.8.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.9.desc: »Captain's log, stardate 51682.2: I've forged a coalition with the Nihydron and the Mawasi. Together, we're preparing to attack the weapon ship.«
sth_krenim_flavour.9.name: Year of Hell
sth_krenim_flavour.99.desc: We can now recruit Temporal Agents.
sth_krenim_flavour.99.name: Temporal Defense Authority founded
sth_military_complex_dig_site.1.desc: [Leader.GetName] has reported a figure appeared before them upon approaching the facility on [From.Planet.GetName], citing 'Who meets the challenge?' before swirling away into dust, as hordes of drones begin to pour out of the large stronghold facility on the planet.\n\nBeaming down reinforcements, [Leader.GetSheHe] has discovered that the fire is coming from autonomous drones which are being produced within the facility. [Leader.GetName] has a choice on which tactics they'd prefer to pursue.
sth_military_complex_dig_site.1.name: Beachhead
sth_military_complex_dig_site.2.a: Study technology to disable drones and advance.
sth_military_complex_dig_site.2.desc: [Leader.GetName] has reported their team has breached the facility on [From.Planet.GetName].\n\nThe autonomous drones continue to put up a substantial defense and are delaying the advance into the facility. The facility is heavily armoured and protected from orbital bombardment. The technology within appears to be powered by an unknown power source stored somewhere deeper inside, which is producing these defenses. [Leader.GetName] has a choice on how to proceed.
sth_military_complex_dig_site.2.name: Into the Facility
sth_military_complex_dig_site.3.a: Utilise facility as a strategic location.
sth_military_complex_dig_site.3.desc:
[Leader.GetName] and their team have breached the final chamber within the facility on [From.Planet.GetName].

As they do so, the air begins to hum and vibrate, and energy pulses through the bodies of the fallen. The once dead casualties begin to breathe once more as a mysterious figure forms out of the air in front of [Leader.GetName].

'I am Portal, the guardian of this facility. Your skill and tenacity is unmatched and you have defeated the test put before you. Perhaps there is much to learn from this younger race. Take what you have earnt here today.' the figure speaks, before a power surge erupts throughout the facility.

As the sparks diminish, most of the technology has been destroyed as the away team stands firm - unwounded and unbloodied. [Leader.GetName] now has a choice in what to do with the facility.
sth_military_complex_dig_site.3.name: Confronting the Portal
sth_pulse.101.desc.01: The [From.GetName] have suffered heavily from recent wars. Many factions on [From.Capital.GetName] have pushed the government to forgo exploration and expansion into the galaxy in favour of a [From.Capital.GetName]-first policy.
sth_pulse.101.desc.borg: The [From.owner_species.GetBorgSpeciesNumber] empire known as the [From.GetName] has suffered severe material losses in recent cycles. The species now appears to forgo exploration and expansion.
sth_pulse.101.name: [From.GetName] Isolationism
sth_pulse.106.desc.01: The [From.GetName] have looked with envy on their galactic neighbours' growth in territory and prestige. Many factions on [From.Capital.GetName] have pushed the government to commit to exploration and expansion into the galaxy and reject the isolationism of the past.
sth_pulse.106.desc.borg: The [From.owner_species.GetBorgSpeciesNumber] empire known as the [From.GetName] has commenced exploration and expansion ending an extended period of dormancy.
sth_pulse.106.name: [From.GetName] Expansionism
sth_pulse.570.desc: Here in the Q Continuum we see and hear all things... and we notice that you have switched to playing a pathetic minor country. Now I am sure there is a method to your madness, but would you like me to remove the minor race lockdown so that you can attempt to make something of this Empire? \n\nRemember this is a one-time deal and once the cat is out of the bag... it won't go back in, it has claws after all.
sth_pulse.570.name: From Minor to Major
sth_q_events.10.desc:
Allow me to introduce myself. I am Q. Just Q. From the Q Continuum. 

Trust me, I'm 'all that'. Omnipotent. Omniscient. Handsome... 

Anyhow, I've deigned your pathetic civilisation to be at least somewhat worthy of my advice and guidance. The challenges ahead for you here are different enough from what you've encountered before that you'd be a fool to turn down my offer. 

As a token of my generosity I'd like to offer you an... uh, hmm. Ah! Have a nice shiny mineral.
sth_q_events.10.name: Bonjour, mon capitaine!
sth_q_events.1010.desc:
Oh my, you are a rather strange one, aren't you? Those Humanoids skulk around on their vaunted M-class planets, but not you. You can walk freely on planets in environments that would kill them in seconds. 

You can control these environments; sculpt planets that are far too hostile for any other being in the galaxy but yourselves. Bravo, mon gentillement. Bravo. 

This comes with a price, of course. Terraforming will be limited and expensive, even if it will let you access planets other species cannot. 

Make sure to read your terraforming technology description carefully, as it will mention the planet classes you can terraform. 

You can also construct environmental domes on low-habitability worlds to make them seem a little more like home...
sth_q_events.1010.name: Environmental controls?
sth_q_events.1020.desc: Hmm, you are rather desperate, aren't you? You seem to ghoulishly steal organs of other sapients and graft them to yourselves in order to stave off that Phage of yours. How ghastly! You should be aware that you can only use enslaved races that way if they are set to the Livestock slavery type. Only the most robust and strapping of races will do, as their organs are the most desirable. Like all things in the universe, however, they will eventually run out. You will need to move on to new pastures, or in this case, donors.
sth_q_events.1020.name: Medical Experiments
sth_q_events.1030.desc: Let loose the hounds and let's start the hunt! What's that? You only hunt worthy prey? Well then, that's only sporting of you! If you insist on hunting intelligent races, the least you could do is make it a spectator sport! You should be aware that you can only use enslaved races that way if they are set to the Livestock slavery type. Only the most clever and strongest of prey will do, however, as they provide the best sport! Be sure to replenish your preserves once in a while, lest your hunters go hungry!
sth_q_events.1030.name: Good Hunting?
sth_q_events.1040.desc: You know you really should be careful when playing with high explosives. But then again what do I care if you get burned? In this case however, I must insist on caution. You've discovered a way to destroy a star, something that should not be done lightly. If you insist on playing with fire, simply go to the system primary ( that's the big ball of plasma that they system's named after) of a system that you own, authorize the use of Armageddon Bombardment, and make your Descision. Be sure you have nothing you will want later, as the supernova will leave nothing behind. Also, your neighbor's will be a bit upset, as their children will be unable to sleep if their favorite night light gets snuffed out.
sth_q_events.1040.name: Let's be careful now...
sth_q_events.1050.desc: You know you really should be careful when playing with high explosives. But then again what do I care if you get burned? In this case however, I must insist on caution. You've discovered a way to destroy subspace, something that should not be done lightly. If you insist on playing with fire, simply go to the system primary (that's the big ball of plasma that they system's named after) of a system that you own, authorize the use of Armageddon Bombardment, and make your Descision. Be sure you have no intention of going back, as the explosion will make warp travel impossible. Also, your neighbor's will be a bit upset, as their tourists will no longer be able to visit.
sth_q_events.1050.name: Let's be careful now...
sth_q_events.1060.desc: Oh my, I see you've started you primitive species collection with the Klingons, poor honourable killers that they are. But you should be careful: there are some races that will object to being sampled likes moths in an entomologists collection. Once you acquire an interesting species, your curators will need to go through their possessions and DNA to find anything of interest. This can only be done on your capital or dedicated museum world, and there can only be one pop that is representative of the species. You should be aware that you can only use enslaved races that way if they are set to the Livestock slavery type. The stranger and more unique the species is, the greater prestige for your little Empire! You may also recover some trinkets after curating them.
sth_q_events.1060.name: Starting your collection?
sth_q_events.1070.desc: You've recently discovered how to hire mercenaries in smoky bars to steal shiny trinkets from other Empires! Well done! I can tell you will try to make use of it, at least once... maybe twice. Still, you can go look at your stolen trinkets on the Relics screen, and go hire some mercenaries! If their expedition is successful, you will find any stolen artifacts on the same screen. Be careful though, as Empires will object to stealing their cultural treasures if your involvment is discovered! If the heat is too much, you can auction the relic off like a 'hot potato' onto some other Empire, and make a nice profit in the process! I can go on and on about my adventures with Vash, but oh! Look at the time! See you... out there!
sth_q_events.1070.name: Need to expand your collection?
sth_q_events.11.b: We don't need you or your mineral.
sth_q_events.11.desc: Did we really have to start off like that? I go out of my way to help you savages and you just throw it back in my face. Well, if that's how you're going to be, I'm taking my mineral back. \n\nDev Note: If you ever find yourself missing Q and his settings you can reach him from Contacts and there selecting Adv Diplomacy and Speak to the Q.
sth_q_events.11.name: Oh, come now...
sth_q_events.140.desc:
Allow me to introduce myself. I am Q. Just Q. From the Q Continuum. 

The galaxy hasn't been kind to you Suliban has it? Though the loss of your homeworld all those centuries ago hasn't stopped you from clinging on, so to speak. 

The challenges ahead for you are such that you'd be a fool to turn down the offer of assistance I now make to you. 

As a token of my generosity I'd like to offer you an... uh, hmm. Ah! Have a nice shiny mineral. 

Now go ahead, ask me anything!
sth_q_events.140.name: Ah, there you are!
sth_q_events.141.desc:
All alone in that cold, empty Helix of yours. So many tiny cells clinging together for warmth... 

Still, I suppose you want to build more of them considering you don't do the whole 'colonising planets' thing. So listen up.

You cannot expand via colony ships. Instead, you can use your constructor ships and the megastructure menu to build a new Helix. 

It will require a great deal of resources, and you can only build a Helix around a gas giant inside your own borders, so consider establishing an outpost station first to stake your claim to the system. 

In the right hands, you can spread across the galaxy like the genetically-engineered menace that you are.
sth_q_events.141.name: Helixes
sth_q_events.2000.desc:
So you've discovered a new ship class and now you want ME to tell you how to build them? 

No? 

Well tough, listen up. 

Head into the ship designer, the F9 key should work. There you'll find the new ship class design has been provided for you. 

If not, just use the 'New Design' option in the top left of the window, and select the new ship class ship size. 

I assume you know the difference between the sections that make up a starship and the utilities equipped?
sth_q_events.2000.name: Ship Designer
sth_q_events.2010.desc:
A good ship design is like a work of art: subtle, nuanced, and with eyes that follow you across the room. It's important to know what you are doing, and that's why I'm here. 

One of the most important things to consider is how you assign utilities. There are three types: Tactical, in red, Engineering, in gold and Support in blue. Each utility offers different benefits and optimisations for you to exploit. 

Tactical slots typically grant a variety of bonuses to weapons damage, range and performance. Engineering utilities offer additional defensive capabilities. Support slots generally afford bonuses to speed, evasion or accuracy. 

You can place additional backup reactors in all three utility slots for additional power. 

Beyond these are the auxiliary slots which provide a variety of out-of-combat bonuses.
sth_q_events.2010.name: Starship Utilities
sth_q_events.2020.desc:
Your military starships - pathetic as they are - are configured in two sections. 

The first, on the top left of the ship designer screen, controls the weapon loadout of the starship. The stuff that goes 'pew pew'.

The second, on the top right of the ship designer, controls the utility consoles. These are things that generally will either keep you alive longer or make you more deadly, but that don't have any offensive capacity of their own.
sth_q_events.2020.name: Starship Sections
sth_q_events.2100.desc:
It's a rather presumptuous name, isn't it? $saber$. I can see your ship from up here and it doesn't look that big.
Well, at least it makes up for it in aggressiveness. $saber_plural$ are very versatile and can equip a very wide range of different weapons. They are also the first ship you possess that can equip consoles, powerful little components that boost your ship in a specific way, from cloaking generators to various bridge stations.

But what the $saber$ really excels in, is handling fleets of smaller ships. $saber_plural$ computers have a bonus to tracking, which helps against ships with high evasion. More importantly, $saber_plural$ are the only ship that can install Snare auras - like tractor beams and illegal subspace weapons. Naughty. Those auras can slow your enemy and even trap them in the same solar system, so you can kill at your leisure.
sth_q_events.2100.name: $saber_plural$
sth_q_events.2110.desc: Solid. Dependable. Simple. No, I don't mean you, of course, I mean your new ship class! The $sovereign$! I have no doubt very soon it will be the backbone of your fleet. It may take a major investment of resource and time to build, but they are powerful, sturdy and easy to maintain. It is, in fact, one of the most durable ships you will ever be able to build.\n\nBut wait, there's more! The $sovereign$ is a very capable platform - while it does not have the pure offensive capability of other classes, it does compensate it with the ability to equip a great deal of utilities and even a unique Debuff aura. Those powerful modules can play havoc with your enemy, using every dirty trick in the book. I approve. Why, it's almost like the $sovereign$ is a honorary Q!
sth_q_events.2110.name: $sovereign_plural$
sth_q_events.2120.desc:
Ah, the $steamrunner$. You've finally done it, you worked your little peons to death and now they know how to make this truly wonderful craft. As powerful as a shark, as nimble as a Tungasian Space-Beetle. What's that? You've never heard of the Tungasian Beetle? Marvelous creatures.

The $steamrunner$ is all teeth. It also has a unique Long-Range Combat Sensor, that turns your $steamrunner$ into a mobile artillery that pummels your enemies from afar. Not only that, but a $steamrunner$ is a pure military vessel. You can produce it quickly and cheaply, and spring back to action after a defeat.

These noble vessels are to be protected, however! They do not have the protection your $sovereign_plural$ have, and caught unaware, they can easily be destroyed. In fact, it is almost inevitable. I have half a mind to just make you forget about this ship altogether, because we both know you're just going to let it die. But in the right hands and with the right weaponry, it can be a true power.
sth_q_events.2120.name: $steamrunner_plural$!
sth_q_events.2130.desc:
My, that seems rather heavy, doesn't it? Are you sure you need so much plating? Well, just say hello to your new $adv_cruiser$. 

While you may still develop, in time, even more powerful ship classes, none will ever be as enduring, with the strongest shields and hull. Not only that, but it carries powerful support systems that can tip the scale of the battle in your favor by helping your allies and snaring your enemies.

Just remember that a $adv_cruiser$ does not have the most powerful weapons, so it should be in the middle of a fleet. 

They're expensive and hard to replace. So, don't misplace like you're always doing with your keys.
sth_q_events.2130.name: $adv_cruiser_plural$
sth_q_events.2140.desc: It's true, I have made fun of your pitiful little ships in the past, but I LIKE this one. The name $strike$ doesn't really convey its almost delightfully psychotic tendencies. It is violent to a fault, stacked full of short-range and heavy-duty weaponry. It also comes equipped with a unique Short-Range Combat Sensor that gives your ship an extra kick at close range.\n\nDespite having a fire-power comparable to a $adv_cruiser$, it is as small and maneuverable as a $saber$. While it is indeed not as durable as other, larger ships, its high evasion often allows it to stand toe to toe with the most powerful ships in the fleet. Use these ships well, and they can turn the tide of battle.
sth_q_events.2140.name: $strike_plural$
sth_q_events.2150.desc: Fast, deadly, puffy... wait, not that last one. Still, do we really need to go through it again? This is the sixth time... oh, very well. Yes, the $assault_cruiser$ is a new class of ship. Yes, it is deadlier than all those that came before it. More expensive too, and most probably the last ship your puny scientists will be able to develop.\n\nThe $assault_cruiser$ is as nimble as a much smaller ship, while carrying almost as much defenses as a $adv_cruiser$ and the most powerful weapons in your arsenal. It can take down a small fleet by itself. That being said, it is very expensive, hard to maintain and lacks the degree of specialisation $sovereign_plural$ and $adv_cruiser_plural$ have.
sth_q_events.2150.name: $assault_cruiser_plural$
sth_q_events.30.a: We will take your mineral, Q.
sth_q_events.30.desc:
Allow me to introduce myself. I am Q. Just Q. From the Q Continuum. 

You Klingons have quite a reputation for violence, don't you? Of course you're not mindless killers like some I could mention... but your primitive code of honour can still lead to some hilariously vicious misunderstandings. 

The challenges ahead for you are such that you'd be a fool to turn down the offer of assistance I now make to you. 

As a token of my generosity, I'd like to offer you an... uh, hmm. Ah! Have a nice shiny mineral. 

Now go ahead, ask me anything!
sth_q_events.30.name: Well Qapla' to you!
sth_q_events.31.desc:
The Great Houses act as semi-independent subjects. They can be a source of great strength to a savvy ruler who knows how to manage them. 

They all want the same thing, honour, favor, and governorship over the empire's worlds. Provide, and their fleets will grow, and your enemies will worry. Withhold, and you may suffer rebellion. 

You can interact with them via the contacts window or select their flag from the fleets or planets UI. 

New factions represent the Noble Houses of the empire, these Houses are not as powerful as the Great Houses but can be promoted, which can upset the status quo!
sth_q_events.31.name: Great Houses
sth_q_events.3100.desc:
Mortals - so easy for you to... snuff it. 

Well at least you've got your hospitals and voodoo doctors to help you with that.

Now that you've unlocked access to your first advanced medical facility, you will find you can also access the healthcare policy menu. 

Changing your healthcare policy will change the benefits your hospitals provide across your empire. They'll boost growth rates and can help you generate food, research or increase your Prosperity levels.
sth_q_events.3100.name: Healthy mind, healthy body
sth_q_events.3110.desc:
It never ceases to amaze me how much you mortals love shiny things. I mean, at least you don't use silly paper pieces any more, but honestly, MONEY? How gauche.

Now that you have a proper stellar empire, your people will generate £ $TRADE_VALUE$. That will automatically be converted into £ $sr_latinum$, which I hear is all the rage nowadays. $sr_latinum$ is spent by building starports on your planets. Starports will produce traders that will exchange £ $sr_latinum$ for a variety of special goods. You can even use decisions to change what your traders on each planet are importing.

By having a series of starports across your empire you can accumulate all sorts of goods you might be missing!
sth_q_events.3110.name: Starport Xtreme
sth_q_events.32.desc:
Every few years, the Klingon High Council will convene to decide important matters of state. Well, in between the drunken fighting and singing that is. 

The next Chancellor can be nominated here, and feuds between the Great Houses can be ended. 

Resources can also be contributed and withdrawn from the militias each Great House maintains.
sth_q_events.32.name: The High Council
sth_q_events.40.desc:
Allow me to introduce myself. I am Q. Just Q. From the Q Continuum. 

You Romulans have quite the penchant for deception, don't you? With your Tal Shiar out there doing your dirty work. Not that I don't find you far more amusing than those stuffy Vulcans you descended from. 

The challenges ahead for you are such that you'd be a fool to turn down the offer of assistance I now make to you. 

As a token of my generosity, I'd like to offer you an... uh, hmm. Ah! Have a nice shiny mineral. 

Now go ahead, ask me anything!
sth_q_events.40.name: Jolan Tru to you!
sth_q_events.41.desc:
The Tal Shiar is the pinnacle of the Romulan capacity for duplicity and general underhandedness. Meeting in alternate years, its governing subcommittee can be directed to target Romulus's enemies, both at home and abroad. 

Foreign relations can be sabotaged, alongside their ships and buildings. In the same vein, troublesome leaders can be... disappeared. 

The amount of overall funding assigned to the Tal Shiar can be set in the Policies interface under the 'Espionage' selection. This will have a substantial impact on the success rate of various actions, but it can be offset by the 'Counter-Espionage' funding levels in the target empire.
sth_q_events.41.name: Tal Shiar
sth_q_events.6000.desc:
So you finally did it. You've put your ridiculously inconsequential differences aside and formed a federation with your neighbours. Kudos to you! 

But don't forget that a federation is for life, not just for the Lohlunat. 

You'll notice your influence income has fallen. You don't have to be omniscient like me to realise this is a consequence of the difficulties inherent to forming a new galactic state. But don't worry, it won't last forever. 

Now, do I have to hold your hand with anything else here?
sth_q_events.6000.name: Federation? Whoop-ti-doo!
sth_q_events.6001.desc:
Your Federation may be a state in its own right, but the homeworlds of your members are governed by their own politicians. 

Member Worlds will organise their own local defence forces, and contribute to the Federation budget. They may even be able to contribute unique technologies through the Federation Council. 

Just don't expect to be deciding what [member_world_1.GetSpeciesName] school children eat for lunch.
sth_q_events.6001.name: You can't control everything...
sth_q_events.6002.desc:
Every other year your Federation Council will sit in session. Frankly I find the whole process to be utterly boring, but to someone of lesser potential such as yourself the outcome may end up being very useful. 

The Council allows you to stick your nose into your neighbours' affairs, either by inviting them into your little club, or praising or condemning their behaviour. 

You can also consult with your science advisors to boost research or fund projects from your Member Worlds.
sth_q_events.6002.name: Who doesn't like a good Council?
sth_q_events.6003.desc:
So you're growing bored of your current Member Worlds and want to bring in some fresh blood, so to say? Well to get the ball rolling on inviting the neighbours into your Federation, you'll need to start negotiations through the Federation Council. 

Keep in mind that you can only invite governments that are already in a coalition with you. 

Every other year you'll be able to commit more resources to the integration process, until eventually all that remains is the accession signing party.
sth_q_events.6003.name: Grow, grow, grow.
sth_q_events.6020.desc:
As the years go by I suppose retirement holds a certain appeal to mortals like yourself. 
That appeal can stretch to starships too. 
While a ship class may have served you well for decades there comes a point when it is kinder to move on... such as when you unlock their replacements. 
A short grace period will allow you to maintain and upgrade these designs, but eventually you should consider decommissioning them. 
Worry not though, as there are ways to prevent this. 
Building a Naval Museum and interacting with it will allow a small number of older ship classes to be re-activated.
sth_q_events.6020.name: Retiring ships
sth_q_events.6040.desc:
Ah yes, truly wonderful crystals! 

You can accumulate them in a few different ways - either build a mine on planets that have a £ Dilithium deposit on them, or build a mining station around a £ Dilithium deposit on some forsaken asteroid.

Dilithium is the key to build and maintain the warp engines of your burgeoning space fleet, so make sure you exploit every deposit of it that you find!
sth_q_events.6040.name: Dilithium for the Masses
sth_q_events.6050.desc:
You know you really should be careful when playing with high explosives. But then again what do I care if you get burned? 

You've just unlocked the ability to build minefields. Go to your quaint little starbase and then go to your facilities menu . 

Once you've done that you can build it just like any other starbase facility, just be careful.
sth_q_events.6050.name: Minefields, eh?
sth_q_events.6100.desc:
You strange beings with your mewling cries over finding a livable part of the galaxy to live. Don't you know how rare it is to find a world just like your own? Well now you can complain that a planet is either too hot, too wet, or too cold for your needs.

I'm sure if you are from a cold world that you aren't too bothered about whether it's glacial or arctic. Likewise, someone from a hot world isn't going to worry about whether the desert has oases within it, or just scorched rock.

That means habitable colonisable worlds are now categorised into Torrid, Wet, Polar or if you're a crystalline-based lifeform and want to be special, Demon whilst maintaining their uniqueness.
sth_q_events.6100.name: I'm too hot. I'm too cold.
sth_q_events.6200.desc: Districts - they have changed again? Well it doesn't matter really, I'll tell you what is what.\n\nThe different district types now provide jobs that produce many of your basic resources like energy, minerals, food, research and unity to your little pops, whereas most buildings that used to produce these now instead provide a percentage boost dependent to planetary output based on how many buildings of a certain type you have. So if you want more food, you can build more agricultural districts or you can build more base farming buildings to boost the output of the farming jobs that you already have.
sth_q_events.6200.name: Districtly Come Dancing
sth_q_events.6201.desc: Want to specialise your planets as a penal colony or perhaps a giant fortress world? This is now available as a decision that can be taken on any planet that is habitable and isn't your capital world once you have researched the relevant technology. When you specialise your planet, new unique districts will appear on the planet allowing you to further boost output in your chosen specialisation.
sth_q_events.6201.name: Districtly Come Dancing
sth_q_events.6300.desc:
You Borg and your assimilating ways. You're almost as bad as the insipid Federation. You like to assimilate things, we all get it. Here's the catch - it's extremely costly on the Collective's resources to assimilate new lifeforms and bring them into harmony with the Collective, and this is simplified nice and easily for you in 'War Exhaustion'.

When you capture, assimilate or harvest entire worlds, your war exhaustion will rise dramatically to simulate the discordance brought upon the Collective by the new minds. Be careful not to overtax yourselves, or else it could be early curtains for the Borg's dream of perfection. Lacy gentling wafting curtains.

So stop every few worlds and have a little rest. You've all earnt it.
sth_q_events.6300.name: War Exhaustion and You
sth_q_events.6301.desc: Ah there is nothing better than toiling the day away... who am I kidding, we Q have no use for such chores. However the Undine have a great benefit from sending out their Undine Gatherers out into Fluidic Space and beyond to generate resources. What better way to use your excess pops?! Simply build the Undine Toiler Outpost on one of your bio-worlds and you will have an abundance of Gatherer drones from your pool of simple drones. Voila!
sth_q_events.6301.name: Toiling the day away
sth_q_events.6400.desc:
Unless you want to get up to your ears in micromanaging every aspect of your little space empire, creating sectors is advisable. 

You can do this by first going to your homeworld and creating your first sector from there, by choosing the Create New Sector option. After which you are free to create new sectors from planets of your own choosing. Provided of course that they are more than 4 jumps away from your first sector. 

And once you have painstakingly got your new sector set, you can choose a focus for it from the Planets and Sectors menu, after which your sector will valiantly try to follow it, as long as it has resources of course. You can even point a leader for your sector to oversee the dirty work being done there. So that you don`t have to.

Simple really.
sth_q_events.6400.name: Sectors. How do they work?
sth_q_events.7050.desc:
Well, well... the human desire to have what is not allowed. You may be aware that there are now certain systems which you may want to own, but you are unable to hold. 

There are now two new types of systems which you cannot own. The 1st type are environmental systems, such as certain black holes, nebulas, or hazard systems, these are designed to split up the map for gameplay reasons. 

The second type called event systems, are added for event purposes and may serve a story purpose at a later stage.
sth_q_events.7050.name: What is so special about a special system?
sth_q_events.7060.desc:
Recent advances in Field Manipulation research have led to the invention of Cloaking Devices. These power-intensive components allow for the selective bending of light (and other forms of energy) to render a starship or other object completely invisible to the electromagnetic spectrum and most sensors, allowing our science and military vessels to hide from conventional sensors.

However, these cloaking fields will negatively affect $MOD_SHIP_BASE_SPEED_MULT$, $MOD_SHIP_WINDUP_MULT$ and $MOD_SHIP_SHIELD_HP_ADD$ while active.

Cloaked ships that remain undetected can ignore Closed Borders.
While Cloaked, our Science Ships will be capable of conducting Active Reconnaissance on foreign colonies, increasing the Intel we gain on other empires.
sth_q_events.7060.name: Cloaking, hide and seek
sth_q_events.7061.desc: Recent advances in Computing research have led to the invention of Starbase buildings capable of detecting cloaked ships. Installing these high-powered external sensors on our Starbases will grant the ability to detect Cloaked fleets.\n\nThe sensitivity of these scanners can be increased by researching the next level scanner or building multiple scanners on a Starbase. With further research, we should be able to use Science Ships to further increase boost these detection capabilities. The range of these devices is limited by the starbase's Sensor Range.
sth_q_events.7061.name: Cloaking Detection
sth_setup.1.a.tooltip: Confirm your current selected empires and move on to the next step.
sth_setup.1.b: Select from the well known minors.
sth_setup.1.desc: Ah so you wish to select how you want your galaxy to look manually, well thankfully your friendly Q has given you that option.\n\nYou can select from the more famous empires or you can dig deep into your knowledge of Trek history and select from the more obscure species that have cropped up over the years! The choice is yours mon capitaine!
sth_setup.1.name: Manually add Minors
sth_setup.2.ant.tooltip: Made an appearance in both Enterprise and Lower Decks. The species were involved in a long war with the Denobulans prior to the events of Enterprise.
sth_setup.2.baku.tooltip: Appeared in Star Trek: Insurrection as the ageless native species of the planet Ba'ku. Some of them can't swim.
sth_setup.2.bar.tooltip: The Barzan's and their wormhole appeared in TNG, with Discovery expanding on them from their second series onwards.
sth_setup.2.brik.tooltip: The big rock people from the novels and Rok-Tok (ST:PROD).
sth_setup.2.cap.tooltip: Species and planet appear in TOS and is expanded upon in the novels with the introduction of Leonard James Akaar.
sth_setup.2.chal.tooltip: Big and ugly, dont mess with them!
sth_setup.2.cor.tooltip: The natives of Coridan III, appeared across Trek history and beta sources list the empire as an early member of the fledgling UFP with large dilithium deposits.
sth_setup.2.desc: These empires are considered some of the more well-known minor races, feel free to select them all or add each empire individually.
sth_setup.2.edo.tooltip: A rather lovely species, with a unique sense of dress... don't step on the flowers Wesley!
sth_setup.2.edos.tooltip: Three armed species who were seen as part of TAS.
sth_setup.2.efr.tooltip: Background species that hosted at least one Federation President and in the novels an engineer who helped design the Luna-Class.
sth_setup.2.ela.tooltip: Melora Pazlar appears as a representative of this race in DS9.
sth_setup.2.graz.tooltip: The Grazerites were a sentient, humanoid species, whose homeworld was a member of the United Federation of Planets by the late 24th century and held high-ranking positions within the Federation.
sth_setup.2.kelp.tooltip: Made famous in Star Trek Discovery, thanks to Saru and the awesome performance from Doug Jones.
sth_setup.2.kree.tooltip: Like good table manners, ritual cutting up of trees, don't let your Beagle pee on their trees!
sth_setup.2.kta.tooltip: Watch out their games are more addicitve than STNH.
sth_setup.2.mal.tooltip: Appears in the TNG episode 'First Contact'.
sth_setup.2.mari.tooltip: Telepathic race from the Delta Quadrant with rather strict views on violent thoughts.
sth_setup.2.mir.tooltip: The Miradorn were a spacefaring humanoid species from the Alpha Quadrant. According to Quark, they were typically known for being 'a quarrelsome people'.
sth_setup.2.miz.tooltip: Mizarians valued peaceful non-resistance over confrontation; because of this reputation, they were conquered six times in a matter of three centuries. The Mizarians survived these invasions by offering absolutely no resistance.
sth_setup.2.name: Manually add Minors
sth_setup.2.oca.tooltip: Made famous by Star Trek Voyager and the character Kes.
sth_setup.2.or.tooltip: Makes a memorable appearance in TNG episode 'Symbiosis'.
sth_setup.2.pac.tooltip: Available to those with the Aquatics DLC Only.
sth_setup.2.risa.tooltip: Who hasn't heard of Risa? All that is ours is yours.
sth_setup.3.akaa.tooltip: Pre-industrial civilisation seen in ENT 'Civilization' who later achieve warp travel by the 32nd Century.
sth_setup.3.akr.tooltip: Delta Quadrant empire with a harsh draconian justice system.
sth_setup.3.alt.tooltip: Referenced across Star Trek in particular in the 'Amok Time' episode.
sth_setup.3.ant.tooltip: Native to the planet Antos IV and famous for their benevolence and peaceful pursuits... according to Spock.
sth_setup.3.arba.tooltip: A Federation member world with whom the Bolian Ambassador Vadosia described as rather repressed.
sth_setup.3.ard.tooltip: Famed for the Anti-Grav city of Stratos in the TOS episode 'The Cloud Minders'.
sth_setup.3.are.tooltip: Debuting in 'ST Lower Decks' when Peanut Hamper crash lands on their planet to her dismay.
sth_setup.3.arg.tooltip: The Argelians are a humanoid species from the planet Argelius II in the Beta Quadrant. Some Argelians distinguish themselves by the street they lived on. From TOS 'Wolf in the Fold'.
sth_setup.3.atr.tooltip: A tip of the hat to Frank Herbert's Dune novels.
sth_setup.3.cal.tooltip: Caldonians are a tall humanoid species with dark brown skin. They have a large cranium that housed their advanced brains perfect for negotiating the sale of the Barzan wormhole in TNG's The Price.
sth_setup.3.dene.tooltip: Humanoid natives, the Bandi culture were referenced in 'Encounter at Farpoint'.
sth_setup.3.desc: These empires represent a trivia master's guide to Star Trek! Add these empires to your galaxy to flesh out your galaxy. \n\nSelect them all or add each empire individually.
sth_setup.3.doop.tooltip: Perhaps the most annoying new race introduced in STLD... oh no they're duplicating!
sth_setup.3.dor.tooltip: North American Indian tribe who settled on the planet of Dorvan V, in what would later become the DMZ.
sth_setup.3.dra.tooltip: Feline species famous for perhaps, 1,2 or 3 things in particular.
sth_setup.3.drac.tooltip: Reptilian species from Draconia, may have also appeared on Doctor Who.
sth_setup.3.ekos.tooltip: The result of non-interference gone very wrong. From the TOS episode 'Patterns of Force'.
sth_setup.3.elt.tooltip: Think Greek mythology, but with more TOS era Trek!
sth_setup.3.ev.tooltip: Diminutive species seen at the start of 'Star Trek Insurrection'.
sth_setup.3.gal.tooltip: First seen in the pilot of Lower Decks, friendly culture... mind the spiders and bugs!
sth_setup.3.hill.tooltip: Home of a pre-warp civilisation and the Mugatos.
sth_setup.3.iot.tooltip: The result of leaving Chicago Mobs of the Twenties unattended during a visit to the planet in TOS 'A Piece of the Action'.
sth_setup.3.kel.tooltip: Humanoid species native to Kelis' homeworld, a Class L planet located in the Delta Quadrant. Individuals of the species believed in the existence of beings whom they referred to as Eternals.
sth_setup.3.kil.tooltip: Natives of Kiley 279 as seen in ST SNW opening episode.
sth_setup.3.kre.tooltip: Reptillian species close to the Cardassian Border known for selling Botanical DNA.
sth_setup.3.lap.tooltip: The empire involved in Carol Freeman's First First Contact.
sth_setup.3.mad.tooltip: The Coalition of Madena was an interplanetary alliance of two inhabited planets in the Omega Sagitta system, Atlec and Straleb. Both worlds were populated by a humanoid race who colonized the system during the 22nd century, and co-existed, according to Jean-Luc Picard, 'under a precarious but successful treaty'.
sth_setup.3.menk.tooltip: Appeared in Enterprise Series 1. In which Phlox faced a moral dilemma.
sth_setup.3.min.tooltip: You should ask yourself, Who Watches the Watchers?
sth_setup.3.name: Manually add Minors
sth_setup.3.onlies.tooltip: Earth-like planet that was devastated by a horrific degenerative disease and is now populated entirely by impossibly old children.
sth_setup.3.tark.tooltip: Referenced throughout trek history, but first seen in Enterprise's Regeneration episode, in which the Borg took a shine to them.
sth_setup.3.tarl.tooltip: Green skilled race featuring in the TOS novel, Planet of Judgment.
sth_setup.3.ter.tooltip: It was inhabited by Humans transplanted there by time traveler Gabrielle Burnham, who they revered as the Red Angel.
sth_setup.3.tor.tooltip: One of several clans of an unnamed race native to an unnamed planet. They were the ruling caste on the planet, where they lived in Torothan City, and were opposed by Zobral's clan of rebels.
sth_setup.3.ver.tooltip: A famous Starship was once hot-dropped onto a planet in their neighborhood during Star Trek Generations.
sth_setup.3.xah.tooltip: Having appeared in the DIS short trek episode and then again in Series 2, this species is an expert in manipulating Dilithium.
sth_setup.5.desc: These empires will make up your main allies, rivals and competitors during your STNH playthrough. These screens will allow you manually select the empires you wish to add to your galaxy map, depending on the map size you have picked!
sth_setup.5.name: Manually add Majors
sth_setup.6.ant.response: Antedians are fish-like in appearance, but of similar size as many other humanoids and capable of bipedal motion. Worf liked them, Lwaxana Troi thought they looked like dinner.
sth_setup.6.antsel.tooltip: Two waring species from the Beta Renner System with a history of eating each other!
sth_setup.6.aur.tooltip: The Aurelians are an ornithoid species who have been associated with the Federation since at least the mid-23rd century. Most frequently seen in the various animated Trek series.
sth_setup.6.baj.tooltip: May the Prophets guide you my child.
sth_setup.6.bet.tooltip: Well known species seen in TNG known for their telepathic skills and the Troi family.
sth_setup.6.bre.tooltip: The Breen are a reclusive, powerful, and warlike humanoid race native to the planet Breen in the Alpha Quadrant. Shrouded in mystery, the Breen are also one of the most underestimated races inhabiting that quadrant of space.
sth_setup.6.cat.tooltip: More than just cats in coats! First seen in TAS before cameoing in multiple films.
sth_setup.6.che.tooltip: Black and White, or White and Black race appearing in TOS: Let That Be Your Last Battlefield.
sth_setup.6.chel.tooltip: First appearing in TMP and confusingly named Rigellian, we've adapted this race based on appearances in Apocrypha.
sth_setup.6.del.tooltip: Another race that debuted in TMP and most recently season 2 of ST:PIC, they are physically indistinguishable from Humans but are identified by their bald scalps.
sth_setup.6.desc: Please select which empires from the Alpha and Beta Quadrants you would like to manually add to the playthrough. Remember that the Major powers are automatically included in each map.
sth_setup.6.fed.tooltip: Due to the mechanics involved the Federation Founders must be added together. Including - Andorians, Vulcans, Tellarites, Rigellians, Denobulans and Trill.
sth_setup.6.gor.tooltip: Reptile species that first appeared in TOS and again throughout Trek history, most recently with terrifying new characteristics in SNW series 1.
sth_setup.6.hus.tooltip: An aggressive and hostile race thought extinct, however anything is possible in STNH.
sth_setup.6.hydr.tooltip: Species that appeared in Star Trek Fleet Command as the enemy of the Lyrans.
sth_setup.6.kes.tooltip: Appearing in arguably one of the best ST games, Bridge Commander, we couldnt wait to bring them into the mod.
sth_setup.6.kho.tooltip: Another novel only species of bat-like military theocrasists, why wouldnt we want them in the mod??
sth_setup.6.kri.tooltip: The vast Kriosian Empire was once ruled by two brothers, named Krios and Valt. Before a centuries-long war between the two worlds and subdivided the species into the Valtese and the Kriosians.
sth_setup.6.kzi.tooltip: Aggressive Feline species that went to war with Earth on at least 4 occassions, they have only appeared in the animated shows.
sth_setup.6.lis.tooltip: Appeared frequently in DS9 often getting in between the Cardassians and Maquis.
sth_setup.6.lur.tooltip: Who mourns for Morn? Answer, us of course!
sth_setup.6.lyr.tooltip: Appearing in the game 'Starfleet Command' The Lyran Star Empire is the name of the official state of the Lyran species. Not much is known about it except that it is somewhat feudal.
sth_setup.6.med.tooltip: Non corporeal race that appeared in early TOS and more recently in ST Prodigy with the young Medusan named Zero.
sth_setup.6.mor.tooltip: Exploited and potentially hunted by the Orions, this former power is rebuilding. Expanded by the mod after appearing in FASA.
sth_setup.6.name: Empires of the Alpha and Beta Quadrants
sth_setup.6.nau.tooltip: Strong, powerful and bad tempered species, within minimal government, who prefer working as pirates hunting in the Alpha Quadrant.
sth_setup.6.ori.tooltip: One of the older races in the quadrant, the Orions have known to be involved in the shader side of the galaxy.
sth_setup.6.sau.tooltip: The Saurians are a reptilian humanoid species affiliated with the Federation.
sth_setup.6.she.tooltip: The Sheliak Corporate is the official governing body of the Sheliak having been involved in a brief conflict with the United Federation of Planets. Eventually, the conflict was resolved with the signing of the Treaty of Armens.
sth_setup.6.so.tooltip: Playing a significant part in ST Insurrection as well as playing a vital part in the Dominion War.
sth_setup.6.tal.tooltip: The Talarian Republic is the sovereign nation of the Talarians. Its capital planet is the Talarian homeworld in the Alpha Quadrant.
sth_setup.6.tho.tooltip: Silicoid species that have appeared across ST history made famous for their webs, silk and love of the heat.
sth_setup.6.tib.tooltip: Background race that have appeared across Trek and in particular in DS9.
sth_setup.6.tze.tooltip: The Tzenkethi Coalition is a state located in Alpha Quadrant near the Cardassian Union and Ferengi Alliance. Mentioned in DS9 and expanded upon in the novels.
sth_setup.6.val.tooltip: Another species added in DS9 working with the Cardassians during their occupation of Bajor.
sth_setup.6.xep.tooltip: The Xepolite Guilds have a unique governmental structure. They appear in DS9 and are known for their illicit dealings with the Cardassians.
sth_setup.6.yri.tooltip: The Yridians are intergalactic information brokers and smugglers, often considered devious and untrustworthy by other races.
sth_setup.6.zak.tooltip: The Zakdorn are a sentient, humanoid species who appeared in TNG. Known for their strategic minds.
sth_setup.7.arg.response: Appeared in DS9 episode 'Hard Time' and inflicted another punish O'Brien episode!
sth_setup.7.desc: Please select which empires from the Gamma Quadrant that you would like to manually add to the playthrough. Remember that the Major powers are automatically included in each map.
sth_setup.7.kar.response: Appearing as one of the friendlier members of the Dominion and then again in Lower Decks.
sth_setup.7.name: Empires of the Gamma Quadrant
sth_setup.7.nyb.response: Not much is known about the Nyberrite Alliance, except a brief mention in the fantastic episode of DS9 'The Way of the Warrior'.
sth_setup.7.os.response: One of the 1st species from the Gamma Quadrant seen in the 6th episode of DS9 'Captive Pursuit'.
sth_setup.7.pa.response: Debuted in an DS9 episode called 'Whispers' and then appeared as background species throughout the rest of the series.
sth_setup.7.rak.response: Not much is known about the Rakhari except they believe the Changelings are a myth... so not the brightest.
sth_setup.7.tro.response: During the 16th century, the T'Rogoran conquered a race of farmers known as the Skrreeans.
sth_setup.7.wad.response: Allamaraine, count to four, Allamaraine, then three more... We'll say no more!
sth_setup.7.yad.response: Another victim of the Dominion from the Gamma Quadrant, seen in DS9 'Shadowplay'.
sth_setup.8.ban.response: The Banea are a feathered humanoid species native to Banea seen in Voyager.
sth_setup.8.bru.response: The species in which Icheb belonged, before they cast him to the Borg.
sth_setup.8.cp.response: Waring machine races from Voyager, who destroyed their creators and continuned their war.
sth_setup.8.desc: Please select which empires from the Delta Quadrant that you would like to manually add to the playthrough. Remember that the Major powers are automatically included in each map.
sth_setup.8.dev.response: The Devore Imperium is the authoritarian government of the Devore in the Delta Quadrant. As of 2376, they controlled eleven star systems across three sectors. (VOY: 'Dragon's Teeth')
sth_setup.8.el.response: Made famous by Guinan, little is known about their society pre-Borg. Their future is undecided in STNH.
sth_setup.8.hat.response: Locked in a brutal war and occupation that is hidden by the charismatic outward appearance of the Talaxians.
sth_setup.8.haz.response: Reptillian species known for their mercenary behaviour.
sth_setup.8.kob.response: A unique species that added to their population by reanimating the deceased corpses of other species, altering their DNA to transform them into fellow Kobali.
sth_setup.8.kra.response: The Kraylor are a humanoid species indigenous to the Delta Quadrant. One distinguishing characteristic is their gill-like structures that extends from the bottom of their ears down the ridge of their jaws.
sth_setup.8.kt.response: The Kazon were first seen in the 1st episode of Voyager and their history with the Trabe was expanded on in season 2.
sth_setup.8.ly.response: Very little is known about the Lyridians, in this mod we have combined the species with Jaylah's from Star Trek Beyond.
sth_setup.8.ma.response: Very literal garbage people, prepared to dump their waste on others to keep their society clean.
sth_setup.8.mo.response: Aquatic species living within the oceans of an artificially created water world.
sth_setup.8.name: Empires of the Delta Quadrant
sth_setup.8.nor.response: The Norcadians invented the popular, much watched, and violent martial arts sport of tsunkatse.
sth_setup.8.tur.response: They are the keepers of the underspace corridors that traversed the Delta Quadrant. This underspace was once the property of the Vaadwaur.
sth_setup.8.vau.response: Seen in Star Trek Prodigy, we'll be expanding this species as more is revealed.
sth_setup.8.vid.response: For two millennia, the entire race suffered from a disease called the Phage that ate skin and internal organs, and damaged their DNA.
sth_setup.8.za.response: Appeared at the start of the Voyager 2 parter 'The Year of Hell'.
sth_start.100.a: I would like the extra challenge.
sth_start.100.desc: Greetings Grand Admirals! We've noted that you are playing on the highest difficulty level, which suggests you enjoy a challenge. If you are interested in a further challenge that keeps the canonic major powers strong, then we invite you to try our Extra Challenge Mode \n\nDev Note: Please note you will lose no game content by opting into this mode and it can be disabled at any time by firing event id sth_start.100 from the console.
sth_start.100.name: An Extra Challenge
sth_start.1001.a.tooltip: Challenge Mode. Available for players playing Grand Admiral artificially gives a boost to major empires to give the toughest STNH experience.
sth_start.1001.b.tooltip: Apply a scaling modifier to each empire slowing down technology progression.
sth_start.1001.borg.tooltip: Warning. This is a one time option that will remove the Borg and all Borg events from the game, it can only be picked on game start and is irreversible.
sth_start.1001.borg1.tooltip: This mode activates a feature that puts the Borg into a sleep cycle for a decreasing amount of years (length decreases as each era passes) this sleep cycle prevents the Borg from declaring war whilst they are in it.
sth_start.1001.borg2.tooltip: This mode activates a feature that puts the Borg into a sleep cycle for a decreasing amount of years (length decreases as each era passes) this sleep cycle prevents the Borg from declaring war whilst they are in it.
sth_start.1001.c.tooltip: If you would like to experience a number of wars from the series, this is the mode for you.
sth_start.1001.can.tooltip: Sets a modifier than heavily influences the AI to pick the canon decision if presented with the option. Note that scenarios still occur depending on the game state. Dev Note: This is WIP. Decisions will be added over time.
sth_start.1001.crisis1.tooltip: All STNH Crises will have the normal random chance of spawning. \n\nIncluding - The Dominion War, Iconian War, Andromedan Invasion, Borg Crisis & Romulan Supernova.
sth_start.1001.crisis2.tooltip: All STNH crises have been disabled and will not spawn. \n\nIncluding - The Dominion War, Iconian War, Andromedan Invasion, Borg Crisis & Romulan Supernova.
sth_start.1001.d.tooltip: This mode is available if the AI is playing any of the Federation Founders, which will help ensure the UFP is formed in your playthrough.
sth_start.1001.desc_01: Ah greeting mon capitaine! Yes it's your benevolent supreme STNH overlord Q here, I see you are gearing up for another game of ST: New Horizons, before you do we must set up your galaxy with a click of my finger.
sth_start.1001.e.tooltip: Begin the game with the current set up, any options not selected will automatically be switched off.
sth_start.1001.ethics.tooltip: Our testing has shown pop ethics and pop factions cause additional load on the mod; they can be managed here via this menu.
sth_start.1001.ethics: Manage STNH Ethics and Pop Factions.
sth_start.1001.k.tooltip: An event will run every 5 years once you are 60 years into the game, which may randomly promote a minor empire to a major empire.
sth_start.1001.k: Increased Minor to Major Promotions - ON.
sth_start.1001.l.tooltip: Minor Empires will only experience a very small chance of being promoted to a major or medium empire, as it has always been.
sth_start.1001.l: Increased Minor to Major Promotions OFF.
sth_start.1001.name: The Q Continuum
sth_start.1001.navalpen.tooltip: Adds an additional harsh penalty to ship upkeep if the country goes over the Navy Cap.
sth_start.1001.nsurvey1.tooltip: Return survey speeds to Vanilla levels.
sth_start.1001.qpedia.tooltip: Access the mod Q-tips and specifically created guide articles to help you navigate the STNH experience. Dev Note: These guides are a continued WIP, for more information join us in our Discord server or check our FAQs. Are we missing something, feel free to have a go at writing it and send it to us.
sth_start.1001.qpedia: Access the knowledge of the Q.
sth_start.1001.sb1.tooltip: Enabling Sandbox Mode will prevent the vast majority of story-based events from firing, this includes Crisis events. Species Mechanics and crucial events such as UFP formation will still occur with the same chance as normal.
sth_start.1001.sb2.tooltip: Disabling Sandbox Mode will allow all story events to fire as normal. Events are still subject to the usual RNG odds.
sth_start.1001.storm1.tooltip: Storms will spawn if you have the Cosmic Storms DLC, but be aware they do carry a small performance decrease.
sth_start.1001.storm2.tooltip: Prevents storms from triggering regardless of whether you have them active.
sth_start.1001.survey1.tooltip: Slowdown expansion of the galaxy by significantly increasing survey time. Dev Note: This mode is still under beta testing, so play with caution.
sth_start.1001.tng.tooltip: This option will allow you to unlock specific technologies not part of the TNG era setup or pick technologies choices that are not enabled by default.
sth_start.1001.war1.tooltip: This mode creates more war scenarios resulting in countries that dislike you being more inclined to declare war on you, even if you think your fleet power makes you safe.
sth_start.1005.a.tooltip: This option creates all available minor races depending on whether their homeworld spawned as part of the map size option you selected.\n\nWarning! This option is intensive and will take some time to complete as it creates all the minor empires.
sth_start.1005.b.tooltip: The combination of minor/warp-capable races will be selected from those available at random.
sth_start.1005.c.tooltip: Automatically selects the empires appropriate to the quadrant map size you have selected.
sth_start.1005.c: Create Quadrant Specific minors and Warp-Capables.
sth_start.1005.d.tooltip: Manually decide which minor empires to add to your game.
sth_start.1005.desc:
The galaxy is full of tiny empires, doing their tiny empire things, all of which are far beneath the Q, but the more empires the more we get to meddle... what fun! 

From this screen you can define how many Star Trek minors and Warp Capable species you would like to have in this playthrough. 

The empires available depend on the map you have selected and remember if you try to create all minors at once, it will take time to process, if your setup can't handle it, load them one at a time. 

Now don't forget the powers of the Q are almighty and once an empire has been created it cannot be undone! Now go enjoy!
sth_start.1005.desc_01: You are almost there! You now need to set the tech level of the Minor Races... \n\nDev Note: Adding the TNG era technologies is a resource-intensive activity and can take between 2-5mins to complete, during which time the game may freeze, let it complete and it will.
sth_start.1005.e.tooltip: Moves onto the next screen. Warning: Once selected you cannot come back and add more empires.
sth_start.1005.mult.tooltip: Creates a random selection of minors, which is less taxing on the multiplayer setup. It should only be selected by one player.
sth_start.1005.name: Galaxy Creation - Minor Empires
sth_start.1005.tng.tooltip: This option will add all the applicable TNG era Minor Empires and convert them to the TNG era. \n\nDev Note: This is an intensive action and could take up to 5 minutes to complete, at this point, it may appear to have hung... allow the process to complete and the next screen will appear.
sth_start.1006.a.tooltip: Add all appropriate Alpha Quadrant Major Powers.
sth_start.1006.b.tooltip: Add all appropriate Beta Quadrant Major Powers.
sth_start.1006.c.tooltip: Add all appropriate Gamma Quadrant Major Powers.
sth_start.1006.d.tooltip: Add all appropriate Delta Quadrant Major Powers.
sth_start.1006.desc:
The galaxy is run by the powerful, but thankfully there is always a bigger fish and the Q are the sharks!!... Now where was I? 

From this screen you can define how many Star Trek main species you would like to have in this playthrough. 

The empires available depend on the map you have selected and remember if you try to create all majors at once, it will take time to process, if your setup can't handle it, add them by Quadrants. 

Now don't forget the powers of the Q are almighty and once an empire has been created it cannot be undone! Now go enjoy!
sth_start.1006.desc_01:
Ahh, can you smell the winds of change in the air? It's the dawn of the 2300, but It's been a long road, getting from there to here... well actually it's been a few clicks of the galactic map! 

Now I suppose you will want your good pal Q to snap his fingers and transition from 2150 to 2300? Well, it's not quite that simple, I have selected the major empires for you to play as... but remember I am mending the rules of time and space here, so please wait and be patient while the magic happens! 

Dev Note: Adding the TNG era technologies is a resource intensive activity and can take between 2-5mins to complete, during which time the game may freeze, allow the process to complete and it will.
sth_start.1006.desc_02:
Ahh the Mirror Universe is so alike, yet so different. A playground for a little chaos! Now I imagine you are going to want your benevolent Q just to snap his mighty fingers and make it 2300 already? Hah Humans are so predictable! 

The course of history is so fragile and all it takes are a few small changes to set the galaxy off on a different track... I can't wait to see what will happen next Mon ami. 

Dev Note: Adding the TNG era technologies is a resource intensive activity and can take between 2-5mins to complete, during which time the game may freeze, allow the process to complete and it will.
sth_start.1006.h: Manually add major Empires and Factions.
sth_start.1006.mult.tooltip: Select this option if you are playing multiplayer and the host or another player is selecting the player conditions and you don't want to duplicate them.
sth_start.1006.name: Galaxy Creation - Major Empires
sth_start.1006.tng.tooltip: This option will convert all the applicable TNG era Major Empires to the TNG era. Dev Note: This is an intensive action and could take up to 10 minutes to complete, during which time the game may freeze, allow the process to complete and the next screen will appear.
sth_start.102.a: Ensure the formation of the UFP.
sth_start.102.desc:
For players who enjoy a more canon version of the STNH game, which includes the United Federation of Planets with all four member worlds having formed the Federation in the early 2160s then this mode can be switched on. 

As long as each of the four member worlds are played by the AI and they all exist by the time the Babel conference is due to take place then this mode will assist in the formation of the Federation. If any of the Founding member states are played by the player then the game will continue without interfering. 

This mode is entirely optional and if left off the formation of the Federation will progress, or not, without force.
sth_start.102.name: The United Federation of Planets
sth_start.110.desc: We recognise that many of our players enjoy to emulate a canon version of the Star Trek universe and to have their technologies become available at the 'right' time. For those fans who want this type of experience we have a game modifier that artifically slows progress to match the era. Should you wish to remove the modifier (non-ironman games only) then run event sth_start.110 to bring this screen back up.
sth_start.110.name: The Great Technology Race (beta)
sth_start.1200.desc:
Disabling Pop Factions and Ethics has been shown to provide a boost to game performance, but at the cost of pops having ethics other than their countries and forming factions. 

Currently, this mode is set to: 

Ethics & Factions ON.
sth_start.1200.desc_01:
Disabling Pop Factions and Ethics has been shown to provide a boost to game performance, but at the cost of pops having ethics other than their countries and forming factions. 

Currently, this mode is set to: 

Ethics & Factions ON for human players.
sth_start.1200.desc_02:
Disabling Pop Factions and Ethics has been shown to provide a boost to game performance, but at the cost of pops having ethics other than their countries and forming factions. 

Currently, this mode is set to: 

Ethics & Factions OFF for all.
sth_start.1200.name: Pop Ethics & Faction Options
sth_start.13.desc: Important Alert!: \n\nThere has been a rare error in the map set up when this game was loaded, and this may have caused a corrupted map. We strongly recommend that you return to the main menu and start a new game using the same settings.
sth_start.13.name: State Of The Galaxy
sth_start.16.desc:
You have selected a race that is not placed in the Delta Quadrant map. This is not supported and may lead to buggy situations. 

If you wish to know which races are located in the Delta Quadrant, either choose the Borg or one of the other green empires on the selection screen, or open the in-game ST:NH Wiki by pressing the '?' icon at the bottom of the UI. 

If you would prefer to continue to play as this race, we strongly advise you to start a new game either using the main galaxy maps or full quadrant maps. 

Alternatively you may proceed at your own risk.
sth_start.17.desc:
You have selected a race that is not placed in the Gamma Quadrant map. This is not supported and may lead to buggy situations. 

If you wish to know which races are located in the Gamma Quadrant, either choose the Dominion or one of the other purple empires on the selection screen, or open the in-game ST:NH Wiki by pressing the '?' icon at the bottom of the UI.

If you would prefer to continue to play as this race, we strongly advise you to start a new game either using the main galaxy maps or full quadrant maps. 

Alternatively you may proceed at your own risk.
sth_start.23.desc:
You have selected a minor race that is not placed in the 900 star Milky Way map.

This is not supported and may lead to buggy situations.

Proceed at your own risk.
sth_start.5.b.tooltip: Game may still be playable, but some bugs could occur. Your start location is unoptimised and random.
sth_start.5.desc:
You have selected a race that is not placed in the Alpha/Beta quadrant map. This is not supported and may lead to buggy situations.

If you wish to know which races are located in the Alpha and Beta Quadrants, either choose one of the blue or red empires from the empire selection screen or open the in-game ST:NH Wiki by pressing the '?' icon at the bottom of the UI.

If you would prefer to continue to play as this race, we strongly advise you to start a new game either using the main galaxy maps or full quadrant maps.

Alternatively you may proceed at your own risk.
sth_start.5.name: Warning! Non-Alpha/Beta Quadrant race selected!
sth_start.50.a.tooltip: This is the default galaxy, containing every single major species and the minor species you have previously selected. Minor races are randomised on game start and will not expand. This is considered the Default STNH experience.
sth_start.50.a: (Recommended) Default Play with all selected Empires.
sth_start.50.b.tooltip: This form of the galaxy contains ~25% of the regular empires, chosen by the developers and the minor empires previously selected. This is the most optimal in terms of performance.
sth_start.50.c.tooltip: This form of the galaxy contains ~50% of the regular empires, chosen by the developers and the minor empires selected in the previous screens.
sth_start.50.d.tooltip: This form of the galaxy contains ~75% of the regular empires, chosen by the developers and the minor empires selected in the previous screens.
sth_start.50.desc: The multiverse and timeline is constantly in flux. Please select your choice for the amount of Empires on the main map before gameplay begins in earnest. This will allow you to tune your experience and performance further.
sth_start.50.e.tooltip: This form of the galaxy is a special version created for running a full galaxy with only major empires capable of expanding. It has every single selected race but randomly reduces all selected minor races to static non-expanding empires only.
sth_start.50.e: (Randomly Convert:) Minor Empires to Static Empires.
sth_start.50.f.tooltip: This form of the galaxy contains ~25% of the regular empires, chosen randomly. Some minor empires will randomly be assigned major status or primitive status for randomisation.
sth_start.50.g.tooltip: This form of the galaxy contains ~50% of the regular empires, chosen randomly. Some minor empires will randomly be assigned major status or primitive status for randomisation.
sth_start.50.h.tooltip: This form of the galaxy contains ~75% of the regular empires, chosen randomly. Some minor empires will randomly be assigned major status or primitive status for randomisation.
sth_start.50.j.tooltip: A Beta Trial set up, which converts all selected Empires to Static, which means they will not expand.
sth_start.50.j: Convert all Minors Empires to Static Empires.
sth_start.50.k.tooltip: Special Blend of random major, medium and minor empires in random map positions.
sth_start.50.name: Start Conditions
sth_start.77.c: Set all Minors to Static (Add periodic pop increase).
sth_start.77.d: Randomly set Minors to Static (Add periodic pop increase).
sth_start.77.desc: Start a playthrough with a number or all of the minor and primitive races set to use static population growth. These countries will not add to the late game population density of the galaxy unless they progress to a full power or are absorbed into another larger empire, such as the UFP or Dominion. It is theorised that pop size contributes to late game lag.\n\nDev Note: Use at own risk and does not guarentee improved performance whilst in trial mode.
sth_start.77.f.tooltip: This experience is recommended for the full experience, with no empires downgraded to static. Run at own risk.
sth_start.77.name: Start Conditions
sth_start.999.desc_19:
Hello from us all at PDG! The 1st major patch of our 10th Anniversary year, ready to support the 4.3 release. 

We have included our 4.3 base compat changes, including significant changes to ship and fleet sizes. 

The economy changes from the base game have been worked into the mod 

Significant performance improvements have been included to give our best performance yet. 

Long running events and mechanics have been re-written and tightened up to reduce errors and improve performance. 

Many fixes and other changes listed in the patch notes. 

As always we will need your help to report balance and bugs as we work through this major change to STNH, We hope you enjoy this ST New Horizon's experience.

All the best from the PDG Team.
