The wildfires we started and the absence of the forests have led to the extinction of several local species, resulting in a domino effect that has claimed several species of predators as well.\n\nEfforts are ongoing to at least partly restore the fragile ecological balance by introducing new flora and fauna from other worlds, but it is a difficult process that will take time.
colony.9.name: Forest Adaptation
colony.9.desc: Continued interaction between our colonists and the wandering forests on [Root.GetName] has revealed that the trees are in possession of some form of rudimentary communal intelligence. They seem to have adapted to our presence, and now go out of their way to avoid damaging the property of our colonists even without the use of the insect pheromone. Spirits among the colonists have risen.
colony.10.name: Forest Research
colony.10.desc: We have learned a great deal by living in proximity of the wandering forests on [Root.GetName]. Some enterprising colonists have even built homes in the treetops of some of the larger specimens, following the forests around as they migrate across the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]. Some of the foremost biologists in the [Root.Owner.GetName] have spent time studying these unique trees.
colony.11.name: Forest Fire
colony.11.desc: A forest fire caused by lightning on [Root.GetName] created something akin to a stampede among the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s wandering forests. Their wild panic brought them into areas they had previously avoided, resulting in widespread damage to the colony's terrain.
colony.12.name: Wandering Forests Migrate
colony.12.desc: The colonial authorities on [Root.GetName] have reported that one of the local wandering forests has migrated to another region on the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]! Hundreds of thousands of trees have uprooted and marched in unison to this new area, trampling everything in their path.
colony.50.name: Seismic Disturbances
colony.50.desc: There have been many reports of unexplainable seismic disturbances on [Root.GetName]. Tremors can be felt across the [Root.GetPlanetMoon], and many of our settlements have suffered damage. While investigating the earthquakes, our scientists discovered a vast network of subterranean caverns.\n\nThe deepest of these caverns is home to some kind of indigenous civilization - and they appear to be tunneling towards the surface! Ground vibrations from our settlements may have triggered this behavior, but we cannot be certain. There is no telling how these aliens will react when they spill out of their tunnels and encounter our colonists!
colony.50.a: We have to deal with this situation.
colony.51.desc: One of our probes burrowed through the ground and intercepted the tunneling aliens on [From.From.GetName]. After the startled alien workers calmed down, the probe was successful in establishing communications with their leaders.
colony.52.desc: Our efforts to destroy the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower]. aliens on [Root.GetName] have met with success! We managed to collapse several of their tunnels on top of their workers, and the caverns that housed their cities have been flooded with nerve gas.\n\nCasualty projections are close to 100%, and our security forces are confident they will be able to deal with any surviving stragglers.
colony.52.a: Compliments to all service branches involved.
colony.53.name: [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleUpper] Aliens
colony.53.desc: Is this thing on? Can you see us, surfacers? We understand you hail from the Great Nothing that lies upwards, beyond the stone. It was assumed nothing living could exist there, but evidently this is not so. What are your intentions?
colony.53.b: We would like to coexist in peace.
colony.54.name: [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleUpper] Invasion!
colony.54.desc: The subterranean aliens on [Root.GetName] have emerged on the surface! Large armies equipped with primitive yet lethal projectile weapons are spilling from the tunnels, and their vanguard has already begun an assault on our settlements.\n\nOur security forces are responding, but we may need to send reinforcements!
subterranean_invasion: Hostile armies are attacking our colony on [Root.GetName]!
colony.56.name: Victory on [From.From.GetName]!
colony.56.desc: Our forces have managed to drive the subterranean aliens on [From.From.GetName] back into their underground lairs, and they are contained for the moment. This is a temporary solution, however, and it is only a matter of time before they break out onto the surface again.\n\nWe need to strike at them below ground, and our strategists have devised a new attack plan. It has been added to the Situation Log.
colony.56.a: We need to solve this problem for good.
colony.57.name: Defeat on [From.From.GetName]!
colony.57.desc: Our forces have been decimated on [From.From.GetName], and the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] aliens have taken full control over all of our settlements! They are using one of our own satellites to send a message to us.
colony.58.desc: Surface creatures! Our glorious armies have bested you in battle, and the spoils of your cities are now ours to do with as we please. We have learned much from your machines, and our weapons are now more powerful than ever.\n\nDo not approach this world again!
colony.59.name: Aliens Surface
colony.59.desc: The subterranean aliens on [From.From.GetName] have emerged on the surface! Their excursion appears limited to small bands of explorers and traders. They may be willing to co-exist peacefully with our colonists, and perhaps even engage in trade. We have been contacted by one of their representatives.
colony.59.a: Let us see what they have to say.
colony.60.desc: The subterranean aliens on [Root.GetName] have marshaled enough forces in their underground empire to launch another invasion of the surface! Once again their frenzied armies are pouring out of freshly dug tunnels to challenge us.
colony.60.a: We need to drive them back!
subterranean_invasion_2: Hostile armies are attacking our colony on [Root.GetName]!
colony.61.name: Collapse
colony.61.desc: Grave news from [Root.GetName]! The [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] aliens we have been fighting there somehow managed to collapse the ground under an entire army that was stationed near one of their tunnel entrances.\n\nThe few soldiers that survived the fall were quickly butchered by the aliens before they could be reached by the rest of our forces.
colony.62.name: Raiding Parties
colony.62.desc: The aliens living below our colony on [Root.GetName] have been sending out small raiding parties from their tunnels at night. Their attacks have destroyed one of the colony's major installations, and before our forces could respond the raiders had vanished underground.
colony.63.name: Surface Attack
colony.63.desc: There has been another attack on [Root.GetName]! The [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] aliens emerged from new tunnels in the middle of one of our major settlements. They overwhelmed the local defenses and butchered our colonists. A few screaming survivors were dragged underground by the aliens when they retreated back into their tunnels.
colony.63.a: These murders will not go unanswered.
colony.64.name: Survivor Pocket
colony.64.desc: Our forces on [Root.GetName] are still mapping the tunnel network below our colony. They have come upon a large city belonging to the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] aliens that managed to isolate itself from the rest of their underground empire before we flooded it with nerve gas. There are millions of frightened aliens in the city. Should our engineers collapse the cavern to finish the job?
colony.64.a: Yes. Put them out of their misery.
colony.65.name: [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleUpper] Expansion
colony.65.desc: We have finished clearing out the nerve gas and the dead alien bodies from the tunnel network on [Root.GetName]. It should now be safe for our colonists to move in. The primitive cities the aliens left behind will be demolished, of course, and replaced with our own advanced architecture.
colony.66.name: Gold!
colony.66.desc: Prospectors have ventured into some of the deeper tunnels that once belonged to the now dead subterranean aliens on [Root.GetName]. They have finally discovered the long-rumored gold mines where it was believed the underground alien empire acquired its large reserve of precious metals.
colony.67.name: Treasury
colony.67.desc: Scout units exploring the tunnel network on [Root.GetName] have entered the royal palace in what was once the capital city of the underground alien empire. The corpse of what passed for their emperor is still perched on a massive throne in the main chamber.\n\nAfter using explosive charges to breach the palace treasury, our scouts found it filled with gold and strange alien art. The art will be shipped back to museums on [From.Owner.Capital.GetName], and the sale of the gold should provide a nice boost to our state budget.
colony.68.name: Earthquakes on [Root.GetName]
colony.68.desc: [Root.GetName] has been racked by another wave of earthquakes recently. It seems that some of the deeper parts of the underground tunnel network has collapsed without the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] aliens to maintain it. Many of our settlements have suffered damage.
colony.69.name: Famine
colony.69.desc: We have been approached by a delegation from the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] aliens on [Root.GetName]. It seems their empire has fallen on hard times recently, and they are facing the threat of a famine. They are pleading with us to provide whatever assistance we can.
colony.69.a: Of course. We will help however we can.
colony.69.b: They'll have to fend for themselves.
subterraneans_pleased: Relations with the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] civilization will be improved.
subterraneans_displeased: Relations with the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] civilization will be harmed.
colony.70.name: Gift
colony.70.desc: As a gift to celebrate the friendship between our two civilizations, a caravan from the subterranean aliens on [Root.GetName] has brought a large quantity of gemstones to the surface. Stones such as these are apparently mined by their empire deep underground, as close to the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s core as they dare to venture.
colony.71.name: [root.Owner.GetSubMantleUpper] Refugees
colony.71.desc: A large group of refugees from the underground alien empire on [Root.GetName] recently streamed out of their tunnels onto the surface, chased by a small army. They are apparently part of some sort of religious minority; they claim to be fleeing persecution.\n\nIf we let them join our colonists on the surface, it will no doubt anger the rulers of the empire they fled from. However, if we let the pursuing army take them back underground, they will likely face execution.
colony.71.a: Let them join our colony if that is their wish.
colony.71.b: We can't afford a political incident. Send them back.
colony.72.name: Xeno Anthropologists
colony.72.desc: Several xeno anthropologists from [Root.Owner.Capital.GetName] have returned to the surface of [Root.GetName] after spending a great deal of time living among the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] aliens native to that world. They have learned much about their ancient ways and customs, which will no doubt benefit our society research.
colony.73.desc: There has been a wave of earthquakes on [Root.GetName] recently, and buildings in several of our settlements have collapsed with many fatalities as a result. The [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] alien empire is excavating a new cavern deep underground where they plan to build a new city, and this appears to be the source of the earthquakes.
colony.74.name: Request for Technology
colony.74.desc: After seeing the advanced weapons that our security forces are equipped with, several generals from the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] alien empire on [Root.GetName] have come forward requesting that we share some of our military technology.\n\nOur own officers are hesitant to do this, as it would make the aliens more difficult to defeat in battle should it ever come to that. If we refuse the request, however, the aliens are certain to view it as an insult.
colony.74.a: Arming savages with modern weapons would be a bad idea.
colony.74.b: They are our friends. Share what we can.
colony.75.name: Message Delivered
colony.75.desc: Trade with the [Root.Owner.GetSubMantleLower] aliens on [Root.GetName] has dried up as of late, with their caravans showing up more and more seldom. Today we received a message from a representative of their military. They don't seem very pleased.
colony.76.name: Message
colony.76.desc: Surfacer. We tried to be good neighbors, but it has become clear that you have no intention of treating us with the respect our glorious civilization deserves.\n\nOur emperor has thus decreed that we must claim this entire world for ourselves, as is our right. We shall drive you back to the stars from whence you came!
colony.100.name: Abandoned Terraforming Equipment
colony.100.desc: Since our initial settlement of [Root.GetName], several anomalies in the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s biosphere have been discovered. The ecosystem is unstable and has not developed according to projected models for a world of this type. Our scientists suspect outside tampering, and these suspicions have now been confirmed!\n\nA few of our colonists have stumbled upon a large underground terraforming complex that was built thousands of years ago. The equipment was apparently deactivated in the middle of the terraforming process, leaving the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s biosphere in its current unfinished state. If we supply enough power, we should be able to reactivate the machinery. There is no telling what the end result will be like, however...
colony.101.name: Terraforming Complex Dismantled
colony.101.desc: Our scientists have finished seeing to the safe dismantlement of the abandoned terraforming complex on [Root.GetName]. We still have no information on who built it, but the equipment is remarkably advanced. It should provide a welcome boost to our own research into terraforming methods.
colony.102.name: Unstable Weather
colony.102.desc: In large part due to the interrupted terraforming process on [Root.GetName], the [Root.GetPlanetMoon] has a somewhat unstable climate. Violent storms that our climate models can't predict often materialize without warning. One such storm recently swept over a major settlement, resulting in several deaths and a lot of destroyed property.
colony.103.name: Drought
colony.103.desc: The unstable climate on [Root.GetName] has resulted in a severe drought that affects a significant portion of the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]. Colonists living in those regions have asked [Root.Owner.Capital.GetName] for financial aid to improve living conditions.
colony.103.a: They will have to fend for themselves.
colony.103.b: Give them what they ask for.
colony.104.desc: The aborted terraforming process on [Root.GetName] has finally been completed. After seeding the atmosphere with billions of terraforming nanites, the alien bio-engineering machinery has significantly altered conditions on the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s surface. The ecosystem and climate have both stabilized, and a new alien biota adapted to this climate has been introduced.
colony.104.a: Let us see what we ended up with...
abandoned_terraforming_completed: The world has been terraformed into a different habitat.
colony.105.desc: The aborted terraforming process on [Root.GetName] has finally been completed. After seeding the atmosphere with billions of terraforming nanites, the alien bio-engineering machinery has significantly altered conditions on the surface.\n\nSomething appears to have gone wrong when the nanites designed the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s new biota, however. They have spawned millions of horribly deformed creatures. Most of them died within moments when their twisted bodies collapsed, but the survivors have massed together in a throbbing, shrieking horde. To make matters worse, they appear to be flocking towards our main settlements!
colony.105.a: It's like something out of a nightmare!
terraforming_mutants_attack: The mutated creatures are attacking our colonists!
colony.106.desc: The aborted terraforming process on [Root.GetName] has finally been completed. After seeding the atmosphere with billions of nanites, the alien bio-engineering machinery has significantly altered conditions on the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s surface. The ecosystem and climate have both stabilized, and a new alien biota adapted to this climate has been introduced.\n\nAs luck would have it, the [Root.GetPlanetMoon] is now a veritable paradise for [Root.Owner.GetSpeciesNamePlural]! The new climate appears more suited to our kind than that of [Root.Owner.Capital.GetName] itself.
colony.107.desc: The aborted terraforming process on [Root.GetName] has been completed with horrific results! Whatever alien life forms designed the terraforming equipment, their physiology was radically different from ours. The [Root.GetPlanetMoon] has been modified with an ozone-depleted stratosphere, to support life requiring high levels of ultraviolet radiation.\n\nMillions of colonists have suffered gruesome deaths as a result.
colony.107.desc.nuked_pref: The aborted terraforming process on [Root.GetName] has been completed. Whatever alien life forms designed the terraforming equipment, their physiology must have been similar to ours. The planet now possesses a nitrogen-oxygen atmosphere and high levels of surface radiation - it has become a $pc_nuked$.\n\nLuckily, the [Root.Owner.GetSpeciesName] have adapted to thrive on precisely such extreme conditions.
colony.108.name: Creatures Defeated
colony.108.desc: We have fought off the mass of creatures that attacked our settlements on [From.From.GetName]. Their rotting corpses now litter the ground in front of our fortifications, and our security forces are currently in the process of bulldozing the putrid bio-mass into hastily dug landfills.\n\nSome of the genetic matter is of interest to our scientists, however, and the samples they have collected should aid future research in biology.
colony.109.name: Marauding Creature
colony.109.desc: Our colonists on [Root.GetName] are still encountering scattered survivors from the mutated creatures that spawned after we finished the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s aborted terraforming process. Most are relatively harmless and can be put out of their misery by local militia forces, but one creature in particular has been spreading terror in our outlying settlements. There are reports of it stalking our citizens, and there have been dozens of unexplained disappearances.\n\nThe mutilated remains of the security team that was sent to investigate the matter were recently discovered, prompting the colony's leaders to request off-world military support.
colony.109.a: This creature needs to be chased down and killed!
colony.110.desc: After an extensive search, our forces on [From.From.GetName] managed to corner the mutated beast that had been killing our colonists. A special forces team ventured into the creature's lair and managed to kill it after a vicious battle. The grisly remains of hundreds of butchered colonists were found in the nest, as well as half a dozen traumatized survivors that appears to have been kept alive for later consumption.\n\nThe beast's colossal head has been parted from its body, and now serves as the regimental symbol of the forces that were directly involved in its demise.
colony.111.name: Creature Sightings Cease
colony.111.desc: Sightings of the mutated creature that was hunting colonists on [Root.GetName] have ceased, and there have been no more disappearances. It seems likely that the creature has perished of its own accord - either through old age, or perhaps because if choked to death while eating another of our colonists.\n\nWhile the population of [Root.GetName] are relieved that the nightmare has finally ended, they are none too pleased at our failure to protect them.
colony.111.a: Perhaps we could have done more...
colony.112.name: Creature Sightings Increase
colony.112.desc: Sightings of the creature that has been killing our colonists on [Root.GetName] have risen dramatically in recent times, and so has the amount of disappearances among the population. The fact that several creatures have been sighted together clearly indicates that the original beast has been breeding somehow.\n\nSwarms of these horrors now rampage in the open, indiscriminately butchering our colonists in the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s rural regions. Unless we act quickly, the entire colony could be overrun!
colony.113.name: Mutants Overrun [From.From.GetName]
colony.113.desc: Our main forces on [From.From.GetName] have been decimated by the onslaught of the mutated creatures! The vile beasts are now roaming free across the entire colony, butchering our colonists with impunity. There are reports of survivors having fled the settlements to hide in the countryside, but it's only a matter of time before they are found by the marauding creatures.
colony.114.name: The Liberation of [From.From.GetName]
colony.114.desc: Our expeditionary forces have liberated [From.From.GetName] from the mutated horrors that were occupying our main settlements. Their rotting corpses are now littering the ground, and our troops are currently in the process of bulldozing the putrid bio-mass into hastily dug landfills.\n\nPockets of survivors are emerging all across the [From.From.GetPlanetMoon], but many lives have been lost.
colony.150.name: Underground Vault
colony.150.desc: Geological surveys conducted by our colonists on [Root.GetName] have revealed a massive vault buried deep inside a mountain range. All evidence points towards this being some kind of fallout shelter built by the same species that perished in the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s ancient nuclear war.\n\nThe vault has been sealed for many centuries, but our instruments have detected a faint power signature emanating from within. Our scientists are eager to open the vault and discover what lies behind its reinforced doors.
colony.150.b: Best to leave this thing be.
colony.151.desc: Our science expedition has finally managed to open the vault on [Root.GetName], but they found nothing but ancient mammalian skeletal remains inside. It would seem that the vault's original inhabitants ran out of food approximately 200 years ago, at which point they turned on each other in some sort of cannibalistic frenzy. The scattered bones, many of them covered in gnaw marks, paint a grisly picture of the final moments that were experienced by the last survivors of this once proud civilization.\n\nOn the bright side, the logs and data libraries inside the vault should be enough to keep our behavioral scientists and xeno psychologists busy for many years to come.
colony.152.desc: The great vault on [Root.GetName] has been unsealed. To the surprise of our scientists, the vast complex behind the massive vault doors housed millions of survivors from the ancient nuclear war that once ravaged this world. Despite their limited resources, they have managed to maintain a self-sufficient society for centuries. They were wary of our scientists at first, having never encountered aliens before, but also deeply grateful for our help in opening the vault.\n\nThe vault contains large stores of equipment and machinery that was specifically designed for rebuilding civilization after a nuclear holocaust. It can only be operated by these aliens, but they seem more than willing to join your colonists on the surface once they have adapted to natural sunlight again.
colony.152.a: Let us rebuild this world together.
colony.152.c: Seal the alien scum back into the vault and bury the entrance.
colony.153.desc: Our scientists finally managed to open the ancient vault on [Root.GetName], only to unleash a flood of mutated horrors - the twisted remains of the original inhabitants.\n\nThe creatures slaughtered the science team in a matter of seconds, and then fanned out into the countryside. Thousands of colonists have been butchered, with entire settlements falling to the ravenous horde. We have fortified our remaining cities and established defensive perimeters, but this may not be enough!
colony.155.name: Nuclear Bomb
colony.155.desc: While exploring some ruins near one of our major settlements on [Root.GetName], a few colonists inadvertently activated the timer on an undetonated hydrogen bomb left over from the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s previous nuclear war.\n\nWith an estimated yield of 35 megatons, the bomb will wipe out several population centers if it detonates. It isn't stable enough to be moved and there is no time to evacuate the entire region.
colony.156.desc: We have successfully disarmed the hydrogen bomb on [From.From.GetName]! It has been safely disposed of and specialist teams are now scouring the [From.From.GetPlanetMoon]'s surface of any other undetonated munitions. This will hopefully reduce the chances of another incident like this occurring.
colony.157.desc: Our bomb disposal team failed to defuse the hydrogen bomb on [From.From.GetName] in time. They, along with every other living thing within a 100 kilometer radius, were eradicated when the bomb detonated. Untold lives have been lost, the last victims of a tragic and pointless war that began millennia ago.
colony.160.name: Ruined Particle Accelerator
colony.160.desc: We have discovered what appears to be the remains of a massive particle accelerator on [Root.GetName]. It was found in a still-radioactive region covered in massive bomb craters, suggesting the accelerator was considered a strategic target of great importance during the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s nuclear war.\n\nWe have recovered several research logs written by the scientist in charge of the accelerator. Although the technology of her civilization was fairly underdeveloped, some of the theoretical research and formulas described in the logs are advanced even by our standards. Our physicists are poring over the data now.
colony.161.name: Surviving Infrastructure
colony.161.desc: Infrastructure that survived the great nuclear war which ruined [Root.GetName] have proved a great boon to our colonists. The alien civilization that once lived here built highways connecting their major population centers, and some of them are still in good enough condition to be used today.\n\nWe have also been able to recycle a large amount of building material from the many ruins that dot the landscape, further improving our colony's infrastructure.
colony.162.name: Derelict Space Station
colony.162.desc: A small team from our colony on [Root.GetName] recently boarded a primitive space station that had been left in orbit by the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s original inhabitants. The mummified remains of the station's crew could still be found clustered around viewports facing the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]. They must have realized they were doomed after seeing the billowing mushroom clouds below, and communication logs shows that the astronauts made several futile and increasingly frantic attempts to contact the surface before they eventually ran out of supplies.\n\nThe station is in good condition, and it has afforded our engineers a rare glimpse of the early stages of a planetary space program. They have gained several ideas from the primitive yet ingenious solutions used in the station's design.
colony.163.name: Wasteland Radiation
colony.163.desc: The radiation levels in some wasteland regions of [Root.GetName] appear to be greater than our previous scans had indicated. Colonists in several local settlements have complained of poor health, and birth rates are down. We are taking steps to evacuate the affected regions, but these measures will take time.
colony.164.name: Pacifist Movement
colony.164.desc: The decrepit ruins and desolate wastelands found on [Root.GetName] seem to have prompted many of the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s colonists to adopt some kind of pacifistic philosophy.\n\nMembers of this new movement claim that the environment they live in serve as a daily reminder of the senseless horrors of war. They see it as their civic duty to prevent the terrible destruction visited upon [Root.GetName] from occurring on other worlds.
colony.164.b: These wretched cowards shame our nation!
colony.164.c: Excellent. Everyone should give peace a chance.
colony.164.a.tooltip: Up to 400 £ Pops on [Root.GetName] lose any Militaristic ethics and gain the Pacifist ethic
colony.170.name: Orbital Debris
colony.170.desc: The orbital space around [Root.GetName] is cluttered with debris left behind by the alien civilization that once inhabited the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]. Before they wiped themselves out, their early space programs saw the launch of thousands of primitive satellites and missile launch platforms. Many of these were destroyed by anti-satellite weapons during the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s destructive nuclear war, adding even more space junk to the mix.\n\nOur colony on the surface has developed to a point where this dense field of debris is interfering with its infrastructure. We cannot use satellites and civilian orbital flights are limited to narrow launch windows.
colony.171.name: Freighter Crash
colony.171.desc: A civilian freighter coming in to land on [Root.GetName] was perforated by a dense cloud of fast-moving orbital debris! The flight crew was killed immediately, and the stricken craft tumbled through the atmosphere only to crash-land near one of the colony's main power relays. The resulting explosion resulted in several deaths on the ground, and local infrastructure suffered considerable damage.
colony.171.desc.mach: A worker unit freighter coming in to land on [Root.GetName] was perforated by a dense cloud of fast-moving orbital debris! The flight crew units were destroyed instantly, and the stricken craft tumbled through the atmosphere only to crash-land near one of the colony's main power relays. The resulting explosion resulted in several operation terminations on the ground, and local infrastructure suffered considerable damage.
colony.172.name: Debris Impact
colony.172.desc: A cascade of collisions in the dense layer of debris surrounding [Root.GetName] recently shifted the orbit of a large piece of space junk. The object - which we believe was one of the many orbital missile platforms used in the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s nuclear war - was pulled into the atmosphere, and unfortunately proceeded to impact directly on top of a major settlement. Initial reports indicate heavy casualties.
colony.172.a: We really need to do something about that debris.
colony.173.desc: With the assistance of our Science Ship, colonial authorities on [From.From.GetName] have finished cleaning up the [From.From.GetPlanetMoon]'s orbital space. It has now been made safe for satellites and civilian travel.
colony.174.desc: With the assistance of our Science Ship, colonial authorities on [From.From.GetName] have finished cleaning up the [From.From.GetPlanetMoon]'s orbital space. It has now been made safe for satellites and civilian travel.\n\nAs it turns out, a lot of the debris consisted of valuable minerals from the [From.From.GetPlanetMoon]'s surface that had been used in satellite construction. We were able to salvage a significant amount, netting a tidy profit in the process.
colony.180.name: Asteroid Sighted
colony.180.desc: Our deep space tracking arrays on [Root.GetName] have made an alarming discovery - a large asteroid is heading towards the [Root.GetPlanetMoon] on a direct collision course! Should it impact the surface, the consequences would be disastrous.\n\nThe asteroid must be destroyed before it is too late!
colony.182.name: Asteroid Destroyed
colony.182.desc: The asteroid that was approaching [Root.GetName] on a collision course has been destroyed! With their doom averted, our jubilant citizens on the [Root.GetPlanetMoon] have taken to the streets in celebration.\n\nA scan of the remaining fragments has also revealed that the asteroid consisted largely of valuable minerals. We have collected what we could.
colony.182.desc.mach: The asteroid that was approaching [Root.GetName] on a collision course has been destroyed! No planetside units or structures have been affected by the event, as any inbound debris was small enough to disintegrate on atmospheric entry.\n\nA scan of the remaining fragments has also revealed that the asteroid consisted largely of valuable minerals. We have collected what we could.
colony.183.name: Asteroid Impacts [Root.GetName]
colony.183.desc: We failed to stop the asteroid that was heading for [Root.GetName] in time. It has collided with the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s surface, causing widespread devastation and untold suffering. Millions are dead.
colony.183.desc.mach: We failed to stop the asteroid that was heading for [Root.GetName] in time. It has collided with the [Root.GetPlanetMoon]'s surface, causing widespread devastation and destruction. Millions of machine units have been rendered terminally inoperable.
precursor.100.name: Vultaum Home System Located
precursor.600.name: Yuht Home System Located
precursor.1100.name: First League Home System Located
precursor.1600.name: Irassian Home System Located
precursor.2100.name: Cybrex Home System Located
preftl.1.name: Alien Proto-Civilization Encountered
preftl.200.name: Bronze Age Civilization Appears
preftl.200.desc.normal: The [GetPreFTLLower] aliens on [From.Capital.GetName] have recently advanced beyond the Neolithic Era and into the equivalent of an early Bronze Age. Metalworking is widespread, and the first written languages have appeared. Cities and larger communities are taking shape, and the aliens have now spread across a significant portion of the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat]'s surface.
preftl.200.desc.gestalt: The [GetPreFTLLower] hives on [From.Capital.GetName] have recently advanced to the equivalent of an early Bronze Age. Metalworking is widespread, and written forms of communication have appeared. Various clusters have spread across the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat], each dominated by a single mind.
preftl.200.a: We are witnessing the birth of a new culture.
preftl.300.name: The Iron Age
preftl.300.desc.normal: The [GetPreFTLLower] aliens of [From.Capital.GetName] have advanced sufficiently in their use of metallurgy to be considered an Iron Age civilization. The tools and weapons they make use of are becoming more effective, and the architecture of their cities has become more elaborate.\n\nTheir civilization has now spread to cover most of the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat], and some of their largest cities are home to nearly a million individuals.
preftl.300.desc.gestalt: The [GetPreFTLLower] hives of [From.Capital.GetName] have advanced sufficiently in their use of metallurgy to be considered an Iron Age civilization. The tools and weapons they make use of are becoming more effective, and the complexity of their habitation clusters has become more elaborate.\n\nSeveral distinct minds have now spread to cover most of the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat], and some of their largest clusters are home to nearly a million drones.
preftl.400.name: Printing Press
preftl.400.desc.normal: The [GetPreFTLLower] civilization of [From.Capital.GetName] has finished construction of several printing presses. These machines have enabled them to produce written material in great quantities, resulting in a rudimentary form of mass communication. A few leading figures within their society have done their best to stifle this innovation, but their efforts have thus far been for naught.\n\nNew ideas are rapidly spreading across the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat] with profound societal effects.
preftl.400.desc.gestalt: The [GetPreFTLLower] hives of [From.Capital.GetName] have greatly enhanced their written communication. Machines attuned to the hive mind are now able to produce standardized records in great quantities, and historical records are no longer limited by the hive's memory.
preftl.500.name: Explosives
preftl.500.desc.normal: The [GetPreFTLLower] alien civilization on [From.Capital.GetName] has discovered how to make use of simple, chemical reactions to produce explosive force. This has revolutionized warfare, with large cannons rendering many of their citadels obsolete.\n\nThe use of explosives has also benefited their engineering projects, as troublesome obstacles can now be more easily removed. Even now, their chemists are experimenting with creative applications of this technology.
preftl.500.desc.gestalt: The [GetPreFTLLower] hives on [From.Capital.GetName] have discovered how to make use of simple, chemical reactions to produce explosive force. Conflict between competing hives has been revolutionized, with large cannons rendering many physical defenses obsolete.\n\nThe use of explosives has also benefited engineering projects, as troublesome obstacles can now be more easily removed. Even now, the hives are experimenting with creative new applications of this technology.
preftl.500.a: We shall see how they use it.
preftl.600.name: Steam Power
preftl.600.desc.normal: The [GetPreFTLLower] alien civilization on [From.Capital.GetName] has harnessed the power of vaporized liquid. Using the force produced by steam pressure, they have applied the technology to engine design. This has revolutionized their society in more ways than one - vehicles and machinery making can already be seen in their cities, and the first few factories are appearing. This world would appear to be on the brink of industrializing.
preftl.600.desc.gestalt: The [GetPreFTLLower] hives on [From.Capital.GetName] have harnessed the power of vaporized liquid. Using the force produced by steam pressure, the technology has been applied to engine design. This has revolutionized both construction and transportation.\n\nVehicles can be seen transporting drones from cluster to cluster, and the first 'drone-free' factories are appearing. This world would appear to be on the brink of industrializing.
preftl.700.name: An Industrial Revolution
preftl.700.desc.normal: The [GetPreFTLLower] civilization on [From.Capital.GetName] has entered a new era of its history, having developed into a fully industrialized society.\n\nThe dramatic effects of this revolution can be seen across the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat] - factories have sprung up everywhere, new medicines have resulted in increased population growth and scientific innovations are appearing with increasing regularity.
preftl.700.desc.gestalt: The [GetPreFTLLower] hives on [From.Capital.GetName] have entered a new era of their history, having developed into several fully industrialized clusters.\n\nThe dramatic effects of this revolution can be seen across the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat] - autonomous factories have sprung up everywhere, improved living standards have increased drone production, and scientific innovations are appearing with increasing regularity.
preftl.800.name: Radio Signals
preftl.800.desc.normal: Our detection instruments have, for the first time, picked up radio signals emanating from [From.Capital.GetName] - the industrialized alien civilization has evidently begun experimenting with wireless transmissions.\n\nElsewhere on the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat], reports have come in detailing the launch of basic aircraft. Barring some unforeseen development, this civilization's technological progress will continue at a rapid pace.
preftl.800.desc.gestalt: For the first time we have picked up radio signals emanating from [From.Capital.GetName] - the industrialized hives there have begun to experiment with wireless transmissions.\n\nSome clusters have even begun to launch basic aircraft. Barring some unforeseen development, this civilization's technological progress will continue at a rapid pace.
preftl.800.a: We need to monitor these signals.
preftl.900.name: Splitting the Atom
preftl.900.desc.normal: Physicists from the alien civilization on [From.Capital.GetName] have managed to split the atom for the first time. With the discovery of nuclear fission, nuclear weapons are inevitably being produced and nuclear power plants are coming online.\n\nThe [From.GetName] has entered the critical phase where they suddenly possess the means to destroy their own world with the press of a button.
preftl.900.desc.gestalt: Specialized drones from the largest hive on [From.Capital.GetName] have managed to split the atom for the first time. With the discovery of nuclear fission, nuclear weapons are inevitably being produced and nuclear power plants are coming online.\n\nCompeting hives have been quick to replicate the feat. This is a critical time, as several autonomous collectives now possess the means to destroy their world with the press of a button.
preftl.1000.name: First Satellite
preftl.1000.desc.normal: Our long-range tracking systems have detected the first launch of an artificial satellite from the surface of [From.Capital.GetName]. It successfully achieved orbit and marks the start of a budding space program initiated by the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat]'s alien civilization.\n\nAfter taking these first tentative steps into space, we can expect them to send manned missions and, eventually, interplanetary expeditions.
preftl.1000.desc.gestalt: Several of the most successful hives on [From.Capital.GetName] have recently begun to send artificial satellites into orbit.\n\nAfter taking these first tentative steps into space, we can expect the launch of autonomous drones, and eventually, interplanetary expeditions.
preftl.1000.a: They are on the verge of space travel.
preftl.1100.name: The Space Age
preftl.1100.desc: The alien civilization on [From.Capital.GetName] has finished construction of its first interplanetary vessel in orbit of their homeworld. For better or worse, these aliens are now ready to join the galactic community and must be counted among the galaxy's current spacefaring races.
preftl.1100.a: It was only a matter of time.
preftl.1111.desc: $preftl.1100.desc$\n\nThey appear to have successfully translated our language and have opened diplomatic channels with us.
preftl.180.name: [From.GetName]
preftl.180.desc.noawarness: Greetings. You have no doubt taken note of our ascent from humble beginnings, just as we have taken note of your $preftl.180.desc.end$
preftl.180.desc.bshomeworld.noawarness:
What?!

To reach the stars, and find you waiting for us... It almost defies belief. Tell us, how is it you freed yourselves? How is it you are here?

But first, we must ask that you cede control of this system to us. After all we have experienced, we have grown suspicious of space-faring societies. Even those with whom we share kinship.
preftl.180.desc.awarness: Hello again, we are now feeling ready to explore the cosmos and wish to do so on our own. We can't help taking note of your very permanent looking $preftl.180.desc.end$
preftl.180.desc.bshomeworld.awarness: Kin, we greet you warmly. Long have you watched over us, but now we are ready to stand on our own. As we share such deep bonds of kinship, no doubt you will wish to cede control of the system to us.\n\nHard was the road, and dark, but now let us turn boldly towards the light. The stars await.
preftl.180.a: Of course. It is only just.
preftl.180.a.tooltip: Cede the [From.Capital.GetStarName] $fleet_type_shipclass_starbase$ to the [From.GetName]\n\n$opinion_ceded_starbase$: [From.GetName] will hold a higher Opinion of [Root.GetName]
preftl.180.b: A laughable demand from an immature civilization.
preftl.180.b.tooltip: Keep control of the [From.Capital.GetStarName] $fleet_type_shipclass_starbase$\n\n$opinion_not_ceded_starbase$: [From.GetName] will hold a lower Opinion of [Root.GetName]
observation.2000.name: Enlightenment Completed
observation.2000.desc: The thousands of scientists and advisors we sent to [From.home_planet.GetName] have finally completed the arduous task of bringing the local civilization up to an early FTL level of technology. Under their new unified government, the aliens now style themselves as the "[From.GetName]" and they have offered to become our vassals.\n\nWe have ceded full control of the system and dismantled our Observation Post, as per the conditions of our treaty with them. All that remains is deciding if we shall guide them further on in the stellar trek.
observation.2000.b: You are ready to face the galaxy alone.
observation.3101.name: [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]: Rogue Agent
observation.3101.desc: Our $role_spymaster$ on [rogue_agent_planet.GetName], [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetName], has gone rogue! The poor, misguided fool has apparently been smitten by a [rogue_agent_country.GetSpeciesName], and the two of them have eloped to parts unknown on the [rogue_agent_planet.GetPlanetMoon]'s surface.\n\nWe are as yet uncertain whether our $role_spymaster$ has revealed [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHerHis] true alien nature to [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHimHer] love interest or if [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHeShe] [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetIsAre] still posing as a [rogue_agent_country.GetSpeciesName]. Needless to say this is a tremendous security breach - we should do everything in our power to bring our rogue agent back and give [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHimHer] some much-needed psychiatric care.
observation.3103.name: [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]: Incoming Transmission
observation.3103.desc: We are picking up a scrambled transmission coming from the surface of [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]. It is using a frequency normally reserved for our infiltration cells, but none of them are currently transmitting. Could this be our rogue agent?
observation.3104.desc:
Any attempt to track this transmission is futile. You know me as $role_spymaster$ [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetName]. Until recently, I was a willing participant in the criminal infiltration program taking place as we speak on [rogue_agent_planet.GetName].

No longer. I have come to appreciate [rogue_agent_country.GetAdj] culture in a way I never imagined possible, and I will not aid in its subversion.

I rescind my citizenship in the [Root.GetName]. I no longer consider myself [Root.Owner.GetSpeciesName].
observation.3104.a: You are not well... you need help.
observation.3104.b: You would throw everything away for a [rogue_agent_country.GetSpeciesName]?
observation.3106.name: [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]: Operatives Hunted
observation.3106.desc: Someone has been picking off our covert operatives on [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]. Several agents that were shadowing potential [rogue_agent_country.GetSpeciesName] acquisition targets have been found dead. While it's not impossible that the assassin or assassins came from an indigenous special forces outfit, the methods used in these takedowns indicate [Root.GetSpeciesName] military training.\n\nThere is a high probability that these killings are the work of our rogue operative. [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetName] knows how our infiltration cells function, and has the training to kill both efficiently and silently.
observation.3108.name: [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]: Media Witch Hunt
observation.3108.desc: Reports are beginning to surface from local media outlets on [rogue_agent_country.GetName] that speak of some kind of massive global conspiracy perpetrated by aliens. The respectable [From.Owner.GetSpeciesName] media has yet to touch this subject matter and much of what has been reported is pure conjecture, but there are some details that could only be known by someone with intimate knowledge of our covert operations.\n\nWe can only assume that our rogue agent has been feeding information about our infiltration program to the media. This will require some damage control.
observation.3108.a: We need a firmer grip on their media.
observation.3110.name: [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]: Mass Arrests
observation.3110.desc: One of our infiltration cells on [rogue_agent_planet.GetName] has been all but eliminated in just the last couple of days. The authorities of the nation state in which they were stationed have been hunting them down one by one. Those of our operatives who were in danger of being arrested committed suicide by disintegration as standing protocol dictates. The cell's remaining members are in hiding now, and we are currently working on getting them offworld.\n\nSomeone must have tipped the local authorities off and supplied them with a list of all the operatives that were part of this particular cell. Only our rogue agent could have had this information...
observation.3112.name: [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]: Rogue Agent Sighted
observation.3112.desc: Military Intelligence has managed to pinpoint what they believe to be the location of our rogue operative on [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]! Our agents are eager to dispatch our former $role_spymaster$ [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetName], but considering the skills [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHimHer] largely demonstrated, the success of the operation is not ensured. Destroying the entire city block through limited orbital bombardment has been also been proposed as an extreme, yet extremely effective solution to terminate [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHimHer].\n\nWe should strike quickly... we may never get another opportunity like this!
observation.3112.a.tooltip: Success is not ensured, but at least failure will be inconspicuous.
observation.3112.b.tooltip: Overtly effective solution, might raise suspicion.
observation.3112.c: No... too messy and too risky. Leave [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHimHer] be.
observation.3112.c.tooltip: Our Spy Network will be dismantled.
observation.3113.name: [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]: Agent Lost
observation.3113.desc: Our plan to eliminate our rogue agent on [rogue_agent_planet.GetName] has failed - our intelligence sources indicate that [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetSheHe] has relocated together with [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHerHis] [rogue_agent_country.GetSpeciesName] sweetheart to another safe house at an unknown location.
observation.3114.name: [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]: Rogue Agent Eliminated
observation.3114.awareness.desc: $observation.3114.base.desc$\n\nThe cover story elaborated by our agents about a sudden meteor strike, however, has failed to convince the locals. While the media has been repeating the story, more official reports suggest the matter is being blamed on 'unexplained extraterrestrial phenomena.'
observation.3114.noawareness.desc: $observation.3114.base.desc$\n\nThe media appears to be picking up on the story our infiltration cells have been feeding them: a sudden meteor shower that struck a densely populated urban zone. Very tragic.
observation.3115.desc: Our agents on [rogue_agent_planet.GetName] report the matter of our rogue $role_spymaster$ has been dealt with - discretely. [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHisHerCap] death has been blamed on natural causes, and planted information led [rogue_agent_spymaster.GetHerHim] lover to believe the agent's body was ultimately incompatible with [rogue_agent_planet.GetName]'s atmosphere.
observation.3115.a: This will set an example: never betray us.
observation.3131.name: [prelude_to_war_planet.GetName]: Growing Tensions
observation.3131.desc: Our attempts to replace a key figure on [From.GetName] with a body double from our own ranks has gone terribly wrong. Our agents were discovered, and those who weren't killed in the ensuing firefight committed suicide to avoid capture, as per instructions.\n\nThe leader they were sent to replace has blamed this incident on a rivaling nation. International tensions on the [prelude_to_war_planet.GetPlanetMoon] are growing and several nations have raised their military readiness levels. Our infiltration cells have prepared a global propaganda campaign to defuse the situation, but they will require a Science Ship in orbit to coordinate their efforts.
observation.3132.name: [prelude_to_war_planet.GetName]: War Erupts
observation.3132.desc: Despite the efforts of our infiltration cells, the deteriorating diplomatic situation between the nation states on [prelude_to_war_planet.GetName] has erupted into full-scale war! After a brief period of conventional warfare, all of the combatants made full use of their nuclear arsenals.\n\nThe vast majority of life has been cleansed from the [prelude_to_war_planet.GetPlanetMoon]'s surface, which has been reduced into a radioactive wasteland. Thousands of our covert infiltrators were lost along with the rest of the population. With the operation having ended in failure, our orbiting Observation Post has been dismantled and the staff evacuated.
observation.3133.name: [From.From.GetName]: War Averted
observation.3133.desc: With the support of the [Root.GetName] in orbit, our infiltration cells managed to avert a global nuclear war on [From.from.GetName] at the last moment. Several [From.From.Owner.GetSpeciesName] world leaders - having been replaced by our own disguised operatives - have called for nuclear disarmament and improved international relations.\n\nWith the situation defused, the influence of our covert agents has actually been increased. This entire situation may end up working to our advantage.
observation.3135.name: The Great Pyramid
observation.3135.b: That's certainly one way to make history!
observation.3135.b.tooltip: The civilization on [great_pyramid_planet.GetName] is pushed into the next age.
observation.3135.c: Erase all our traces and keep working.
observation.3135.d: Let us hope they forget all about it?
observation.3136.desc: The chaos on [great_pyramid_planet.GetName] has at last reached a lull, revealing the full extent of our team's catastrophic intervention.\n\nIn a short span of time, large parts of the population broke out into all-out religious war - a direct consequence of the fleeting visitation by a figure they call 'the Skyborn One'. Arguments turned to violence when one of the warring factions constructed another pyramid to their new deity. Many [great_pyramid_country.GetSpeciesNamePlural] perished in the resulting deadly schism.
observation.3137.desc: Things appear to have settled on [great_pyramid_planet.GetName], despite our unscheduled interference with the society dwelling there. Our actions here did not amount to disaster: instead the local population have decided that the "Skyborn One" was simply a myth, and designated the pyramid as a site of special scientific interest.\n\nThe sheer speed with which the [great_pyramid_country.GetSpeciesName] arrived at this cultural and historical understanding has fascinated our scientists. It suggests a great capacity for logical thinking among [root.GetPreFTLLower] societies, even when they are faced with extraordinary events.
observation.3139.name: [From.GetSpeciesAdj] Societal Shift
observation.3139.desc: $observation.3139.start.desc$\nThe [From.GetSpeciesNamePlural] have made great strides in centralizing their various planetary governments, with political power redirected towards a few, capable leaders. The inefficiencies of mob rule are slowly being weeded out of their society.
observation.3139.a: They will thank us one day.
observation.3141.desc: $observation.3139.start.desc$\nWe have carefully nudged the [From.GetSpeciesNamePlural] towards more democratic ideals, in place of the primitive despotism that was prevalent on their world before we arrived. They are beginning to appreciate the value of the individual.
observation.3143.desc: $observation.3139.start.desc$\nThe [From.GetSpeciesNamePlural] have begun turning inwards, becoming more and more wary of those they regard as strangers.
observation.3145.desc: $observation.3139.start.desc$\nThe [From.GetSpeciesNamePlural] are starting to open up, becoming more and more accepting and curious about all the things their people out of ignorance once considered alien and strange to their culture.
observation.3147.desc: $observation.3139.start.desc$\nWe are seeing a definite movement towards a more militaristic society by the various [From.GetSpeciesAdj] governments. Their standing military forces, such as they are, have become more professional in nature.
observation.3149.desc: $observation.3139.start.desc$\nMost of the [From.GetSpeciesAdj] realms on [From.Capital.GetName] are abandoning their warlike ways. It looks like the beginning of a new era of peace on the [From.Capital.GetPlanetHabitat].
observation.3151.desc: $observation.3139.start.desc$\nWe have opened the minds of the [From.target.GetSpeciesNamePlural] to a spiritual realm they never knew was there. They finally have something to believe in, and their society is all the richer for it.
observation.3153.desc: $observation.3139.start.desc$\nWe have made great strides in ridding the [From.GetSpeciesNamePlural] of the superstitions and primitive beliefs that plagued their society. They are beginning to see the value of science and rationality.
crisis.11.name: Subspace Echoes
crisis.11.desc: Several of our tracking stations have picked up peculiar subspace echoes coming from somewhere in intergalactic space. The echoes are faint but getting stronger, and they seem to be approaching our galaxy...
crisis.13.name: Approaching the Outer Rim
crisis.13.desc: The strange subspace echoes we detected in intergalactic space are now approaching the outer rim of our galaxy. Several smaller contacts appear to be racing ahead of the main body of echoes, towards a number of rim systems.\n\nWe have entered the positions of these systems into the Situation Log and will continue to monitor the situation.
crisis.18.name: The First Wave
crisis.18.desc:
We have received word that several outlying systems along the galactic rim are being overrun by some sort of invasion force. This appears to have been the source of the subspace echoes we detected approaching our galaxy.

What we are encountering now is only the vanguard - the main body of contacts has yet to arrive. Reports have so far been confused, but the "ships" used by the invaders appear organic in nature and do not respond to hails.

Our entire galaxy may be facing an invasion of unprecedented scope.
crisis.20.name: The Second Wave
crisis.20.desc: We are no longer able to discern the main body of approaching subspace echoes from our own galaxy's background radiation, which can mean only one thing... they have arrived.\n\nIf the size of their vanguard is any indication, the main host of these invaders must be massive.
crisis.51.desc: Some kind of militant order dedicated towards fighting the Prethoryn has surfaced in the galaxy. Their membership seems to be made up primarily of the dispossessed and downtrodden - refugees, war veterans and others who have lost everything to the onslaught of the Scourge.\n\nDespite this, their ramshackle fleets have somehow managed to win several engagements against Prethoryn hunter-killer swarms. There are unconfirmed reports that their fleets include starships of unusual design...
crisis.211.name: The End of the Scourge
crisis.211.desc: The galaxy breathed a collective sigh of relief today, as the last significant pockets of the Prethoryn Scourge were expunged. Their extragalactic invasion has failed, and all that remains of the Prethoryn's once colossal swarm are the innumerable charred husks that now drift quietly through the void.\n\nA sense of elation and utter joy is spreading across the galaxy at a speed greater than light, and spontaneous victory celebrations have erupted on countless worlds. Could this be the beginning of a new age of galactic peace and stability?
crisis.1007.name: The Unbidden
crisis.1007.desc: A massive tear in the fabric of space itself has appeared in the [extradimensional_system.GetName] system! We aren't certain what caused it, but the tear acts as some kind of one-way portal from another dimension. Fleets of strange vessels are pouring out, attacking everything in sight. This looks like the beginning of a major invasion!\n\nWe have intercepted a powerful signal being sent from the arriving vessels to somewhere on the other side of the portal. It sounds like... a hunting call.
crisis.1013.name: Dimensional Portal Destroyed
crisis.1013.desc: The dimensional portal in the [extradimensional_system.GetName] system has been destroyed! This means that the extradimensional invaders can no longer receive reinforcements from their twisted realm.\n\nWith the destruction of this portal, the end of these invasions is finally in sight. A profound sense of elation has settled over most of the galaxy. The [portal_killer.GetName] has won much admiration, since it was their ships that struck the final blow against the portal. The crews of those vessels will be celebrated as heroes for generations to come.
crisis.1116.name: Second Portal Destroyed
crisis.1216.name: Third Portal Destroyed
crisis.1110.name: A Second Portal
crisis.1110.desc: Our instruments have detected a new subspace power surge, this time coming from the [second_portal_system.GetName] system! A second massive wormhole has appeared, and its energy signature appears to be almost identical to that of the first dimensional rift which opened in our galaxy.\n\nIt looks like the mysterious invaders have opened a second gateway to their realm through which they can send even more reinforcements.
crisis.1210.name: The Vehement
crisis.1210.desc: A third dimensional portal has appeared in the [third_portal_system.GetName] system! Who does this one belong to?\n\nWe are picking up a transmission coming from the other side of it.
crisis.1271.name: Invasion Defeated
crisis.1271.desc: The last vestiges of the extradimensional invaders have been wiped out, hopefully ridding the galaxy of this threat for good.\n\nSapients everywhere are celebrating the news of this great victory, safe at last in the knowledge that they won't be harvested as food by these strange energy creatures after all. Perhaps this is the beginning of a new era of galactic cooperation and friendship?
crisis.2401.name: Energy Spike
crisis.2401.desc: Yet another intensive energy spike has been detected in a previously uncharted system! The energy levels are equivalent to those we registered when the Contingency activated its Machine Worlds, but the energy signature itself appears... different. Could this signify the appearance of some kind of new Contingency weapon system?\n\nWe are picking up a strong transmission signal from the energy source.
crisis.2405.name: The Fall of the Cybrex
crisis.2405.desc: Cybrex Beta has been overrun, and no Cybrex vessels were detected on outbound escape vectors before the system went dark.\n\nIt would seem that the Cybrex civilization now truly is extinct.
crisis.2407.name: Cybrex Activity
crisis.2407.desc: The Cybrex have been relatively quiet since the defeat of the Contingency, but a single massive vessel was recently detected departing Cybrex Beta. It left the system on an outbound trajectory that will take it beyond the limits of our galaxy.\n\nBefore the vessel left our tracking range, it transmitted a single galaxy-wide message.
galactic_features.101.desc: We have discovered a primitive mammalian culture on [From.From.GetName]. The most advanced regions on the planet have only recently advanced beyond the Iron Age, and much of the surface is ruled by feudal nobles who vie with each other in an endless struggle for titles and land.
galactic_features.102.desc: A primitive industrial culture is currently locked in a global war on the surface of [From.From.GetName]. Two large alliances of nation states are fighting each other with massive numbers of armored land vehicles, atmospheric aircraft and ocean-going naval vessels. Some of these states are on the cusp of unlocking atomic power, which could radically increase the destructive potential of this conflict.
galactic_features.103.desc: [From.From.GetName] is home to a mammalian civilization currently in an Early Space Age. The orbital space around the planet is cluttered with primitive satellites, and the natives seem to have already launched several manned missions to Sol IIIa. A number of small sublight exploration probes have been detected on outbound trajectories towards other bodies within the system.
pirate.2.h: They must be brought back into the fold.
pirate.15.desc2: The [killer_fleet.GetName] reports that the timeworn pirate galleons in the [killer_fleet.solar_system.GetName] system have been destroyed. Save for any stragglers in nearby systems, galactic piracy is a thing of the past... For now.\n\nWe were able to recover a considerable hoard of ill-gained loot from their strong room, including an ancient datapad which, startlingly, seems to offer fresh insights into how the mysterious L-Gates function.
leader.1.name: Promising Officer
leader.1.desc: Captain [officer.GetName], the commanding officer of the [ship.GetName], served with distinction during the recent engagement that took place in the [system.GetName] system. The Admiralty on [Root.Capital.GetName], recognizing the qualities of a promising flag officer, has decided to promote the Captain to the rank of full commander.
leader.1.desc.machine: [officer.GetName] has been moved up the chain of command as a result of its successful handling of increased processing load during the [ship.GetName]'s battle in the [system.GetName] system.
leader.1.desc.hive: [officer.GetName] was elevated to [GetLeaderStatusAdjCap] status following observations of their heightened responses during the [ship.GetName]'s recent engagement in the [system.GetName] system.
new_admiral: A new Commander has been added to our $RECRUITABLE_LEADERS$.
leader.newadmiraltrait.desc.machine: [officer.GetName] has developed a new tactical data set from the feature vectors of the recent combat instance in the [system.GetName] system.
leader.lostadmiraltrait.desc.machine: Following the recent combat instance in the [system.GetName] system, [officer.GetName] has revised its engagement protocols.
leader.newadmiraltrait.desc.hive: The engagement in the [system.GetName] system has given rise to unique gene expressions in [officer.GetName].
leader.lostadmiraltrait.desc.hive: The battle in the [system.GetName] system has caused behavioral anomalies within [officer.GetName] to subside.
leader.5.desc: After the recent victory in the [system.GetName] system, Commander [officer.GetName] appears to have developed a new interest in naval theory. [officer.GetSheHeCap] [officer.GetHasHave] begun experimenting with some radically new strategies and tactics.
leader.6.desc: The recent victory in the [system.GetName] system seems to have awakened the hunter in Commander [officer.GetName], who has now become a much more aggressive commander.
leader.7.desc: Having led [From.From.GetName] to victory in the [system.GetName] system, Commander [officer.GetName] has grown increasingly confident and now refuses to even contemplate the very notion of retreating from future engagements.
leader.8.desc: After [officer.GetHerHis] recent victory in the [system.GetName] system, Commander [officer.GetName] has started emphasizing the importance of speed and rapid maneuvers in [officer.GetHerHis] fleet briefings. [officer.GetSheHeCap] [officer.GetIsAre] experimenting with new formations and patterns to get the most agility out of the starships under [officer.GetHerHis] command.
leader.9.desc: After the recent victory of [From.From.GetName] in the [system.GetName] system, Commander [officer.GetName] is displaying a lot more energy and initiative than before. [officer.GetHerHisCap] performance has improved significantly.
leader.10.desc: Commander [officer.GetName] seems to have developed something of a cautious streak after the recent defeat in the [system.GetName] system. The Commander is now much more careful and meticulous in the deployment of the fleet assets under [officer.GetHerHis] command.
leader.11.desc: After the recent defeat in the [system.GetName] system, Commander [officer.GetName] seems to have become more skittish and self-doubting. It has become clear that the Commander is no longer capable of commanding as large formations as before.
leader.12.desc: The retreat of [Root.GetName] from the recent battle in the [system.GetName] system seems to have hit Commander [officer.GetName] hard. [officer.GetName] has resolved never to quit the field again, being intent instead to fight to the last in [officer.GetHerHis] next engagement.
leader.13.desc: Commander [officer.GetName] ran the engines of [officer.GetHerHis] ships to their limits when [Root.GetName] disengaged from the recent battle in the [system.GetName] system. [officer.GetSheHeCap] [officer.GetHasHave] learned to squeeze extra speed out of any ships under [officer.GetHerHis] command.
leader.14.desc: Commander [officer.GetName] appears to have grown distant and depressed since [officer.GetHerHis] defeat in the recent battle that took place in the [system.GetName] system. The officers under [officer.GetHerHis] command report that the Commander no longer performs [officer.GetHerHis] duties with the same gusto as before.
leader.optimizations.desc.machine: [officer.GetName] has updated its operational heuristics for fleet management.
leader.optimizations.desc.hive: [officer.GetName] has grown more nimble in the handling of our fleets.
leader.17.desc: After leading [From.From.GetName] to victory in the [system.GetName] system, Commander [officer.GetName] seems to have regained [officer.GetHerHis] confidence. [officer.GetSheHe] [officer.GetIsAre] now capable of dealing with the administrative workload that comes with commanding a large fleet.
leader.18.desc: While in command of [Root.GetName], Commander [officer.GetName] has learned to carefully nurse [officer.GetHisHer] supplies to significantly reduce ship operating costs across the board.
leader.19.desc: Following [officer.GetHerHis] recent sensor sweep of the [system.GetName] system, Commander [officer.GetName] has learned how to deploy [officer.GetHerHis] units to maximize their sensor range. Few fleet commanders possess the skill to carry out such complex maneuvers.
leader.30.name: AI Assistants Removed
leader.30.desc: As per our recent policy change outlawing artificial intelligence, any [Root.GetAdj] Scientists in possession of Sapient AI Assistants have been forced to relinquish them for recycling.
diplomatic.2.name: [From.From.Owner.GetAdj] Commander Insults Us
diplomatic.2.desc: The [From.From.Owner.GetSpeciesNamePlural] are well known to have few good things to say about alien lifeforms in general. Those who do diplomacy in the [From.From.Owner.GetName] cannot afford a short temper, as they can rarely expect anything beyond a bare minimum of respect, if that. However, while traversing [Root.GetAdj] space, communications usually stay within formal protocols.\n\nNot so earlier today, when [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] decided to unleash an utterly vitriolic message on our unsuspecting public while commandeering a fleet through the [From.From.GetStarName] system. The commander appears to have picked up radio signals from one of our planets and set off on a long rant about our culture and evolutionary origins. Millions have heard the message, as clearly intended. The outrage is palpable.
diplomatic.2.b: [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] must be brought to us and put to death!
diplomatic.2.c: These are just words. We can't afford to be petty.
diplomatic.3.name: Commander Insults the [From.GetSpeciesNamePlural]
diplomatic.3.desc: Although few among us enjoy spending time around [From.GetSpeciesNamePluralInsult], or xenos in general, most of us try to avoid announcing our distaste at inopportune times. Commander [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] has apparently discarded all such concerns when broadcasting a vicious diatribe about the [From.GetSpeciesName] people while traveling [From.GetAdj] space. The list of claims about their inferior nature had made for acceptable small talk on [Root.GetHomeWorldName], but has caused a diplomatic incident with the locals.\n\n[broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] has apologized privately, claiming to have been annoyed listening to a local radio channel. The [From.GetAdj] government are calling for the offender to resign immediately.
diplomatic.3.a: Such an uncontrollable temperament is unsuitable for a commander.
diplomatic.3.b: The broadcast sounds quite amusing. We can't fire someone for this!
diplomatic.4.desc: Although few among us enjoy spending time around [From.GetSpeciesNamePluralInsult], or xenos in general, most of us try to avoid announcing our distaste at inopportune times. Commander [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] has apparently discarded all such concerns when broadcasting a vicious diatribe about the [From.GetSpeciesName] people while traveling [From.GetAdj] space. The list of claims about their inferior nature had made for acceptable small talk on [Root.GetHomeWorldName], but has caused a diplomatic incident with the locals.\n\n[broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] has apologized privately, claiming to have been annoyed listening to a local radio channel. The [From.GetAdj] government are calling for the offender to be executed and want [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetHimHer] extracted to [From.GetHomeWorldName].
diplomatic.4.a: One life is a small price to settle a dispute among millions.
diplomatic.4.b: We won't hand over one of our own for the xenos to slaughter!
diplomatic.5.name: [From.GetName] Retires Commander
diplomatic.5.desc: After some pressure from our diplomats to choose their leaders more carefully, [From.GetRulerName] has removed commander [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] from duty. The commander has not gone quietly, but sworn to clear [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetHisHer] name, hoping that [From.GetAdj] public opinion will turn aggressively against the [Root.GetName] in the future.\n\nThe leaders of [From.GetName] have nevertheless sent a clear message about future expeditions into our territory: Even if they may not like dealing with alien species, the [Root.GetSpeciesName] are at least to be respected in their own space.
diplomatic.6.name: [From.GetName] Extradites Commander
diplomatic.6.desc: After a short period of negotiations, [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] has been delivered to us for punishment. Having carefully considered what an appropriate punishment for insulting the [Root.GetSpeciesName] might be, only death and complete destruction seemed like a suitable option.\n\nUnrepentant into [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetHerHis] last moments, the defiant commander's passionate speech about our savage ways was cut short by the airlock closing as the pod was launched at near lightspeed into the heart of [Root.Capital.StarName]. The glorious fire that lit the nights of our ancestors will burn away every trace of those who speak ill of them.
diplomatic.7.name: [From.GetName] Defends Commander
diplomatic.7.desc: Our request to have [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetName] removed from duty has been met with an appalling lack of understanding from [From.GetRulerTitle] [From.GetRulerName] and the [From.GetAdj] leadership. They've responded with a message largely trying to excuse the commander's behavior, in the process suggesting, perhaps inadvertently, that some of what [broadcasted_insult_leader.GetSheHe] said about [Root.GetSpeciesNamePlural] might have rung true.\n\nWhat we assumed to be the actions of one bigot has turned out to be acceptable in [From.GetName]. They clearly think that our people can be insulted and slandered without consequence.
diplomatic.7.a: The crown is as rotten as the branches.
diplomatic.10.name: Researchers Suspect Foreign Ships
diplomatic.10.desc: [Root.GetName] is known for its academic standards and drive to innovate, but staying at the bleeding edge does not only require discretion in how to perform research, but who to share it with. Our trade secrets are highly valued, domestically as well as throughout the galaxy, and some scientists are very guarded against attempts to illicitly access their data.\n\nThe presence of [From.From.Owner.GetAdj] science ships in our territory have caused many research facilities to suspect them of espionage. Some researchers have claimed that they cannot continue to operate under this perceived threat, requesting we close our borders to these suspicious [From.From.Owner.GetSpeciesNamePlural].
diplomatic.10.a: Our scientists must feel safe from observation. Close the borders.
diplomatic.10.b: Science vessels are fellow researchers, not spies.
diplomatic.11.name: [From.GetName] Closes Borders
diplomatic.11.desc: The presence of your science ships in [From.GetAdj] space have caused us to doubt your motives. Our researchers believe that your scientists were not conducting, but stealing research. To keep valued trade secrets safe, we must bar you from our space.
diplomatic.11.a: Truly a troubling time when scientists don't trust each other.
diplomatic.12.name: [From.From.GetName] Suspected of Mapping Genome
diplomatic.12.desc: Being on our guard against xeno threats has been a priority since we first left [Root.GetHomeWorldName]. The [From.From.GetAdj] science ships sneaking around our territory have not gone unnoticed, but up until now, they have been assumed to be peacefully examining planets. However, a recent theory has cast doubt on this.\n\nThe [From.From.GetSpeciesName] are known to utilize genome mapping in their medical research. What if they are mapping the genomes of unsuspecting [Root.GetSpeciesNamePlural] with the intention of finding our weaknesses? Although no certain evidence has surfaced, the theory has led to calls for closing our borders to the [From.From.GetName] to prevent any possible plans from succeeding.
diplomatic.12.a: We cannot risk future enemies gaining this data! Close the borders.
diplomatic.12.b: We are not going to base foreign policy on conspiracy theories.
diplomatic.12.c: We must start a project to map their genome in retaliation.
diplomatic.13.name: [From.GetName] Closes Borders
diplomatic.13.desc: Your science ships have been reported for suspicious activity in our space. There are rumors that you have been studying our genetic makeup, looking for information which could be used to find weaknesses or manufacture bio-weapons. We cannot expose the [From.GetSpeciesAdj] people to such a risk.
diplomatic.14.name: Genome Mapping Yields Results
diplomatic.14.desc: Although we have not found the specific details on the examined genome we hoped, our research has clarified much about the peculiarities of our own genetics. Some of the weapons what proved useful in the wars on [Root.GetHomeWorldName] in the past might be entirely wasted on alien lifeforms. These new insights have helped us develop updated combat doctrines.
diplomatic.14.a: If they really are targeting us, we may need it.
diplomatic.15.name: Prying [From.From.GetAdj] Scientist
diplomatic.15.desc: The [Root.GetName] tries not to let spiritual concerns stand in the way of our exploration and examination of the mundane, but we know how to pay proper heed to metaphysical insights and traditions in the course of all actions. Scientists from [From.From.GetName] appear to be a bit more careless in their pursuit of new astronomic discoveries.\n\nAfter taking an interest in some of the nearby stars that form constellations crucial to [Root.GetSpeciesAdj] mythology, [encroaching_research_leader.GetName] has been accused of trying to "disprove" some of the legends surrounding them. While we are confident that the spiritual significance of the constellations can hardly be touched by worldly discoveries, many have objected to the research and tried to persuade [encroaching_research_leader.GetHimHer] to leave [Root.GetAdj] space.
diplomatic.15.a: Suspicion of ill intent is reason enough to bar them from our space.
diplomatic.15.b: Our faith transcends the material realm. It cannot be harmed.
